rating 4.0. Good for transporting full Switch setup Has dedicated spots for Switch, docking station, Pro controller/Grip, and HDMI cable. There’s no natural spot for the power cable and adapter so I squeezed it in on the side. There’s also no spot for games, so they end up bouncing around the case when you transport them.

While I could fit the whole setup in the case, it still has room for improvement by adding a dedicated slot for the power adapter and games.

rating 4.0. Plus I generally like Amazon Basics items I purchased this case as an alternative to the Nintendo branded case to save a few bucks. Plus I generally like Amazon Basics items. When I received the item in came in the normal Amazon Basics packaging, so nothing new there. Upon opening the case and checking it out I was quite pleased with my purchase. The case is large but not too bulky, the material is nice a study on the outside but soft on the inside, and its layout is decent. I do like that I can fit a pro controller in the case which is a major plus.

The side areas of the case however are lacking. They are decently sized and have an elastic strap across them to hold items down. However, it was difficult to fit the charger in and it seem reasonable, there are no spots for extra Joy-Cons to strap down which was a downer.

Overall, I do enjoy this case as it can hold everything needed for your switch assuming that you leave joy cons attached to the tablet and take a pro controller. For this price it is a win!

rating 1.0. Misleading design leaves much to be desired... After trying this for a few short excursions, I have nothing but regret for this product. It is a well-built case, but whoever designed this either made "guesses" regarding the accessories, or was a complete idiot.

1) ZERO options to store ANY physical games securely, other than "one" in the console.

2) The "slot" where one is expected to store the Nintendo OEM power adapter is awkward. I would never want any prongs of a power adapter exposed, and there just is not another way to store it.

3) The console and screen are semi-exposed, which makes any owner somewhat nervous.

4) You can only bring either a Pro Controller OR the JoyCon grip adapter. So anyone with both, (or two Pro Controllers,) must leave one behind.

5) There is room for the OEM Dock, but I do not know anyone hauling their OEM Dock everywhere. I would recommend investing in either a compatible USB C (HDMI) adapter, or any number of third-party docks available for less than Nintendo's $90 option.

Overall, I would give it a solid 4-stars for the quality, but the lack of functionality negates any usefulness and longevity.

I would NOT recommend this to anyone.

input: rating 2.0. Eight dollars too expensive. In three words, how would I describe this product? Cheap, cheap, and cheap.  For $8, you can do far better and get a full silicon grip for the whole controller.  These things barely fit the controller and look to be clearly cracking the moment I put them on.  Compared to other joystick grips and caps I've tried, these are by far the cheapest.  Stay away.

rating 5.0. Great and Unique Design, Multiple Configurations! These are fantastic and the option of different configurations is great. They do not feel to be low quality like a previous reviewer mentioned and showed no signs of cracking or tearing while putting on. They don't slip off, don't even budge at all. This is a great design and a step up from much of the competition out there. I would definitely recommend these.

The joy-cons buttons are fairly close to the analogs, so I didn't feel comfortable using the FPS configuration on the right stick, but that is a matter of personal preference, not a fault of these excellent grips. I'd recommend the pro controller for more control in aiming anyways.

rating 5.0. Excellent quality, and they never slip. These are the best. They never slip off or even move, and the color match is perfect for the red/blue joycons. I use the medium size grip on the left joycon and small grip on the right one. A++ Quality

rating 5.0. Only fits Joycons, but solid grips! It's nice getting a few sets, I wound up settling on the smallest of the three for now. The smooth tops are nice, compared to all the nubby ones for every other controller.

rating 5.0. Makes a Great Product Even Better I will start by saying that the thumbsticks on the joy-con are well-made even out of the box. That said, there isn't any reason to not make them even more functional. I've tried every pair of grips available, and these are by and far my favorite. The FPS Pro grip style is on my joycon to stay. They pave the way for superior accuracy, better grip, and a slightly wider thumb pad (which really helps when you have thick thumbs). I own these in every color, and I can say that they match their intended joycon very well- just as neon and fill of vibrancy as the controller itself. Highly recommended for every switch user.

rating 5.0. Wish I had these from day 1 Ok. These things are amazing. I honestly would give an extra star if I could.

I bought two sets of these (to match a pair of blue neon and red neon joy-cons / his and hers switches) so I could use biggest grip evenly on Left and Right joy-con. These honestly have made me far more reluctant to use the pro controller. And they make handheld mode far more viable. Seriously, since buying these my pro controller is just collecting dust. This product has rendered joy-cons far more functional.

I am an above average height guy with fattish fingers. These sticks have spoiled me so much. I prefer the longest of the three available sizes. With the grips on, I definitely get a Dual Shock PS4 controller vibe. Not completely there, but way closer than without the grip. Somehow using these really made a difference for me. Feel far more capable in many games when using these grips. Really, really like this product, and will apply to any joy-cons I have.

rating 5.0. Both controllers were defective and replacements worked perfectly Used both items less than a hand full of times. Both items worked great when they arrived as they both synced to the system. I put the black one away and used the white one. On the third time i used the white one (20 days later), i noticed that the controller wont connect to the system and keep blinking. I tried to sync it with the Wii U but it doesnt do anything. I turned on the black one and it connects fine. I take the white controller to a family members home and tried to sync it to theirs and it doesnt sync. Definitely defective even though i didnt even use it much. Theres no telling how long the black one will stop syncing and no longer sync. Poor timing on my end as the 30 day has been up since i found this out on the 32nd day. I contacted the seller this morning to see what they can do and havent got a response yet.

Update 1: Seller responded (within a few hours) stating that these items are quality checked before shipping out and this was probably a one off. They are willing to send me another one free of charge. I will post another update as i get the replacement and see if it does the same thing,

Update 2: Four days after i wrote this review, i picked up the black controller to play the Wii U.. no power to the controller, nothing. My first thought was that it was out of battery (even though i charged it completely prior to the last play). I plugged in the charger and it doesnt even light up. So to sum things up..
Observations:
White one, played with around less than an hour of game play, no longer syncs to any system i try
Black one, played with around less than 30 minutes of game play, no longer even turns on..

Not sure if you call this me having bad luck or the product is just not good. I have contacted the seller again regarding the black controller and will update once i hear back from them.

Update 3: After a few weeks of waiting, both my replacement controllers arrived and for the last 5 days i have tested it, it seems to be working fine. It was probably just one of those bad batches that i got.

rating 5.0. My New Favorite Mouse I've been using this mouse for several months now and am entirely content with my purchase.

Pros:
Wireless - supports both 2.4GHz via wireless adapter, as well as bluetooth for use with mobile devices.
Comfort - fits wonderfully in my hand, feels very similar to the Logitech G602 and Steel Series Rival 600.
Battery Life - unbelievable, I've switched batteries a total of only 3 or 4 times in the last 8 months.
Grip - the texture of every part of this mouse is perfect, slightly grippy on the sides, and the perfect amount of light texture on top.
Responsiveness - the tactile feedback on all of the buttons is splendid, sensitive enough to not be annoying, but stiff enough to prevent accidental mouse clicks.

Cons:
Scroll wheel - this mouse lacks a way to release the scroll wheel and have it spin freely, hardly worth mentioning
Standby - after a very short amount of idle time, the mouse will go into power save mode and you may need to move it around for a half second for it to wake back up and register mouse inputs. This is hardly an annoyance as it is partly responsible for the fantastic battery life, i just wish it would wait to idle for a little longer. Important to note though is that this has never EVER caused problems with gaming.

rating 2.0. Left button not registering prolonged clicks. I'm sure it has to do with wear, however, this has happened rather quickly. The mouse registers as if I've let go of the left mouse button even though I am holding it down (no click sound indicating that it is no longer depressed). This becomes problematic when dragging emails, files, basically anything. Trying to select specific text on a web page for example, becomes quite the chore.

Worked great for about 60 days, now it's just aggravating.

rating 1.0. Sent an email on June 8th and so far Nothing! I sent an email on June 8th, no one even bothered to answer yet, my mouse is Not working properly, Battery wont last 2 or 3 days Max, sometimes do crazy things on it own

rating 1.0. disconnects all the time I'll be using the mouse and then all of a sudden it stops working randomly almost everyday. I will not buy a mouse from this company again. I don't recommend this at all.
rating 1.0. Breaks after a month of use After one month of using for MineCraft and COD the right mouse button no longer works. The trigger inside is broken because it's so cheaply made. Do not buy.
rating 1.0. Frick This mouse is small pp do not buy. Bad.

rating 1.0. Expensive for what it gives back I found this mouse to be expensive. I was expecting a better feel, but it is way too lightweight. The wheel is not the best. The wheel was way better on the previous version of the Basilisk. For those who say you need the software to adjust the DPI, they haven't play with the mouse enough. The button on the top adjusts the DPI without software. Also, this mouse is not as nearly as precise as the previous version. I got it because my previous Basilik died just after warranty. I am an old dude with big hands, so I have gone through a lot of mice. There are only two current mice that my hand feels comfortable in. This one and the SteelSeries 500. I went for the wireless this time, I like the ergonomics a lot, but the lightweight, imprecise movement and that awful wheel kind of puts me off. I am going to try it for a little bit more to see if i get used to it. If not, I may get the wired one or the SteelSeries 500. The only problem with the SS500 is longevity. They only last me a year. The Basilisk last me two and are normally at the same price point. The both fail the same way, that left click button.

Edit: Month and half after. Well, after having this for month and half, I have to say that this mouse is not for heavy use. I will replace it and throw in the laptop bag, which I use sporadically. After month and half, the left button started to fail. It hasn't failed completely, but I am starting to get those click gaps that makes the mouse double click or I have to click again to regain operability. I have three monitors, if I start dragging something from the leftmost monitor, it does not reach the mid of the center monitor before it stops responding. Great mouse while it works. That hyperspeed gimmick really works, but having the left button is starting to really get under my nerves. The day starts OK, but about 30 minutes into the work, bang stops working. Then I have to get a cheap Logitech (not a gamming one) to continue working or playing. Which annoys me even more, because it is very slow. I pretty much doubt that I do 50 million clicks in month and half. I haven't had this happen in a while. I don't think this is the same quality as the wired one, or maybe I just been using it too much given that I am working from home and using it 12 hours a day vs the normal 2 or 3. I guess that is what these mice are designed for. More than a couple of hours a day and they fail sooner than expected.

rating 2.0. It Is NOT REchargeable Razer Basilisk X Hyperspeed Wireless mouse is NOT rechargeable. This mouse comes with one replaceable battery and it is not a rechargeable battery. It is a basic model. So NO Optical Switches. You do not really sense the "HyperSpeed" feature it is named after.
When compared to Logitech G502 Proteus Spectrum, Razer Basilisk X HyperSpeed is clearly smaller and lighter than G502.

rating 1.0. Might Be Great - Lousy Support Arrived an out of box failure. Contacted support, they had me fill out a gazillion questions and then they never got back to me.

rating 1.0. Wouldn't pair with its USB dongle Piece of *junk*.  Completely not worth the trouble for the cost.  I went elsewhere and found something more reasonable and workable.

rating 2.0. Goes to sleep EVERY. FIVE. MINUTES. And it takes 5 seconds to wake up every time you click to turn it on again.
You can't just move it to turn it on. Massive hiccup to workflow. And be sure to right click when you wake it up because the cursor disappears so you have no idea what you'll be clicking when you turn it on.
rating 1.0. Horrible Battery life Battery life is horrible and the mouse is constantly losing connection.

rating 1.0. Mouse fails to connect when switching between OS's via Bootcamp I bought this mouse for work and gaming. I spend a lot of time working on my computer, many hours a day. I wanted a mouse that would work for that, and could be used for occasional gaming. I currently use a Logitech G502 (which I love), but wanted something wireless to clean up my desk.

I only used the 2.4Ghz mode, I am not interested in using Bluetooth due to the fact that I use several different computers. I use 2 MacBook Pros, one for work and one personal. On my personal laptop, I switch between Windows and MacOS w/ bootcamp (for gaming). I use a TB3 hub where the mouse dongle stays plugged in all the time. That should allow the mouse to work with both computers and both OSes without issue.

The first day or two, the mouse was great! Very fast response and worked well. However the trouble came when I used it over a longer period of time. I started getting the occasional 'stutter' where the cursor would lock up for 2-3 seconds. It was disappointing but I expected that from a wireless mouse. However, the main issue is that when I would switch computers (by unplugging the TB3 hub from one and plugging the hub into the other computer), the mouse would not be recognized immediately. I also had the same issue when switching between MacOS and Windows. The mouse would not function at all until I removed BOTH the battery from the mouse AND the dongle from the hub and replaced them both. Basically a hard reset. That happened any time I switched OSes and more than 1/2 the time I switched computers. After the 3rd time I returned the mouse and went back to the wired G502.

The G502 has better buttons, the material feels better, everything is better all around except that its NOT WIRELESS. Sadly this mouse sucks and I won't be buying another Razer mouse (I love their other products though).

rating 2.0. Not long lasting Was so excited for this mouse. Got it and what a let down. After 2 months the mouse doesnt work well. It auto shuts off, the right click is Stuckey and goes off when it wants to. Spend the extra money and get a better mouse!! This not for gaming!!

rating 2.0. Poor Quality I bought this mouse for my gaming laptop. The USB transmitter fried within a few uses, so cant use the 2.4 Hz setting and using the device on Bluetooth causes screen shakes on a game like siege cause its poorly optimized. also sucks that its battery powered. thinking about getting the ultimate version but this is putting me off from getting it. terrible mouse.

rating 4.0. A huge leap from cheap common mice. I don't think I've ever really been interested in a gaming mouse. I always thought the idea of a really complex looking mouse was silly. That being said, I had no actual knowledge of things like dpi and mms years ago. Now that I know about them and have a very basic understanding of how they and other aspects of mouse tech works , I can say I both physically and mentally know the difference between this and the cheap $30 logitech mice I've been buying and replacing over the last decade.

For one, the fluidity of the mouse's movement are more than a difference. I never noticed how choppy my cursor was with the basic mice I'd been using; I'd gotten so use to the mouse, at closer glance, "skipping frames" so to speak while moving. Nothing too dramatic for normal use but the difference is all too noticeable when I can almost see every minor jump and wiggle in my hand's movement from left to right on the screen; things I surely have never noticed before, and my hands aren't shakey. For some reason it gives me a sense that I'm moving faster than I think I am too, since even the slightest twitch in movement can move the mouse. It's not unwieldy but I'll definitely have to adjust.

Aside from that, the addition of being able to connect via bluetooth is also pretty neat, and the thumb rest is perfect for me. All-in-all, I like it. I'll certainly be a bit more conscious about the smaller details in these things from now on, if nothing more.

rating 2.0. Nice Mouse, Bad for Games Very good mouse, wouldn't use for gaming, the laser was good ensitive to the point where I would lift the mouse above the mouse pad and it would still move, very bad for gaming

rating 3.0. I really wanted to like it, but it disconnects. I love the ergonomics of this mouse and I really wanted it to work. I bought a Logitech g305 wireless and wanted to try them both. I was going to keep the Razer Basilisk X Hyperspeed, but every time I shut off my computer and turned it back on, the Razer would not work and I would have to take out and reconnect the receiver. I liked the ergonomics of this mouse much better than the Logitech. The thumb rest is great and the rubber around it makes my hand not slip. It actually also makes my hands feel cooler for some reason. I also liked how smooth the mouse is on my mousepad.
I looked around and the disconnection issue is very common with this mouse.
I thought long and hard, but I decided that having to live with the annoyance of reconnecting the receiver everytime is just not worth it.
I really wish this wasn't an issue. I still give it 3 stars because if you are a person that leaves their computer on all the time or doesn't mind unpluggin/replugging the receiver, then this is an excellent mouse.
Also, one little thing; the "click" sound that it makes is very high-pitched compared to the Logitech
rating 2.0. Mine shipped without usb dongle I had been using the mouse with bluetooth so I didn't even realize it until months later, but no usb dongle to use for 2.4 mhz option.

rating 1.0. Dead after 6 months. After an initial negative review for its compatability with Mac's, I started using it with a PC. It was great for 5 months. rating 2.0. Such a great mouse...till the double clicking started... I was so excited when I got this mouse and it worked great for a month than it started double clicking all the time when I was just trying to either hold the left click down or just single left click. I can barely move a window around my desktop without having to retry 3 or 4 times due to the double clicking. I've searched around the web to find that this was a known issue. rating 1.0. TERRIBLE The mouse I got is not the same one as shown in the pictures. TERRIBLE SERVICE.

rating 1.0. DOA - would not power up with included battery or any others Mine was DOA! I treid multiple AA batteries including the one they include. It would not work. No lights, nothing. The LED is supposed to at least blink to let you know you have a dying battery, and that didn't light up - and YES, i turned the switch on the bottom to both 2.4 wireless and bluetooth, neither worked.

Even if it did power up, I doubt I would kept it - the scroll wheel is not adjustable and is very tight. You can't spin it fast, it has way too much resistance, and there's no adjustment on the bottom like other Razer mice (or free-wheel switch like a Logitech).

rating 1.0. Died after 6 months of regular use I bought this mouse to replace a corded mouse I used to use because the cord kept getting in the way. I have had a track record of Razer products dying on me after 6 months of use, but I decided to go against my better judgement and assume Razer had managed to invest in their quality control department. Sadly, this is clearly not the case. Right out of the gate, the mouse chose to randomly disconnect and sometimes actually try to reconnect at about a 50% success rate, with me usually having to switch the mouse off and on again a few times. Occasionally, I'd have to pull the battery out completely. As the headline stated, the mouse actually completely died after 6 months of regular use. I've never once dropped the thing or done anything that one doesn't normally do with a computer mouse. I have no pets, no kids, I was the only one using it for the entirety of its short life.

In summary, avoid this product (and pretty much anything Razer sells) at all costs. Clearly, Razer still hasn't managed to tackle their quality control issues.
rating 3.0. jumpy on macOS works fine over 2.4ghz, macOS Bluetooth is jumpy sometimes, and it's very annoying.

rating 1.0. My first Razer product, Did not live up to the hype * changed to 1 star* I have been looking for a decent mouse. Only really play one game but I also use my personal computer for work as well. I received this mouse on March 20th 2020, and as of today.... April 14th 2020. It is unusable. I wouldnt waste the money on it... you will be spending it again on a different mouse.  Heres to hoping the replacement i just ordered is better than this.

rating 1.0. Bad radio in the Razer Basilisk X HyperSpeed I was looking for a Bluetooth mouse that I could seamlessly use with my gaming notebook and my office computer, a Dell Latitude 7480. Unfortunately, this is not the mouse to fit the bill. I was interested in the mouse because of the ergonomics and multiple DPI settings. I play a lot of Civilization and City Skylines, and thought that with so many DPI settings, I could more easily lay roads and scan across maps. Alas, I will never know because the mouse is practically unusable as a result of the bad radio in it. While I updated my personal gaming notebook, an HP Pavillion, with proper Razer drivers, I don't have that privilege on my work computer. As a result, once the mouse went to sleep, it never reconnected with the Dell that was using the standard Microsoft HID drivers.  Even with the proper drivers on the gaming laptop, the mouse only reconnected with the computer after going into sleep mode 50% of the time. Sadly, Razer only allows you to put off sleep mode for up to 15 minutes -- presumably for battery savings. There is no way to tell the mouse to never sleep, or even set it to 30 minutes. I plan to return the mouse.

rating 1.0. Poor quality. Mouse worked great for the first couple weeks. But after about a month of casual use the right clicker button started going out. Now it barely works at all. I expected better quality coming from a name brand like Razer. And they only offered 30 day returns so now I’m stuck with a $50 gaming mouse that I can not use to play games.

rating 2.0. Comfortable, looks good. Poor battery life and fails to connect occasionally. Edit - Deducting 2 stars. Here's why: After about a year and a half of service, I've found the battery life with both the Safeway brand budget batteries *AND* the best batteries I could afford -- Energizer Max -- to peter out after about a week. The driver software reports a low battery warning generally within two days of installing new battery, and occasionally outright fails to connect or come out of "sleep" mode.
I am a bit disappointed in this, as I am generally content with this mouse when it actually works correctly.

Original review still otherwise stands; mouse is still comfortable in the hand and has most of the same function as my old M510:
"Ordered this as a replacement for my venerable Logitech M510. I find this to be comfortable, and it looks good. Only feature I miss is the left/right scroll in the wheel. Thumb rest is a nice touch, and the DPI selector is nifty. Overall, not bad."

rating 1.0. Bluetooth connection is not reliable I got this mouse to use with my work computer as I was looking for something with a high DPI rate that could be set via physical buttons on the mouse (rather than requiring software that I wasn't allowed to install on my work computer). This mouse seemed to check all of the boxes for what I wanted. However, once I received it I quickly realized that the Bluetooth connection is not very reliable. I don't know if it's the mouse to blame or if it's just an overall issue with Windows 10 and Bluetooth mice, but in my case the mouse would routinely simply fail to reconnect after it went to sleep. So if I stopped using the mouse for a few minutes while on a phone call, etc. and then went to move the mouse again it would no longer be working and I would have to either open my laptop and use the touchpad on the laptop to reset the Bluetooth connection and re-pair the mouse, or plug in my old wired mouse and do the same thing. Obviously this wasn't an acceptable situation so I ended up returning the mouse. I am now using the Logitech G502 wireless mouse which includes a separate USB wireless receiver (rather than using Bluetooth) and it has been 100% reliable for weeks. I highly recommend getting the Logitech mouse or something similar that does not rely on Bluetooth.

rating 1.0. Dont buy for gaming On the 3rd month I started having issues with double clicking. I press right mouse button and registers two click. I would never buy razer again. Do some google search and you will see it is a common issue.

rating 1.0. Quit Working After 3 Months I enjoyed this mouse overall for online gaming. It worked quite well. So it is junk and I can't return it. I can not recommend you buy this mouse.

rating 2.0. It broke from a small drop I've owned this for 15 days. It's works great, has a great feel. I dropped it from my coffee table onto wood floor and the left click broke. That's about a 2 foot drop. My prior mouse was the wired basilisk from a year ago - my girlfriend drops it all the time and it's just fine. Maybe I just got unlucky. Either way, this is the first Razer mouse in over a decade that I'm disappointed in. For the price, I guess I expected a better build quality. If you're someone who get aggressive with your mouse, I would avoid this one. I want to get another one, it really is a great mouse, but I'm worried it'll be just as frail.

rating 3.0. Some slight issues, but solid. The item itself looks the part, but it does feel very cheap, very plasticy. The problem that I have is hitting both B and A at the same time seems to be really hit-or-miss. It's a real issue when playing games like TMNT III: The Manhattan Project. Also, the d-pad is a bit finicky. It seems to move left or right when you are holding down on the d-pad, which can make issues in some games where you need to crouch.

It looks like a standard NES controller, except minus the Nintendo logo. The overlay for the buttons however seems to be a very cheap vinyl, and really looks like someone slapped this together.

I have had no issues with plugging it into my NES-001 Control deck, it fits fine. Some other reviews on here have had issues with it, so I wonder if it's a manufacturing error or another company is making these now.

Overall, if you have to have an NES controller brand new, this isn't a terrible option, but if you really want a quality pad, do yourself a favor and buy a refurbished original pad.

other_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Awesome Game This game is very challenging and takes a while to get the hang of it; however, it is now my ultimate favorite NES game. I absolutely love it. You can switch between six counselors while trying to defeat Jason and his mom. You have to find weapons, keys, notes, medicine, and other goodies while entering the cave, forests, trails and lake area. This is pretty tough to do while Jason kills your kids and counselors non-stop. If you buy this game, here is a tip: If you use a counselor to go to the cabin just outside and above the cave road, you will find a note that directs you to the torch in a cabin by the lake. rating 5.0. Amazing for the price and lots of fun Alright alright, the Sims is not bug-free, of course, and Origin... kinda sucks. Installing wasn't too difficult and didn't take long (I had the CD because physical copy master race) but it takes a while to start up.

BUT. But.
If you love Sims, the starter pack is definitely the best value. It comes with two expansion packs that, honestly, aren't necessary to fully enjoy the game (10/10 would recommend Pets if you want hours of joy), however, they do add a bit of spice and are fun to mess around with, especially Late Night. I never would've gotten the expansions had it not been for the starter pack.

The Sims 3 itself usually costs $20, and the expansion packs cost about the same amount from what I've seen. You're basically getting two free expansion packs, and if you're looking into buying The Sims, you might as well get this. It's the best 'bang for your buck' and really, why wouldn't you add expansion packs? (Plus, Late Night comes with an entirely new town you can live in and it's fantastic)

All in all, this is an amazing value and you should definitely get it. I mean, come on, there's BUTLERS in Late Night. (They're pretty buggy, but... they're there.) It's fun and exciting, just get it.

rating 5.0. 11/10 there's dogs The only expansion pack you need. The best part is that I created a Sim based on me and I have no goals in life because I'm a loser, and now my Sim's lifetime wish can represent that! All she wants to do is befriend 15 dogs while her husband wants to be a five star chef. I love dogs. Life is great. For them, anyway.

Really the only downside is that the shelter animals are really ugly for the most part. Makes me sad.

input:
rating 5.0. Standard price. Nothing more. I picked this up because I had a Amazon giftcard and I figured this would save me a few months of fee's since I'm already playing the game.

It is what it is, as mentioned in another review. It's your standard price for a standard giftcard. It's PRIME eligible so no shipping if you have a Prime account.

As for people complaining about "just needing the code" and shipping. It's a retail boxed product. They can't open your boxed product, scratch off the code on the card, and then email it to you. That'd create too many liabilities. They offer a card. They send a card. It's an MMO.

It's not a great deal, as it's normal priced, but it's not mis-represented either.

rating 5.0. Great option for 60 days gametime I saw the negative reviews for this but I wanted to say that this was priced correctly roughly 30 bucks for 60 days of game time. The shipping was fast through Amazon Prime.

rating 5.0. It's exactly what you expect: pre-paid subscription time for SWTOR Listen, the people giving this item poor reviews are morons. Here is why you should ignore them:

Reason one: Many people are leaving reviews based on their feelings about the game as a whole. This is irrelevant. Good or bad reviews about the game should be submitted for the game itself, not for this item which is merely a prepaid 60-day subscription card. It is the exactly the same as what you could buy from any other retailer.

Reason two: Many people leaving reviews are complaining about the price, the service of the seller, or the actual validity of the item received. This item is sold by many different sellers on the Amazon website. Not every seller is going to offer the product at the same price, the same shipping expense, or even necessarily be as trustworthy as Amazon themselves. None of the criticisms about the price of the time card being too high actually apply to the product being sold directly from Amazon. Amazon has always sold this product AT or BELOW the retail cost, which is $29.99.

Chances are if you are reading this review, you are already familiar with the game. By the time this review is live, the game will have introduced a restricted Free-To-Play option. Anyone considering the game can try it out for nothing, then purchase a subscription if and only if they feel they want to enjoy the game without the restrictions placed on free players.

Do the smart thing - put this item in your cart and watch the price for a while. Make sure you've chosen to purchase it from Amazon and not a 3rd party. You will notice the price can fluctuate, but will never go above $29.99. If you see it drop down to the $26-27 range, then pull the trigger and select free shipping, and you have gotten a bargain.

rating 5.0. The Card Quality is definitely on the higher end... This card deserves at least 5 stars for how well it performs it functions.  Not only is it a solid plastic material with clearly written characters, but it is sent in a full sized plastic game case.  EA pulled out all the stops for this intensly packaged 60-Day pre-paid time card, and it just goes to show how much they care about their customers.  Yeah, EA may be throwing DRM in their games, and making decisions like Sim City where everything has to be connected to a server, even though it doesn't have to be, or limiting city sizes, even though it doesn't have to be, or holding out on content to release DLC, but you have to realize that they're doing this for us, and as a faithful customer for them, it's my job to show my satisfaction with a non-biased 100% truthful 5 star review of how great this plastic card fulfills it's function: by giving me a stupid code.

rating 4.0. Delivered on time Obviously, there is not much to the actually product. I will say that I did get an extra month. I'm not sure if it was a promotional thing, but I got more than I paid for, and for that, I am more than happy with my purchase.

As for subscriber status, its worth if you enjoy the game. Some perks:
- You can use artifact items (purple items)
- You can level past 50 and cap at 55
- You will have access to new additions like the new Star Fighter arena
- You advance in level faster with experience and legacy
- You can do a lot more with your character looks wise
There is more events and benefits you do receive by being a subscriber.

In all honesty I do enjoy the game but the way that EA/Origin sells things on the market is kind of... well over priced.

You don't get everything unlocked. One species is still locked, some cosmetics like white eyes, and hair styles that can all be purchased at the cartel market with cartel coins. I grew up with games that when you purchased them ONE TIME, you received everything from the game that it had to offer.

I understand that they have to make money to continue to maintain the game and to pay the staff. The game is defiantly not perfect and does continue to have glitches, but minor ones that can be over looked.

All in all, yes, you should buy this product and be a subscriber for 60 days. Why? Because you get a chance to see if you really want to continue past that subscription. Its a 60 day, do NOT enter your card number here, we will NOT bill you after the 60th day, subscription. Hope this helped.

rating 1.0. PSA: Do not buy this controller. It has a design flaw that causes the left bumper to detach after extended use. TLDR: The Elite controllers have a critical design flaw that causes the left bumper to detach after extended use, and there is no warranty that covers more than 90 days.

My friend and I play Halo pretty regularly, and we each bought an Xbox One Elite controller last spring because it looked like a pretty awesome controller and we thought we could use the extra paddles. The hardware itself feels really sturdy and we were both impressed with them. The paddles were a difficult adjustment at first, but now it's hard to play without them.

My friend's controller broke within a couple months of use. The left bumper has a piece of plastic that snapped off, leaving the bumper detached. We discovered that the 90 day warranty didn't cover the controller up to this point, so he just bought another controller so we could continue gaming. We were willing to give Microsoft the benefit of the doubt.

Fast forward another couple months, and my controller breaks. Same issue. Left bumper is broken. The little piece of plastic connecting the device to the paddle broke. Not one hour after mine broke, my friend's broke again. The exact same issue. Left bumper broken.

Customer service refuses to acknowledge the design flaw, and will not replace any of the controllers. As a PSA to other Xbox users, please do not continue to purchase these controllers. It's a known issue Microsoft will not fix, and will not refund. Warranties are only eligible for 90 days with no option to extend it, so there's no way to protect yourself from the inevitable failures. There are also additional post on reddit and on alternative forums illustrating the issue. Please do not continue to support the product.

rating 1.0. Cheap parts, not worth it Seemed great at first. While the controller is very solid and has a great feel, there are too many cheap parts used causing it to fail early. Like many other users my left bumper popped out and it is a cheap piece of plastic. It now just rests where it used to be in a very loose manner. My brother had the same thing happened to his. The sticks have also worn so that the dead zone no longer works. Whenever you were in a menu the cursor will move on its own, and if you are standing still you will walk forward automatically slowly. You are better off buying a new standard controller once a year than investing in this garbage.

rating 1.0. Buyer Beware - Poor Build Quality and only 90 Day Warranty After about 6th months of use the left bumper started malfunctioning - it hangs halfway off between presses and registers maybe 1 in every 5 clicks. After calling Microsoft, they informed me that my only 2 options I have to get a working controller is to pay $160 to repair the current one ($10 more than the MSRP), or simply buy a new one. Doing some research I found that this is a pretty common issue, and one that consumers have no recourse for outside of an embarrassingly small 90 day return window.

For a supposedly premium product this is insane policy, be careful before buying or you might end up with an 150 dollar paperweight. Shame on you, Microsoft.

rating 1.0. BROKEN BUMPER JUST DAYS AFTER 90 DAY WARRANTY EXPIRED! BROKEN BUMPER JUST DAYS AFTER 90 DAY WARRANTY EXPIRED! Do not waste your money on this controller!  Xbox one bumpers are notoriously fragile and the right one on this Elite Controller snapped after just 92 days of moderate use.  You can see the problem area in the picture at the end of my finger.  This is not a fixable issue because the right and left Elite bumpers are connected with one another as one large plastic piece (xbox one bumpers are separate from one another). rating 5.0. Expensive but the quality is there! This may be the most comfortable controller I've ever held. It is definitely the most comfortable XBOX controller I've ever held. Dare I say because it feels more like a Sony PS controller with the sleeker design and more compact battery layout. (minus not having both analog sticks front and center).

The shortened bumper switches are a godsend for just about every non-driving game I play. The bottom paddles take some getting used because I kept accidentally bumping them but I'm slowly getting used to it. Having those to access as alternates could prove useful for casting spells in Gauntlet or other games where your right thumb alone isn't fast enough for rapid button combos.

The metal thumb sticks. D-pads, and bottom paddles really make the thing feel solid and the magnetic snap lock for the sticks and pad mean you don't have snap on grooved plastic that will wear and break over time. Fox an XBOne user this controller is a must since it is so much nicer than the factory XBOne controllers. If you are a PC gamer, value is going to be based on how much you use it. I play mostly FPS with keyboard and mouse but I play a lot of action and driving/racing games like Tomb Raider, Road Redemption, NFS, GRID, Gauntlet, etc so it while it was pricey it was worth it to me. If you don't use a controller that much on your PC, save your money.

rating 1.0. Do NOT Buy Beware glitches without remedy after only 100 days! NEVER - is my reply. Hopefully this will help you since my $150 is now gone :-(

rating 1.0. Failed just after 3 month manufacturer warranty (Amazon refunded me) This controller quit working properly after 4 months of use. The left analog stick no longer registers forward movement. Contacted Microsoft several times about the issue. They told me to buy a new one.... a 3 month warranty on a $150 controller? Ridiculous.

Edit: Amazon gave me an 80% refund for my defective controller. Microsoft would still not provide support. I maintain the 1 star score for poor product quality and lack of support through the manufacturer.

rating 1.0. BEWARE 2 Controllers have broke now only moments after warranty expires - Only 3 month warranty! The Good, Bad, and Ugly of this controller...

Good - Simply a good controller while it works. I found paddles useful in racing games only.

Bad - I find it overpriced by no less than $50. At $100 or less, this controller would be of much better value and would have been in line with it's poorly manufactured parts and terrible warranty.
Bad - Directional Pad inside controller never sat perfect. It was off center right out of the box. Not a big deal but for $150 you expect perfection.

Ugly - 6 months after purchase the left joystick eye came undone from it's inside clips. While the joystick still does work, it now has a 1cm up-and-down hop within the socket. This is the best description I can give. This is very annoying DEFECT. This is a defect because this controller has never been dropped and was gently used.
Ugly - Only has a 3 month warranty. At $150 this should have, and I assumed it would have, a 3 year warranty. As a product Microsoft was touting as it's "Professional Gaming Controller" is backed by a warranty so terrible that refurbished products by other manufacturers are warrantied with more vigor and trust in their products.

This review will remain unchanged here on Amazon until Microsoft repairs my controller. So essentially forever.

Update: August 2016 - Microsoft after talking to supervisor after supervisor agreed to replace my controller. Will update further if issues begin with new controller Microsoft sends me.
Update: August 2016 - 2 weeks and new controller in hands. Not bad.

Update: March 2017 - New controllers right joystick is bad. The joystick is not loose but still without touching it, it somehow registers feedback in the down position. This means now in EVERY game, I have to fight changing camera views. This is especially frustrating in racing games as the feedback from the stick generally "unsticks" a camera view which usually results in crashes due to not being able to fix the camera view mid turn. The irony is I bought this controller for games like Forza, now this controller makes the game unplayable with most others almost unplayable.

I will update this review again if Microsoft repairs this controller or replaces it. Until then people... don't buy this piece of crap.

rating 5.0. Review From a Serious Gamer - This Controller is a Must Buy ! I absolutely love this controller. As a major gamer who plays games on both all of the consoles (X1,PS4,WIIU) as well as gaming pc this controller is a huge step forward in the evolution of gaming. Microsoft really hit the nail on the head with this one! The amount of options this controller gives you to customize it to your needs is absolutely wonderful . It is very easy to switch the physical components of the controller as well as the button mapping. You can set up the custom button mapping ahead of time for each game you play.
In order to customize your controller all you have to do is download the Xbox Accessories App and you can set up all of the button mapping ahead of time and make it so that when you switch the games that you play the button mapping automatically switches with the game change. You can also have two different sets of button mapping for each game and you can switch between them by easily switching the button from 1 to 2 in the middle of the X1 Elite controller. All of these custom options really allow you to play games the way that you want to play them !
For me as a very experienced gamer the changes that I have seen in console gaming especially with the X1 have been incredible. Adding the option to purchase a controller like this one is just one many things X1 users will love. For me this is the next step in the evolution of console gaming and I highly recommend that you purchase this controller if you are a serious gamer. If you are a casual gamer the original X1 controller will be just fine but for those of you out there who are like me this controller is a must buy !

rating 1.0. Save your money When I originally received my controller, I really liked it. It has a nice weight, ceramic rings around the control sticks for smooth movement, etc. That said, after about 6 months of use, the control stick began suffering from stick drift, where it would register input even when there was none. The rubber grip also started peeling off for no apparent reason.

Microsoft only offers a mere 90 day warranty...and there are no options for repair.

So, if you're willing to buy two of these a year, this is for you. Otherwise, stick with the controllers that come with the console. They hold up better than the Elite controller.

rating 3.0. Poor ergonomics and warranty support for Premium price. Purchased this controller mainly for a birthday present for my son, but I use it occasionally as well. Fortunately, I was able to find this new for 66% of the MSRP price, making it more pallatable. The main benefit that this has over the standard controller are the paddles underneath the controller that allow a gamer to use their other fingers to access the "A-B-X-Y" buttons without lifting the thumb off of the control sticks. Of course, this is most useful in FPS games where reaction time and getting off a shot is important. However, I found this highly uncomfortable to use as you have to be careful not to grip the bottom of the controller too hard for risk of accidentally hitting the button. I must not be the only one who felt this way as my son, who requested the controller in the first place, has now detached the bottom paddles, making it an overpriced standard controller.

Not only that, but I will also verify that the warranty period on this product is laughably short. For a multi-billion dollar company (Microsoft) to warranty a device that costs 3 times as much as the standard controller and label it as "elite" yet will only stand behind it for 90 days should be embarrassing and robbery. Yes, these are input devices that will used regularly and can probably be expected to wear out eventually, but even a $20 keyboard that gets used regularly typically carries a 1 year warranty. I say this as a shareholder of Microsoft--I am glad for their profits, but make a product that is expected to last and stand behind it.

Overall, I would not recommend this at all for casual gamers. Even for the market that it is promoted for--the hardcore FPS gamer--I don't feel the ergonomics are there to warrant the price premium and the essentially nonexistent warranty support.

rating 5.0. One great controller. Truly the best controller available. Very good quality. Nice weight. Quick response buttons. Versatile for different game setups. Programmable. Would have liked option for buttons to light up.

rating 5.0. Expensive, worthy, but still with room for improvement (First, this controller retails for around $150. If it's still early on, there IS currently a bit of a supply problem. That means DON'T BUY the $300 listings. Wait if you can for Microsoft to restock.)

I would fit in the "luxury" market for the Elite Wireless Controller, in that I'm no professional competitive gamer. I'm not even that great a gamer. But if you can afford this controller, I would highly recommend it. The workmanship is top notch, the parts are durable, and the controller is highly configurable.

Everyone has already spewed praise for this controller, so let me highlight my gripes, despite the mostly-earned 5 star review.

- You can't remap paddles to stick directions. This would be useful to me in pinball to control nudges. You also can't map to the View or Menu buttons. I do have an instance where that would be nice.
- You can't define custom stick curves, just the default, slow, fast, and instant curves. I feel like more precision tuneability was promised than that.
- The "Xbox Accessories" app does not work in snap mode for fast on-the-fly adjustments.
- You only have two slots on-board the controller, despite the many more you can save on your console. You will wish there were more on-board once you start playing more than one game or using multiple configs in a single game.
- The app feels like it was written by interns. It's functional, but unrefined and a little clunky. I hope it will be rewritten.
- The sliver triggers are plastic with silver finish. If you were expecting metal, you might be disappointed.
- The main surface of the controller has a "grippy, matte rubber" finish, the same finish I had on controllers from five years ago, and that material degraded to a tacky, used-duct-tape-like residue. I hope the same fate does not await this controller.
- Despite a claim that a third-party will produce new/replacement parts, I have yet to see them in the wild.

Despite all this, the controller still comes highly recommended for the many reasons already espoused. Many of the issues above can be fixed with updates and firmware. Others are potential issues that may or may not pan out. Overall, I would give this a 4.7 star review. It is worthy, but there is room for improvement.

rating 5.0. Better than Scuf Controller My son's friends were getting the Scuf controllers for their game systems and while my son would talk to me about them he had not talked about getting one for himself.  I decided to surprise him with one as a "just because" present.  Because I worked as a Purchasing Agent for so many years, I cannot buy something without reading reviews, price checking, watching videos, etc.  My research on the other Scuf controller led me straight to this one instead. While the price is comparable, the Scuf costs more for additional features that come included on this controller.  This controller allows the user to save two separate customized game settings which comes in handy when playing two entirely different types of games such as a driving game vs. a shooter game where different settings may be desired.  Also, being able to switch "hair trigger" on and off with just a switch of a button is "awesome" according to my son.  With the Scuf, this is not a quick change to the controller requiring several steps that take you away from continuous uninterrupted play.  This was a winner.  My son is ecstatic.  His friends who play with the Scuf controllers really liked playing with this one and talked about how they wished they could switch "hair trigger" off and on so quick.  So as a mom, who doesn't need a hair trigger anything, I hope this review helps someone else who may be considering purchasing this for themselves or their kids.  While the controller is expensive, it is definitely worth the improvements that my son has said that he has seen in his gaming.

I have had 2 of these break in the period of a single year. One has had the handle grips peel off on both sides and has thumb stick drift on the left thumb stick. Thumb stick drift is when the thumb sticks internal circuit acts as as if you are moving it in the upward direction perpetually even though you aren't moving it in that direction or moving it at all. The second controller of these I have had break as both thumbsticks have the drift effect on them rendering the controller unusable. Save your money and buy something that actually works and doesn't break like a cheap toy you bought at a swap meet while you are using it exactly as intended.

input:
rating 4.0. Good Alternative To The PC Version I have been playing The Sims since the first game came out on release day so I have a more than a couple years in now. I previously purchased The Sims 3 for the PC and also on the Xbox 360. If you are accustomed to playing The Sims on the computer, the Xbox version will be a bit of a change for you. There is some missing functionality that you might be accustomed to. But I find that it is still a very entertaining game and one of the few games in this genre available on the 360 console.

Here are my findings so far. Please let me know if I got any of this wrong!

Some things I was disappointed about:

*Still no edit town button on the town screen. I like to fill the town with my created sims vs. the developer created ones. Could not find a way to do this on the Xbox version.
*Maintaining/deleting unwanted downloaded clothing and items is still a pain. Why can't I delete unwanted content from the Edit Sim screen???
*This is not an expansion pack, it is a completely separate game. So be ready to say goodbye to the sims you created in the Xbox version of The Sims. Time to create some new ones. I was hoping for an import option from one game to the other sort of the way Fable or Mass Effect works. Missed opportunity here in my opinion.

And here are some things I liked:

*The pets are really great. By far the best pets experience you will have in The Sims so far. Being able to control the pets adds a lot to the game. There are no horses in the console version but that doesn't bother me personally. I'm more of a cat person.
*The Digging and Hunting options for the dog and cat are really fun!
*There are some sort of town mysteries that you can figure out. I didn't get to try these out much last night, but they seem like they could be fun.
*I really like the new town, much better than in the previous Xbox game. There are a lot of nice parks and it seems like there is more open space between lots.
*The Karma Powers are just as cool as on the first 360 game, but there are even more of them now.
*Challenge Points are a great way to make you try out everything the game has to offer.
*Achievement Points. Need I say more?

Overall I find this game to be a great addition to my Xbox game library. It might not be able to compete with the PC version in expandability and configurability, but what console game can? If you don't have a computer fast enough to play the PC version, buy this. If you like playing games on the console instead of the computer, then give it a try. It's fun!

rating 4.0. Another very fun Sim's game for the console. The Sims 3 Pets is a great new addition to my collection of games for my Xbox 360. The console version of the game adds cats, dogs, town mysteries, new karma powers, and many other exciting new items and features to the game. The fact that you can control the pets makes for a lot of new and exciting activities to do in the game and will surely keep any lover of The Sims busy for hours on end. You'll often find yourself trying to get your cat to go hunting or making your dog dig holes in the neighbor's yard. The Sims themselves also have new actions in the game, such as, digging for items and they can also now invent. They can even teach their pets tricks, which to me is very fun.

Even though the game adds all of these great new features it still lacks in a couple departments. Firstly it's a standalone console game, you're not going to be able to use it as an add-on to your previous Sims 3 console game. This isn't a huge problem since the Pets version seems to have so much included, but it still would had been nice. Also the game itself seems to be very "glitchy" at times, for instance often times when I'm having my Sim carry a pet around the pet will just float in midair beside them. Sometimes the Sims also seem to freeze up for a while at certain times when doing small tasks like filling a pet bowl, it always straightens out fairly quickly, but it can still be a bother. Most of the small problems though will most likely be patched with an update so don't let them stop you from giving the game a try!

Altogether I think this game is a very fun and should be added to any Sim lover's collection.

rating 4.0. Great Game, but Tough to Control on the XBOX The Sims is clearly a PC game, all the way. There, the controls are simple, with a point and click interface. Here, not so much. Note that we have no problems with voice commands on any other Kinect games, so it isn't a sound calibration issue.

Still, it is a great game, and you do get the Sims 3 with the Pets expansion without having to buy them separately. Definitely worth it!

Bought for my 360, basically under 30 dollars for a game that, in my opinion, is BETTER than sims 3 in general. There are a couple new cool things in build and buy mode, As far as I know the PC version is the only one with other animals other than just dogs and cats. Still way worth it even if there is only 2 species. With high free will, the animals are pretty much spot on with real actions for the animals, so it's fun to watch. Animals are aloud to have jobs which is cute :) Basically, in the grave yard, police station ect. Animals have only one skill level they can progress. Dogs: Digging, you can find seeds, gems, rocks, bugs, and relics. The museum gives you money for finding them. Cats: Hunting you can find bugs, and spirits. You are able to give you're findings as a cat, to your owner as a gift or you can keep the bugs/spirits in your house, and of course sell all that the animals find.
As for the sims in the careers, the skill levels they "need" to get a promotion, have higher expectations. In Sims 3, it was basically 'work on getting your logic up you skill point. Next promotion another skill point.' For this one I've noticed the first expectation for your job is to have it up 3 skill points, thankfully skill points don't seem to matter. For example my sim's are up to level 7 & 8 with all skill levels on the low side, and are still progressing.

Highly recommend this, can't beat the price for more added in the build and buy, plus being able to have animals.
Can't wait to see if there will be an ad on for 360

rating 4.0. Good game with a few exceptions After spending some more time with this game i have decided to write a more in depth review of this game.First off let me say that i think that this game is good.It however is not that much different than the previous sims game for Xbox 360 (which i also own) except for the addition of a few new things such as the addition of different pets (Cats & Dogs) and a new town as well as the Mysteriologist addition which in my opinion is more of an annoyance than anything else.Now on to the bad (at least in my opinion) First thing is this game lags there are times when you will be going to a store or the movie theater and be waiting to walk in and your character will just be standing there for awhile before entering,there have been times when my character has been trying to have a conversation with another sim and will just be standing there like a dumba**.There are also bugs in this game, my character has literally gotten stuck in grass,my dog has had part of his body inside of a wall and the other part outside of it..Seriously EA ?.Also as far as the pets go its cool but EA could have done more with it.I think that by this stage in The Sims games they could have included a few exotic pets such as ferrets & possibly a few species of reptiles to add a little something different to the mix.Overall i feel that EA could be doing more with these games and also feel that they should take the time to tweak these games so that they perform better than they are before releasing them..Overall this is a good game but it has a few things that are just downright irritating.

rating 3.0. I wasn't quite prepared for Sims 3 Pets I was so looking forward to getting this game because I prefer playing Sims 3 on my XBOX 360. My daughter plays Sims 3 on her PC and has every Sims 3 add-on/expansion pack available. Naturally when this game came around for the XBOX I thought "cool, I can add it to my already established Sims households I have saved on my XBOX". Unfortunately, when I popped the game in, I realized I couldn't "add-on" to my Sims 3 game but had to start an entirely new family etc. It is its own entire game and does not correlate whatsoever to the regular Sims 3 XBOX game. I hate to put this misunderstanding onto EA but I never once heard anyone mention it wasn't a game add-on/expansion exactly like the PC version. I might not have bought it if I knew that. I find some serious issues with the game play itself as well. The characters movements are very choppy and sometimes one of my household members completely slides across the room like a statue on wheels and then gets stuck while the game tries to decide if it's going to lock up for good or not. This strange movement might be good for a laugh initially, but after doing it time and time again when I hit the "fast forward" button, it becomes irksome. It seems compared to the regular Sims 3 game the colors are bleached out during game-play and no matter how I adjust my TV or the brightness sliders inside the game I cannot get it to look right. There are some good points to the game that have helped it keep its 3 star rating from me though. The controls are very close to those of the regular Sims 3 game so it will come naturally to those of you who have the original. There a few new karma powers and new challenges involving the pets which helps keep the game fresh and interesting as well. The pets are very much like having another family member in the game and their independence makes its easier to get your skills up in the beginning without having to worry about constantly caring for them. With that said, I feel like it was a waste of money to either have this game or have the original Sims 3 since I cannot play one while playing the other and they're really the same game. When I'm playing the Sims 3 pets I look longingly at the Sims 3 box and feel like a bad parent whose abandoned their child. I created all those people, helped them grow and become strong and healthy, and now I'm leaving them to rot in the corner for a less superior game. Hmmmmmm, not sure what the right path is. Looks like the only solution is to buy another XBOX 360, place it next to the one I have now and try to play both games at once. Or maybe it would've been easier to not have spent the money on this game or on the original. This game is a cruel reminder that you can't always have your cake and eat it too.

rating 2.0. WAY too glitchy, and no customer support :( I played Sims 2 several years ago and thought it was fun enough, I think I stopped playing it because everyone peed their pants and cried about it way too much... I figured I could handle a dog or cat peeing on the floor in front of their friends so I gave this game a try. I guess I didn't read enough about it because you actually play people too, I'm ok with that though (haven't seen a single sim wiz themselves in this one, thank heavens). I actually like the game, but I can't recommend buying it for a few reasons:

Due to limitations from lack of memory, there's a limit on how many items you can have in inventory and in your household. If you love building it takes a lot away from the game, but even if you don't care, it causes issues with different challenges you may want to complete. It also makes buying a larger house kind of useless. (This isn't in the PC version by the way.)

There are lots of glitches in the game. Some are harmless but annoying, some can cost you money and items (I've had my entire inventory disappear, and I've had to build doors and floors under my sims that somehow ended up standing in the air outside the second story unable to move) and some that can potentially keep you from completing certain quests and using items in the game.

There is no customer support for this game, when you call or go online for ea support it's only for Sims 3, and it's mainly for the PC version. When I talked to someone on the phone they couldn't help me with any of the problems that I was having, and tried directing me to a website that was for PC version only. When I explained that you needed a registration number from the PC version to post there, he just told me that he unfortunately couldn't help me.

Because of these reasons, don't buy this game. I haven't played the PC version, but I'd recommend buying the PC version before this one.

PS- This is George's wife, I think he'd be upset if someone thought he played this game. He's ashamed that I play it.

rating 2.0. If you know anything about EA, you'll know what to expect from this game. TL:DR

Great game, but lack of QA and updates from EA means glitches stack up until it becomes unplayable.

The game itself is wonderful. Tons of fun, casual and relaxing. However, this game was developed for PC and then ported over to consoles, and as such the QA work on the console seems to have been almost nonexistent. Glitches and bugs accumulate as your playtime grows longer until a saved game becomes unusable. After 4 weeks (game days) one of my furnishings could no longer be interacted with by any Sim, but when I sold it and replaced it, it worked fine. After 3 more game days, display items started suffering from severe graphical issues, however these could be resolved by exiting and then reloading the game. A few more days later and one of my Sims could no longer view any of the detailed menus, but all the other Sims still worked fine. Then the game started giving me notifications that my Sims had collected various items (from gardens, fishing, and hunting) but then those items were not being loaded into my inventory. It's now week 5, day 3 and the in-game clock no longer advances the displayed time, however time is still progressing and so while the calender says 5:37 pm on Sunday, I just sent my Sim to work at 8:00 am on Tuesday. Because of the clock glitch, though, the alarm to wake them up for work no longer goes off, and the carpool never arrives. The newspaper is no longer delivered and the mail doesn't come. All of these issues are retained if you save the game, unless you save it in a NEW game slot each time. Doing this allows me to load that save and play for about 20 minutes before all the glitches start to manifest again.

rating 4.0. Great Alternative! I played the Sims 2 on PC and loved it. Well.... when Sims 3 came out, my computer just couldn't handle it and I stopped playing. Then the Sims 3 Pets came out for xbox 360. I was a very happy individual.

This game is honestly great. Especially came in handy for wall paints, carpets, and expensive furniture. Controls are good and easy, great graphics, fluid movement, variety in interaction, interesting traits to give your sims that actually come along with unique dialogue/actions, etc.

Cons: The only thing really is that you can't have more than one family in a save. For example: I made a family. The mom and dad get a divorce, I want to kick the dad out. Well.... if you kick him out, he's gone. You can't move him into a separate household and still interact with him that way. They are just deleted. That sort of thing.

Oh and the lack of extra expansions, but hopefully more will come. Although, if you had the regular Sims 3 on xbox and wanted to get the Pets one, you had to completely restart and buy the new version, instead of it being like an expansion pack and downloading it.

rating 5.0. Shipping Problems Resolved I too had the same problem. Amazon promised me delivery on October 7th, I got an email saying it would not be able to come in until next week so they will refund the shipping cost. I admit I was a little disappointed, until they sent me an email that very same night giving me the digital download codes for my Xbox. I downloaded it when I got home so I was able to play with a buddy the next day. I still am receiving the hard copy as promised Monday, and I got to enjoy the full game before it even arrived. I am very impressed with Amazon for taking the extra step and ensuring I get to play the game early. That's quality service in my book and quite frankly unheard-of in this day in age from a huge company.
Now, as for the game, you won't be disappointed with the story especially if you're as dedicated as I am with this franchise. Easily one of the best gears I have played so far. Everything from the graphics, to the voice acting, to the addition of different weapons are top notch and well thought out. Can't wait until the next Gears of War!
