rating 5.0. Extremely Addicting! I never EVER thought that I would like a game like this. I grew up playing video games, starting with Sega as my first system. I started playing computer games when I went to college because I didn't want to bring any of my systems with me. Let me tell you, I always thought of WOW as a nerd game. I laughed when someone would say they play it because it to me was like Dungeons and Dragons.. or Pokemon... But then my best friend admitted that they played it and it was amazing. I decided to give it a try and I am so glad I did. I can not explain how amazing this game is. I have never played a "never ending" game before and it just blew me away. The landscapes are great and the "world" is huge. There is so much stuff to do it is ridiculous, whether you want to quest... do dungeons... farm for money... do pvp.. there is a ton of things to do. This game is worth the money and it can be very addicting.

rating 5.0. There is a difference between a "game" and a "game card" As I was reading some of the lower reviews for this product, I was more than a little confused. I just recently realized that these game cards were available for purchase on Amazon. Since I am both an Amazon and a WoW addict, this works out great for me. Now perhaps at one time Amazon was charging a ridiculous price for this game card, but at the moment I'm typing this, it amounts to less than buying a card at the store, or paying Blizzard per month directly. At the moment, the Amazon price is only $.26 less than buying it at the store, but if you subtract the shipping (free, for items over $25, which this is) and tax, which is not charged, this is still the better deal. I would also like to point out that there is a difference between the game and the time card that allows you to play the game. If you have an issue with the game itself, you should post that on the game's product page, not here.

rating 4.0. World of Warcraft I've been playing the game for almost 2 months now and the game is amazingly addicting. Make sure your willing to lose sleep, but it is extremely an exciting game. The visualization of the game is stunning and Blizzard again has hit a home run with this title. You can use your credit to pre-order through the game itself monthly, 3 months, etc. Blizzard will give a discount on depending how much you pay up front. Amazon has a very competitive price for the 2-month game card, which makes it the best option. Make sure if your doing the Super Saver free shipping that you order 2 weeks or so in advance. I find that Amazon will have like a delay of one week before it even ships out. The price is worth if you do it in advance.

The reason for 4 stars is that Blizzard needs to work on some of the Realm's performance issues. Some realms are more effected than others. I'm now playing on one that has no issues at all. The best way to find out how much lag a game my be like is to venture to Ironforge or Stormwind during peak times and compare to other servers what the overall lag is like. Ironforge is a huge city period so no lag will be hard to find, but you will see a difference through different realms.

rating 3.0. Good, but wasteful My wife and I use these to keep our playing habit under control. We have to make a conscious decision every 60 days to keep going, and we get to keep our credit card information to ourselves.

As others have mentioned, however, this is extraordinarily wasteful. They are overpackaged and the entire package and everything in it hits the landfill the second the code is entered.

Ideally Amazon could just show us the code on their site; they're doing a lot of other digital delivery these days.

Hence three stars: five, minus two for the sickening inefficiency.

input: rating 5.0. A very fun gaming system. So much fun. I read some of the reviews so immediately tested all of the things the people said and the system works great! rating 5.0. I love it! I've only had the system a few hours but it's awesome. I'm really enjoying the graphics and the ease of use in general. You definitely won't be disappointed.

rating 1.0. Screeching fan noise required console replacement; 6 days later Kinect hardware failure ("Kinect is unplugged") Preordered and received the Xbox One Day One Edition. My optical disk seems fine however I have a loud screeching sound from the fan which sounds like there is either something caught in the fan blades, or a bearing that is not rolling quietly. As I was updating the day one update I hear this sound like fingernails on a chalk board which is louder than the 360 or PS3 that I turn on next to it. Definitely not consistent with developer statements that the Xbox One is virtually silent. Xbox Support informed me that the console needed exchange as it needed to be serviced for diagnosis.

Amazon graciously rather than having me send the DOA unit to Microsoft, sent me an advanced exchange unit from their supply which was whisper quiet (so the fan is designed to be barely audible FYI). While that fixed the console problem, I have been using the console intermittently for 6 days and suddenly while trying out Kinect Rivals Preseason, mid-game the Kinect says "Kinect Unplugged" and thereafter despite reboots, hardware resets, rechecking connections, the Kinect is undetectable. Swapping the 2nd Kinect unit from the replacement console that I received solved the problem instantly.

For two hardware failures, console fan and Kinect unit from the same machine, I'm extremely leerly about quality control on these units. Kudos for Amazon for making things right amazingly fast. Microsoft? Epic stumble.

rating 5.0. Great Console. Truly a great console, and I'm not a fanboy. I got my Playstation 4 last week at midnight and played it all weekend and I wasn't sure that the Xbox One could provide me with a better experience then this. We'll to put it simply it went above and beyond what I expected it to do, the Kinect works about 90% of the time which is a HUGE step up from the old Kinect. The controller has such a natural feel in your hands and it has a good feel and weight to it as well. I could go on and on about it but I want to keep it short, it's worth it, buy the Xbox One!

rating 5.0. You'll want to get One! This console is such a leap ahead into the future that I'm still having a hard time wrapping my head around it. Everything works as advertised with a few minor hiccups here and there.

One of the first things I did was start watching tv on one channel, changed to a new channel, then remembered I wanted to buy and download Forza 5 so I went to the store, found Forza 5, purchased it, started downloading it and pinned it as a favorite so it'd be there when it was done downloading.

Now all of that may seem fairly routine, but I did all of that just using my voice! Without having to repeat anything. Once you learn the right voice commands and cadence it's seriously like living in the future. It's a real treat to use.

Another big highlight for me is the Game DVR and the Upload studio. It's so easy to record big moments in your games and share them that you won't believe it!

It's truly a great system with great features. Believe the hype. Get One! ;)

rating 1.0. Xbox One Defective When I received the Xbox One console, the package that held the device looked like it was crushed. Furthermore, the bottom of the box was busted open and the boxing tape was pealed off of the box. When inspecting the console itself, the box that held it was damaged as well. There was a puncture to the Xbox console package that was deep enough you could touch the actual device with your finger. Overall, a pretty crappy boxing job by Amazon especially for this expensive item.

Upon setting up the console, everything worked fine, but there is a defective disc drive that won't load game discs nor blue-ray discs. I've tried multiple times re-loading various discs and went through the trouble shooting tips from Xbox online without any success. Amazon sold me a defective product.

Lastly, I called Amazon to make this botched job right, but was pushed away and was referred to calling the Xbox hotline and Amazon wouldn't be able to send me a replacement Xbox One.

Overall, I'm shocked and disappointed with Amazon, especially how my Xbox One came to me in pretty poor packaging shape which probably contributed to my Xbox One being damaged during transit via FedEx. I am not a happy customer.

rating 1.0. Excitement. Frustration. Disappointment. Anger. Where to begin... I can't remember ever regretting a purchase this much in my life. I got both systems PS4 and Xbox One. I take this thing out of the box and it's twice as big, has a huge power brick, plus the camera is kind of huge also. Plugged everything in, did all the updates, took a really long time before I could actually do anything with it.

Then it got stuck on startup for a really long time... Then it worked but it will not recognize my cable box even though it is the standard Verizon Fios box. Tried all kinds of ways and nothing. So the whole TV multimedia thing... nope. Then it recognizes my voice commands less than half the time. My apartment is very quiet. Set it up so that it turns on when you say "xbox on"... usually have to say "xbox on" 5 times before it recognizes it. Now it will randomly turn on all the time!! Happened 3 times already today. I'm assuming it is mistaking room conversation or what's on TV with commands.

Finally, just 2 days after getting this thing, I unplugged it, put it back in the box and I'm debating wether to return it to Amazon or sell it.

rating 3.0. Glad the Xbox One has improved Honestly, I have a love/hate relationship with the Xbox One. I've been an Xbox customer, since the first Xbox was released. Initially, I was pretty disgusted with the quality of the Xbox One, considering the fact that I had to send the console back in to Microsoft on 3 separate occasions before I could finally receive a working Xbox One. I will give Microsoft credit they definitely tried to appease the situation by offering me free games and xbox live. In total, I received 3 free games, and I believe about 6 months of Xbox Live. I think the thing that bothered me the most with Microsoft is that they basically shot themselves in the foot with how they went about rolling out the Xbox One. For starters, they essentially made it seem like Kinect was a complete integral part to owning the Xbox One. I can honestly say, I have had my Xbox One for a year now, and I have actually used the Kinect with my games a handful of times. I used Kinect somewhat in Dead Rising 3 and Kinect Sports. Kinect was pretty basic and simple in Dead Rising 3, and absolutely shoddy in Kinect Sports. Overall, I only use Kinect to basically say "Xbox On" and "Xbox, Turn Off." So, needless to say, I absolutely welcomed the decision for Microsoft to start selling Xbox Ones without Kinect being bundled. It's completely a $100 add-on, and I definitely suggest you do yourself a favor and save the $100. However, I am really glad to see that Microsoft appears to be taking steps in the right direction. From what I can see, there's a lot of interesting and fun titles coming to the Xbox One, and a lot of the updates on the Xbox One has made the user interface not so clunky. I wish I wouldn't have had to wait an entire year for an actual decent gaming lineup, but now I'm excited to at least see titles around Christmas season, such as Sunset Overdrive, Halo: Master Chief Collection, and GTA V on the Xbox One. Hopefully, Microsoft takes more clues from Sony on their Games With Gold program, since to date the titles offered for the Xbox One have been abysmal at best. Overall though, despite my initial negativity surrounding the Xbox One due to my bad experiences and Microsoft's poor choices, I am actually looking forward to the positive changes that Microsoft and the Xbox One has to offer.
*Update 03/25/15*
I finally decided to sell my Xbox One, and made the leap to the Sony Playstation 4. While I appreciate that Microsoft has made vast improvements with the Xbox One, it was too little too late for me. Further, I much better enjoy my gaming experience with the Xbox One in comparison to the Xbox One. Sony won me over this console generation, better luck next time Microsoft.

rating 4.0. The Xbox One met my expectations but didn't blow them away. I really enjoy the new system and feel like it lives up to my expectations as far as the operating system and kinect usability. I love the voice control and that the kinect can run my TV and DirecTV box. I don't like that my Turtle Beach X41s don't work and that there isn't an adapter available yet, but I knew that going in and hope that the adapters come out sooner than next year. I am fortunate to have not had any defective components. The controller feels natural and I like that they got rid of the bulge in the back, but the location of the guide button takes some getting used to. I dock the console one star because it met my expectations but didn't blow them away.

rating 5.0. Great System with Lots of Potential I'm completely satisfied with my Xbox One so far. No problems whatsoever. It looks nice, the controller is slightly better than the 360, the Kinect 2.0 is noticeably much better, and it definitely feels like a next-gen console rather than just a souped-up version of the last gen. Microsoft has made the first step to taking gaming and entertainment into the future, and I'm excited about the potential this system shows, particularly with some of the non-gaming entertainment features that don't have much use yet.

I would recommend it, though I would add that if you're on the fence about spending the money there's no shame in waiting a few months and getting it when more games are out.

rating 5.0. WOW WOW WOW XBOX ONE! Great experience with this new console.  Great upgrade over the Xbox 360!  Forza 5 looks great, this is one happy customer!  Much better experience day one than with the PS4.

rating 4.0. An excellent addition to my entertainment center. Setting up my Xbox One to turn on my receiver, TV, cable box and itself on with a single command was a snap. It works great, changing channels is usually accurate (there are times it misinterprets what I say, but they're seldom).

Navigating between applications is incredibly simple and very fast. I was worried the Kinect was going to be wonky, but I've had no trouble using it to do nearly everything, including adjusting the volume on my receiver.

The new controller feels great and is very responsive. I love the improved D pad (much needed). I can use Eneloops with the controller so that's a plus. I've been using it a ton over two days and have yet to swap the packed in batteries.

I've played a few games (Killer Instinct and Battlefield 4), they've been running smooth and look good.

Nothing was better than waking up this morning, as I walked through the living room, to say "Xbox On" and "Xbox Watch ESPN" as I keep walking toward my morning coffee and to have everything up and tuned in before I made it across the room.
Very pleased so far and I only expect it to get better.

rating 2.0. Xbox One after experiencing PS4.... I might rate it better if I hadn't just been playing a PS4 for a week. I was at least excited to see what the Kinect and voice/gesture control offered from the much touted UI. Came out completely disappointed. Found it clunky and slower than even PS4's voice commands. Which is surprising considering it's an afterthought on PS4 and a huge focus on Xbox. System looks alright , but what the hell is up with the power brick? XBO is significantly larger than PS4 yet ALSO has a giant external PSU. Shoddy design compared to the sleek package of PS4.

Really in the end you're paying $500 for a game system that is less powerful than the $400 competitor. And imo the $400 competitor is superior or matching every feature except the TV pass through. Which at least in my case and I'm sure most of the younger generations - is completely worthless because we dropped or never had cable tv.

Xbox One isn't bad. The competition just outclassed them in every area that matters.

rating 5.0. Very happy with the XB1 purchase. I ordered the Day One back in June so I've been waiting for my product to show up. The initial start up took about a minute and then it informed me that it needed to download a 500 meg update which took less than 3 minutes to download and install. After the boot up the product has worked perfectly. I've had no issues with the disk drive and I purchased and installed all of the following (Battlefield 4, COD-Ghost, Assassins Creed 4, Lego Marvel Superheroes, Zoo Tycoon, Dance 2014, NBA Live 2014, Madden 25, FIFA 2014, Need for Speed, Fighter Within, Dead Rising 3, Forza 5 Limited Edition, Ryse - so it's gotten a good workout). The snap feature is great, as I've watched ESPN while playing Madden. The Kinect picks up my voice pretty well, I have had to repeat myself once in a while but not that much, and I love that I can scan in bar codes. The graphics are sweet - as sharp as the PS4 that I have. I love the fit and handle of the controller. The share function on the XB1 is not as good as the PS4, which is far easier to post to facebook. All of the multilayer modes of Battlefield 4 are working on the XB1, unlike the PS4 (I purchased the game for both systems so that I could compare them, the PS4 is currently unable to run Conquest and Rush). The Kinect is able to recognize me a I walk into the room, which is a cool feature, and the TV control/pass through is really sweet. I can't praise the snap feature enough. I purchased the extended warranty (which I did for the PS4 as well) since this is a launch item and there can/will be minor glitches I'm sure, but this is a really great product and I would recommend it to all my friends.

One side note, I do think that the PS4 does install disks to the drive somewhat faster, but not by a significant amount that I'm pulling out my hair.

rating 4.0. Slow CD install. Great gaming experience! Wow.. everything works fine but the disk drive seems to have issues. Everything is all set up so I pop in Ghosts (the only game I bought) and after 15 minutes the install is now at 1%. I am not hearing any loud sounds but it does sound like its just trying to read the disk over and over.

Disappointed in both launch systems.

Update- after a 30-45 minute install from the disk its ready to play.

I think the last time I had to wait this long to install a game was in the 90s. Bumped to 3 stars.

update-

So if you insert your disc, it starts to install the game and it seems to stand at 0% and you might think it did not even start. What it really does is downloading the huge patch, so the first 0-5% of the install is actually a download and it might take a while, depending on your internet connection. From this point on the install is much faster and you can start the game way before the install is even finished. While you play, the install will happen in the background and you won't even notice.

So no panic needed. Bumped to 4 stars.. would put it at 5 stars if MS would tell you this when its happening.

rating 5.0. Nice upgrade for your living room. Great system to impress guests when they visit! Ordered 2 systems ( one for brother ). Happy to say that both worked flawlessly. Easy setup within 15min.

rating 4.0. Feels like it's in beta, but lots of promise I can't give an incomplete product 5 stars, but you can see the potential with this one. Everything about this system will improve overtime from the OS to the games, to the kinect functionality.

As of now (day after launch), the Xbox One still feels like it's very much in beta. Much of the kinect functionality has promise, but is cumbersome at the moment. Voice commands are very hit-and-miss. If you just use the controller, the OS is very responsive, and snappy (no pun intended). Aesthetically, everything looks clean, if not perhaps a bit OVER-simplified (oddly enough). I found the lack of menus or categories in the game marketplace to be a strange omission - this will need to be fixed as the game library expands, and as the ability to search for particular kinds of content increases.

Live TV functionality is actually useful, and you can use the xbox one as your "receiver" of sorts. Turning on the system can now turn on your TV, your audio setup, and your cable box without the use of any remotes, if you can get the "xbox on" command to work consistently.

Right now, this system stands shoulder to shoulder with the PS4 in terms of games and functionality, given that Kinect isn't really a helpful functional addition right now (though I'm hopeful this area will be much improved soon enough). However, the Kinect adds $100 to the system price compared to the PS4. You do the math.

For now, it is not a must-buy, particularly if you're a gamer or you otherwise don't find the whole Live TV/Netflix/ESPN lifestyle isn't your cup of tea. If you have to be at the cutting-edge all the time, then of course you'll want to be an early adopter by virtue.

PS. Please, Microsoft, get rid of this power brick ASAP. It's 2013.

rating 4.0. Getting better This is a revised review.  My original purchase had hardware problems and it took a week to get it replaced from Microsoft.  I still do have an issue with the TV connection losing audio after a few days.  Multiple support calls to Microsoft have gone no where. rating 5.0. Better but not by much. Awesome. Could use some work in the interface. It seems to learn as it goes but it keeps hiding some apps on me. Quieter than a mouse, fast, responsive.

Controller is not that much improved but it did not need to be. Dualshock 4 is actually better.

It is not that big in size. Most entertainment hardware you own is about as big.

Kinect works amazingly, even at shorter distances than recommended. I have it at just 3 feet. It takes a bit to get used to you and you to get used to it. But it gets better the more you use it. Cannot wait to see how Microsoft evolves the technology.

Smartglass is pretty cool, I strongly recommend using it. It allows you to use your phone or tablet as a keyboard and remote. A whole peripheral for free.

Connectivity is very good. Not to the level of PC but nearly there.

Xbox live is still heads and shoulders over PSN. I own both the One and PS4 and trust me there is a huge difference. Microsoft built a $700 million facility to handle it's new server system and it's paying off. Sony failed to upgrade PSN for the PS4. I am very surprised that such a large and experienced company did so poorly with the PS4. They have been around much, much longer than Microsoft. They have one of the best records for system design in any industry, and this is their fourth iteration of the Playstation. I hope that Sony learns and comes out swinging and that Microsoft does not sit on this as is.

rating 5.0. Great gaming system Awesome system. Mine didn't have the disc drive issue I've seen posted around, discs, movies, CDs all work fine. Played a TON of killer instinct and need for speed and everything is just unbelievable. My only complaint is that I don't have a faster internet connection so I can buy games faster!

rating 5.0. Good Start First off some of these fake 1 star reviews are just funny. Don’t believe any of them. I actually own both the PS4 and Xbox One and will share my thoughts.
I’ll get the bad out of the way first. The installation process isn’t that great. First thing I did when I got the system up and running was go to the marketplace and download some games. Then I popped in NBA 2k and it started to install. Once it got to the point you can play a game I started playing. Shows the install progress at the bottom left corner just like on the PS4. Decided to go check the progress of my downloads from Xbox live and they were all in queued status. Apparently you cannot download anything during the disc install process like you can on the PS4. Only one download/disc install at a time. I was able to have 4 items downloading and have the disc game installing on the PS4. So 2k finishes installing and I pop in COD Ghost. Looks like everything is fine and I go check on it and its at about 40 something percent when it just switched to downloading killer instinct out of nowhere. So I go back to installing COD and it starts back at 0% and won’t budge. Cancel that and start over. Now it gets stuck on 1%. Now I try madden and it installs fine. Than back to COD and it finally installs correctly. So much for playing games that night as it was all wasted on installs that are really slow on both systems. Give PS4 the edge as it could at least multi task the disc and network downloads.
And that’s about it for my gripe. The rest has been a really good experience. The TV feature works well and to my surprise it controlled my century link prism IPTV box, receiver, and TV. Turning the system on turned all my devices on and the same thing when you turn it off. Pretty nice feature. The dashboard always beat my TV in the boot up process so systems boots really fast from standby. One thing I still can’t figure out is how you know when the system is being put in standby versus being completely shut off. It just says turn off. The PS4 has a turn off selection and a standby selection so it’s pretty obvious. PS4 also has the orange standby light to let you know you’re in standby.
Voice control really doesn’t excite me but it works really well on the Xbox. Only time I had to repeat a correct command was during turn on. Only problem is you have to say what you want to do verbatim. Used the VR code for the day one achievement and “Xbox, use code” worked flawlessly. Than later I had a $25 card to redeem forgot the command and said “Xbox, redeem code”. It did absolutely nothing because I didn’t say “Xbox, use code”. I would think the Xbox would be smart enough to know what the heck I’m talking about. But I guess as you learn the commands it will be fine. Another example is “Xbox, turn off” works but “Xbox, turn on” doesn’t. Just weird but it will evolve.
UI is nice and features galore on the Xbox. PS4 seems to be a bit faster and smoother but a much simpler UI. Kinect camera is pretty nice and picks up things very well. Haven’t had a chance to play any Kinect games yet though. Don’t have PS camera so can’t compare. Controller is awesome and I just prefer it over the DS4 although it is much improved. I’m not into the whole streaming my gaming thing so can’t really speak on it. No matter what system you pick you will be happy. They are both awesome but just be aware of the install times. Terrible on both. If you have a family like me the Xbox is the clear choice. If you just don’t care about all the extra’s the PS4 will save you some dough. But I much prefer to have the best of both worlds.

rating 5.0. Exceeds Expectations (which were already very high), A Great Machine For The Next Generation of Gaming! After seeing all the 1-star ratings from people who didn't even have it verified I wanted to put in my two cents. It is a great machine. Set up was easy, haven't gotten TV working yet but that's an issue with my TiVO, not the XBOX One. The graphics are amazing, the interface is slick, although does take awhile to pick up on how to use. The Kinect works AMAZING, 100%, when I'm the only person in the room. When my wife is in with me and there's a little more background noise I'd say it works about 90-95% of the time. This may be due to its placement close to a speaker as I can't move it anywhere else yet.

A lot of Playstation fans have come in and left 1 star reviews, like I'm sure some XBOX fans have done to them. My advice is, if you are on the fence, go play it in store. If you like it, buy it and you won't be disappointed. Buy the system that suits what you want and game away!

rating 5.0. Everyting I expected and more! Everything I expected and more. Easy to setup and very user friendly. My kids loved the Kinect games and I really like the ability to have live TV, Netflix, internet and gaming all on one dashboard. No switching outlets etc. Still getting used to the voice commands but without any training I have had good success using them and the software knowing what I am saying. rating 3.0. Xbox One Beta tester  ? I have high hopes issues will be fixed in future patches but I feel like a beta tester this time around, I have had every Microsoft console day one and even tested xbox live about 6 months before it ever released to the public and I have never been so disappointed in a early purchase. Everything setup seems like it will be great but most of the features for Kinect are hit or miss even in a near silent room speaking as clearly as possible I found myself repeating myself over and over to get commands to work Xbox open NETFLIX as the twitch app opens. The snap feature ugh not even close to working yet it seems real buggy and we couldn't get Skype to work when playing a game maybe its not implemented yet? The User Interface/Dashboard has too much going on similar to Windows 8 and is very sluggish. I wont get into the games as they were probably rushed trying to keep up with Sony plus I am not sure they are true next gen as they are not up to par with quality of PC games from 4 years ago. I wanted to get rid of my Roku 3 and use the Xbox instead of that or my PS4 that way we can use snap and the wife and I can watch and play at the same time but for some reason the Netflix app kept pausing and buffering and it was not an issue with my connection because my other player continued to function smoothly when we switched over to them. The new controller is nice except for the bumpers feel like they are in a weird place and it felt awkward to move my fingers up to them from the triggers. Also its almost 2014 and we still have to purchase a battery pack separate as the console does not ship with it and come with regular batteries instead, battery life is awesome either way because the kinect senses when you place the controller down and puts it to sleep but come on REALLY. I could go on for days but I will spare you my horrible writing skills. I would wait to purchase this item until next year sometime, plus I read they are going to make a revised version Q1 2014 which at a $500+games is maddening.

rating 5.0. The future is here, but set your expections Set your expectations accordingly. What do you get for an extra $100 over a PS4?

Identical graphics quality in games. Yes, the games will ultimately be identical (differences are hardly noticeable now).
Live television integration
The most advanced motion and voice detection system available.

Is it the best? No, but we're at Day 0 / LAUNCH! This thing will rock within a year, and the PS4 will never touch it. Anyone who falls back on hardware specs is purely ignorant - the same argument was made when the PS3 was launched, and 9/10 games looked better on the older and underpowered Xbox. The same will be repeated here. Take the plunge and invest in hardware that will lead the console gaming environment for the next 8 years.

rating 5.0. I couldn't have expected more! Gorgeous. As someone who has played all 3 consoles (WiiU, PS4, and now XB1), I am most impressed with the XB1 so far. The voice commands have incredibly fast response times, I've already made great use of the snap feature, and it plays games wonderfully. Moving my review down to 1 star. No 3D or Dolby surround support both of these were available on the 360. More and more the xbox one is a step backwards.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Xbox one did not disappoint This is an excellent buy. I was debating getting the PS4 but I stuck with Microsoft because I liked the 360. I got it today and played it for a good 4 hours. Mine had no problems like some of the problems that have been reported. I set it up in about 15 minutes and then had to wait another 20 because of updates. The audio and video picture is a lot better. Going in and out of gameplay and the dashboard takes 1 sec tops, which is a big improvement from the 360. You can just just click the game icon and it loads right where you left off. The new controller is awesome, the feel of it is much smoother with the gameplay.  The kinect works way better than expected, this new version is a major improvement to the last one. The commands you can tell the kinect work like a charm. It also works way better inside the gameplay. Kinda weird to say xbox on and the thing turns on but who's complaining? The xbox one did not disappoint me.

input:
rating 2.0. isn't compatible with my new Samsung PN59D7000 series I just bought the Samsung PN59D7000 59-Inch 1080p 600 Hz 3D Plasma HDTV (Black) and was excited to find some decently priced PS3 glasses. But these are not compatible. And, compared to my other glasses, they are heavy and awkward over standard glasses.
I am returning these to Amazon and hope someone reads this review and doesn't make the same mistake of ordering for this type of TV.

rating 4.0. Good but not great I purchased Sony's new Playstation branded 3D glasses for 2 main reasons, rechargeable built in battery and the claimed "reduced" crosstalk and ghosting. After testing the glasses and comparing it to my old Sony BR100, I can honestly say it is a good compliment but not a good replacement. I tested Uncharted 3, Ico/Shadow, Sly Cooper, Super Stardust, Wipeout HD, a couple of 3D videos, and Bolt to compare the picture quality and "reduced crosstalk/ghosting" features. And judging from my viewing experience the glasses fair almost completely the same with regards to the reduced crosstalk/ghosting. For the most part, I couldn't even differentiate the differences between the two glasses. The only way I could see a difference was to pause/freeze the frame but even then it was so negligible it hardly made a difference. The main difference I could judge from this glasses compared to the BR100 is its lightness and color contrast. These glasses are extremely light, easy to wear, and provide a natural viewing experience. My old BR100 produced an extremely light/bright viewing experience ,I never noticed before, similar to the "cool" color option in any tv's picture setting. These glasses produce more of a "warm" picture setting and colors seem more natural.

What I didn't appreciate about these glasses is the extremely reflective black lenses. Unlike the BR100, these pair of glasses has lenses that will turn black when it connects to a 3d source and the inner lens will reflect images from the side. It's something viewers will have to get use to to, but I had a very hard time adjusting. Though the glasses are light weight it does come with a cost. The plastic use feels really cheap compared to my BR100. The BR100 feels as if it was built like a tank, very sturdy earpieces, thick plastic, and extremely heavy compared to this pair.

I also noticed some viewers have issues with the glasses being too small. I don't want to include it as a negative because I didn't have any issues with the size, but I can also see why some viewers would. I would however include this aspect as "Neutral" and warn customers, it's ultimately up to them but these glasses do seem fitted for Asian/European sizes.

Positives:
-Built in rechargeable battery
-Extremely light weight
-Produces better "warm" picture
-Most affordable Sony 3d glasses

Negatives:
-Black reflective inner lens is a distraction
-Claimed "reduce crosstalk/ghosting" is negligible/unidentifiable
-Plastic feels cheap

As mentioned earlier, these glasses are good as compliments to any older Sony glasses. If anyone is looking for extra glasses with built in rechargeable battery I would definitely recommend these pairs. But if they are expecting these glasses to "reduce crosstalk/ghosting" like the product claims I wouldn't recommend it.

rating 5.0. Works great I bought a pair to go along with the set I got with my Playstation 3D Display.  They work perfectly fine and I don't really find any discomfort.  I do suggest taking them off before answering the door, I have gotten many strange looks stepping out with them on.

rating 5.0. Nice pair of 3D glasses I bought these PlayStation 3D glasses to see if they would work on my Samsung 3D TV and they did. The original 3D glasses that came with my TV weren't good for gaming, these are! They are nice because you don't have to charge them much to get power. 30 minutes of charging time equals 30 hours of battery life.
rating 5.0. Great Glasses! The glasses are pretty awesome! they are light and comfortable. You'll really enjoy the 3d effect on the playstation 3d display or any 3d tv.

rating 5.0. Great pair of Universal 3D glasses! Sony's Playstation 3 3D glasses work great as an alternative to the more expensively priced glasses made for specific 3D TV's.

I have a 60" Plasma Panasonic 3D TV and there was no lag or ghosting using these glasses (ghosting can be more intrusive or less intrusive depending on the movie you're watching). There was no sync up needed, I just put in my 3D Bluray and started watching; there's no setup required(my 3D bluray player is a Samsung, by the way, and not Panasonic - the brand of 3D bluray player you have doesn't have to match the brand of 3D TV you have, as long as you have these glasses or your 3D TV's brand of glasses).

Other reviews have stated that these glasses are uncomfortable, and while I wouldn't say they're comfortable exactly, I didn't have any serious problems with them. If you have an average sized head, I can't imagine you'll have too many issues with discomfort.

One thing you'll want to keep in mind when getting these glasses, you have to make sure that your 3D TV uses Infrared and not Bluetooth, as these glasses are only compatible with Infrared 3D TV's. You also want to make sure that you're not blocking the Infrared transmitter (mine is located on the bottom part of my TV) so you'll want to make sure you don't have any speakers or anything else in front of your TV, otherwise the glasses won't work.

All in all, if you have an Infrared 3D TV, you can't go wrong with theses glasses. They're half the price of the name brand 3D glasses (the one's matched up with their respective 3D TV's) and they work great. They're extremely light and require zero startup to get working. I'd highly recommend these glasses to anyone looking for a less expensive alternative to the higher priced 3D glasses out there.

rating 4.0. Viable alternative to Panasonic 3D glasses I bought these to watch 3D movies on my 2011 Panasonic plasma TV. (65ST30) Panasonic's OEM glasses are $100, if you're lucky enough to find a pair. These cost half, so I gave them a shot. Later, I bought two lightly-used Panasonic glasses from a friend, so now I have both to compare.

I have two pairs of these Playstation 3D glasses, and two pairs of [[ASIN:B0058SYDTI Panasonic TY-EW3D3MU 3D Active Shutter Eyewear for Panasonic 3D HDTVs (Medium)]]. I have a normal guy-sized head, and my wife has a very small, nearly child-size, female head.

For me, these glasses were noticeably, but not overly objectionably, heavy. For my wife, she was complaining about the weight shortly after the 1.5 hour mark. She made it through the movie, and wasn't thrilled by the glasses. She was willing to wear them again, but again, not overly thrilled by them. This is not an observation on the function of the glasses, just the comfort for HER on HER face.

Both of us enjoyed the 3D viewing, and had no problems with the actual operation of the glasses with our TV. Her only objection was the comfort, mostly a matter of her small head and tiny nose holding up a fairly beefy set of glasses. I definitely noticed the glasses, but they didn't really bother me.

Then we tried the Panasonic 3rd Gen glasses which I acquired later. My wife LOVES them. VERY lightweight... you barely know they're on. I'm a fan as well. BUT, we did do a comparison (A/B testing throughout an entire movie), and from a purely functional standpoint, there was zero difference that either of us could detect. The Panasonics remained much more comfortable due to their light weight, but both showed the same 3D picture. I though the Panasonics might show a brighter picture, because the tint on the lenses doesn't seem as dark as the Sony's, but in use, again, we couldn't tell a difference.

The Panasonic glasses are getting hard to find, and you pay a premium for them (full MSRP) when you do find them. These are a very acceptable substitute, IMO, and half the price ($100 vs $50). I'm glad I have mine, and will keep them on hand for guests, but I'll usually use the Panasonics, just because I have them. If I didn't have the Panasonics, I'd be reasonably content with the Sony glasses.

rating 5.0. Works with Epson 3010 They work flawlessly with Panasonic 3D bluray player to Epson 3010.  [[ASIN:B005VONPT8 Epson PowerLite Home Cinema 3010, Full HD 1080p, 2D and 3D Home Theater Projector with Integrated Speakers (V11H421020)]] They fit the kids (7 and 11) much better than the larger glasses that come with the Epson.  Hugo was awesome to watch.

rating 4.0. Good 3D glasses. I have the Sony KDL 55NX720 television and bought these to see if they improved the 3D on this set. These glasses are must for the Sony TV's because the other glasses the TDG-BR250 that came free with set work but only if your head is aligned with your TV with a lot of crosstalk and ghosting on some movies. The Playstation glasses lets you watch from more comfortable angles and are lighter and fixes the problems mentioned on the other glasses. If you are upset with your 3D on your television buy these glasses, if you want to see what your 3D TV is capable of you got to get these glasses.

rating 5.0. Works great with 82" Mitsubishi I was skeptical that these would work at first, but after trying, I love them.  I like that they are rechargable and that I could use along with the 2 pair of Mitsubishi glasses that I already have.  I couldn't seem to find the exact pair that I had already, but the playstation glasses work with the emitter from my other glasses.  I would recommend and I'm glad I finally have enough pairs so I can have friends over to watch with me.

rating 4.0. Comparison of Several Active Shutter Glasses I recently bought a Sony Bravia KDL-55NX720 which is 3d capable but comes without glasses. The 3d factor was of interest to me and being a bit of an electronics geek. I had to get 3d glasses and check it out. Rather than getting just one type of 3D glasses I decided to get 3 different compatible glasses and check them out. The three glasses I got are:

Xpand X103
Sony TDG-BR250
PlayStation 3 3D Glasses

I wear rimless bifocal lenses that are 32 mm tall. For comfort I would rank the glasses as follows:

1. Sony TDG-BR250
3. PlayStation 3D glasses
3. Xpand X103

All of the glasses worked over my prescription glasses but I found the Sony TDG-BR250 to fit over my glasses best and to be the most comfortable. I found the Xpand X103 the least comfortable though they are OK and I don't really have an issue sitting through a movie. Based on other reviews, the comfort level varies quite a lot from viewer to viewer. Both the PlayStation glasses and the Xpand were a little more awkward to get adjusted over the glasses. So for me, that was probably the biggest comfort factor. None of the nose pieces that came with the Xpand gave me a great fit. The PlayStation glasses had an adjustable nose piece that worked better for me.

For 3d performance I would rank them in the following order:
1. PlayStation 3D glasses
2. Xpand X103
3. Sony TDG-BR250

The PlayStation 3D and the Sony TDG-BR250 resulted is similar colors and brightness. I think I got the most flickering with the BR250s but hard to say on that count, none were bad. The TDG-BR250 looked good as long as you kept your head level, if you tilted it even slightly to the left or right, the picture got lighter and there was abundant crosstalk. The PlayStation glasses have a polarization filter that reduces the crosstalk and it definitely works. The Xpand 103 glasses were similar to the PlayStation glasses with crosstalk performance. You could tilt your head left or right and the picture didn't change much. However; the picture was a touch darker with the Xpand glasses and the colors were a touch different. I liked the brighter picture and the color rendition of the Sony glasses better. You could probably adjust your picture so that the Xpand glasses would give you a similar color/brightness profile if you were just using Xpand glasses. The color and brightness wasn't so different that watching programing with the different brands would be a big deal.

I know the Xpand 103 glasses work for brands other than Sony. They were easy to synch with my Sony Bravia TV. I believe the SonyPlaystion glass are also universal for infrared (but not the newer Bluetooth) systems. They are listed as compatible with Sony, Samsung, Panasonic, Mitsubishi, Toshiba, and Sharp Active Shutter 3D. At least for the Sony system, they synced automatically. The TDG-BR250 are meant for Sony systems.

Both sets of Sony glasses are rechargeable. The Xpand uses an easy to get inexpensive battery. I haven't had the the system long enough to know which I prefer. It is really going to depend on how long the battery lasts on the Xpands versus how often the Sony's need to be charged and how well they hold their charge if unused for a period of time. Since I use the Xpand battery type for a locator system I use to find my cats when they go outside, I always have a ready supply of the batteries. I'm guessing I would actually prefer the battery system, which I assume will not need batteries very often, to recharging. The Sony's are supposed to only need 3 minutes of charging for an hour of viewing so they charge fast. Still, stuff that recharges tends to not stay charged if it sits around unused for a period of time. I can see it being a pain if someone comes over and wants to check out the 3d but the glasses need charging. Same goes if we get more content on TV. Batteries tend to not run down for a long time when idle and can be changed immediately (IF you have the batteries on hand) if they are dead.

Here's my final rank

1. SonyPlaystation 3D glasses
2. Xpand 103 3d glasses
3. Sony TDG-BR250

There are polarizing filters available from Sony for the TDG-BR250 that are supposed to correct the crosstalk issue. I ordered a pair from Sony a couple of weeks ago but still haven't received them. If they work, I might raise the Sony TDG-BR250 to the top of the list since, for me, they are the most comfortable. Without the filters, the Playstion glasses definitely outperform the other sets. I gave them 4 star rating because they don't really fit perfectly. Also, if you have lights on behind the viewing area you get a fair amount of reflection in the glasses.

rating 5.0. Battery does not hold charge. Works great with Epson 3010 Projector One of the two glasses will not hold a charge if left turned off and sitting more than a day. Just figured this out, and I am outside of the 30 day return window.

The PS3 glasses work great with the Epson 3010 3D projector. They are smaller than the Epson glasses that come with the projector and fit my wife and kids better.

I like that they are USB rechargeable and about 1/2 the price of the Epson glasses. I did not conduct any through testing of the glasses, however they seem to work about as well as the Epson glasses, fit better, USB rechargeable and best of all about 1/2 the price. These will be used for my kids.

Do not confuse these with the Sony TDG-BR250/B 3D glasses. I mistakenly ordered the TDG-BR250/B glasses and they did not work with the Epson 3010 projector. Thanks to Amazon I was able to ship them back for free.

Bottom line the Playstation 3 3d glasses work as a great lower cost alternative that fit the kids for use on the Epson 3010 projector.

rating 5.0. Much better than the Epson provided glasses Do it yourselves person, I plan to create a media room. Here are the points in to consideration

* Budget around $5000 including the seats

* Use good receiver, projector, speakers available in marker

Here is the combination I found

From Home depot

From Monoprice

Packaging was really really great. Glasses are really comfortable compare to the one with Epson 3010. Kids will like this glass much better.

I recommend this product.

rating 5.0. Fit smaller heads, Epson 3010 compatible, and work great. A MUCH better fit for my 8 year old's head than the Epsons.  Those she had to basically hold on to, otherwise they would fall off.  So far everyone reports that the Sonys are comfortable, and the 3D is excellent.  And you have to love the price!  I have 3 pair now and will likely pick up a few more before long.  I wish the power button was a little easier to activate, but the flip side of that is it's impossible for them to be powered on/off accidentally.

rating 1.0. Playstation 3 3D glasses [[ASIN:B00562O8U0 PlayStation 3 3D Glasses]]I recently purchased this item from amazon and the battery is not charging the led light on the glasses comes on for about one minute and then comes off, after I unplug the glasses from charging and the led light on the glasses flashes for less than a minute then turns off.it not working.

rating 3.0. Does NOT work with all Active Shutter TV This glasses is also marketed as a "universal active shutter 3D glasses" that works with Panasonic TVs. However it uses IR receiver with sync up with the TV. All Panasonic TV that produced after 2012 has switched from IR emitter to RF (Bluetooth) emitter for their 3D glasses. In other words, it will NOT WORK with your Panasonic TV if it's a newer model. There are very little information about this. I spend an entire night searching on the web to find out why it wouldn't work with my TV (TC-P55UT50). Many vendors even market this glasses along side with my TV even though they don't work together.

rating 3.0. universally compatible - IR only - does NOT work with RF like epson 3020 I should have paid attention to the details and not think that because it works for the epson 3010 it would work with my epson 3020... it doesn't - those glasses are for infrared (IR) 3d systems; not the newer RF ones

rating 1.0. Don't Work with ACER 9500 These glasses are probably fine for use with some other TV or projector but not mine. There does not seem to be a source of information that will match glasses with TVs. I bought these as an experiment. I had hoped that they would work, but I wasn't very surprised that they didn't.

I have a new Acer 9500HD projector. I comes with one pair of active DLP-Link glasses. I know from the forums that the Optoma glasses also work but those ones are seventy dollars, I tried these at half the price. Experiment failed.

I don't blame Sony. My bad.

rating 5.0. Works with Panasonic Plasma ST30 I have a Panasonic VIERA TC-P55ST30 Plasma TV and these Sony glasses work better than the Panasonic 3D glasses. While the Panasonic 3D glasses are very good, these glasses seem to keep the image more stable with less noticeable fast scene blurring or flickering. They are noticeably heavier than the Panasonic glasses though. I am playing 3D games mostly using a PS3 though, not sure if this is the main reason why they look better. For the price these are good bang for your buck.

Update: Something I have noticed when compared to the Panasonic glasses. The Sony glasses 3D image is clearer, and keeps a tighter depth, which does look more accurate. The Panasonic glasses seem have more blur especially noticeable on smaller readable text, and also the 3D image appears more stretched back with more depth, but looks exaggerated which is more apparent the way people look in 3D movies. However in 3D games this can be a nice effect. So two different effects, while slight, but they are noticeable to me. I've spent many hours with each and can pick up the differences. Too me seems like the Sony glasses have a higher refresh rate which would make sense on the differences I see, that may not be the case, but seems like the effect.

rating 3.0. Passable active 3D glasses I bought these as a 2nd pair of 3D glasses for my wife. I went with the Playstation 3D glasses because they are name-brand universal active shutter 3D glasses and were discounted to $29.99 at the time. Compared to the $60 I paid for my first pair of active shutter 3D glasses (Panasonic TY-EW3DMU), these were cheap enough for my wife to agree to purchase them. Since I have both these glasses, I will mainly compare my thoughts on the Playstation glasses against my experience with the Panasonic glasses.

1) Weight: The Playstation glasses are noticeably heavier when picking them up compared to the Panasonic glasses. All the weight is in the front as you would expect. Maybe to compensate for the heavier weight in the front, Sony decided to make the back of the glasses wrap tighter around behind the ears so they hold to your head rather than slip down your face. I could just be spoiled with the lightweight Panasonic glasses, but these are probably the heaviest glasses I've worn, and I've been through my share of eyeglasses with thick prescription lenses.
2) Comfort: Because of how tight the glasses hold behind the ears, there is a definite amount of pressure felt when I first put the glasses on. It didn't bother me after settling in to watch a movie, but I could see this maybe hurting someone with a big head if they wear it long enough. The positive here is that at least the glasses feel secure and don't gradually slide down or move around if I turn my head like the Panasonic glasses do on my face.
3) Fit: Since I wear prescription glasses already, I'm always mindful of how 3D glasses fit over them. One of the main complaints I have with the Panasonic glasses is that they don't sit nicely with my eyeglasses since there isn't enough space around frame and lenses to sit over a pair of glasses. Instead, the Panasonic glasses just sit in front of the eyeglasses instead of fitting over them, and I feel like I'm constantly readjusting the Panasonic glasses since they don't feel properly seated on my face. Fortunately here with the Playstation glasses, there is plenty of space to fit a pair of eyeglasses under them, so wearing glasses underneath them doesn't interfere with their positioning.
4) Performance: I let my wife use the Panasonic glasses first, and she remarked about how dark it made the picture. I didn't really notice it before, but switching between the Playstation glasses and the Panasonic ones really highlighted how much the Panasonic glasses darken the picture. I didn't have a problem with it, but my wife definitely prefers the Playstation glasses because it doesn't seem to dramatically change the picture's brightness. Otherwise, I haven't noticed any performance difference between the 2 glasses with both of them producing a good 3D effect without any ghosting.
5) Design: Here is where I have the most problem with the Playstation glasses. When the glasses are folded up, the glasses are still very thick since the plastic holding the lens juts out a couple inches. I suppose it is a tradeoff to do this so that the 3D glasses have enough space to fit over prescription glasses, but I expected Sony to make their 3D glasses as sleek as the Panasonic ones. Instead, these seem clunky. Instead of placing the power switch in an obvious and easily accessible location on top of the glasses (like the Panasonic ones), the Playstation glasses places a switch labeled "battery on/off" on the inside right part of the frame. Instead, the microUSB charge plug is on the top of the frame is next to what I assume is a button to re-sync the glasses with the TV. The problem with the microUSB charge plug is that there is a hard plastic cover over it to keep out dust, and it is not easy to remove since it is flush with the frame with only a tiny side available to pry it up.

Overall, the Playstation 3D glasses are functional and a good cheap alternative to the Panasonic active 3D glasses. It's also great that these glasses are meant to be compatible with other brands of IR-emitting 3D TVs. If you need some cheap active 3D glasses for friends and family to watch 3D content with you, these will do the job nicely. I've seen these priced as low as $19.99 this year, so we've come a long way since the $100+ per pair of 3D glasses. However, for long periods of use, I would probably go with the lightweight Panasonic glasses especially if you don't wear prescription glasses.

rating 1.0. Read the fine print, IR glasses not RF, wont' work w. 2012 Panasonic Plasma GT50 models These are IR capable not Bluetooth/RF, my 2012 Panasonic is the latter so I have to order the Panasonic or other RF capable glasses.

Rating is not a reflection of the quality of the glasses, but the product description which doesn't call out clearly that there is a difference between IR and RF active 3D glasses.
rating 5.0. Awesome Works with the Epson 3010e without any synching or configuration. Lightweight and comfortable. Can't beat it for the price. Limited to 3 per purchase though?
rating 1.0. These glasses do not work with LG TVs I thought these glasses would work since I have a PS 3, but I was wront. They only work with Sony TVs not LG!!
rating 1.0. 3d glasses these glasses work great , but they are so uncomfortable. you could not sit through a movie with these. try elsewhere

rating 5.0. Great 3d glasses. These work really well with my sony 55' bravia LED. I have a ps3 so there was no need to buy to expensive sony 3d glasses. These ps3 glasses are far more superior and are light. Minutes of charging will give you hours of 3d viewing. I bought four of these glasses. Highly recommend them!!

rating 5.0. Excellent universal IR 3d glasses for Panasonic 2011 TV Excellent glasses for Panasonic 2010 and 2011 3D TV. Flawless syncing and excellent 3d with no issues. Be sure not to put anything in front of the TV lower panel, where the lights are., such as center channel speaker, as it blocks the signal. I thought my glasses were defective until I moved the speaker. Then I had 100% sync and bright and crisp 3d.

Note: not for Panasonic 2012 models. They use a different tech.

rating 5.0. Fantastic for a Sony 3D TV I first learned of using these with the Sony TVs on the AVS forum. I did try the BR250 and 750 as the look was nicer, but the crosstalk issue was present on both pair and the 750 just let in too much light around the lenses and allowed for too many disctractions. Further, both the 250 and the 750 were subject to increased crosstalk and color degradation when one moved the head very far in any axis.

The playstation glasses however are made for gamers...and anyone who is a gamer or knows one, knows that the head of a gamer doesn't stay stationary! So, the filters in the lens greatly reduce the issues. They darken the picture a bit, but that is easily remedied if it is bothersome.

Now, the looks were kind of a factor as these don't look as cool as the others...but that was first blush. Actually when everyone has them on watching a movie or you happen upon someone watching, they really do have a seriously retro-cool factor! At least my household and I think so...lol.

Bottom line they perform better than the others at a fraction of the price. I whole-heartedly recommend them. In fact I hav now purchased 6 pair.

rating 5.0. Perfect fit for what I need! I bought 3 pairs for my Epson 3D projector.  These are as good as, if not better than, the two pairs came with the projector.  They are rechargeable thought mini-USB, so they are better in that regard, because the original ones are better powered, especially for the great price: the total for 3 pairs is less than one pair from Epson!

rating 2.0. eh, sub par, okay? I bought these just before they went on clearance everywhere else, but I still got them for a decent price on amazon. They work, but they're not very comfortable. They're heavy on the nose and sit up too high and let in too much ambient light from underneath because of it. There's so much space between the bottom of the lenses and your cheeks that the reflection of any ambient light on things behind you get reflected in the lens as well as the image of your own eyes. It's incredibly annoying in anything other than a perfectly darkened room. And then, even the light from the display itself gets reflected off your cheekbones, interfering with your view.

Also, the glass in the lenses aren't uniform either. You can see the distortions, or waviness, in the glass. I'm wearing them as I type this and as I move my head, the image wobbles and has fuzzy areas from the uneveness in the lenses. I've got $5 sunglasses that don't distort this badly. All, in all, a poor effort by Sony. I'd be pissed off if I paid the original MSRP for these pieces of crap.

And don't apply too much pressure when cleaning the lenses, you'll break the lcd pixels pretty easily causing black sploches on the lenses. I swear I try not to touch them at all, yet they constantly require cleaning. They're like dirt magnets.

I have not tried them on any other tv other than the playstation display I got this year. They work fine on that. They do work, which is the only good thing I can say about this product.

rating 5.0. Fabulous 3D Images with these 3D Active Shutter Glasses The 3D images are fantastic using these 3D glasses. The images seen using these active shutter glasses are much better than the images seen on the big screen in the theaters using passive glasses.

The price of these has dropped so much since I bought my 55" Sony 3D Television, [[ASIN:B00391Q7AY Sony BRAVIA KDL55HX800 55-Inch 1080p 240 Hz 3D-Ready LED HDTV, Black], that I now have five pairs of these glasses. The glasses are suppose to work with many other brands of televisions, but I have only used them on my 55" Sony LED TV.

The glasses also work with simulview games on the Playstation 3, which is where a two person game shows two full size images on the screen at the same time, but the two glasses used are set 180 degrees off from each other so that the two game players see two entirely different images on the screen.

I also have used these with my television set to simulate 3D on non-3D movies. Works great doing that too.

The only negatives are that the usb cover for charging on top of the glasses is a little difficult to pry off without using a knife blade, and the on/off switch on the inside of the frame is a little hard for me to flick on or off using my finger nail, but these are just minor gripes. The glasses come with a soft case that doubles as a wipe to wipe the lenses if you accidentally smudge them with your finger.

The glasses charge fast, in like 20 or 30 minutes, but they last for many hours. Each pair also comes with a short mini USB charging cable.

With the price drop to around $20 a pair, these is no reason not to get a few pairs of these. Some of the graphics on the screen look like they are in the living room with you. Incredible depth perception with these glasses.

rating 2.0. not compatible purchased a new lg 55" 3d led sart tv was told these glasses would work to play with tv and ps3 games purchased two pair only to find out they are simulview not dual play and not compatible with the lg  tv or ps3 game call of duty black ops 2. will be returning and getting the lg dual play glasses instead

rating 3.0. NOT Compatible with NEW Samsung TV's I am unable to give much of a review because these glasses ended up not being compatible with my tv. I have a new SAMSUNG, but Samsung this year switched their tv's from syncing w/ 3d glasses via IR to syncing via Bluetooth. As with any 3DTV purchase... pay extra attention to how your 3dtv syncs w/ glasses before purchasing these.

rating 3.0. Works perfectly but very tight across a wide adult head These are solidly built and function well for months, allowing a full 3D view at a high angle even if worn over spectacles, or slipped down the nose. They cut out a fair bit of light, but that isn't distracting. They work fine with 2 player Simulview.

The downside with them is comfort. Maybe I have a wide brow, or maybe they are designed for kids but I'm surprised by the strong pinch between the arms of these glasses applied to either side of my head while I'm wearing them. A 3d film has to be more engrossing than three stars or I get distracted by that pinching sensation (and in that case, I'll watch the second half of the movie without them on - the blurry 2d picture is less distracting to me than the pain across my forehead).

Considering the discomfort & that the PS3 3d line has basically been discontinued and that the PS4 won't be backward compatible I'm not sure if I'd recommend these.

rating 5.0. Great deal Bought these for a media room to view 3D movies. Only 20.00 when the original glasses were going to be 129.00. Thank you Sony support for telling me these would work just as good with my system.  Bought 6 pair so everyone could watch the movie together. That's a lot of money saved!!

rating 4.0. Improvement in 3D Quality, No flicker, Not very Comfortable I picked up a 3D setup as soon as I could and I have enjoyed it more and more each year.

I originally purchased 2 pairs of the [[ASIN:B004U5QE4W Sony TDG-BR250/B Rechargeable 3D Adult Glasses, Black]] and while they were comfortable, they were killing my eyes just after a few minutes of watching a 3D movie with them. I eventually just dealt with it and tried to adjust, until I started to hear good things about these Sony Playstation 3D glasses. They are much less expensive and over the years of 3D Sony must have reevaluated their technology. These glasses are so much easier on the eyes that the Sony TDG-BR250's are. There is no irritating flicker or strain at all.

Now, these Playstation glasses are not near as comfortable as my TDG-BR250's are, however I can deal with it because my actual eyes are not sore when watching. These are of a very similar design to other glasses, but feel a little cheaper though they are very light. The nose pinches and the top brow part simply makes them less comfortable. But I am willing to get past all of that due to the low cost and improved quality of the 3D presentation.

Some reviews are saying that these glasses do not work with all Active 3D HDTV's. I have a 55" Sony LED-LCD and they work great! Read some reviews if you are unsure if these 3D glasses will work or no with you current 3D HDTV. If you have a Sony, I'm sure you're safe.

I will keep my TDG-BR250's around for when I have some friends over, however when reaching into the 3D glasses bin, I'll be grabbing for these Sony Playstation 3D glasses first! Recommended!

rating 4.0. Not bad, but not amazing. I mainly chose this wheel because I wanted the largest possible diameter from what Thrustmaster made.

The construction is very nice, the leather feels great and I like the metal center. The only negative I have on construction is the that feel of the paddle shifters is not exactly the same comparing left and right sides. Neither feels bad, but they're slightly different.

The two things I don't like about this wheel relate to buttons. First off, they're not labeled (aside from things like Pit, Wash, Scroll, etc, which aren't actually helpful) so it can take a while to figure out the controls. Secondly, comparing to the wheel that came with my TX servo (I go the 458 Italia Edition) it has very few buttons. Not a very big deal for the Xbox, but when using it with a PC I find myself switching back to the (smaller, cheaper feeling) 458 wheel to have more buttons for games like Beamng.Drive.

Overall, I think it's fairly priced. Maybe a little bit high, but the construction is solid, unlike the Thrustmaster F1 wheel. It is much more comfortable than the 458 Italia wheel it replaced, and I don't think I'll go looking for another any time soon.

rating 5.0. An absolutely phenomenal wheel for the price... As an owner of WAY too many high end wheels and racing peripherals, it's really hard to understate what an incredible wheel this is for the price. 180 bucks is DIRT cheap when it comes to racing wheels - let alone steering wheels with integral button boxes and paddle shifters. I've owned some cheap sub 200 dollar 'Alcantara' ebay wheels before, and the quality and stitching of the Alcantara on the TM is much, much better. Feels incredible in the hand.

Just for an example of the type of bargain this is - a stock Momo Mod 30 (one of the cheaper suede racing wheels, very popular for sim racing) will set you back 230 dollars for the wheel alone. No mounting options, and certainly no buttons or paddle shifter. You would obviously need to fabricate or purchase those items to be able to use it on a TM wheel base. The same wheel (Momo Mod 30) customized with an added button box, mounting hardware and shifter goes for (on average) about 600 dollars from custom sim hardware vendors online.

Another huge factor is the weight. The TM 599XX Evo wheel, with all of its buttons, shifters and mounting hardware weighs as much as a bone stock Mod 30 with no add-ons (roughly 2.5 lbs). Add on the custom button box, shifters and mounting hardware and that makes for a ton of weight for a lower end wheel like a T500/300 to drive. You're going to lose a LOT of FFB feeling. Custom aftermarket wheels like this are really made to be driven by a high end servo wheel, with gobs and gobs of torque.

If I didn't know the price points, and I had to compare a high end, customized Momo and the TM 599XX Evo wheel then of course the custom wheel would win hands down. There's really no question there. The Momo is an actual real-world racing wheel, designed to steer dedicated race vehicles, sometimes with no power steering. It is designed to handle tremendous amounts of torque and its feel and build quality definitely reflect that. The TM wheel is designed to handle the torque output of consumer grade sim racing wheels, roughly 4.5 N/m max (for reference, powerful servo wheels can easily output 5-6 times that amount of torque) and, similarly, it's construction reflects that as well. Also a lot of aftermarket sim-racing button boxes are of superb build quality, often using military grade switches and connectors and real carbon fiber housings. The TM 599XX Evo button box is, while well built and sturdy, made from consumer grade material like an Xbox or PlayStation controller. Like I said, no question as to which is a better overall piece of equipment.

BUT!! With the knowledge that the custom high end wheel is easily 3-4 times the price, and knowing how much more it will weigh compared to the TM 599XX Evo (wheel weight is CRUCIAL for consumer wheels with lower torque output. Just an extra few ounces will drastically reduce the apparent feel of the force feedback) means there really is no comparison; the TM is the clear winner.

As long as you go into this purchase realizing you are not buying an ultra high-end custom peripheral, you will be in love with this wheel. Especially if you are coming from a wheel OTHER than the T500 (the factory T500 wheel is a VERY, very nice wheel). The difference in quality and feel between the 599XX Evo and the factory plastic TX wheel is staggering.

Overall, this wheel earns my ultimate praise and I have recommended it to everyone I know who owns a TM wheel base. Superb wheel at a great price.

rating 5.0. I love it! Great feel I love it! Great feel. I wish it had more buttons, and I wish the knob on the bottom right was a multi-position switch, not a spring back switch (meaning the switch springs back to center after you twist it up/down). However, you can push the twist button in, so that's a plus. But overall, this wheel is superb! You can always download software that will allow you to assign keystrokes to a combination of button presses if it is that serious, but for most, it probably won't be. The alcantara feel is awesome! The paddle shifters give you a nice positive click. I wish they were adjustable (for me, I have fairly large hands, but I would like the shifters to be just a few centimeters closer to wheel rim). Then again, maybe not, because I would lose the agility of being able to get my hands out of the way of the wheel on those fast switch backs.

All in all, again, great wheel! The quality is outstanding. I have only praises for this wheel!

rating 3.0. Fatal flaw: pausing the game for everyone when changing gear/abilities. I'm not a fan of Diablo as a whole, but I have friends that are; explaining my purchase. The story is nothing special and feels very drawn out; and the combat simply isn't that interesting. But worst of all; if you're playing with your friends next to you, you'll know the biggest problem with this game immediately. If a player wants to change their gear or level up or modify their abilities; they have to pause the game for everyone. Only one person can do this at a time. With the loottastic playstyle of Diablo 3, you'll all be picking items up constantly; and everyone's going to want to see what they've got. Pause, pause, pause, pause, pause, it really does ruin the game for everyone.

input:
rating 5.0. I have enjoyed the old games with Final Fantasy 4 Man, what can I say. I have played Final Fantasy since the beginning with Final Fantasy 1. I have enjoyed the old games with Final Fantasy 4,5.6, being my best. Then I play Final Fantasy 7 and the characters actually start coming to life and I can think of them as really characters.

Now I get to see the characters as they were meant to be see, real life. Playing on my PS4Pro has been a great time and this adds to the experience. Do not miss the story and play all the side quests you can. This is one of the best Final Fantasies I have the pleasure of playing. Have fun everyone and don't miss this one.

rating 5.0. Worth the wait I have been waiting for this game for 10 years. You bet I came prepared for this release. Got a PS4 Pro and a 65" 4k tv last week just to play this game in all its glory. We sure have come a long way from the days of 8 bit. I'm not a graphics snob by any stretch of the imagination, but wow is this game beautiful. I already know I will sink a lot of hours into this game based on what I have played so far. If you haven't gotten the game yet, or are still on the fence, stop debating and get it.

Disclaimer for those who know little of the game. It is a very open world game between the story segments so get used to travelling around vast areas. You start off with the car to make travelling much less painful (the car stays on the roads though), and you can rent chocobos too. I don't know about late game but most final fantasy games had flight and other means of travel for mid to late game play. There are 'on map enemies' that you can see and avoid/engage as you see fit, but there are also 'random encounters' where the enemies can appear around you while travelling. The biggest thing for new players to look out for is night time, in the first area you explore, level 30 iron giants will spawn at night and they will WRECK you if you get close, not to mention they have a gravity attack that pulls you to them.

The Voice acting and characters seem fluid and have real chemistry when interacting. It feels like what it would be like when you small talk and interact with your friends almost. rating 4.0. I've liked most Final Fantasy games with no particular favorite (although ... I'm a moderate RPG player and have played the Final Fantasy series ever since I played Final Fantasy on the original Game Boy. I've liked most Final Fantasy games with no particular favorite (although I'm slightly more partial to VI, VII, VIII, IX & Tactics). Final Fantasy X wasn't bad; never played XI; liked XII; didn't care for XIII but I did play it; and XIV was a great MMORPG, I just didn't have enough free time to justify paying the monthly $15. Oh, and Type-0 wasn't too bad either. I liked the darker tones in the plot and cut-scenes. I

So, at long last, Final Fantasy XV. It is better than the whole XIII saga, I'll give it that. Some may argue that I'm comparing apples and oranges due to the changes in combat systems but, if that were the case, then anything past VI would be in its own category. Personally, I like the action-based combat system and Square Enix never disappoints with the graphics. So far, everything feels pretty fluid and the plot feels fresh to me. I'm not far enough along to really analyze the plot but it's captured my interest for now. I do like the modern-medieval mixture Square has going on with this game although one of the reasons I liked Tactics, IX, and XII was the medieval "culture" from the architecture and dialogue.

rating 4.0. I chose amazon over gamestop or other vendors because I liked their offer the best and they did not follow ... The package itself arrived as promised, but as with many I pre-ordered mainly for the exclusive bonuses offered through the Final Fantasy roadtrip. I have done all 3 of the quests and the side quests and received no codes for the in-game items - the only code I received was for the gold chocobo skin for the car. Amazon sent me two codes after I ordered it in October (why 2, I dont know) and I tried them both - only one of them actually worked for the roadtrip page and I proceeded to do a quest a week for the next 3 weeks. Now, my page says I have completed all of the quests but when you click on "redeem" it is still greyed out and will not allow me to do anything or receive any codes. I wrote to amazon and they offered a $5 amazon promotional gift card to be added to my account, but this does virtually nothing to change the situation - I pre-ordered for the bonus and I did not receive that bonus. I chose amazon over gamestop or other vendors because I liked their offer the best and they did not follow through with that offer. I don't think, that being the case, that I will any longer use amazon to pre-order any titles which have any digital bonuses. It's pretty ridiculous to wait 10 years for a game and pre-order for the bonuses - and not have something as simple as delivering 3 codes for 3 in-game bonuses (which legally they are obligated to provide) happen. In the future, I suggest amazon provide the codes up front and simply not allow them to be USED until launch day. That would be far more simple. As it stands, several of us out here were lied to and amazon should be ashamed of that.
