rating 5.0. Arrived safely via AMZL_US A magnificent Final Fantasy experience, and though I will probably never want to wait ten+ years for a game ever again, I'm okay with the fact that it was for FFXV. I almost cried when I finally started it up. I waited too long for the chance at UCE, but the Deluxe edition is beautiful--the Royal Raiment and Masamune had me salivating [Noctis in a suit sold the game for me in all those earlier trailers]. Yoshitaka Amano-sensei's art on the steelbook is stunning as ever~ [my next purchases no doubt are going to be the artbook and soundtrack].

As the headline suggests, this shipped via Amazon Logistics. If you Google around, you will find horror stories for days about damaged and lost packages when shipped by amzl_us, and I will say I was prepared for the very real possibility of needing to stab someone if this didn't arrive as I expected. When I pulled up to my house I just barely saw it by the door, laying down flat, as it looked like an empty flattened box. It actually wasn't a box really, but rather one of those folded cardboard quasi-origami envelope kind of things, with a tear tab. I would've been a little more worried about it being damaged in transit if it were not a steelbook, but even then it still seemed bare-boned. But it arrived safe and sound, so here's one positive review for Amazon Logistics if you're looking for a hope spot.

Get your hands on a copy now. Just go for it. Enjoy the nakama-squad road trip feels train.

current_product_reviews:
rating 3.0. Good but not great. Inaccurate advertising. May contain -Spoilers-
There are a ton of trailers, previews, sneak peaks, and gameplay that did not make it into the game or aren't in the game. With that said this is a decent game depending on what you want. That's the most important part. What type of game do you want? If you want a good Final Fantasy game or are a Final Fantasy fan you will not love this game. If you want a game with a lot of things to do, has good graphics, and a decent fighting system then you'll most likely enjoy this game. I can go on for a while but I'll keep it simple.

Pros:
- A good battle system that works for the most part.
- Good graphics.
- Good music.
- The food looks good and makes you hungry.

Cons:
- Horrible camera system that often times kill the great battle system.
- A lot of bugs and glitches (even with the patch) that will kill some of your progress, waste your time, or forces you to restart or reload.
- Very short. The actual game is less than 20hrs long.
- Little or no story. The game is only about seven chapters. The rest is mainly there for you to explore the world.
- No character development, explanations, lore, backstory, or buildup. Things kind of just 'happen' as you go along.
- The skill and magic usage is very watered down and simplified.
- Pacing is one of the worst in any game. Most chapters or scenes feel either dragged out or cut too short. Not a good balance.
- Not a lot of good replay value. The saying, "Seen one, seen them all" shines very true here.
- Game is too easy.
other_product_reviews:
rating 4.0. Good Yoshi game for first timers and family. Not great for avid fans or gamers. Good game, not great. As a avid Nintendo fan, I've always loved all the Yoshi games. This game is a good example of the 'great idea, but only decent execution' issue. The game is beautiful! Very creative and fun in the design and feel of the game. Yoshi + wool? Come on! That's adorable. However, behind the design and cutesy art style, the core and substance of the game runs a bit shallow compared to other Yoshi games. Keep in mind that I'm reviewing this compared to all the other Yoshi games. If this is your first time playing a Yoshi game then you'll most likely enjoy it without knowing that it could have been so much better.

The levels are pretty average, mostly taking advantage of the art and design instead of cleverness and creativeness. Most of the levels are similar with one or two things added here and there so it isn't exactly the same. A lot of the good things are only in very few levels and should be implemented more. Most of the game's levels are predominantly a left-to-right style compared to the previous games that had more versatile levels that isn't locked to left-to-right. Me and my fiancee played through two worlds and nothing in the stages stood out to us. We both agreed that the best thing about this game is not the gameplay itself but rather the side content and features.

There are five 'yarn' bundles in each level. If you collect them all in the game, after the game you'll be able to stitch them together to form a new Yoshi that you can use. For example, you can unlock a 'cow' Yoshi that will be black and white with a pink nose! You can then slowly collect these 'skins' that you can use whenever you want. Our favorite thing about the game is finding out what the next Yoshi skin will be and that curiosity keeps us churning through the levels and finding all the secrets even though we don't quite enjoy the levels themselves. Overall I still get to have fun and spend time with my fiancee and that's what it's all about.

Pros:
- Art and Design are beautiful and fun to look at.
- Yoshi
- Creative wooly concept.
- Yoshi skins!
- 2-player co-op mode.
- lot's of play-time and levels.

Cons:
- Too easy, even for new players.
- Could be more creative in level layout design.
- Slightly repetitive in some levels.
- relies too much on the art design to carry the game.

rating 5.0. A must have for all Pokemon fans hands down. An amazing leap ahead in gameplay graphics compared to all the previous generation of Pokémon games, even the newer Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire. This game was actually designed from the ground up for the 3DS and takes advantage of the faster system as well as a new battle system and core game play. Pokémon X and Y definitely made a huge improvement that you do not want to miss out.

Note: Although Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire are also amazing games in their own right, they are simply a 'reboot' or 'remaster' of the original versions designed for the Gameboy Advance and a lot it shows through. The game will sometimes feel a bit 'off' and slightly clunky at times. The experience in X and Y is a lot smoother.

rating 5.0. Slight improvement over the older 3DS XL but not by much. Overall I like the system more than the original. I haven't used any of the new buttons yet (only been playing Pokémon). I like the new volume button on the side of the screen to instantly turn it up and down. I only wished they added a brightness button as well. The speakers seem to be a tad louder than the original. The 3D tracking works relatively well. It tracks your face to keep the 3D looking smooth and fluid but you can see it adjust for like half a second every time you move. Not a huge deal but it's there. Also works in the dark. It had some trouble tracking my face when I'm playing 3D laying sideways on my pillow in the dark. With some angling it still works ok. Overall it's a better 3DS and only worth it if you're getting a 3DS anyways or are planning to play a lot of future games on it, as a lot of new games require the NEW 3DS to work. If you're going to play old games or just Pokémon like me it may not be worth it.

NOTE: The CON, the main thing I dislike about the NEW 3DS is purely aesthetic. I love the grey/black color but the inside of the 3DS is matte and the outside is Gloss!!! It's slippery and attracts fingerprints like there's no tomorrow! I personally love the dark matte color when you open up the 3DS and wished they did the whole system that way. Because of the nature of the system a lot of grease, oil, and fingerprints will show on the outside after you play it and for me, a neat freak, it drives me crazy.

The NEW 3DS XL vs the OLD 3DS XL... they can still be confusing for many sellers/buyers.

Major physical differences of the NEW 3DS XL:
1. colored XYBA buttons.
2. small 'stick' right above XYBA buttons.
3. volume bar to the left of screen.
4. home button ONLY below the bottom screen.
5. an extra L and R button on top.
6. Power button moved below and 'start/select' is there instead.

Major internal differences on the NEW 3DS XL;
1. slightly faster processor. (noticeable but not by a huge margin)
2. 3D function has face tracking to stay more fluid and consistent.

Similarities:
1. Still does NOT support Wireless-N.
2. Similar size.
3. Same screen.

input: rating 4.0. Very good mouse The Rival 710 is a very good mouse.  It glides well on my mouse pads and seems very accurate.  It reminds me of my Rival 300 which I like very much. rating 5.0. Very comfortable for long sessions. My previous mouse died after a 2.5 year stint (rather disappointing for a higher end mouse) so I decided to switch companies and try out something else. While I've used SteelSeries mousepads before, I've never purchased any of their peripherals so it was a bit of a leap of faith to put down money on a high end mouse. I don't really do much gaming anymore, but I tend to find that gaming mice have some of the best ergonomics for long sessions and this certainly holds true here. The build quality feels top notch right out of the box and the aesthetics are a bit on the understated side, which I prefer over some of the in-your-face models that look like some sort of stealth fighter with LED's everywhere. The software to control the lighting is simple enough to use and the mouse has plenty of buttons for my own use. There is one eyebrow raising feature, which is the LED panel on the side of the mouse which shows the SteelSeries logo by default, but can be customized with pixel art to show something else. I'm not entirely sure how many people are going to be staring at the inside of my mouse, but I suppose it can be a cool place to customize it with your team or sponsor logo if you're into eSports? Aside from that, it seems like a rather useless feature.

Anyway, the important things are that the mouse feels extremely good in the hand, which makes it comfortable to use even if you're on the computer for hours at a time. It is extremely responsive and has a button that allows you to quickly toggle between two preset sensitivity settings, which is useful both in games and for applications such as Photoshop where you might want to reduce your mouse sensitivity for some tasks. Again, the software to set this up is incredibly intuitive and well-designed.

I haven't owned the mouse for very long so these are initial impressions more than anything else. I cannot speak to the longevity of it, but out of the box, it's a wonderful mouse and one of the best I've ever used. My only real complaint is that it did not come with a SteelSeries sticker in the box which is irrelevant to anything other than my fetish for brand stickers.

rating 5.0. Quality mouse with a ton of options I have used a lot of good mice in my days and i have always noticed that getting the right size for my hand was difficult to narrow down, even during the days that I actually left my home to shop. Some just had the wrong shape; some were just huge. This mouse is about as good as I have seen to fit my hand in the correct shape and size.

Tons of options in the software for gaming profiles and reprogramming buttons. Although I have not a huge fan and certainly don't need the color change icons, they are pretty sweet looking. I have had this mouse for easily 3-4 weeks and I am STILL dialing it in. The acceleration and dpi were actually overwhelming in the beginning. When you are used to a little lag and "inaccuracy", pinpoint precision actually caused me some problems. I would point right at something "perceived" and I would be just off on the top side or something. This was consistent. I took the accel down a bit and the dpi down and I am good.

I haven't noticed any real failures or anything not working and the piece is well made. When I "feel" quality and see it as well, I am good. My last mouse (Logitech G9) lasted me a very long time and I STILL use like 3 Logitech MX518's in other places, I hope this one does the same.

Been quite some time since I paid this much for a mouse and I normally do not think long about buying something. I slept on this one a day because of the price, but I couldn't narrow down another mouse that fit what I wanted. I would buy this again.

rating 2.0. I am a mouse collector These are the SteelSeries models which I am reviewing. First, you can tell I have a vast variety of mice, I have the Rival310, Sensei310, Sensei [RAW] and latest one, the Rival710. I also reviewed the Razer series.

SteelSeries RIVAL710
After a mixture of emotions, disappointments and money wasted, I've come to the conclusion that these mice are not suitable for much "esports". I mainly use a Logitech G403. No SteelSeries "gaming mice" can't hold a candle to the G403. The feel on my hand is so much better with the G403 just for the big "lump" or the hunchback it has which makes it ergonomically comfortable. NONE of the SteelSeries have this. This is important for long periods of gaming. If at all, the Sensei RAW has a similar feeling, but it doesn't have the rubberized/textured sides which the rest of the SteelSeries have. I guess there's no perfect combination; I wanted to update my mouse but I guess I'll stick to my good, old, unbeatable G403.

The latest SteelSeries mouse that I got, the 710, is way too heavy. I tend to move my hand quickly (and lift it a bit) and this mouse does not let me be as fast as I am. Not aberrantly horrible, but it's there. Another letdown is the annoying left-click noise. It sounds horrible to me. Like an absent, 80's beige-colored mouse. Not fancy, not crisp. It lacks finesse. For such an expensive mouse, you'd expect quality, but it's as dry and deep-low click with an after double-clicking noise taste that leaves your ears wishing for more. It's a $74 mouse, I expect more of this.

Lastly, this is a personal opinion, but plastering "SteelSeries" and "Rival" all over each side of the mouse bloats the look of it. I am a simplistic person and I believe the logo is enough. All in all, comparing to what I have

rating 1.0. Sigh...... Received this mouse and connected it to the computer all was fine. Went to the manufacturer website to register the product and found you couldn't. rating 5.0. My favorite mouse now. Don't trust that there will be apps that work as expected but otherwise this has been a great mouse. This mouse is quite a bit smaller and denser than my older mouse and my hand already feels much better using it. I prefer to use the System monitor app but honestly it only works when it wants to.
rating 1.0. DO NOT BUY THIS MOUSE!! The left click stopped working after 4 months and the tech support spent two weeks with useless instructions and after that told us that they cannot do anything!!

rating 5.0. By Far, the Best Mouse I've Used! Like most gamers, I've had no small amount of mice over the years. Some that felt like they could glide on air, and some that had more than enough buttons for my style. However, none of my previous mice really had all of what I needed.

Enter the SteelSeries Rival 710!

This mouse has it all! Hearty weight, all the function buttons I could ever want, smooth and easy gliding, and pinpoint accuracy. However, it has even more than that! With both the laser and cable being modular and replaceable, I'll never have to worry about getting a new mouse when this one can be maintained for $35 every few years (no more acclimating to a new mouse)!

Another feature that I never thought I'd like is the haptic motor (it definitely contributes to the weight, but I like that aspect), which I honestly didn't think I would've needed until I actually set it up. After that, I found it not only useful, but even entertaining (honestly, who doesn't like to laugh when the "Sad Trombone" vibrate pattern buzzes your mouse?).

There are only two (ableit extremely minor) downsides to this mouse. First is that the haptic can't be picked up by games nor provide control to that game unless someone develops an app-based extension for that game (which also makes it seem like the game would have to be modded to gain that feature). In the future, it would be nice if SteelSeries could add that type of support. The only other downside is the same, but for the OLED display.

That said, this is still very much an excellent mouse, and is worth the money!

rating 2.0. Overpriced gimmicks it's just a regular mouse - nothing better than logitech G300 for 1/3 of the price. OLED is practically useless, you can't view any useful info on it while playing anyway. Vibration interactions are underdeveloped - timers? really?
Vibration mechanism is too weak to be felt significantly during the heat of the battle.

Bottom line: OLED + Vibrations = Gimmicks, mouse sensor is nothing special and practically the same as found in much cheaper mice. If you want RGB mouse with an OLED screen then this one works fine, but if you do not like to pay premium for useless features - look elsewhere.

rating 2.0. Poor build quality, warranty only if bought directly from SteelSeries I have had this mouse for just under a year now, but the problems started about 6-months after purchase. The right click fails to actuate constantly and consistently happens during use. I assumed something got caught in the mechanism and cleared everything out with air, but the issue was persistent. At the time I looked into an RMA with SteelSeries only to find out they only warranty products purchased directly from their site.

I was able to get the right click to operate a little more consistently, at least enough to play a few rounds in Overwatch, by breaking a tab that held the right click in place. While the issue still comes up, it was at least somewhat bearable. As I now approach the 1 year mark of having this mouse, I have decided to just junk it in favor of my 6-year-old Corsair gaming mouse that I replaced with the SteelSeries.

I wanted to like this mouse, but it's an expensive mouse to throw away after a year and I will be looking at other vendors moving forward.

current_product_reviews: rating 2.0. Inconsistent The mouse has a good feeling in the hand with texture and side grips. Software is not bloated. Software is intuitive for the most part and easy to setup. Software can be buggy sometimes when trying to launch the application but a simple fix for me was to end the services related to the app and re launch. Discord integration is a nice feature but could be improved upon with more option for specific notifications and a setting for longer display of said notifications. Also display is to narrow and at an angle for you to read. Notifications don’t seem to fully display only the beginning part of a message. The weight of the mouse is a turn off. I purchased this with the intent of capitalizing on the features but I was hugely disappointed. Tactical feedback is weak on highest setting making the build feel cheap especially considering the weight of this mouse. The on board memory is somewhat bugged. For example if I set button 4 to mouse button 1 and toggle repeat my clicks won’t register on button 1 or button 4 so I have to open the software reconfigure the settings. It might work for sometime but then the issue happens again and when I start the software it shows some my bindings have changed even though I have all of my profiles duplicated on board. So on board memory and software integration is a inconsistent at best. For example toggle hold works fine for me but repeat on hold does nothing. At this price point I was willing to compromise for a heavy mouse if the feature were worth the price but unfortunately they are not.
other_product_reviews: rating 2.0. Don’t buy for the macros This keyboard would be acceptable at this price point if it had better macro functionality. There is no way to program more complex macros through iCue software. Which is unfortunate because for membrane keyboard it feels decent. They arm rest is acceptable although I don’t suspect it would last very long it’s plastic hinges seem very weak. The thing that bothers me most about macro keyboard is that macro keys need to be a different type or height to prevent accidentally pressing them which will happen if your new to macro button keyboards. AOC make a keyboard that is a great example of that.

rating 2.0. You don’t get what you pay for I really wanted to like this keyboard but unfortunately it does not preform well. I own asus tuf gaming monitor that exceeded my expectations. This is the first and last asus peripheral I intend to purchase. The reason being the software required is bloated out. Unless you already own a asus laptop or desktop I would not recommend their peripherals because the software is bundled into other services you don’t need. For example Logitech ghub is a software that is solely focused on the peripherals and nothing more. Asus armory crate software is designed for asus laptops/desktops because it allows you to control various aspects of your asus machine. If a standalone version of their software was offered that focused on the peripherals alone they might be able to offer better support by debugging it. Another major issue with the tk3 is that it is advertised as “mechanical” when in fact it more of a hybrid membrane. The keys are mushy like other have stated. I purchased this keyboard with the intent of upgrading from a Corsair membrane keyboard. Unfortunately it felt like a downgrade because the mushy keys also have a sticky feeling to them. The magnetic arm rest is a joke it’s plastic and offer no support just like the keyboard. It offers only one usb pass through but in order for it to work you have to plug in two usb cables one for the keyboard and another to power any device connected to the keyboard. I mean what’s the point of that? My Mac keyboard from 2010 has two usb connections on it that allow me to connect ssd hard drives to it with no issue and it only requires a single cable keyboard usb connection. Don’t fall for branding like I did if you already own an asus machine the experience might be better for you but if you don’t look else ware. They are much cheaper no name brands that probably function better than this name brand junk. Even though it might look cool you don’t get what you pay for.

input:
rating 5.0. Trying not to compare too much to 3 First off, this game is absolutely awesome and I just got it yesterday and have only played about 2-3 hours worth.

The first I've played in the series was Etrian 3: The Drowned City. I think that this 4th one is a tad easier on Normal than the others were at default. The 3D effects in the game are GREAT. The menu windows overlap each other like plates and stack outward in terms of the 3D and it looks great.

If you liked 3, you'll like 4; it's very similar in terms of the flow of the game. There are some new things too as the other reviewer noted. For some reason, these games are extremely hard to put down. If you have even the slightest bit of OCD like I do, you will be in all of your glory drawing your maps and annotating things -- it is absolutely satisfying.

I'm level 3 as of today after about 3 hours if that can gauge how quick the leveling is for anyone. (I invested some time [about 20-30 min] into my guild and their names and leveling)

One other note: In the direction things are going in the digital movement, don't expect an instruction manual to come with the game. I thought it might have been a mistake (and maybe it was...?) HOWEVER, when you press the "HOME" button on the 3ds, you have one there in digital form to view so no big deal. It's useful for the new features like Burst etc.

The extras (CD + Artbook) that come with the first issuing are pretty cool. If you collect Atlus games, you'll definitely be happy with them. Frankly, if you're casual or a huge fan, you're probably only going to look at it max 2 times anyway. Thank you Atlus for the love! Keep these extras coming!

rating 5.0. Great Game, Horrible Amazon Packaging Well...the game is amazing. Been a fan since Etrian Odyssey 1, and EO4 was the only reason I bought a 3DS. I preordered it from this site because I also wanted the artbook and music CD that comes with preorders.

Be warned! Amazon's packaging was horrible. The game box came in a soft envelope and was mailed via USPS. It arrived with the box crushed and the art book inside bent. If it had been pressed on any further, the music CD would've cracked too. Very disappointed with how Amazon sent it. Couldn't they at least ship it inside a cardboard box?

rating 5.0. Difficult, engaging, and beautiful I have never played a previous Etrian Odyssey title, and my husband warned me that they can be difficult dungeon crawlers.

My playthrough has been on normal so far, and I've probably clocked about 10-12 hours, my main party is level 15. This is a turn-based rpg with wondeful little animated monsters, whose animations change when they get close to death. The game will punish you on normal mode for not grinding and levelling up. Big bosses that roam the screen, unlike random encounters(although there is a meter in the bottom right to signal when an attack is impending), can destroy your party in a single hit if not properly levelled up. I'm still on the first map, still going through the BF3, and have to return to town to heal and restock after fighting one large boss. Be warned, expect to grind normal enemies and harvest lots of food to pay for your new gear and items.

The QR codes have been useful, which are easy to find online. You can get free items, weapons, and dungeons. The game caters to creating different parties to switch between, but I've decided on five classes and only one core party as I don't think I have the patience nor time to grind three different guild parties. If streetpass is enabled, you can share and receive other people's playable parties, which may be great in a pinch if your lucky to get a high level guild ticket.

Finally, I love skill trees, and the options don't disappoint. It's tough to choose, although I tend to stick with how I play. Defense and healing must not be ignored or your party will die (unless maybe you're playing on easy.) Some skills are also dependent upon certain types of weapons or shields being equipped. I've enjoyed the characters and minimal dialogue so far, and we'll see where this tale takes me!

rating 5.0. I love this game!!! I would only recommend this to hardcore rpg dungeon fans. It's a great game if you try rp'ing it, seriously though that may take some time. It can be hard at times and can be a grind fest, but the music is good, the classes are straight forward. if you're more casual play it on casual mode, the story is simple enough but I still enjoy it.

rating 5.0. Best Etrian yet. From the sweet art style to the minimally hand holding dungeon exploration and monster fighting this game checks all my boxes. I've had a lot of fun with this, and I'd definitely recommend it to anyone who enjoys Etrian games, although if anime art style turns you off or you hate grinding monsters to become more powerful / better equipped you should steer clear.
rating 5.0. Great Game Awesome game.  The flow is superb without too much over and over again play.  I love needing to draw out my own maps.

rating 5.0. A great place to jump into the Odyssey! First let me ask you to try out the demo, it will be better able to tell you if you will like this game or not. And the bonus of being able to continue the full game from what ever point you stop the demo, whether you buy the retail or the digital e-shop, is a massive plus.

Gameplay:
This game is a maze RPG, where you have to draw a map (of the detail level you want) while exploring the various locations. The main story has goals to follow, and there are side quests to undertake if you so chose. The major part of the game is a first person, turn based exploration of the world and dungeons around the town. The City is accessible through a menu to visit every important area, like the Inn, and the Shop ect. and to the optional Tavern to take the side quests.

Besides the City, there are three major types of areas.
The World Map
This is where you fly your airship and explore the world. In the world map you have no risk of random battles, all dangerous monsters (Called FOE's) are clearly visible on the map. Various activities become unlocked as you gain different tools to equip to the airship. Some of the early activities include gathering food, dowsing for treasure, and launching food to trick the Monsters from harming you. Your primary focus is exploration and documentation of the world and the various "Caves" and the "Dungeon".

Caves:
The caves are the general name for single level, and small map sections to explore and to "loot" various items from the resource points. (Take, Chop, or Mine) Sometimes an event can effect the difficulty of such areas, so always be prepared. The game seems to encourage the exploration of the various caves to help lessen the grind for exp, or at least give you more than one place to grind for exp.

Dungeons:
The main focus of the game is to explore and conquer the large and multilevel Dungeons. You will probably have to go back to town a number of times before you have a complete map of any single floor of the Dungeon. Random battles are likely in the Caves and Dungeons, so there will plenty to battle and to collect from the various monsters.

General Tips:
*When mapping an area, for the first time, make sure to look in all four directions, sometimes there will be no visible hint to clue you in to a secret.
*Re FOE's, The color scale on the FOE's are usually correct. If you enter combat with a red FOE, make all effort to run from it.
*Resources from drop points (Take, Chop, Mine) always reset at Midnight, and unlike the previous games, each resource point has it's own limit to how much can be gathered from it.
*Seek out the QR codes that are out there, while still in the first overworld area there is some very useful equipment to "Download".

Final Word
This game is a massive improvement over the DS EO games. If you can handle Final Fantasy (1-6) then you'll not want to waist time with the Easy difficulty.

*****/5 Massively Epic

rating 4.0. No Instruction Manual in the game case intended?[EDIT: Yes it was. Sorry collectors.] The game is lovely so far, and being that I've played the last 3 this shouldn't be too bad. But, seeing how each new game usually introduces something new... character classes, etc... I wonder why we couldn't get an actual booklet to go with the game.

The 'art' book was ok. It only really had what a normal EO game would have in the instruction manual, save for actual full blown town illustrations like this one had, still there seems to be a major oversight in not including a game manual IN THE CASE. All I got was the game itself with the usual NDS Health and Safety Precautions Booklet. That is a real let down. If you wanted us to put the 'art' book with the CD in out game case, why didn't you pack it in there in the first place???

All in all the presentation is really weak, feels like the budget for the game was used up on the CD (which is lovely) and the very sparse 3 illustrations included with a short promo comic at the end page.

Thankfully the game itself is fine. But I won't lie and say that I feel I like I bought a used game that came with no game manual... I happen to enjoy the art and see the tidbits of what new mechanics I'm going to be thrown at before I start a game.

Also, being 3DS it would of been nice if we could of gotten an option to enter codes from our last games to unlock past character variants for each class. Two character pallets is really tame for a 3DS outing.

If this game is indeed supposed to have a game manual aside from the art manual, please let me know so as I can return mines and get one with said manual.[EDIT: Thanks to those who answered this question. Rating has been updated to 4 stars as intended. Still, not a fan of the watered down system. Casual mode should of been exclusive and they should each have been allowed their own save slots. I happen to love with other gamers, 3DS gets used I wouldn't want my file accidentally overwritten again.]

rating 5.0. Wonderful game I've played all of the previous Etrian Odyssey titles (if briefly in the case of EOII and III), and I have to say I am very pleased with this one. For a while I hadn't even been paying attention to my 3DS but since I received EO IV I find that I'm playing a lot more, even during my breaks at work. A lot of 3DS titles forget that they are on a handheld platform, but with Suspend saves and auto-battle I find that it's easy to play for just a few minutes at a time. Mapping  out dungeon tiles is wonderfully tactile, and I enjoy it to the point of stopping nearly every step just to draw in another wall. The way that the skills are visually linked in the tree is fantastic. It makes it easier to decide which skills I want to focus on, based on what comes later rather than what I think looks good at the moment.

rating 5.0. let get lost together what can i say that every single gaming website havent already say? nothing lol. but let repeat it anyway for the heck of it.  this is a dungeon crawler in it purest form with newer technology, and that my friends, make for a damn good game. drawn map, strategic battle, loot, equipment, quest. this game have everything that could make a really long boring week into a fun time.  some flaw here and there like the invincible character in the dungeon u have to find, but hey aint nothing in the world perfect right

rating 5.0. Old school styled rpg done right. I never really liked old school rpgs(old school as in nes era or styled after it) because, they always felt like they were meant purely for masochists.
But this game's new casual mode helped me not feel like i had to grind 5 hours to fight a boss.
You'll still need to grind but the amount of time you NEED to grind in casual mode is maybe 5-10 battles if you don't constantly run.

rating 5.0. My skepticism was unwarranted I'd played and beaten the first 3 games in the series, and after the third one (which was my favorite) overhauled so many things for the better, I was sure the 4th couldn't top it. Especially since they went back to what looked more like the original class structure. I also wasn't sure how I'd feel about there being several dungeons as opposed to one, or the integration of balloon exploration into the main quest rather than being a side diversion like the ship in 3.

Well, it turns out I had nothing to worry about. There's a lot that's different in 4, but a lot that remains the same in a good way. The interface continues to improve in so many little excellent ways that it would be tedious to make a complete list. The transition to animated models works better than I expected, and seeing FOEs stomp around on the field (instead of being balls of light) is pretty scary. I can't overemphasize how GOOD this game looks and sounds. They kept forging, subclassing and overworld exploration from 3, but the way it's all done is tweaked to be less grindy (no more paying to go out exploring, or limited turns, for example, and forging only costs materials, not money). There's a LOT more variety available in your map icons now, and more floor colors and stuff. The way class unlocking is handled is with sidequests, with one exception, and though you start with only 7 classes available there's quite a few more to find. I could go on and on.

Basically, if you liked 3 so much that you thought 4 couldn't possibly top it, buy this immediately. Overall, this seems like a really solid attempt to make EO a more accessible series for newcomers, but it also happens to be the best entry in the series yet.

One caveat re: the look of the game. I'm one of those people for whom 3D doesn't work, so I always play with it off. Can't comment on the 3D in this game.

rating 5.0. Old school dungeon crawler for the modern day gamer I'm a ways into the 4th land and I can honestly say this is one of my favorite games ever, up there with Dragon Quest 8 for the Playstation 2. It is a first person dungeon crawler with turn based combat. If you aren't into turn based combat avoid this game, but if you like turn based combat or are on the fence I highly recommend this game. You will encounter monsters in the various caves/dungeons/labyrinths and there is a color coded indicator of when you will encounter an enemy, so it is not entirely "random encounters", like dragon quest/final fantasy games. Also the graphics are great and the skill system and subclassing are extremely fun and pretty simple and intuitive. I extremely enjoyed drawing my map as I explored on the bottom screen. Everything about this game is so much fun, but bear in mind it can be very difficult if you don't take your time and level up and/or have a solid strategy for each fight.

Game play - 10/10 turn based combat and exploring at their FINEST

Graphics - 8/10 I would have liked to see your characters on the battlefield but the scenery and monster models are fantastic, but the 3D is kind of lacking

Music - 10/10 I didn't care much for the town music but once you leave the town the music in this game is some of the best I've ever heard and I've played games for 15+ years.

Difficulty - 8/10 I haven't had too many problems, but sometimes you kind of have to trial/error some battles but make sure you SAVE OFTEN.

Wrap up - If you like dungeon crawling and don't mind turn based combat this is an Odyssey you don't want to miss, they really nailed every RPG aspect in this game.
rating 5.0. loved it a very good old school dungeon crawler,with a lot of content and things to do,somr very hard librincts though,over all very good

rating 4.0. A Good Entry Point To The Series I have never played any of the previous three entries to the Etrian Odyssey series, but my friends suggested I try the demo. I found a great experience. The mapping system really draws you into the dungeons, as you try to plan out how you can manuever when you get long sight ranges. The game includes powerful enemies that appear on the map, called FOEs, that serve as a challenging miniboss, or as a danger waiting to attack you when you come in sight.

The main downside is that the class system is not set up in a way that explains exactly what you're doing. You're basically left to experiment on your own. Luckily, you are punished too much if you deside to redistribute your skill points (you lose two levels, while previous entries apparently penalized 5+ levels). However, it still stings a bit, especially as this would sometimes leave you underleveled for the fight you tried to buff yourself up for. That said, this rarely comes up. All around, this is a great game.

rating 4.0. $40 and no manual? Really? Look, I really love these games. I do. But when I pay $40 dollars I expect a nice full color manual. Yes a lot of companies are forgoing the manual these, but there is no excuse for this. It doesn't save the environment and we all know it. It saves these companies money and that is the only reason they do it. Atlus should know better. They pride themselves on niche games with good packaging and art. Come on man, don't cheat the people who support you. Leave the profiteering to EA and Capcom and the like. Ugh.

rating 5.0. Amazing. A great return to retro RPG form, extremely content heavy with a very refreshing art-style and gameplay mechanics. Only thing to note is the game's difficulty is extremely high and it will tear you apart if you are not prepared.

rating 3.0. Fans of the series should like it. It's more of the same just spread out over larger areas. Hardcore dungeon crawling with fun draw your map mechanic. But, even on easy mode this game will be a grind. I found the 3D lackluster and fuzy at times So, I recommend playing with 3D turned down.
rating 5.0. Amazingly Good Dungeon Crawler! Wow!  I can't recommend this game highly enough, at least to those who enjoy epic dungeon crawlers with lots of customization and exploration.

rating 5.0. A bit easier than its predecessors in some ways, better in every other way I was a little surprised that the game at least starts out a lot easier compared to 1-3 where your party can get wiped out almost on a whim. Still, FOEs are far more numerous and even more dangerous and tricky than before. Sometimes they can be avoided, but sometimes you just have no choice but to deal with them. The difficulty does go up a bit as you proceed however, so there's still some challenge to be had, it just eases you in more. Overall the RPG system is very well done and while it's easier than the previous games, it still would be a bit punishing for someone new to more serious RPGs like these (in particular, the Final Fantasy people might want to be prepared for a few deaths...) The classes have been changed around and aren't really the same as before, so there's a bit to get used to even for those familiar with the previous games (there's a bit more of a balancing between them with each having strengths and weaknesses, so you'll be a bit more inclined to have a bit of a mix to balance each other out and deal with a larger variety of enemies.) However, they do let you create a lot of characters and form different parties (which you can store as "squads" and then muster them quickly rather than having to manually rebuild the party over and over) so this does give one options there as well (though my personal preference is one versatile group rather than specialist groups and the game definitely allows for this.)

As expected, the graphics are nice for a 3DS game. You still get the limitations of the 3DS versus, say, the Vita or something, but overall the labyrinths and caves actually look pretty nice. It's worth noting that instead of one giant labyrinth that changes in theme after a certain number of levels, you actually get a bunch of labyrinths and caves. Each can be small or large with possibly only one level or more, and there are a lot of different themes to be found for a bit of nice variety that the previous games couldn't match. Also, character art and 2D backgrounds are all drawn very well, further contributing towards the overall atmosphere of the game, and that's one thing you can definitely say it has: atmosphere. Lots of it.

Story-wise, you wouldn't expect much, but actually I'm seeing a bit of character development from people I've met and there's a lot more back story so far compared to the previous games (or at least what I had played of them. I'll admit I didn't really complete them for various reasons.) Overall I'm actually getting into it more than expected. I believe I also see some twists around the corner (I don't want to spoil of course!)

Finally, and for me a big one, this game sounds wonderful. The music is just the best of the series by far and actually better than the grand majority of 3DS games to be frank. In fact, I'd go so far as to say that it's better than most mobile games of any kind (though there are a few good games music-wise on the PSP I'll admit, at least two of which I do like better in that respect, but they are very different sorts of games.) Overall it's not just nice, but high quality too. I listen with my headphones and the instruments are extremely well done. I thought the 3DS had a terrible output and couldn't handle even lower ranged audiophile headphones, but I guess it was just the games and the built in music player because this game sounds absolutely great even without an amp. But the music isn't just nice and catchy, it FITS. Each tune is used well, fitting the area it is used in extremely well to further improve upon the game's atmosphere. For me one of the most important aspects of a game is its music, but I did not expect much and frankly this game positively blew me away...

I just can't think of anything this game hasn't not only done well, but, frankly excelled over most like it and pretty much anything you can get on the 3DS right now. Well, it would be nice if there were some way possible to get it digitally without re-buying if you bought the cartridge, but that's less a complaint at the game and more Nintendo's distribution system.

rating 5.0. An improved version of the Wii U pro controller. This controller is incredibly comfortable. I was a huge fan of the Wii U pro controller and used that controller constantly. rating 2.0. A game with many glaring flaws and missed opportunities. This game is what many would consider a masterpiece. It certainly has alot of content to play with and is the ultimate party game. It is one of those games that feel fresh and not aged at all. As fun as this game is, there are allot of glaring flaws that are often overlooked. My issues with this game started well before it's release date. The creator of Smash Bros had announced that they were taking away the story mode and cutscenes just because they were leaked online. It is clear to me that Masahiro Sakurai does not really care about the fans and are willing to make everyone suffer because of a couple of videos leaked. Another issue I have with this game is it's punishing difficulty, certain characters they chose to have in this game, and DLC. This game is a challenge, it goes without saying. But this game comes off as being cheap and unfair. The "master fortress" is one of the worst ideas they have ever come up with in Smash Bros. At the very end of classic mode, you need to fight master core and its last form is him turning into a maze. It is a very narrow maze with hazards coming from every direction.It made clearing classic mode past 8.0 difficulty pretty much impossible. Now the challenge panel was done very poorly. The biggest issue I have with the challenge panel is that there are certain panels you cannot break with a hammer and many of these challenges are pretty much near impossible to beat or require you to accomplish difficult tasks with undesirable characters like Duck Hunt which leads me to my next issue, the character lineup. This game gives you a good number of players to play from, but has a large number of characters that do not belong in the game such as Duck Hunt, Villager, Lucina, Shulk and Robin. My last issue ties in with the character lineup and that is the DLC selection. Why on earth are we getting characters like Ryu, Cloud and Bayonetta? The DLC we have been getting are just overhyped and really do nothing to make the game more fun, although I gotta admit, they have some really good DLC stages. The kind of DLC we should be getting is DLC that enhances the single player experience such as a new game mode, additional event match stages, a second challenge panel. But, with the final DLC announcement closes the chapter on what could have been a game that had alot of potential, but now has been killed off. Way to go Masahiro Sakurai, you ruined Super Smash Bros.

Also in super mario world, the first new super mario bros and super masrio 3D land, you get to hold a secondary item that automatically drops down when you become small mario. There are star coins in the game you can collect, but there is almost no incentive for collecting them. In the previous mario games, collecting star coins in the previous mario game allows you to unlock certain gates or play bonus levels in the overworld.
Another problem I have with this game is the challenge mode. The pacing of the difficulty in this challenge mode is just terrible. There is a difference from challenging and just being cheap and unfair. Many of them need to be done perfectly and in a specific way to be completed. The challenge mode is just hours of frustration, But one major complain i have about this game is that it is not in 3d. It is basically the same game from the DS from 2006. There has not been enough 3D mario games that are inspired by super mario sunshine or super mario 64. Nintendo does not care about its true fans, they are only releasing gimmicky games that look nice but take little to no effort to create.

rating 4.0. Great alternative to the AC adapter, but not perfect This cable is a great alternative to the AC adapter, it much less bulky. You can plug it into the Wii U's usb port and charge it while you are playing. There are a few issues though. The connection may cut in and out if the Cable is not drawing sufficient power to the gamepad. You will notice the orange charge light rapidly flickering. Examples are using a dual USB adapter and trying to charge 2 things at once. This does not happen if you charge it by itself or plug it into a computer. It did flicker for a short time while plugged into the wii U's USB port, but for 95% of the time, the wii U's USB ports will work fine although I have not tested out how it would function if a USB flash drive was plugged into the wii U and a game was running off the drive.

This is my review for now, it may change down the line if I experience any additional problems.

rating 3.0. Dust particles easily get trapped underneath it, but not too obvious with the screen turned on This is my first screen protector that i bought. I have always been reluctant to purchase screen protectors for this reason. The initial application is pretty easy to do and leaves almost no air bubbles, but it leaves behind debris and dust particles that get trapped under the screen protector. Trying to remove the screen and getting rid of it is a nightmare. I tried to remove one spec of debris under the screen protector and got a ton of dust particles. When i tried to put the screen protector back on, i got a ton of air bubbles. So unless you are in a dust-free controlled environment, you will get alot of dust. The bottom of the screen protector that attaches to the screen is a dust magnet because of it's static cling properties. It took me 45 minutes to get the screen with minimal particles.

Edit: I have had the wii U for a while and got the screen protector that day i purchased it. When the screen is turned on, the dust is not too obvious and does not affect the gameplay that much. I originally gave it a one star, but i now gave it 3 stars. I may change it down the line depending on how scratch resistant it is. The touch sensitivity and smoothness of the screen are juast fine. The screen is practically invisible with the exception of debris that gets trapped underneath.
rating 1.0. very boring game This is a very boring game that gives me no motivation to get in shape. All they have is boxing, thats it. The controls are bad too.

rating 5.0. Big improvements over mario kart wii. This game is very fun just like mario kart ds was. It has the great graphics of mario kart wii with the fun you can expect from mario kart ds.

The major problem with mario kart wii was that it was very luck based because of its imbalance of items and lack of any real strategy to recover from getting hit with 5 blue shells every race. I also like how there are 8 players instead of 12. The new items like the fire flower and tanooki leaf make the game even more interesting. As tradition, mario kart has 16 new stages and 16 old stages. I am pleased with the selection of the retro courses and how new features such as paragliding are in the old stages.

Online play is really fun and improved over the previous games. You can play battles online and are not forced to play in teams. But as great as the online is, it does have issues. Sometimes when there is a communication error, you will get this ''please wait'' message for 3-5 minutes. You may be able to speed up the process by turning off the wireless switch, but for the most part it is a 5 minute wait. So despite the occasional hangups this game has online, it is still worth it for the mario kart fans.

rating 1.0. Way too difficult in the beginning of the game, bad controls. This game seemed interesting to me at first. I have never been a big donkey kong fan, but i do like adventure games. The overhead world looks nice, and the graphics are pretty decent, But what good is a game that starts off way too difficult on the first level? The time trial is way too difficult, but like most games the requirement for getting the gold medal for a time trial or other challenge is not too demanding. Another complaint i have is the controls. The wii remote is good for alot of things, but not for this game. To execute this DK rolling move, you shake the remote. Now why do we not have the option to use a gamecube controller like we did in mario kart wii and SSBB, it is not like the wii was incapable of using the gamecube controller port for wii games. That DK roll comes in handy with the time trials, but it is almost impossible to get the gold medal in the 1st level because it is a distraction to have to shake the remote while using the D-pad to move and a button to jump. An ideal way to play was to have the option to use a gamecube controller and have the DK roll assigned to the X button, but for some reason you cant use a gamecube controller. I know this is the wii and people want to utilize the wii's motion controls, but in this situation, it would have been best to use a gamecube controller. Games like mario party 8 and zelda twilight princess actually fully utilize the wiimote's motion controls.

In conclusion, this game does have nice graphics and presentation, but what kills it is the punishing difficulty and bad controls.

rating 1.0. Frequent framerate drops and slowdowns make it unplayable on xbox 360. I was looking forward to playing this xbox game on my xbox 360. Original xbox games are known for having glitches when being played on the xbox 360, some are negligible, and some make the game unenjoyable and unplayable. This glitch is a big problem for the more skilled players, because it is a distraction when the game randomly goes into slow motion for a second.

A very fun game with decent graphics compared to other systems, ruined because of this frame rate issue. I was really looking forward to playing this, but i cant. The disc is clean and almost no scratches and setting the system to 480p, 720p or 1080i, does not fix the problem. The game runs in 720p, and even if you choose to run it in 480p, which makes the game a pixelated mess, the game STILL slows down.

rating 5.0. Great product for those who want that authentic n64 experience This product is great for using on the project 64 emulator, it is not perfect, but there are only a few minor flaws that can be easily fixed. when you first plug in the controller, the analog stick will need to be calibrated, that is because when testing the controller, you do not have the full range. Some C buttons did not work for me when using jabos controller plugin in project 64, just switch to N-rage's conbtroller plugin in the project 64 settings so all of the buttons work. When using this crontrollers for other emulators, there may be some minor complicatyions when configuring the D-pad like in visualboyadvance, like if you wanna configure the up button, it will show "joy1 button 12" AND joy1 hat switch up, but it can be easily fixed, i just configure the D_pad first. Also the player 1 and player 2 ports seemed to be switched, it is not a big deal and you can just label each port as player 1 and 2.

Not trying to bring alot of negativity to this product, just giving useful hints to some minor flaws. I like the fact that this has 2 player ports. Since i use a laptop, i do not get alot of USB ports to use, so it is nice to have 2 controller ports take up one USB port. I may consider buying a second adapter, depending on if i can get 3 or 4 players to play some n64 games. It is worth mentioning that in my experience, games like pokemon snap and paper mario are glitchy to the point of making it unplayable, but they are available on virtual console. but most of the best 4 player games work great on project 64.

As for other emulators, i tried this on the snes emulator and the d-pad works really well, this controller works well on other emulators.

rating 4.0. Great game and guitar, Iffy setlist. This game is really fun and underrated. You may have seen official reviews from certain video game review websites that did not review it properly. I will fist start off with the mistakes this official reviewer from that well known video game website. He says that party play is "buried under a sea of menus" when all you need to do to access party play is to press the yellow button on the guitar at the main menu. He also goes to say that the 5 star paradigm you know and love is gone" which is also untrue. Quest mode will have you shooting for about 7 stars because of the new powers, but in quickplay+ you have the option of playing the traditional way with just earning 5 stars and not using powerups.

Lets start with the gameplay, like i said the game offers a new way of playing with the new powerups, which i find fun and i will list all of them for you
speadfreak: keeps multiplier at 2x and you earn extra stars for keeping it over 3x
crowd hyper: earn extra stars for hitting notes while the rock meter is full
star power generator: earn 5% star power for every 10 notes hit
star power amplifier: earn 2x the normal amount for hitting star power phrases
mulitplier extender: earn up to 5x multiplier instead of 4
steak guardian: protects you from breaking your streak for 1 note and earn extra stars for long streaks
Resurrection: earn up to 2 ankhs for playing well and each ankh you earn will protect you from failing
star power nova: earn 3x for star power instead of 2x.

People may not want to play this way, so in quickplay+ you can play the traditional way where you can earn up to 5 stars.

The guitar has a nice design and an improvement over the previous guitar hero guitars. But it is not perfect. The strummer squeaks which can get annoying, plus sometimes the guitar will have a period of time where it will drop notes, but not as bad as the les paul guitar. But all other previous problems have gone away, the whammy bar stays in place and feels very smooth, the star power tilt detection is fixed, previous guitar hero guitars had a problem with star power going off on its own.

Now the problem with the setlist i have is that it has some good artists, but they pick mediocre songs by them. I feel like instead of having bleed it out, they should have had "in the end" by linkin park. instead of burnin for you, they should have had dont fear the reaper by the blue oyster cult.

rating 1.0. Registers double hits. completely ruins the pro drumming experince These cymbals are no good. i originally got these from amazon november 6th, they were slightly defective and two weeks go by and they start to get worse and interfere with gameplay. i called for a replacement november 20th, got them november 22nd and the exact same thing is happening except these replacements are much worse. i am very disappointed with madcatz, they should test their products before selling them, the only madcatz product i have that has not given me problems are the rock band 3 keys.

these cymbals double hit on me, this product should be pulled from the amazon.

input:
rating 4.0. Nintendo GameCube (Color - Black) I bought the Nintendo GameCube console because of the previous console Nintendo 64.The PlayStation and the Xbox are good consoles but I had to go with the GameCube. It would be pretty difficult to live up to the Nintendo 64 but the GameCube did a good job.

Well right off the bat, the console is smaller and takes up less room, and instead of a Cartridge the GameCube uses a disc. (There smaller than DVD's) I guess I am a gamer of the past but I like using Cartridge's better, I feel like they are more durable than discs. Also, there are two memory card slots and four controller slots all built in to the console.

Moving on to controllers, they mastered two things, the joystick and the rumble pack. The joystick for the Nintendo 64 wear out easily and they're rough on the hands, (they gave me blisters) now they're much easier to use and they don't wear out as easily. Also, instead of a bulky rumble pack to go on to the controller, now it's built into it making it lighter. I loved the Nintendo 64 controller for its unique design and I was a little disappointed that the GameCube's design is more like a regular controller but over all they did a good job.

Nintendo 64's memory could be great than terrible. Some games didn't need memory cards because they had built in memory, and then sometimes one game could take up a whole memory card. The GameCube made this simple. There are three memory cards sizes, 59 blocks, 251 blocks and 1019 blocks. Each game takes up a certain amount of blocks, for example, Super Mario Sunshine only takes up 9 blocks and that's a pretty big game. So you get a lot of room on one memory card which means you will be spending a lot less money on memory cards.

Most importantly the games, there are 640 games on the GameCube so there are plenty to choose from and most of them are family friendly games. The PlayStation 2 and Xbox may have more games but most of them are not all that great. GameCube has a lot of great franchises and tons of the same games the PlayStation 2 and Xbox have anyways.

Here's my take on GameCube's graphics. The bars have been raised and the GameCube's graphics have definitely improved. Improved 3D backgrounds and smoother characters make it a good console to get.

One more thing this is a pretty cheap console and most family's can afford it and you get so many fun games so it's worth the price. I love Nintendo and the Nintendo GameCube does not disappoint so why not pick one up.
rating 4.0. Decent product Had taken knocks but came as i expected it, is still running and is a pretty good one. Gamecube is a great console, although Wii totally overwrites it.

rating 5.0. Not the most comfortable case for extended handheld sessions Not the most comfortable case for extended handheld sessions, but. rating 4.0. Durable cover with a few faults I bought this case for my 8 year old son who tends to drop electronics. He likes it because of the hand grip it has and that it even though it protects it's not a clunky cover. The only problem with this case is that you do have to take it off in order to place the Switch in the docking station or want to remove the remote controllers to play as well.

rating 4.0. This is a great product if a slim low profile case is what you need. My major gripes with this case include 2 things, choice of material and a quality control issues.

I ended up returning what would have been the perfect case due to the choice of a glossy TPU material that borders around the case. It's overly shiny so it is very hard to keep clean but it's just a very unsatisfying and cheap feeling material. My second gripe would be that on the top portion of the case that snaps onto the Switch console, mine was cut very unevenly, in terms of protection it made no difference, but cosmetically it was horrendous to look at.

I do think this is a great case, especially if the glossy TPU is not a setback for you. I ended up returning this and going with the larger case that Mumba provides which uses a matte textures plastic that is not only extremely comfortable to use but just looks way better and is easier to keep clean. Mumba seems to be doing great for now, so I hope maybe in the future they release a re-iteration of the slimfit series case with a better choice of TPU material similar to that of the rugged older brother case.

rating 5.0. Awesome Switch case! This is a great case for playing the Switch in handheld. Gets rid of the joycon wobble and makes the Switch feel that much more solid. Feels good in the hand. Adds a little bit of weight but not too much. I will buy another for my wife. Great product at a great price. I highly recommend.

rating 3.0. Mixed feelings about this cover... I'm on the fence about this cover.
While it looks awesome on my Switch & feels amazing, at the end of the day it's probably going to be a non-starter for those of you, like myself, who don't just play in handheld mode only, but prefer to play docked as well.

This cover is very difficult to remove from the device. Several times, I thought I was going to damage my Switch. It's almost too rigid, to the point that if you're not careful you will break or bend the rails that the joy-cons attach to.

Also, there's no opening for the kickstand.

Again, it depends on how you prefer to play your Switch; handheld-only or table top, docked, & handheld. This is definitely going to limit you to handheld-only.

Guns would scope in and out even though I was holding the right click, and they would also stop and start shooting even though I was holding the left click. The issue would come and go so I thought it was an issue with the game. Now, 3 months later it is happening very often and the game is unplayable. I only realized it was not an in-game issue when I started using the mouse for work and I couldn't drag windows or files. Now after reading this I realize it's been an issue with the mouse the whole time. Very disappointed.

rating 1.0. Bad radio in the Razer Basilisk X HyperSpeed I was looking for a Bluetooth mouse that I could seamlessly use with my gaming notebook and my office computer, a Dell Latitude 7480. Unfortunately, this is not the mouse to fit the bill. I was interested in the mouse because of the ergonomics and multiple DPI settings. I play a lot of Civilization and City Skylines, and thought that with so many DPI settings, I could more easily lay roads and scan across maps. Alas, I will never know because the mouse is practically unusable as a result of the bad radio in it. While I updated my personal gaming notebook, an HP Pavillion, with proper Razer drivers, I don't have that privilege on my work computer. As a result, once the mouse went to sleep, it never reconnected with the Dell that was using the standard Microsoft HID drivers.  Even with the proper drivers on the gaming laptop, the mouse only reconnected with the computer after going into sleep mode 50% of the time. Sadly, Razer only allows you to put off sleep mode for up to 15 minutes -- presumably for battery savings. There is no way to tell the mouse to never sleep, or even set it to 30 minutes. I plan to return the mouse.

rating 3.0. Decent mouse with a fatal flaw This is a pretty comfortable mouse. I have medium-sized hands and like to use a claw grip, and this mouse works very well for that purpose. Nice rubberized sides make it easy to pick up the mouse, and the right and left clicks have a satisfying click to it. The wireless has worked flawlessly for me; no lag and no drops. The middle wheel scroll has a cheap feel to it. Each scroll click has a loose feel and a distinct clackity sound to it, same as found on most cheap $15 generic brand mice. Not the best but I got used to it. The thumb buttons are easy to reach but have a little more give to it than I would like. Not a big deal and I got used to that too.

So, what's the fatal flaw? The middle mouse button is garbage. If you use the middle mouse button on a regular basis, it WILL stop working very very quickly. At first, clicks will occasionally fail to register, then gradually the failures will get worse and worse, until only 1 out of 5 clicks will register. Sure, maybe if you push it just right, at the right pressure and angle, it will register. But who wants to deal with that? I have owned two basilisk now, and both of them had the middle mouse button fail within 4-5 months.

You might be okay with buying a new mouse every 4-5 months, but not me. I've owned mice that were half the price but lasted three times as long. It's a shame that Razer made a decent mouse at a decent price, but couldn't make it good enough to last more than a few months.

Update:
Instead of throwing the mouse away, I was able to open it up and clean out the electrical contacts on the middle mouse button to get it working again. I shouldn't have to do this on a relatively new mouse, but it's a solution if you're so inclined.

Mouse wheel is okay, dpi changer button is okay, side buttons are okay. Everything is just okay except those terrible left and right click buttons. I have no idea if its the buttons itself or maybe if the mouse was falling asleep or just disconnecting, but my G602 did not do this and nore does my G502 Hero.

I wanted to like this mouse, I loved my G602 and instead of spending 90-100$ on the G604 I figured I'd give razer a try. I regret it.

rating 2.0. Most Uncomfortable Mouse I Have Ever Used First off, I don't think this is objectively a bad mouse; The buttons all feel great, the click is pretty quiet, scroll wheel is nice, and the bluetooth works well. I do however, think it is a bad mouse for certain people. How do you know if you're one of those people? Simple. What type of grip do you use: palm, claw, or fingertip? This mouse is for palm grip ONLY.

I bought this mouse because it ticked all the boxes for what I was looking for, and at the right price. I never really gave any thought to what type of grip I used before using this mouse. Turns out I am about 70/30 fingertip/claw. For me, this mouse is uncomfortable and frustrating to use. I'll tell you why:

The thumb rest is a torture device, plain and simple. I like to hold my mouse between my thumb and ring finger/pinky, with those fingers in contact with the surface I am using the mouse on. The thumb rest makes that impossible and drives me mad. I know other people hold their mouse differently, but to me I cannot fathom why anyone would want this feature, it seems like it offers no benefit at all.

Next, this mouse is very long and narrow. Literally the longest mouse I have ever used, which for a fingertip/claw user is the opposite of what you want. I prefer my mouse to be short and wide. For reference, the most comfortable mouse I have ever used was the Cooler Master Spawn (RIP).

In the end I returned this mouse and replaced it with a Logitech G305, which is far more comfortable for me. It's slightly wider (I wish it was even wider, or with a ring finger rest) and significantly shorter, and most importantly, doesn't have a thumb rest. I also tried a Razer Viper Ultimate which felt very nice as well, but no mouse is worth $150.

My advice: go to a local electronics store where they have all these mice on display where you can try them out and feel them in your hand. That's what I did after returning this, and now I am happy with my Logitech G305.
