rating 2.0. Audio drops completely and often. Sounds good when it works but it takes me out of the experience every time, plus the price. My official Sony Platinum Headset cost a pretty penny, but it won't stay connected with full audio any time I play; the audio cuts out entirely for brief moments, usually about a second or two, and with a frequency between once every 5 minutes to once every 3 seconds. It's pretty consistent, but if I had to give an average I'd say it's about 2 seconds of total audio loss every 10 seconds regardless of the amount of charge in the headset.

Imagine speaking with someone and every 4th word isn't just muted but ALL sound is entirely absent. For the price of this headset I'm not sure how a problem like this is possible, and for the record the headset has had this issue since I unboxed it and without any physical damage of any kind. I've tried to find support but there simply isn't much of any information for the headset except instructions and fixing simpler issues that I have not experienced.

I've tried the following to fix it:
1.) Reset both the headset and the USB dongle per online instructions. No change in behavior.
2.) Changed the channel of my wifi several times (note the PS4 itself is hard-wired into the network). No change in behavior, hard to tell if any of the channels made it worse since the audio loss is always intermittent.
3.) Sat inches away from the USB dongle. Same results as being 6 feet away on the couch.
4.) Completely unplugged both the modem and the router from power to see if they were causing the interference. No change.
5.) Unplugged stereo, all other consoles, laptops, anything that might have generated any interference, no change.
6.) Changed the plug for the USB dongle from the left side to the right and vice versa. No change.
7.) Removed all profiles from Headset Companion App (also a side effect of resetting and repairing the device). No changes.
8.) Sat on opposite side of room. No changes.
9.) Moved PS4 to another room & TV altogether. No changes.
10.) Muted the headset and played single-player-only games with no chat support. No changes.

The headset itself sounds fantastic when it works without interruption. It's comfortable and seems much stronger than the Gold headsets I loved on the PS3. And while the buttons aren't in the same spots as on the Gold headsets, it's easy enough to change the volume and the chat/game crossover without having to take the headset off. The charge lasts an incredible amount of time for its weight, usually around 8 hours of listening with online chat on the entire time. The LED colors make it clear when the headset is fully charged, and while the USB dongle is strangely large it also has an LED to signal connection integrity and always finds the headset quickly. I put the headset on backwards the first few times but I wouldn't knock it for that.

I’m sincerely not sure what else to do or think because I can’t find much help online and because they were expensive. For example, losing chat in Destiny 2’s raid has caused my team to fail, never mind how much the loss of all sound completely takes me out of the game experience no matter what game I’m playing or what film I'm watching.
I bought the headset months ago on Amazon and it’s not a new problem but I guess I thought I could fix it. Regardless, I can’t trade them for a replacement and I’m definitely NOT BUYING ANOTHER PAIR for fear of the same issue.
This headset issue is explicitly the only reason I haven’t yet bought PS VR because I can’t bring myself to play a game in VR if the sound is not going to be functional, and I’d rather buy PS VR than a new headset.

rating 4.0. It's alright. Doesn't feel like a $159 headset. Waited for months, tried for about 6 hours now. Here's my take on the new PS4 Platinum headsets.

Pros:
-connection is solid (within 20 feet line of sight)
-volume is loud, both game sound and chat
-microphone appears to be clear. No complaints from other players yet.
-setup was quick and effortless
-good battery life. 6 hours on partial charge.

Cons:
-bass is really lacking. Even with the bass adjustment all the way up, the sound quality was mediocre.
-no tension adjustment. It is a tad too tight for my big head.
-volume adjustment is slow. If a troll comes along and starts blasting their mic, its quicker to rip off your headset.
-USB dongle blinks when not connected to device. (may annoy you when not using the headset) Edit: it blinks for about 30 seconds and then stops blinking.

If these were $100 headphones, I'd be a bit more forgiving. But to market these as the top of the line Platinum headsets, it makes me wonder why I spent the extra $80 over the Golds. It's not THAT much better.

As for connection issues, I have not experienced any. Walking 20 feet away is fine until you pass by a wall. Then it drops out completely. Upon re-entering the connection zone, it reconnects and no input is required from the user. Same thing when turning the unit on. Just turn on and it connects to the first remote that is connected.

The USB dongle has a blue light that shines solid when connection is acquired. It blinks when no device is found. A little annoying when you don't want to use the headset and leave the dongle in.

The ear cups slide up and down on the band. The band itself doesn't change length. This is the first headset that I owned with such mechanism.

rating 3.0. Large Bug!! - No Response From Sony I purchased these as an upgrade from the Flimsy Playstation Golds. My biggest gripe was the mic quality. Golds had a single mic that could be blocked if you adjusted your ear cup over it accidentally. The sound was awful.

I watched some YouTube comparisons and noticed a significant jump in mic quality. $130 and a ready-to-use headset for my PS4 Pro? Hell yeh.

Before I mention the bug there are some pros when compared to my Golds:
Battery life much longer - about 13h
Metal band instead of Flimsy plastic
3D Audio
Ear cups are the same padding - still comfy
Dual Mics presenting a clearer sound.

Cons:
Headset fits a little tight on my head compared to Golds.
The charge port is very tight and is difficult to insert the mic robust cable without thinking your doing something wrong.

And finally, when you turn your mic up above "Too Low" in the party chat settings your mic will begin to echo background noise and your voice into a loud continuous loop. The only fix is to keep your voice chat low. This makes it difficult for friends to hear you during games.

The Golds I have do not have this problem and do not pick up near as much background sound as the Platinums. The noise canceling mic is obviously not doing its job in a low-sound environment. (Think fish tank filtering water or someone talking calmly in the next room) The Golds I cannot hear any of this even when turning my volume up in the party. I'm unsure if even a firmware update can resolve this problem. Muting yourself stops it so obviously it's the dual mics.

rating 4.0. Good hardware, but a little buggy right now As an owner of both a Platinum and Gold headset, I will be reviewing this product based on my experiences with both headsets.

The Platinum has some improvements over the Gold:

+ hinges and top band quality have gotten significantly better. The Gold headset has known issues with breakage at the fold-in hinges, but this does not look like it will be an issue for the Platinum version.

+ 3D sound feature is pretty cool

+ There is a sidetone feature that allows you to hear yourself better. It can be adjusted with the mute button. I personally prefer the "high" setting, so that I do not feel my voice is "muffled" by the headset.

However, the Platinum has these issues:

- a smaller, tighter headband

- connection issues

Overall, I believe this could be a great product if the bugs are fixed - but for now, the Gold headset is the absolute winner between the two. If you owned a Gold headset and felt the headband was too big, the Platinum headset might fit you better; I personally found it to be too tight.

My Platinum headset disconnects randomly, then reconnects itself, but the left speaker sound goes out. I was able to fix the speaker's sound by entering the Playstation app and restoring the headset. This has occurred so often that I have now decided to return the product and exchange it for another Gold version.

Will mention that you probably can get something pretty similar with the gold headset for half the price.

rating 5.0. rating 5.0. Great wireless headset for PS4. If you had the Gold headset then this one will be just like it. There are some improvements to the way buttons are handled in that they go back to physical buttons that make it easier to feel them to know what they are as compared to the Gold headset in which the buttons were just kind of part of the casing itself. Sounds great and wears just like the Gold headset.

Note that when you load this up via the Headset app on the PS4 that the game options are pretty slim for Platinum. There used to be a lot of games if you pick Gold in the app but that is no longer the case because this is a different headset and the game presets are only for those with 3D audio. For instance, while Watch Dogs and Watch Dogs 2 had presets when looking up the Gold Edition in the app, these games do not appear when you select Platinum. So you have to choose a generic profile for those games now on the Platinum headset.

rating 2.0. Don't buy! So I had been anticipating this headset, couldn't wait to get it and when I finally got it I was really let down. I've used the Sony pulse elites for about 5 years now and it's the best headset I've ever had. I have been searching for a headset with equal or better quality and when I heard the platinum headset was coming out I thought for sure it would be awesome. I was wrong, sound quality is okay but doesn't compare to the elites, bass isn't there, and the cups don't seal around your ears to block out sounds around you. They have a nice design, they are comfortable, but I could go spend $70 and get a headset equally as good as the platinums. DONT BUY THEM!!!

rating 2.0. Very uncomfortable, 3-D audio not supported by games Sound quality is good with no real reason to complain, but the build quality is only average. The actual construction of the headband and cups are very solid, but the switches and controls feel noticeably cheap and flimsy, the toggle switches especially are made of very thin cheap-feeling plastic.

My biggest complaint is comfort, this headset is extremely uncomfortable due to the think rubber/plastic band that serves as a connection wire between the two cups. There is no padding whatsoever on this band and it puts pressure directly on the top of your head since it's isn't flexible either. After wearing for an hour my my head was sore, it felt like some huge buff dude was standing directly above me and pushing a firm plastic ruler into my head putting all his weight into it. The cups themselves are nice and cushiony any comfortable so even after long play sessions ears are not sore (as is the case with many headsets that crush your ears over time).

Overall I find that the PlayStation Gold headphones are much more comfortable due to the fact that they do have padding on the inside of the band. Despite the improved sound quality I'm going back to the old headset and regret this purchase.

One other thing to note is that the much-touted 3-D audio is only supported in Uncharted 4, and newer games such as Horizon Zero Dawn or Nioh don't support the feature. If you download the companion app there are literally no custom profiles for this product (besides Uncharted 4), I find this really confusing. Why did Sony include this feature if they're not going to support it even in first party games?

rating 4.0. Better than Gold My daughter has the Gold set while I just got the Platinum. I was able to switch back and forth to compare. The platinum is a great improvement. Clear and crisp. I am happy with it because it is quite noticeably a better product. It won't compete with BOSE or the like, but for under $200, they are pretty nice.

rating 5.0. Great headset. I like that you can easily adjust ... Great headset. I like that you can easily adjust from standard sound to "extra bass" by a touch of a button (toggle from option 1 to 2). The headset materials feel like a better build than the golds, so they shouldn't have any cosmetic issues as long as you take care of them. Some issues I've seen with the golds is the headband would crack just over time, I don't see that happening with these. The surround sound is amazing, I tested it on battlefield 1 and they outperformed my old razer man o war 7.1 surround sound headset. Well done Sony! Easy set up too. And if you're experiencing any echo, it says how to adjust in the "quick start guide" under mute/sidetone section: the guide states this:
"sidetone allows you to hear your own voice from the headset"; when mute is on, sidetone is turned off.
-Short press to mute or unmute mic.
-long press to choose the sidetone volume (low, high, off)
-one beep= low
-two short beeps=high volume (can hear your voice more clearly, possibly more echo)
-one low beep=sidetone off

Also you can adjust your microphone level under sound/devices ---> adjust microphone level

Hope this helps

rating 4.0. however it is still comfortable overall. Appearance Comfort: 4/5 the ear muffs have some firmness and I’ve used softer, however it is still comfortable overall.

The other issues is these buttons are right by the mic so sometimes people here my hands running right across the mic when I’m adjusting the sound. Last, it does use a wireless usb to connect.
Sound 4.5/5 the sound is great, I love using the mix as well through the PlayStation app which allows you to create your own as well (I recommend maxing the bass, having the mid high and a lower treble for shooters, the bullet noises can be ear piercing if the treble is too high). Lastly an issue is that people hear there voice slightly echo since the ear piece is so close to the mic. Overall I would recommend them, If you can get over a couple of the flaws I mentioned they are worth it!

rating 4.0. Great Device, Hard to "Install" They Device arrived quickly and on time,
There was one problem, it did not come with instructions.
Now, this is a pretty self explanatory device, you connect it to Wii and TV and done,
but this was not the case in my experience, this is what I did to make it work.

1) Plug it in, test it out on the TV, if it works your done if not you have some trouble.

2) If it did not work, its because your Wii is on a setting that your TV does not support.
This means you have to go into the Wii's SCREEN settings and change the TV RESOLUTION.
You can do this in 2 different ways, 1. Connect your Wii using the original cord it came with to TV and change it that way.
Or if your like me and your TV could not support the cable, you HAVE to connect it to another TV that supports the Resolution.

3) Now, when you connect it to the TVs make sure you press the small "hidden" button on the Converter use a paperclip, this resets the connection.
Ones you are able to change the setting on the Wii (My settings that worked were changing it to STANDARD TV 480i) go back to the TV you're trying to get it to work and press the "hidden" button one last time, this should get it to work.

Now, despite the reviews of it not supporting sound, it is completely up to whether your TV supports it or not. Mines worked.
The device does not get hot, but I keep my Wii in a well ventilated are.

Overall great device for a great price. Would have given a 5 if it had some sort of instructions or at least website to go to, of course you have Google but every device should be at least come with some sort of "Warning Change Settings" type of instructions. 1 small paper would be suffice.

rating 2.0. Beware when using with older hdmi tv sets! I ordered this product to work on a older hdmi tv (720p/1080i max resolution Sony Bravia KDL-V40XBR1). The product description says you can manually switch between 720p and 1080p modes by using a needle to press a button in the hole on the front of the unit. This is not the case!!! When I received the product, there was no "hole" on the front to switch between 1080p and 720p modes. So I contacted the seller (received a response after about 8 hours which isn't too bad) and they said:

"We contacted the manufacturer and were told that it could be the compatibility issue. In some cases the Wii to HDMI adapter could not read the TV's EDID correctly, so it could not be recognized by some TVs. They suggest that you connect all devices, turn on the TV first, and then turn on your Wii. If this doesn't help, the unit may not be compatible with your TV. If possible, you can contact Sony technical support. In addition, the manufacturer now only makes the new version (without hole). I am sorry that we do not have the old one to exchange for you."

So, the new "upgraded" version they are shipping will not correctly switch modes to 720p with my setup (and possibly not with other older hdmi tv sets as well), as the Wii2HDMI is sending out a 1080p signal to a 720p tv by default, and there is no way to manually change that with the "upgraded" version they are shipping.

The only reason for 2 stars is because the seller (or the Amazon fulfillment rep) was courteous and helpful even though they could not provide an adequate solution that worked in my case (other than try unplugging, power everything off, reconnect devices in order, etc). They also cannot exchange it for the older "manual" switching version - I asked them that as well.

I am giving a bad review because the item I ordered is not as advertised in the description, even though it probably will work with your setup assuming you have a newer 1080p (or even 720p) hdmi tv. It's kind of like ordering a part for a '67 Chevy and getting a part for a '72 Chevy and having the seller say "well, the new part is upgraded and better". True, but it may not work. So a word of advice to the seller: either ship what you advertise, or change your product description to what is actually being sold. It is a bit misleading to the buyer.

So it is now being returned and I am looking for alternatives in this price range.

rating 3.0. There is a difference, but... I bought this item because I was told that it would slightly enhance the picture of my gamecube games that I wanted to relive on the Wii, and to enhance the picture of actual Wii games as well as the Wii menu screens.

It did absolutely nothing for the gamecube games, not even in the slightest (I have two Wii consoles and had them on separate hd tv's right next to each other to do a comparison, one with the cables that came with the Wii and the other with this Wii HDMI device). As for the Wii games I did notice a slight difference, the graphics and picture looked a little sharper, but the difference was very minor. I note that one game did stand out, Xenoblade, it sharpened the picture considerably, but other than that exception, the other 7 Wii games that I sampled had little or no difference.

Now perhaps I was a bit naive to think that this little device was really going to have a big difference on game quality, but for $20 I figured it was worth the investment, however, it made such a miniscule difference in quality that I decided I would rather spend that money on something I would actually use and I will be returning this item. So if you are a big fan of Xenoblade, perhaps this is a good investment, or on the chance you sample a game I didn't and it looks better, go for it, otherwise, either buy the Wii U or just use one of the many fine Emulators that are now available for the PC. Emulators are free, most of these games can be used with them through Virtual-Roms, and they look beautiful, as long as your computer in up to par.

current_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. Older one works great I purchased one around 2012 and it has worked flawlessly. It is $20 for ease of use and very minor quality improvements.

Primary benefit: The wii is now easy to move between modern hdmi ready TVs.

Secondary benefit: consistent quality. I honestly didn't expect much since the output is still Ntsc. After buying this, I noticed lots of video and audio artifacts from the a/v cable signal (hdmi route yields slighly better audio range, the dirty power related flickering is gone and colors appeared modestly richer). While hdmi adapter does a fairly clean 720 upscale from ntsc, there is a slight loss of sharpness if you look closely. All in all the Hulu / Amazon prime kids shows look better with the hdmi adapter.

Most of the negative reviews regarding poor product quality seem recent. No comment. The 2012 version still works for us.
other_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. Fun family game - first Skylanders built for next gen systems. Swap force is major improvement over the older Skylanders games. The sound and graphics are built for current gen systems (ps4 Xbox one) and faithfully down scaled to PS3/360 systems. The wii version is missing much of the charm. The arenas and bonus missions improve replayability. We haven't done much swapping of the character parts, but the new characters are fun as they are.

Tip: shop around. buy the game starter pack used with game portal and toys for $20-30. You can also buy used figures for $2-10 at GameStop. You'll need about $35 in used swap force figures to unlock all areas. This puts the full unlocked game price close to the original launch price of $75. Also if you have trap team, the trap team portal works with older games.
rating 3.0. Too hard to set up I only played the single player campaigns in the first game.  I would have played more it blizzard didn't make it so hard to start the game.

rating 5.0. Perfect for a solid first grader or an advanced kindergartener. Good phonics training for small children.  Forces them to think of new vocabulary words, grapple with convoluted English spelling rules and engage with their older siblings to solve problems.

rating 4.0. exactly what was expected - just don't pay full price Pro: unlocks some fun arena boss battles with access to 4 stat boosting charms and 16+ portal master stars.

The toy is pretty and my son likes it. The crossbow is detailed and lights up through the magic of power over Nfc.

Con:

[standard price] this toy typically comes in a 3 piece pack for $25. For that price, you can buy a whole game. (IMHO, this has equivalent value to an above average iPhone game)

[content] Compared with the 2 other Swap Force expansion packs, this toy unlocks far less content. Buy this pack last.

[in-game function] As a weapon, the crossbow reloads slowly, has short range and does 40 damage. I can't see using the toy in normal gameplay.

Recommendation: buy used like I did from Amazon or GameStop for a fraction of the price. Only buy this after you've unlocked other parts of the game. Again, GameStop's sale of pre-owned Skylanders moves the Skylanders cost closer to iPhone in-app purchase model.

rating 1.0. Stay away! Unethical review practices and useless dead controller. Would give zero stars if I could. Setup was a bit confusing, but I finally got it paired with the Nintendo Switch. Construction seemed good. Weight and feel was nice. I was excited to set up a two player game with my daughter on her new Switch. On initial setup, it was flawless. Worked great...once! The second time I tried to use the controller it didn’t respond. It won’t connect to the switch, won’t light up, won’t charge. Completely dead and useless. No issues with the charging cable or the console as I tested it with a friends aftermarket controller.
Talk about utter dissatisfaction. Never experienced an issue like this with any controller I’ve purchased for any system in the past 20 plus years.
So now, there is no way to return or get a refund - 35 days from order date...for an inoperable dead controller... LAME!
Don’t fall for all the great reviews either. It’s a scam. They offer you a free controller if you leave them a good review. If that doesn’t tell you something about the company’s sketchy products, nothing will. Do yourself a favor, don’t throw away your money...STAY AWAY!

rating 1.0. Died within the first week I am sending it back for a replacement.

rating 5.0. Perfect "Second Player Controller"! We have a family of four, and often play Switch games together like New Super Mario Bros, Mario Kart 8, etc. I have the official Nintendo Pro Controller - which works great - but my 9-year-old wanted his own - he dislikes the small size of the joycons, and is more used to a standard Xbox style controller. So he used his allowance to get this. We've had it for a week now and so far so good! The controller does have a decent weight to it, the grips on the bottom are nice, and we haven't run into any sync or lag issues yet - I'll update this review if we do in the coming weeks. I was pleasantly surprised how good the rumble feels, and it looks pretty sleek too.

The only issue we've had was at one point I plugged it into the USB-C cable for the Nintendo pro controller instead of the cable it came with, and the controller became unresponsive - the lights wouldn't come on. But (after reading the Q&A here) this turned out to be a simple fix - on the back of the controller there is a small circle in the upper right. Just stick a paperclip in there to click a small hidden button to enter a "reset" state. Doing this and plugging it into the correct cable seemed to bring it right back to life. I wish this was in the instruction manual, but it was a minor annoyance and I haven't had the issue again since that one time.

For me, the official Nintendo pro controller is still a clear first choice, but as a backup or second player controller at around 1/3 of the price (we paid $23), this is a perfect fit.

rating 5.0. Pairing directions Provided instructions on pairing are a bit unclear:

1.) Fully charge controller
2.) Disconnect charging cable.
3.) Using an already-paired controller, enter the Controllers menu, then the Change Grip/Order screen.
4.) With new controller, hold down home button for 10+ seconds. After 3 seconds, all lights will blink slowly but continue holding home button until all lights blink faster. Release home button.
5.) Press L + R triggers to complete pairing.

Controller has nice firm button pressing, joysticks are not sticky, seems to have solid construction. I really like the grippy handles.

rating 4.0. High quality controller for cheap! Overall, I love this controller. There are some pros and some cons but overall a great value

Pros
-Great weight. It's not hollow feeling like some.
-Smaller controller which is perfect for my small hands. That being said, it isnt so small that it would be unusable for someone without tiny hands
-this controller has a turbo function and it's super easy to switch which button turbo controls.
-wireless! I can walk to my kitchen and make lunch while still crafting in game. It's a decent distance from the switch and has a wall in between. Awesome.
-connecting was super easy! It didnt connect initially but I pressed the controller reset on the back and it fixed the problem. The instructions that come with the controller tell you what to do to troubleshoot.
-it has a true arrow pad vs separate buttons like the joy cons have

Cons
- turbo is not quick. I bought this controller for animal crossing and in turno mode I only recieve 7 mats from rocks because the turbo isnt fast enough.
-out of the box the buttons are quite stiff but now that I'm 6 hrs in that issue seems to be gone
-turbo requires you to hold the action button that's tied to it the entire time. Maybe this is just standard, I honestly didnt know that turbo existed until a few days ago. I was hoping I could walk away and still wish on stars.

Both a pro and a con is the turbo. It's super strong so if I'm doing something for an extended period of time that triggers turbo my hands tingle uncomfortably. Also, I haven't noticed a difference between vibrations. What I mean by that is, for example, when fishing in ac each fish has a distinct vibration pattern when you catch them. This controller does not seem to have any distinction between fish.

Would I buy this controller again? Heck yea. I'm already looking at a second one to have when I game too long for this ones battery. I have yet to kill the battery on this one yet.

Edit*
You can charge the controller while you play! Given, the cord included is much too short to use but it uses a usb-c which is very common for other devices.

rating 4.0. TURBO ONLY WORKS IF YOU KEEP HOLDING BUTTON PROS:
Comfortable
Wireless
High vibration sensitivity (could be a pro or con depending on what game you’re playing. For animal crossing I found it annoying)

Con:
Thumb pad is very stiff and hard to press
Turbo only works if you keep holding down the key
Battery charge may or may not be defective (didn’t test long enough)

Details:
I have a switch and decided I wanted a wireless controller with a turbo button so I could do menial things in animal crossing without wanting to quit out of boredom (like buying nook mile tickets, customization kits, and watching shooting stars). After buying the first controller I couldn’t get the turbo button to work, so I assumed it was defective. I then requested an exchange and tried it with a second controller and, wouldn’t you know it, same thing. I then realized the directions never said you could LET GO of the button you were trying to turbo. So if you press A, then turbo, it will keep pressing A but ONLY AS LONG AS YOU HOLD DOWN A. Now I don’t know about you guys, but my favorite part of the turbo button is that I DON’T have to continue pressing ANY buttons. This just negates the need to continuously click, which defeats the purpose of a turbo.
Additionally, the second controller I got was almost dead, and charging it didn’t seem to help much. I didn’t keep it plugged in long enough to see if it would die, but it was weird that one controller came fully charged and the other had almost no battery.

In the end, it’s a nice controller, comfortable, but it didn’t do the one thing I wanted, so I will be returning them both.

2) my daughter is a pro gamer. She competes around the country and swears by the Switch remote. She did not have anything bad to say about the remote but still prefers the Switch remote due to weight and L & R button config.

rating 5.0. Best Value!!! Best charging kit that is not over priced. I love this thing. Can do one pro controller and up to 4 joy cons. Nothing could beat this for 20$. I saw a switch brand charging doc for 60$ and I think that’s a rip off compared to this. 10/10 would buy/ recommend.

rating 4.0. Charges easily, nice design, easy to use I bought this once I had two extra sets of JoyCons for my family and needed a way to charge them off the console. This was a flexible alternative to the Nintendo supported charging dock, since you can also charge the pro controller. I don't have a pro controller, but I figured it wouldn't hurt to have the option if down the road I bought one.

The dock comes with a USB-C cord, but not the wall plug on. So you can plug it into the Switch console, but not the wall unless you have the plug. Technically, you could also just take the Nintendo wall charger and plug that directly into the base and it'll work as well, which I did just to check, and it charged just fine.

The lights work fine, green for go and red for charging, and I didn't have an issues with them. I noticed quite a few of the reviews here said the lights were too bright, but it didn't bother me at all, even at night. It has an on/off switch at the base that you can toggle once they're done, so the stand becomes a place to store them without overcharging the batteries. I really liked that addition, actually, meant I didn't have to unplug the cord itself to use it for storage.

The dock itself does feel a little flimsy, like the plastic is thinner and just not as sturdy, but I haven't had any issues with anything breaking. It just doesn't "feel" as solid, so I saved the original box with the plastic insert in case I wanted to transport it, since I have to take the console and JoyCons between my house and my partner's fairly frequently.

This is a pretty solid deal for the money, it's cheaper with more options than the Nintendo sponsored version, and I haven't had any issues with it at all. It was delivered quickly, no issues with that either. Definitely would recommend, the higher reviews for this product seem to be accurate so far.

rating 5.0. Totally worth it and then some! After we got a second set of controllers for our Switch, it seemed appropriate to get a charger for the joycons given how often my fiance and I play together. He was hesitant considering this isn't a licensed Nintendo product, but I can assure you it works and functions perfectly. The lights indicating if whether or not something is charged is an awesome feature and looks really cool. Yes they are bright but it's easy to switch the charger from on to off with a little switcher in the front. We ended up getting a pro controller as well and again, it functions perfectly to charge that too. For the price and what little space it takes up, I would highly recommend this product. It is a simple, durable design-- I think even my Mom (who has never seen a Switch) could figure out how to use this charger in no time!
Unless you are adamant about name-brand products or want something considerably flashier, do yourself a favor and save the money buying this bad-boy.

rating 5.0. This thing is so easy to use my 3 & 7yr old know what to do. So I see a lot of people that are having issues with this charger. From day one I have not had any issues, my 3yr old and 7yr old can plug the controllers in and bam pow, they're charging like the image.
At first I will admit that I was a little confused as it does look like you should slide the controller in like you do on the game pad. But then I realized that the little piece on the bottom of the controller that directs you when sliding into the game pad is what you plug into the charging station (1 min later). And it works. Simple as that, but if you don't have critical thinking or common sense your going to get Pist and give negative reviews.

5 STARS from me and my family.

rating 5.0. Awesome product and GREAT customer service! It's a really nice product but I think mine is a little defective. The lights are lit up when my joy-cons are plugged in. Overall, it's still a really nice product. I was excited to put it to use.

EDIT: The product is fully functioning now. The USB-C charger on the right side works perfectly. There was a misunderstanding about the lights not turning red when I plug in my Nintendo Switch or my Pro Controller. The green lights do not turn red when those two are charging. But they do turn red when you're charging your joy-cons, which is what the product really entails.

This is definitely the best joy-con charging dock. It has the option of charging 4 joy-cons at the same and also have the option of charging your Pro Controller or Switch as well. The other charging docks only have one or the other, not both. That's why I went with this one. Have not had any issues with it so far.

rating 3.0. Charges, but a bit awkward to connect joy-con The product functions as expected: connecting a joy-con does in fact charge it.

The challenge is actually connecting the joy-con.

The joy-con must slide into a small section at the bottom of each clear plastic pillar. It's a bit difficult to do in the dark, and the brightness of the other pillars makes it even more difficult to see. I typically end up fiddling to get it to slide into place, eventually.

It is equally difficult for young children to get the joy-con slide into relatively small space.

For me and younger users, I would have rather had a longer, more user-friendly path to insert the joy-con, at the expense of the visual effect of the bright green or red plastic pillars.

other_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Great switch (pun intended) I always took my switch when I went away to visit friends. I hated carrying the big bulky dock. It was uncomfortable, obnoxious, and takes up more space than it looks. After picking this up, I don't mind taking it around. Taking out the original board for the dock and putting it in was really simple, as their is only 2 components, the light and the ribbon cable for the usb-C port.  The only thing that doesn't make sense is the air vent along the back. The only way to comfortably fit the cable blocks half the vent, so its an odd design choice, but not really an issue as it doesn't heat up too much on its own anyways.

rating 5.0. Where did the time go I was really excited for this game. Normally I don't care too much about games, I buy the ones I think will be fun, and leave it at that. This, however, was very different. I now didn't need to choose which game I wanted to play, because all the characters are available. Of course there are changes, but I'm a very casual player, so Im not really affected by them. I also like that now you can play on any map in any form essentially. This makes it more fun to do a random map without worrying about a place like 'temple' coming up. Unlocking all the characters was such a journey, but well worth it. I do wish I tried out other characters during that time so I can learn their moves, but I was just trying to unlock them first...so I only know like 5 characters, but with all the echo fighters, and other just pretty similar fighters, it shouldn't be much of an issue, and like I said, very casual gamer here. Can't wait for the online to actually start working properly so I can see how bad I actually am

rating 4.0. Mario party....? I always enjoyed mario games. They've been great, especially with groups, but still enjoyable alone. However this one was quite different.

Pros:
1. Different types of arcade modes
2. New twist on rolling with the dice block
3. New items for a more aggressive approach
4. New minigame category

Cons:
1. Turns drag on for some reason
2. Minigames are pretty short
3. Limited selection of maps and minigames

I think it's still worth a purchase because with a group of friends, it's still really fun. But an hour for a 10 turn game seems pretty long considering that the minigames are only like 30 seconds. how do you spend 5 and a half minutes just rolling and moving?? Solo play is rough, so you need to remind yourself that you're only doing it to unlock more stuff within the game.

The feel was still plastic, but had a touch of softness, like a piece of fabric (idk, its hard to describe other than soft like the name states) which was different, but not in a bad way. the only downside i saw, was that it seemed more like a coating, instead of a molded plastic with that color (as if a paint or something). This led me to see a hint of white around the edges of the shells. I thought that this would bother me a good amount, but its such a small thin strip, that you can barely notice it, especially with the softness of the mint green.

input: rating 1.0. Comically horrible mic, awful software I was surprised by just how bad the quality of this headset really is. The packaging makes it look like a high-quality product and the headset is comfortable enough, but the audio quality and the mic make a fast food drive through sound like an upgrade. The audio of the headset is decent enough, but once you install the software that comes with it, everything you listen to sounds like it's in a bathtub, with tons of reverb and a huge loss in crispness and overall audio quality. The mic, however, puts all the other flaws to shame. This mic buzzes like a housefly. Anything you say, even at a whisper, overloads the mic and causes it to buzz to the point that people can barely understand you. I will be returning this headset, and it will be the last thing that I buy from this company. If I'm going to pay around $100, I don't want people to think I'm going to ask them if they "want fries with that".

rating 1.0. Sound great - when they work I ordered 3 pair of gaming headphones to compare - the Razer BlackShark V2, HyperX Cloud Core and Corsair HS50 Pro.

The HyperX was pretty good, they were very light and the 7.1 surround sound was good but the ear pads didn't extend far enough down to be comfortable for long. The Corsairs were too heavy and the headband was tight.

The Razers were by far the best. Extremely light and comfortable, the mic quality is better than the HyperX, and the ability to fine-tune the sound (including THX Spatial Audio) using the Synapse app is really nice. I already have a Razer mouse, so Synapse was familiar. The THX Spatial Audio sounds cleaner and richer than HyperX's 7.1 surround sound.

EDIT: these are HORRIBLE in Discord - when the mic works at all, it generally has horrible echo and/or stutters and cuts out every few works. Not usable for Roll20 with Discord. The mic is usually not working when checked in Windows audio either. Rebooting doesn't help, it seems very random. Resetting everything to default in Discord will help sometimes for awhile, then the mic will begin cutting out again.

The mic hasn't worked for a week, no troubleshooting steps help. Avoid these.

rating 4.0. Great product. I love em Really great pair of headphones. I’ve only ever used turtle beaches and a cheap $25 Chinese pair from Amazon, my latest pair of turtle beaches was unreliable with sound and would cut out often when I was playing certain Games, and having the charge the headphones even though they were wired was absurd. After about a year they snapped after being dropped, even thought I took great care of them and never abused them. So I decided I had had enough and wanted to switch to a different company or a more reliable set of headphones without breaking the bank. I’ve had these for about a month and I love them. Phenomenal product

Pros:
Don’t need to charge.

Wire is fabric coated and is bendable and doesn’t kink or twist the way rubber ones do. I have no fear of it breaking.

Reliable, good sounds. Lots of depth in high and low ranges, and I can hear foot steps intuitively, really helping me up my game.

Phenomenal noise cancelling. My fire alarm could go off and I’d barely hear it, and it allows you to be super immersed.

Controls are super intuitive, and the volume onow is well made and easy to use. Very tactile feeling to it as well, you can tell how loud you’re making it by feel alone.

Cons:
Very very rare, but the audio has cut out a few times, but often it was only when my controller had low batteries, and it was very minor and quick, didn’t effect me much.

The Jack seems slightly too small, and I’ve had it like out once or twice

I don’t like the plug in mic system. I really wish it was a foldaway or pivoting instead, but some people might think this is a pro. It does make it super easy to replace the microphone with a higher quality one, but I really personally just don’t like having a boom mic in front of my face unless I wanna detach it
rating 1.0. Sound cuts in/out Unfortunatley, these are less than a year old, and sound quality is horrible.  Started off great, but now nothing but cutting in and out.

rating 2.0. Good for YouTube. Thats about it. I like to consider myself to be at least a little tech-savvy. I know my general way around technology. That is not the case for this.

TL;DR at the bottom.

Here are the pros:
As a physical object, its phenomenal. Its lightweight, comfortable, and doesn't put very much pressure on my glasses. In this regard, it is better than my HyperX Cloud 2.

The audio is good... If all you're doing is watching YouTube. Listening to people on Discord is good too, I'll give it that.

Here are the cons:
When its first plugged in, it has you install a program called Razer Synapse. No big deal, some peripherals have that. But wow, do I have absolutely no idea how any of it works. I tried fiddling around with some of it, but I still have no clue.

While gaming, you cannot use Discord. Well, you can. If you want the audio of the game to be near silent. Again, maybe there's a way to fix it in the program you install, but I couldn't find it.

The microphone. Wow. This headset came out in what, 2020? It sounds like its from the 90's. A cheap microphone from the 90's. Yes, it picks up your voice, but it sounds like you're talking through a radio. On discord, surprisingly, its even worse. My friends thought that I was on mobile, the quality was so bad. I listened to it myself, and sure enough, it really did sound like that.

I don't know, maybe theres something with the program that I just don't understand. But with my current knowledge, this is the worst headset I've ever owned.

TL;DR: Lightweight and comfortable with my glasses. Microphone is awful, audio is good when not using discord. Program is confusing. Buy HyperX.

Hey, if you like YouTube, and thats it, and you want an overpriced headset, this is your headset.

rating 5.0. Great sound, an absolutely must have [[VIDEOID:bf4aab95dd01905b3eff60d711301722]] Best headset i own. I just wish the cable didnt coil up as much. But its excellent for games like hunt showdown which audio is the key to victory.

rating 4.0. Wish the 3.5mm was louder They're nice. They feel light on the head and cover my (smaller) ears comfortably. They have a good feel and when paired with the software on PC, they sound great! The ear cups insulate really well and there's very little bleed out into the room. I happen to really like the volume knob on the side of the headset - no searching for buttons or hitting the wrong one on accident.

However... and this may just be a "me" problem, but when they're plugged into my phone or tablet with the 3.5mm connection they're not the loudest. They're pretty quiet for this price range. In fact, I just checked them on PC and phone playing the same Youtube video on both. The PC topped them out at 90 dB, while the phone was just 60 dB. To verify just how much effect the phone was having, I tried the headset these are replacing and the other set I have. Those were 70 and 75 dB on the phone and high 80's on PC. This tells me the software is giving these a significant boost - which is fine if all you will ever use them for is gaming. Unfortunately for me, these are also meant to replace the headphones I wear at night watching movies - which is over a 3.5mm jack.

To be fair, these are marketed as gaming headphones - though I'm not sure how they sound on a console - my kids only left me the PS3.

I'm giving 4 stars for the comfort and the great sound on PC. If they weren't as good as they are there, they'd go back. They just don't sound quite good enough or get quite loud enough (for me) to use as an everyday headset for my phone.

For those who are unfamiliar with what a 10 dB difference sounds like, 60 is a loud electric toothbrush and 70 is freeway traffic. Also, listening to prolonged sounds above 70 dB is bad for your ears, kids!

rating 5.0. eSports Grade I'm a former semi-pro Overwatch player, and these headphones are almost better than the studio-grade sound-canceling ones we used for tournaments. 4 stars for sound quality since the ANC isn't as good, but it still allows me to focus on the game and tune out everything else. rating 3.0. It's razer quality. I don't hate razer and have used a ton of their products. So when I bought this device, I expect the razer quality which is usually mediocre to good.

rating 1.0. Minimalist style; maximum performance! with update after 6 months I am comparing these to the Logitech G430 (probably not a fair comparison) but it's what I'm jumping from. Logitech Gaming Software vs Razer Synapse...six of one, half dozen of the other, it's a preference things; I've got both on my rig and it's not a big deal; they're different, they work. Synapse lets you setup game sound profiles and different profiles for music & video. You get to fine tune the audio profile that fits you best, and that's a good thing, because the default 'flat eq' profile doesn't do it for me at all as I prefer more mid and treble for fps (COD, Destiny, Overwatch). When you adjust the different profiles for video/music, the sound is great, the V2's pump out a ton of bass; now I gotta be fair the "thx spatial surround" doesn't really differentiate 'left' from 'left rear' very well...but I'm still tinkering with it, so maybe I'll get the settings right for me (thus 4-stars).

The fit is super comfy; soft fabric is nice, not like the scratchy coverings on the G430, the cover and the foam and great and help with sound isolation. But the G430's have more passive sound isolation. The adjustable wire guides use friction and tension to keep the enclosures in place; it's a simple, effective system and i hope it'll last, but it's something that's easy enough to adjust to work around. Adjustments are a two handed affair: hold headband with one hand, pull/push ear cover with the other and your done. Again, simple, I like simple, no rgb leds, no fancy knobs/dials on your cable, just a volume dial and a mute button on left side.
Super light, lighter than any of my previous gaming headsets. Granted, being able to pull the mic out reduces the weight, but if I'm not gaming, i don't need it. I thought I'd hate the removeable mic, but I really like the option as I find I'm listening to music more than gaming. Additionally, it can only be plugged in one way (perhaps the guys who are complaining about the microphone didn't know this). I did a ton of mic playback and the audio is clean, without any distortion. The mic 'clicks' into place and gives the impression it's "in there" and you don't feel like it's going to slip/drop out anytime. Having said that, these are not the kind of headsets you can toss around and feel like they are going to take the abuse. They are well made, but you can't just chuck them into your gear bag all the time and expect them to last a couple years...you are going to bend/break/stress out parts.

I've got a pair of sony 1000xm3's, so that's my only 'decent' headset to compare too with respect to audio quality and I'll be honest, the V2's are not in the same category for music/movies, but for gaming the audio is spot on for clean mids and highs (gotta hear those footsteps) these are an excellent choice and they priced them right where they should be given the performance, versatility and value you are getting for $100. A solid 4 stars, I'm keeping them!

2 months later: I'm still like these. I can hear stuff that none of my past (low end) gaming headsets could hear (mpow, logitech, boltune, red dragon). When playing CoD, Destiny2, Overwatch, I can hear opponents near me! It's awesome to have expanded situational awareness.

6 months later: dropped to 1 star...they really need removeable/washable earpads. this one issue makes these headphones "disposable"; so sad. Sound is great, mic still works great...my earpads are sweaty-nasty :(

rating 1.0. Awful, but it looks and fits good Barely worked for 10 minutes. Stopped working ever again. Sound quality was not good in the beginning, worse than my 4 year old headset. Customer support could not help after an hour. Terrible product and support. At least it fit good?

rating 5.0. decent for gaming, better for music if you want higher-quality music and gaming playback, do NOT use the sound card that came with this headset!  my pc's audio card, Sound Blaster Z, has a much better choice for this feature.  highest with built-in card: 48,000hz, as opposed to my Z: 192Khz.  also EVERY game has its own set of audio-tailoring settings, depending on the game.  some are good with the built in sound card.  i prefer plugging directly into the headphone jack on my pc's Sound Blaster Z.  my comfort level: 20% volume in the pc's Z control panel, while the volume control knob on the headset should be set to about mid-way, where it seems to "stop" when you adjust it.  also, excellent voice control options from witin the built-in audio card's control panel for the detachable mic.  it's usually to each their own, but what helped for me are the above.  also, BE CAREFUL when adjusting the EQ!  whether it be ur pc's sound card EQ, or the one that came with the headset.  too high or low, and you WILL damage both your eardrums AND the headset!  one more thing: if you have your sounds set wrong, this headset will let you know it!  noise-cancellation and comfort are tops.  DON'T go XTREME with this headset!

rating 4.0. Happy with the purchase, but there are caveats I bought this to replace my broken Logitech G933. After that one, I decided to go back to a wired headset; each has its advantages and for now, I'm happy with the choice but that's all subjective.

Build quality seems good. I'm happy to report that It is much smaller and lighter than the G933; I suspect this is because of ditching the battery and other hardware needed for wireless and RGB lighting. The cable on it is shorter than I expected so if your computer is not on the desktop next to you, you may want to invest in an extender, which is what I ended up doing.

If you've read any of my other reviews you'll know I don't consider myself an audiophile and don't feel I can offer an objective take on sound quality, but I am pleased with the quality of the audio and those I speak to on Discord have commented that mic audio comes through clean and clear.

It comes with a dongle that offers enhanced spatial audio. In my experience most of these things are gimmicky so I haven't spent much time fiddling with the software to tweak this. I've basically left it on defaults and called it good. From what I've seen it has plenty of settings if you're the kind of person that likes to dig into the small audio details.

The biggest strike against the headset is the software package. There are two versions of Razer's Synapse software, you'll want the newest one. Once I got the right version installed it did little to ease the confusion though. As I mentioned there are plenty of toggles for you to fiddle with, but I found it all very convoluted especially when it comes to the spatial audio.

All in all, it is a good headset with the software being its weakest link. That being said I'd still recommend it.

rating 4.0. "Dragon age Inquisition" Review! My point of view as a newcomer to the series. Let me start off by this declaimer, I have never played any of the previous dragon age games, and I didn't do that decision thing they did for the previous games (Dragon age keep) so basically I went into this Brand new.

I love RPG's and I on the aftermath of hearing that the "Wither 3" got delayed again I decided to try "Dragon Age Inquisition" after seeing all the positive reviews. Well, I have to say that was an awesome decision! Right when I booted up the game I knew I wouldn't be able to resist the comparison with "Skyrim". I mean why not I heard that "Dragon Age Inquisition's" world was not completely open world but broken into sections, now before you think thats a bad thing (like I did) understand that some of these sections are bigger than the whole world of "Dragon Age Origins", and I would say when put together they might even be bigger than Skyrim! I will break my review into sections as I feel that would be the best way to focus.

Graphics / Design:
This game is Gorgeous! and I mean it. The diversity and detailed world that "Bioware" was able to pull of with the help of the "Frostbite Engine" is amazing to say the least. It just seems so crisp and clear running on the Playstation 4, honestly this game really got me excited for what to expect from RPG's a couple years from now when we really can see the potential of these machines. I haven't seen it on Last Gen consoles so I can not comment on that.

Gameplay:
The controls are easy to master, some would say too easy, but i would disagree. To me, they are perfect for just about every type of gamer, this game is made so anyone can pick up and play even if your not a fan of RPG"s. Also, if you want something more strategic then you can use the tactical mode, which was taken from Dragon Age Origins or the 3rd person combat approach which was taken form Drainage 2. whatever the play style you choose, I don't think you'd be disappointed.

Story:
Ok, I thought I would be totally lost in this department for the fact of not playing the original 2. But thats totally not the case with this game, you don't have to have played any of the previous ones to understand whats going on. Not just that, the story is quite brilliant. I loved it! it sucked me in and didn't loose me, The main villain wasn't just some lame guy with a story either, theres was depth put into the characters so they don't just seem robotic.The individuals who form the "Inquisition" can really make you care for them with convincing movie like Dialogue. Pacing in this game had the best system I have ever played in any RPG.

Overall:
Overall I would say that Dragon Age Inquisition did not just meet my RPG needs but also exceeded them. I liked it more than Skyrim! It took me 60 hours to beat the main story and I will continue to do side missions I'm predicting to put a lot more hours in by the time I throw in the towel.
Theres just so much to do in this world!
I came in not knowing by the time I finished I will forever be a Dragon Age Fan and can't wait for the sequel. It really does deserve Game Of The Year.

My score. 9.0

rating 3.0. An almost return-to-form for Bioware Dragon Age: Inquisition is a welcome, albeit stumbling, return-to-form of Bioware's greatness. It falls a star short of being a truly excellent games, but it does many things quite well. I have played the game for about 20 hours now. I'll break down my review of each segment of the game in a pro/con style.

Maps--

Pros: The world is expansive. Gone are the linear, copy-and-paste corridors of Dragon Age 2. It has been said before, but this really is the best realization of the lore that Dragon Age has tried to convey in codex form since Origins. You actually get to visit the places you only read about in the scattered literature or referenced to in conversations from previous games.

Cons: Map design, at least in the starting zones, is horrendous. The terrain isn't terribly exciting, and the way the topography is laid out makes navigation too confusing and objectives/quests hard to complete. This isn't true in all maps, but the excavation tomb site in Orlais is a one giant cluster**** and you'll scratch your head wondering how to reach certain spots that is necessary for you to complete certain quests.

Quests--

Pros: The way you can open new areas of the war map by spending 'power points' is a fresh new twist.

Cons: In the beginning zones, all the quests are terribly executed. You would think in today's day and age, a simple Accept Quest/Complete Quest format would not be a problem to execute. But DA:I really drops the ball here. First, the quests are so mundane [carry a message from one town to the next that a health potion is needed, deliver a letter from person A to person B] that they lack all excitement or interest. Literally, no single side-quest has been memorable for me or had any sort of personality to it. Second, there are standard quests that repeats in almost every zone [collect shards from certain areas of the map, solve astrological puzzles] that again are more of a time-sink than actual compelling endeavors. The beginning zones makes absolutely no attempt to sweep you into feeling as if you're an epic adventurer on an epic mission.

Lastly, and I think most grievous, is the crafting system. First, there are a ton of materials to pick-up in your travels, so much so that you will wonder if you're the Herald of Andraste or an elfroot farmer. It takes 2-3 seconds to pick up a material from the ground, but if you're doing it every five feet, it adds up noticeably. Second, it is NECESSARY for you to pick up the materials, not optional, if you want health potions and upgrades. The game boasts that it has 100+ hours of gameplay. 50 hours of that will be picking up materials from the ground. And did I mention that there is no storage chest in your base of operations? That's right, you have 60 slots in your backpack, and all your materials that you're picking from the ground have to fit in your one knapsack.

Story--

Pros: Voice-acting and dialogue: The individual writing is spot-on, and each exchange of dialogue conveys the political strife and apocalyptic world that the players find themselves in. Also, the personal revelations of each companion as you get to know them better is very compelling. The world that Dragon Age constructs and presents is grand since the days of Origins, and it's great to see that lore being breathed new life on next-gen consoles

Cons: While the background lore and interpersonal relationships are fantastic, the main story itself is unevenly told. The pacing is non-existent; major changes such as the forming of the Inquisition happens in a matter of seconds worth of cut-scenes. It's as if the creative director of the game or whoever was in charge of charting out the experience of the player as he plays this game to make sure it has a semblance of pacing fell behind at the wheel. There really is no consideration for how the player comes to grips with the story that DA:I is attempting to tell. It's as if an anything-goes fanfiction approach was taken and the story is told gracelessly to serve as a makeshift scaffold for all the other game elements.

Combat--

Pro: Really the saving grace of the game. The blend of the tactical feel of DA:O and the frenetic, arcady feel of DA:2 makes the meat of this game, the combat, very enjoyable. The spells and abilities are also much more interesting than DA:O, and the visceral feel of combat conveyed extremely well.

Conclusion--

I bought this game for $40. I am a busy professional, and so I don't have a ton of time to play. This is well worth the entrance fee for any high fantasy nerd who needs to live out the fantasy of questing with powerful friends since it is the only game in town. You can see a lot of individual parts of this game working well and hoping that it would all come together. This game could have used another year in development.

rating 4.0. A step in the right direction I am a long-time player of Bioware games, going back to the original Baldur's Gate. Although I missed the boat on KOTOR, I played and loved the Mass Effect games, and was pleasantly surprised with ME3's multiplayer, despite it's single player story problems.

I'll get this out of the way, I never really liked Dragon Age. It was to me, an attempt at a brandless D&D setting that was all about "going back to the basics" and "dark fantasy", but the story and atmosphere was to me, not compelling. I played about four hours of each of the games and haven't picked them up again since.

DA: Inquisition is not an original game, it isn't a groundbreaking game, it is in RPG terms, pretty simple and progression is extremely combat focused. That said, like Mass Effect 2, it really cut out the annoying and shoddy game mechanics of the prior game(s) and delivers on a solid experience. The story is heavily inspired by middle age Europe, the clergy, language, and inquisition themes are all very soaked in the feel of history, although all the specifics are unique to this fantasy world. It's a compelling story.

The combat is fairly fast-paced and reminds me a bit of a ability-focused Mass Effect 3 multiplayer with auto-targeting. The use of a tactical view is akin to old RPGs like Baldur's Gate where you could pause at any time, deliver orders, then resume, rinse, repeat.

The most interesting thing this time around is the mission structure. No longer going to linear mission areas then leaving, you can literally just run around a single map for 10 hours straight completing missions, fighting and looting. There is a lot to do, and with the Assassin's Creed/open world game elements, it makes exploring, questing and collecting fun to do and very free-feeling.

Someone else put it best, "It feels like a really good MMO without those pesky other players." It's true. The world feels yours but has enough time-wasting side missions and objectives to shake a stick at.

The multiplayer is almost a carbon copy of Mass Effect 3's multiplayer in terms of classes, leveling up, resetting classes, obtaining item packs, etc. But in an interesting twist, the levels are set up as randomized dungeon runs instead of ME3's "horde mode" missions. Overall it's very enjoyable, although I wish it would have some meaningful connection between single and multiplayer.

To me, Dragon Age finally feels like it's own game. It has a ton of content, good story, memorable characters, and it's a game I quite enjoy.

rating 4.0. Fun Times, better than second not quite It's quite fun once you get into the meat of the game (3-4 hours in) Even more fun once the true big baddie gets revealed some more hours past that. Order goes DA:O > DA:I >DA2. There are several minor gripes about game play choices but the overall experience is worth the price of entry. 70hours in and about 2/3 through.

Tips for maximum enjoyment:
-Read Kotaku's article on tips for playing Dragonage Inquistion
- Leave the starting area (Hinterlands) as soon as possible or you'll A) out-level the rest of the game and/or B) get bored thinking this is all there is to the game
-Don't be afraid to play around with the schools/trees, but be mindful when you pick your specialization.
-Can zerg normal enemies but for Dragons/Giants and big enemies use the tactical view to target limbs / parts of bodies for easier kills.
