rating 3.0. Pretty and playable. Not much else. Let me just say I'm rather tough on a lot I games these past few years but a lot of these AAA titles are a lot of graphical eye candy and no innovations grit. Dragon age inquisition is like dating a super hot person that's mean to your mom.
Well, this is an edited review. My original review was regrettably premature because I was only about 30hrs into the game. I had been enjoying the game and given it a very favorable review. But after 130hrs into the game. My attitude was extremely sour towards this broken game.
Let me just let you know something before you read this long and ranting review. If you love mass effect and dragon age, you'll be fairly pleased with this title. But just don't waste to much time playing it. Do the companion quest and main story quest then be done with it. It's not worth 130hrs of gaming.
Quick pros and cons of the game and I'll discuss them more in detail later.
Pros.
Awesome character creation system, very deep and lots of options.
Good character banter and interactions.
Amazing landscapes and regions.

Cons.
Incredibly weak story
Absolutely broken combat
Terrible loot drops.
Controls are laid out wrong. Ps4 v
Side quests are done poorly. If at all
Broken lore codex system.
Leveling system becomes obsolete after lvl17 or 18.
Boring boss battles
To few romanceable companions.
Mounts are worthless
Useless collectables.
No mini games!!!!!!!!
This game just couldn't keep me interested. Its flaws just screamed at me telling me to "stop". I wish I would've ended sooner.
The combat in this game is absolute garbage. For the first 15 or so hours I didn't notice it. It's very similar to dragon age 2 in a sense but even more dumbed down(if that's even possible). While switching between characters is easy but it's the tactical system in inquisition that's pure scat!!! This system is micromanaging at its worst. You basically stop combat to issue a more tactical command in an attempt at a more coordinated fight. The problem is you cannot queue commands. This is a travesty. It makes combat slow and just painstaking in harder fights. (Especially against dragons). I found myself constantly pausing and trying to get my moronic allies to help the way I need them. For example. I always tell my archer or Mage to "hold position" out I harms way because if you don't the while run up to the enemy and fight them face to face. Or follow the tank into the hornets nest like idiots. It seems like telling them to hold a position on the battlefield is an easy command Wrong, there are abilities that the enemy use that will pull or throw them in certain directions. Once this happens the Mage or archer won't move back to their original position even though on the tactical screen it says the hold position command is still in affect. I then have to tell them again to move to said position even though the area is already highlighted saying "hold position". How has this bug made it past testing? The tactical curser was designed by satan. Moving this curser around is a joke. I gets stuck on ledges, objects and even little rocks. It's infuriating. It's next impossible to get it to focus on what you want to focus on because the view of the fight sucks. The combat system is designed to be fast. But the tactical system is slower than growing grass.
Remember in dragon age 1 and 2 how you can "script" your characters to behave a certain way in combat. I.e tank taunts enemy that attacks healer or rogue only attacks weak enemies or tank taunts all elite enemies? All that is taken out of inquisition. Your allies struggle to keep up because of this. The only script you can set are to either "follow" or "guard" a specific character or controlled character. At first I thought this would make things a lot easier. But it seems to just confuse AI controlled allies. For example I set my tank to "guard" Solas the Mage. So if I decide to take control of varric my archer, my tank won't attack or do anything at all untill Solas gets attacked. So while I'm attacking with varric, I'm getting pummeled because my tank just sits there. It's a redundant system that just doesn't work. It's best to set everyone to default. Which means everyone attacks what the controlled player attacks. You can adjust tactically for tougher fights if need be. They decided to get rid of the healing system. Wtf???? Why???instead you use barriers with mages or use this new potion system they implemented. It's incredibly dull. Apothecary stations replenishes all healing potions for free but all other more exotic potions might use a common herb like elf root. Which you'll have plenty of.
As far as classes in combat go, this is where the game falls hard on its face. Once I hit lvl 17 I found myself being able to solo every fight with my tank. In fact I never wiped once in my play though. This is because the tanks class is so unbalanced that it officially ruined the game for me. In fact, if you find your tank taking to much damage or dying a lot. You are doing something seriously wrong. As long as you have good constitution gear and your tank has shield wall and pay back strike; You can and you will want to solo the dragon fights. I got so sick of babysitting everyone in dragon fights, so I stopped resurrecting them. Even my two hander warriors can't stay alive like the tank warrior does. During my 5th dragon encounter I simply soloed the dragon and beat it...easily. No the game is not set to easy no I'm not a super amazing gamer that can brake game mechanics and no I shouldn't have to set the game on a harder difficulty. You shouldn't be able to do things like this in an rpg. The whole point of party mechanics is that everyone plays a part. Without a healing system, to low health for non tank classes and overpowering tank classes you get this mess of a game. In fact i never took a duel wielding rogue. They would just constantly die. I only took archers. Even two hander warriors just couldn't make the cut. Unless your tank is constantly throwing taunts(which is impossible, the cool downs are waaaay to slow) people will die a lot. If you're a fan of dragon age 1 2,baulders gate, wow, neverwinter nights 2, or any other party mechanic rpg you will most likely find i inquisitions mechanics redundant. There's just no effort or spirit in it. But at the same time you may likely put up with its flaws because it's a high quality production game.
The leveling is just as shallow as dragon age 2. You want a tank? Just throw a few points into "sword and shield" and "vanguard" skill tree. Then throw all your points into random skill trees. It doesn't matter. Mages are the stupidest yet. You can again just throw your points into any of the trees. All skills do damage. It just depends if you want to do fire , lightning or frost damage. I would put skills in all the Mage trees. That way you can take on any enemy resistant to certain elements. There's no cool summoning spells, auras, weapon enchantments, healing, weather effect
Spells, manipulation spells etc. nothing but a barrier spell to protect our party for a short duration. Just a bunch of damaging spells and traps. Which are all similar to each other. Mages in inquisition are defiantly dumbed waaaaayyyy down. When you get I about lvl 15 with any class you will just hunt for any passive skills you can find in any of the skill trees. Because you will have you abilities slots full. You will only need to use a few of these abilities throughout your game. Characters don't have enough mana/stamina to use all the skills at your disposal. Combat is "right trigger, right trigger, right trigger, square" repeat. And yeah no kill moves again like in dragon age 1. For the love of god bring back da1 party mechanics. It's all we want.
As for the plot. It's almost a literal rip off of oblivion elder scrolls. I won't give away much but portals to the fade are opening and engulfing the world and you must stop it. I guess mass effect made them run out of ideas for plot. I never really cared about the story of the game because it's pretty predictable and feels like it's written by a nerdy 10th grader. But how does this plot coincide with exploration and side quests within this vast and beautifully detailed world? Well, very poorly. Other than the main story and companion quests. The amazing sandbox regions to explore are pretty much empty and lifeless. Besides mobs of enemies and barren villages there's really nothing to do besides admire the view and whack monsters with swords. How did this happen? Imagine if skyrim had way less loot, wimpy enemies, no caves or hideouts, no one to talk to in villages or other cities, and the only quests are for the main plot. That's what inquisition is. Their are a side quest in each region though, however they are incredibly dull and anticlimactic. For example I found a ring on a dead body and my journal updated. Telling me to take the ring to the widow. No explanation, just a mark on the map telling me where to go(yeah, it tells you where to go for everything). I take it to the widow in a village. With no cinematic or story she just says "oh my poor husband,thank you inquisitor. ".........and that's it!!!!!! That's all you'll get. Nothing feels immersive or sincere in this unfinished game. I feel like they had back story ad lore in the works but they couldn't implement into the game like in DA 1 or even the mass effect saga. If your still not understanding what I'm talking about let me put it to you this way. RPGs are Role Playing Games!!!! This game game plays like assassins creed 3. There's no heart to it. In RPGs you immerse players into unique situations and get involved in the woes of people and places around you. It should all feel tied together. Inquisition just loads into a region(be that a pretty one)and you punch everything.
Your journey throughout the regions in inquisition is incredibly lifeless and dull. Not much happens. No real cinematic dialog or backstory. EA and Bioware must've run out of time and/or money for side quests. It seems like they had ideas for them, but again they just couldn't execute it. I don't understand why??? They did it in the past with pretty much all previous titles. Instead of side quest. Players are forced to collect codex pages and read about the goings on the the region. This is utter crap. Inquisition is a AAA title video game. We should be treated to dialog and drama. I just never cared about exploring in this game. Again I must stress, it's a beautiful world you'll see but with very little to do. For example; there are mosaics scattered throughout the game each one has a back story if you can collect all the pieces. I completed one mosaic(which took a long time to find) and was excited to see what it does. Once I checked it out at the keep; all the mosaic did was add more back story to the codex!! WTF kind of garbage is that? It's a cheap pointless activity the player can do to keep them I interested. It's 2015 people!!!! With a new gen system!! This is not innovation. This is a step backwards and a cheap way to fool gamers in thinking that this is "content". Just play through the story in this game. You'll save a lot of time in your life.
What about the choices you make? Yes you make decisions like should I execute this guy, should I let the mages be free etc. but none of these decisions matter or force the story and the ending anywhere different. The plot moves the same way for every player. It's a lazy attempt at a diverse plot. It's all the same for everyone. Sometimes you get a different agent or a party member leaves based on what you said. But the plot never forks or twists. Like in mass effect 2 if Any party members die based on your previous decisions. Everything i saw In the plot unfolding my friends saw aswell. "Oh I killed that guy" my friend says
"Oh I exiled him" I say
"Then what happened" I say ..
"Nothing really. " he says.
"Yeah me too" I reply.
"Oh"
"Oh"
"Coooooool"
Nothing you do matters in this game because everyone will get the same outcome. Your choices are only choices to give you the illusion that it's playing out differently. Not like in knights of the old republic. Hell even mass effect 3 let you pick a color lol.
It's 2015. How have we not figured out how to do this yet. Take the decisions the player makes and base the ending off it. Jesus!!! Technology and graphics are moving faster than innovation and it's driving me mad. We can't just stick to a formula because it makes money. It's boring and insulting but I digress.
But again we put up with it. Bioware used to have spirit and depth to its RPGs. Neverwinter nights, nights of the old republic etc...When you try to make the game more playable to everyone, developers and publishers are forced to dumb this down and take steps backwards And that, in my opinion kills art. video games are in a sense...art(and entertainment).
Inquisition is a very pretty game to look at. It's so disappointing that they couldn't make it an immersive rpg with deep leveling, combat, and quest system. It has innovation with things like the power system, and war room system. But it's execution was done so poorly. You have to cook the turkey before you poor gravy on it. This is probably the last time I get bamboozled by the dragon age franchise. I will consider mass effect 4, but dragon age is dead in my book. There are so many more and better IRPGs out there. As well as on the way. If you want a visual tour of a fantasy world you may like dragon age. But if you want good mechanics ,immersion, plots, ad most important, heart. Best look elsewhere. Try divinity original sin, wasteland 2 or pillars of the earth which is coming out soon. They used to work with bioware and made great RPGs.
I could go on and on about how jump button make looting annoying, how the loot drops are useless because of the weapon and armor crafting system.(for real. All the armor and weapons I made are better the the drops from every dragon), or how collection grabs took the place of lore and side quests, and limited romances.
Dragon age inquisition isn't a BAD game. But it also is far from good. It's an average playable game for new gen. It has little to offer players besides pretty graphics. Can't approve of these formulated shallow titles anymore. If you really really really want to check this game out go ahead. Don't let my opinions ruin it for you. But if you find yourself Falling asleep because your tired of exploring a lifeless wilderness or closing the same gates over and over. You'll understand my negativity towards this title. I expect more from these AAA games in the new gen area.

rating 4.0. Brilliant game with major flaw An absolutely fantastic RPG that made way more fun than skyrim. Combat and skill system are brilliant and the environments look stunning. That means that while playing on normal past level 10 I pretty much didn't die. That includes dragons. The first dragon at level 13 was hard but the rest was literally face rolling. And I am not playing vivienne knight enchanter. But my 2hand warrior in reaver mode is just slaying through those dragons with constant 4.5k crits. Also, I kill level 19 and level 20 dragons but get item level 14 armor loot. Big fail. I basically have been carrying items from level 14 and I am now in the last mission. Major diaappointment. rating 5.0. Very enjoyable game! Look forward to spending lots of time playing I put over 30 hours into this game so far and I don't even feel like I'm halfway through it. Like most people I really liked the first Dragon Age game and was luke warm on the second one. This game does a great job of combining good parts from the previous two games to make a well rounded and very enjoyable product. I didn't start playing the game right when it was released so i didn't run into any of the 'bugs' that have been reported online. I really like the party management system of this game compared to previous games. Having all your followers in your stronghold lends substance to the conversations. rating 5.0. Great Game (for those who aren't into Dragon age, still a great way to spend your time until the next Elder Scrolls) Depth and Mechanics:
I can't stop playing this game. I love Elder Scrolls and Fallout games (don't get me started with the online), but this has to be the best of the Dragon age series and is comparable to the depth of Skyrim. The mechanics are different entirely, but the immersion and shear number of hours you can throw at this game is incredible. I am already almost 80 hours in and haven't even beat the main story yet.

Adaptability to different play styles:
My wife plays this on easy and enjoys the collecting and missions. The easy setting allows her to get through the combat portions faster and makes it fun for her. If you are an elder scrolls fan like me and can't stand paying 15 buck a month, then this is a great way to spend your time until the next one comes out. Even by itself though, this is a very good game.

Multiplayer:
I am still caught up in the main story line, but multiplayer is similar to WOW dungeon setup, which I find fun. When I get bored with the single player I can definitely see myself putting in a few hours into the multiplayer. You have a few choice characters you can choose from (tank, archer, mage, etc.) and I think you can unlock different ones.

input: rating 5.0. Best gaming mouse ever in my opinion First off the mouse feels extremely well in my hand. I have big hands so some other mice feel too small. Adjustable DPI up to 20,000. It’s insanely quick and super responsive. The buttons all click and feel great. I don’t have issues accidentally pressing the sides buttons when playing. Although I have accidentally clicked the top left button while trying to click the side button. Wireless has no latency at all. Seriously I love everything about this mouse. I also bought one of the used like new to save money and it came in brand new packaging with no sign of use at all. If you play any online games and are in the market for a new mouse I promise you it’s worth the money. Did I mention they come with a warranty through razer? Yeah I have no complains. You can tell the company builds quality products. Some company’s only care about profit margins. Not razer.

rating 5.0. Amazing Mouse! I've had this mouse for just over a year now and I've been waiting to review it until I had been using it for a while because I reviewed my last mouse a week after I got it and then it died a month later...

The ergonomics are grea,t it is a little slick but they sell third-party grip tape that you can put on it and it makes it so nice! Battery life is amazing! I will use my computer for 30 hours straight when I have a project due and I have never had the mouse die on me. Bought a third party magnetic charging dock that works perfect with this. It's even rgb!

I play FPS, MOBA, and RPG games I've never had a problem in any of them. The super high polling rate is very nice! Had a co-worker come over who is also into PC gaming and the very first thing he said was "Your mouse is so smooth!" (Also helps to have a high refresh rate monitor)

Tracking on different surface also works really well. I've used it on wooden table I used it on my pants when I'm not at my desk, or on my couch when I'm sitting on that. Also worked on my carbon fiber wrapped desk. Does not work on glass but neither does any other mouse because they need texture to track on.

Overall 10 out of 10. very good design very good battery very good performance!

rating 2.0. Bluetooth stops working after 1 month I was hesitated to buy razer product, because I bought a razer mouse couple years ago, and it was broken just after few months. I know the durability and cumbersome customer service are not the highlight of this company, however, I love it looks cool, and the fact it does have a good quality and performance.

So just after 1 month, the bluetooth stops working. When you switch the button to bluetooth mode, nothing happens, and the light just won't turn on. After spending hours debugging remotely with the customer service, then eventually admit it's hardware issue. So they offered to replace it. But they want me to ship it to them first, otherwise, I have to pay first as a deposit, if they don't receive the faulty device in 14 days, they will not return the money....

Well, I understand why they do it, but if you compare it with Logitech, it's quite cheap and inconvenient.

Anyway, I love the product, but the durability is just really bad... I would say go with a larger company makes life much easier.

rating 1.0. [Razer DeathAdder v2 Pro Wireless] Multiple bugs, disconnects, poor battery life, etc. I would think paying over $100 for a mouse would get me the top of the line. If these was some random cheap mouse, I'd understand. However, after using this for 2 weeks now, playing with all the software, debugging, reading forum posts about it, I can only assume this product is still in beta.

You have to download the synapse software which monitors your mouse settings. Great, but it doesn't work like it's supposed to due to buggyness. Here's the kicker though, repairing and/or reinstalling Synapse will result in random bugs - let me explain. Bug #1 may be present so you go to reinstall and it fixes the issue - but now bug #2 and #3 are present. I've never experienced software that does this, it's baffling.

So far, the bugs I've found were:

-[Maybe not a bug] For the mouse to even be recognized, you have to plug it into a usb using a wired connection first. I'm not sure if this is intended.

-Synapse reports X% battery life but clicking the mouse to go deeper into the settings shows a completely different battery life percentage. Not just a percent or two off, logging into synapse showed my mouse had for example, 90% battery life but when I click the mouse to change its settings, it would display 0% battery remaining.

-Synapse would think it's a completely new mouse depending on the USB port it's plugged into. This would trigger a "reinstall of required software" and a restart of the computer even though you have everything you need.

-Disconnects several times a day. Every disconnect I experienced was quick, mostly 1-3 seconds and then it would reconnect.

-When switching between DPIs, in the right corner, there is supposed to be a popup that displays what you switched to. Instead, this rarely ever displays and behavior only gets more weird if you plug the mouse into different USB ports. Reinstalling Synapse made it work for a few hours but then it went back to being buggy.

-Battery life, with RGB off and using "ultra fast mode" is more so 25-30 hours than their advertised 70 hours.

I know Razer will likely comment below with a copy paste to contact their support and yet I pretty much have. I've went through many of the help forums, read the responses from Razer, applied their suggestions, and nothing fixes it. Secondly, it's clear to me that most of the issues is in regards to software, not the mouse itself.

“Please understand that the main reason we cannot honor your request is that doing so would not be fair to all of our customers who purchased a Razer Care Plan who covers customer induced damage cases.”

rating 1.0. Irreplaceable rubber padding that wears off in just a few months - Don't waste your money! tl;dr - Deathadder is great, but the Pro is extremely susceptible to wear & tear; go buy the "V2 X Hyperspeed" version instead (or just avoid the ones with rubber pads\sides).

rating 4.0. Deathadder v2 review from a longtime Deathadder user I've owned several iterations of the Deathadder before buying the v2 (Pro), and I'm coming most recently from the Elite. For other people like me who are familiar with Razer's lineup and thinking about the v2, I just want to point out some differences that stood out to me about their newest model:
- Loud: Clicking is EXTREMELY loud compared to the Elite. It also has a hollow 'pop' tone compared to the Elite's more solid and consistent tone. I'm not sure if the sound will get quieter over time, but even if it does I doubt it will ever be nearly as quiet as the Elite. The side-buttons are a bit louder than the Elite as well
- Shorter and fatter cable: I'm planning to use the v2 Pro mostly wired, and the new cable is a few feet shorter, plus it has a sheath that makes it look much thicker than the Elite'
- Better side-grips: In the v2, the entire side-panel has a textured grip, rather than just the lower half on the Elite
- Lighter: Even with the wireless tech, the v2 is lighter than the Elite. I used to have a Mamba as my travel-mouse which was significantly heavier than the Elite. It's great to have a travel-mouse that doesn't have a lot more weight to account for the battery
- Better scroll wheel: The scroll wheel is actually quieter than the Elite, but feels mostly the same (maybe 10% smoother, but that could just be from wear-and-tear on my Elite)

rating 5.0. Great for former Deathadder users. Woke “Data privacy” people need to chill. This review is for the Deathadder V2 Pro Wireless.

The Value: 7/10
This mouse is more on the expensive side, but wow did it come in quick. One day shipping, impressive.

The Shape: 10/10
I loved the design of the first Deathadder, and it still reigns supreme. I have huge hands (easily can palm a professional basketball) and I’ve struggled with “esports mice,” notably the Glorious Model O Wireless. Button placement is great and it’s perfect for right-handed users.

The Buttons/Clicks: 9/10
The buttons and clicks feel a bit hollower compared to the Deathadder 2013. There’s just a slight more space needed to register a click. This is not a big issue and I’ve adapted fine. Response times are really quick with the new technology.

The Quality: 10/10
The mouse is really well-built. The rubberized sides feel good. It’s pretty much the Viper Ultimate specs with a different shell. It’s lighter than the Deathadder 2013 by 20 or so grams, so overall a great mouse.

The Software: 6/10
Here’s where everybody dumps their complaints. I know, the software requiring a login is dumb and invasive. It’s part of a higher-level product offering ploy. The fact that upon first plugging in the mouse, the computer pops up with an install for synapse and slows the computer down. I get it. My advice: setup an account, make your changes to RGB (which mine is off anyway), DPI, polling rate, etc., program multiple profiles using the onboard memory (in this case I have two DPI’s), then request to delete all of your user data through synapse. Delete account, uninstall synapse, then continue to live your life. You’ll be ok. If your worries about a gaming peripherals company “stealing and selling your data,” you’d be surprised of how much of your information you actually give on a daily basis just walking around in public with your Bluetooth on. Logitech has the same issue, so it’s rather hard to avoid.

Overall: 9/10
Tl;dr: Great ergo wireless mouse, bad software with easy workaround. Great for people who don’t like the ambidextrous esports gaming mouse shape.

Highly recommended.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Great mouse so far I've been using the DeathAdder for years and have loved it...with one exception. The rubber bumpers on the side, where your thumb and middle two fingers sit, have started to become unglued and have come off. After buying this mouse, I see that Razer has tried to fix that, by making the entire sides of the mouse rubberized. The mouse is incredibly fast using the 2.4 Ghz connection and doesn't seem any slower compared to my previous corded mouse. The only hiccup was downloading the latest version of Synapse to customize the mouse. I think some kind of conflict was happening with my old version and I had to reinstall everything and then install Synapse 3. After that, things were working great. Synapse is an amazing software that allows you to customize each button exactly how you want things. I also like that you can switch profiles through a button on the bottom of the mouse and don't have to do it through software. You can load up to five profiles onto the onboard memory. I'd been trying to game with an everyday Bluetooth mouse and the differences between that and this is incredible. The mouse is super responsive and the thumb buttons are in just the right places. rating 5.0. Forget about that expensive widescreen TV; this is what you want One of the most amazing moments I've had with the Rift occurred during the setup. From inside the headset, I could see the sensor "scanning" the 3D environment. This strange confluence of the real world and the virtual one was unnerving and exhilarating. From there, you move to the home screen, which is actually a large, modern mansion complete with a crackling fire whose sound comes through one earphone or the other depending on which way you're facing. It's an incredible orientation, but keep in mind one thing: make sure you're in a cool room. The perspiration from your forehead can tend to fog the lenses making what should be a vivid experience, a frustrating one. One other quibble is the Xbox remote, which was very finicky in pairing and made my first few minutes with the Rift pretty frustrating. Be safe and use the extender and plug it into the USB ports that go directly to the motherboard. The ports on the front of my computer case were not working for some reason.

I am just a couple of days into using the Rift, but already love it. One of the best things it does for gaming is free you from the limitations of screen boundaries. Games like Tomb Raider have allowed you to explore a 3D world for decades, but there is nothing like seeing games through the Rift. For example, in Lucky's Tale, the excellent platform game that comes with the headset, you might at some point fall from a tall perch. It's one thing to have the camera track the character, but it's another thing to instinctively look down and navigate to safety during the fall. Exploration becomes incredibly intuitive with your eyes and head serving as the mouse cursor for selecting apps/games. EVE: Valkyrie is another great use of VR. In the dog fighting game, you can have your head on a swivel and use your point of focus as the crosshairs as you blast competitors.

I built my computer from scratch with the goal of using it for VR. I'm using [[ASIN:B01GXOX2BU MSI GAMING GeForce GTX 1070 8GB GDDR5 DirectX 12 VR Ready (GeForce GTX 1070 ARMOR 8G OC)]] along with an unlocked, but not yet overclocked [[ASIN:B012M8LXQW Intel Core i7 6700K 4.00 GHz Unlocked Quad Core Skylake Desktop Processor, Socket LGA 1151 [BX80662I76700K]]] and it seems to be doing well with pretty much everything I throw at it. The graphics are good and clear, but definitely not at 1080p quality. I do look forward to the days when displays and VR headsets are on par with each other, but for most games, the Rift will serve you nicely. The feeling of presence and watching a T-Rex amble toward you is just incredible and something you can't really appreciate until you take the plunge.

Will the headsets of five years from now be better? Most definitely, but if you've been waiting long enough, take the plunge.

rating 4.0. Bugs and Glitches aside... The other reviews aren't wrong. There are tons of bugs and glitches in the game. The graphics could be a lot better, and the open world is almost unnecessary as you're really just driving from point A to B, doing a mission, and then leaving the zone to complete it.  Having said all that, I am actually thoroughly enjoying this game.  The gameplay is great. It feels awesome to stake out an outpost with your drone, tag all the enemies you can see, and then systematically pick them off with your rifle from a distance.  The story is just interesting enough to keep me entertained as well.  Honestly, though, when it comes down to it, I'm a gamer that loves checking boxes via getting collectibles, clearing outposts, and checking off targets from a most wanted list.  If that's what you like, this game does the job.  If not and you want more of the Far Cry 3 or 4 experience, then look elsewhere.  This is definitely not a AAA title, but they did the best with what they had and I am enjoying it.

rating 1.0. 1 - 5 Stars for Game; 5 stars for Amazon Prime 0-1 star for game developers testing.
2-3 stars for graphics, music, dialog
4-5 stars for sniper rifle mechanics/upgrades/drone.
5 stars for Amazon Prime and their return practices.

1. You will not be able to complete the game due to the processor freezing. I made it into Act 1 (of 4) where you steal a truck filled with wine and attempt to drive it to your next action; the processor froze multiple times thereafter preventing further progress in the game. I was at 20% of the game's completion. (see 5 below).

2-3 stars; an initial load time of 1 hr + is required to get the game running due to a huge number of patches required (as of 4/27/17). After that, it requires a minimum of 4-5 minutes to start the game each time you power up. You cannot manually save a checkpoint so you are at the developers' discretion as to how far back you have to go to replay after a death. Graphics are well done; details and traversing them with vehicles and on foot are better than average, however any changes you make to the controller options don't work on initial play thru (i.e.. playing from a walking world into driving an automobile world -- inverted y axis does not work). You must stop and restart the game in order for the reset options to work.

4-5 Stars. The game is a true sniper game. It is comparable to Sniper Elite 4 except with less melee capability. There are multiple sniper weapons
(12 or so as I recall) to earn and upgrade (scopes, magazines, skins, etc.) and the mechanics for distance, windage, breathing are realistic. And the Drone (new to me) is superb. My lack of progress (20%) left me hungering for more drone capabilities. It is used to tag enemies and survey the landscape from approx. 3-40m before you enter action areas -- why don't more games have this ability? Awesome.

5 Stars. Goes to Amazon Prime and their return policy/capabilities. After reaching the point of absolute frustration with the processor freezing I chose to return the game. I quickly printed up a return address (with a return code) to the Fulfillment division in Ky ( an option of the original order), inserted the game in a bubble wrapper, drove it to a near Amazon Locker, used the return code to open the locker and wham-O; the next morning, I received the refund with a message on my iPhone in that regard.

rating 1.0. If you love disappointment, this game is for you!!! If youre thinking of buying this - dont. I waited until completing the game before doing this, but that ending just adds to how bad this game is. I played the first 2 games, and they were horrible, but figured they miiigghhtt have gotten there stuff together to make a decent title. No. Just no.

So, the game is called SNIPER. Most of us think of sniping as long distance engagements. The amount of times I was able to shoot an enemy at 400+ meters I could count on 1 hand. Let me put this even better for you. Getting a kill, with a sniper over 200m in distance, is so rare, that they give you a +50xp everytime you get one!!!! Thats how little it happens!!! Oh, oh, and the few times you find a decent spot, to put enough distance between yourself, and the enemy, they de-render. Again, 400+, is very very rare.

The mechanics are stupid. Why does a supressor have a durability? You need to repair it with a special tool, that you better have on you! And that takes up a gadget slot!

The story is a jumble mess of drunken script writing, and "wtf were you thinking". It freezes once every hour, and autosave is anything but, because when your game freezes in the middle of a mission, you have to start all over from the safehouse!

The loading time, is also the most ridiculous thing in existance. I timed it once, 12 minutes, and 17 seconds. To load a map that is the size of a beta game. With hardly any NPCs. You will never see another car on the road.

Oh! And the road! You could be driving on dirt, snow, or grass, and still have the sound effect of screeching tires on pavement while driving! Who doesnt want that!?

I wanted to like this game. I really, really did. I gave it all the way until the end. And it isnt even one of those games where its good, only because its so bad. Its just bad. If this game is 5$ - dont buy it. It isnt worth it. I may even be willing to PAY GAMESTOP to let me trade it in. At this point, I would rather shop for watermelons in a radioshack, then play this game again.

That is all.

rating 4.0. B-movie levels of action and quality I have a very soft spot for B-movies. I'm also a fan of precision shooting in general.

Those two reasons above give me some crazy bias towards this game, so take this review with a grain of salt.

Sniper: Ghost Warrior has been around for a while now as a solid choice for people looking for a realistic sniping experience. The first game in the series came out in 2011 with a sequel being released in 2013. These games have a certain reputation in gaming circles. They're not the best games, in fact being pretty mediocre most of the time. However, they're budget titles that do one particular thing very well (the sniping mechanics), with everything else being satisfactory or outright sub par.

That being said, these games have found an audience because the sniping mechanics are unrivaled (except for maybe Sniper Elite), and they're charming in the way a low budget action movie can be. It's hokey, cheesy, and sometimes dumb, but there's no denying the fun factor.

Sniper: Ghost Warrior 3 expands upon the premise of the first two titles by introducing an open-world format that mimics Far Cry to a tee. The setting is rather under explored in gaming, so that's a solid addition to the plus column. The game takes place in the Eastern European country of Georgia, and the world is all dense forests with foreboding mountains, and some snowy areas. In this area, the graphics do leave a bit to be desired. If you're looking for extremely detailed textures, and polished visuals, look elsewhere. The game often looks a bit muddy, and if you get to close to the vegetation you'll notice how cheap some of the animations look. That being said, there are moments when the game looks like a proper AAA big-budget action game.

Just like Far Cry, you travel around the different regions via vehicles or fast travel points. All in all the map is easy to navigate and the numerous "Points of Interests" that are scattered throughout the map all offer unique mini-moments where you're investigating a crashed car, or saving civilian hostages, and other similar activities.

Perhaps my favorite mechanic aside from the Sniping is the ability to use a remote-controlled drone to investigate the surrounding areas before executing a plan. This mechanic is similar to using the tamed Hawk in Far Cry Primal, but controlling the drone is much more precise than controlling the hawk. The drone can also be modified with an number of abilities (passive and active) that enhance it's battery life, or enables a night vision mode, or allow you to make a noise to lure enemies, etc. Using the drone to scout ahead for enemies or vantage points is a fun and engaging experience that enhances the game play in a tangible manner.

As a final positive subject, the weapons that are available for purchase are pretty great. There is about 15 sniper rifles available, about 10-12 assault rifles/shotguns/bows, and about 10-12 pistols. Each weapon has different attachments, and so far I've been impressed with the variety available.

As a gamer with a full-time job, I don't really care too much about the quality of the story anymore. While there are certain games that I purchase specifically for the story, I purchased Sniper: Ghost Warrior 3 because I like sniping. However, to my surprise, the story has been entertaining so far. Hokey, predictable, and kind of simplistic, but still entertaining enough for me to want to listen to briefings and pay attention during cut scenes. The basics are this: you're a kick-ass american military operative (marine, I think) who's searching for his lost brother (also a soldier), while fighting alongside a rebel force. Interesting enough.

The biggest con for this game are going to be the loading times. Every time you start a new game, or switch to a new region the game will initiate a 5-minute load time. It's annoying, but at least once you're loaded in there are virtually no load times.

Other than that, the game just feels like it could have benefited from some more development time. The fact that you can only purchase the game with the season pass attached is a bit of a double-edged sword. On the one hand, it's great to be able to count on more content for the game throughout 2017. On the other hand, there is no multiplayer available yet, and it feels like you're paying full price for a half-done game with the promise of the rest of the game coming out throughout the year.

So who's this game for? Do you like Jean-Claude Van Damme movies? Were you aware that there is a series of 6 movies called Sniper with 5 of them being direct-to-dvd releases? Do you perchance own those movies? Then give this game a chance. It's not ground-breaking, it's not life-changing, nor is it a masterpiece. This is a fun, budget title that will appeal to a certain audience.

rating 1.0. Waste of money. Let me state right up front! I am playing this game on a standard PS4, and it does state that the game is PS4 Pro enhanced.

The game looks HORRIBLE! It's the worst looking game I have played on a PS4...It's a joke! Horrible graphics, poor modeling bad animations. Everything about its appearance is just plain bad! If it was JUST the graphics, I could overlook that! I mean, I still play and enjoy PS3 and PS2 games! How does it get worse? Then the game is insanely laggy! Often times, especially when driving the game will freeze, jump and jerk! It's impossible to stay on the road, because of the freeze frame moments! One minute I'm cruising down a road, then the next I'm smashed right into a tree because the game froze for a minute!

Then there's the fact that you might as well not even use your suppressor. Even with it on, one shot and every enemy in the map zeros in on you and comes charging...it's just stupid!

Overall. the game seems like a buggy budget game that should NOT have been released yet! Save your money and buy Ghost Recon Wildlands, or Sniper Elite 4! This game is just a waste of your money!

rating 5.0. Impressive As a multi-platform game enthusiast I can say I am really impressed with the visual fidelity that this new machine provides. I'm using it in conjunction with a Sony Bravia 43 inch 4K HDR TV and the visuals this device delivers are fantastic. rating 5.0. Perfect for 4k home theaters Awesome console for high quality home theaters, but with huge updates to download. I have all the consoles, used to be a PC game, and actually already owned a XBOX ONE. I purchased this as an entry into 4k. My primary gaming setup is a basement home theater a 1080 projector, 4k projectors are still way to expensive, but I was willing to buy a 4k TV for the living room. I purchased the XB1X console in conjunction with a 65 inch Sony 4k HDR capable television. This console is also plugged into a high quality receiver and speaker system. I have 6 games that are enhanced for the console and they are really amazing in action. You can't appreciate the improvement until you see it live in 4k with HDR, any online video in 1080 is not going to do it justice.. Forza 7 and Rise of the Tomb Raider are stand outs. There is a very big difference between streaming 4k signals via netflix or youtube and a native 4k game or 4k blu ray disc. Until this console was available I did not see a good reason to take the plunge. If you want a next level experience and have the cash to burn it is a fun ride once the games are installed.

This console exists side by side with the XB1 which is designed to run games in regular HD, so whenever you install a new game it has to download all the improved 4k textures and assets from the internet. This can be significant, for example in the case of Forza 7, the update was 62GB. Gone are the days when you can insert a game and just play, every one of the enhanced games I have played required a pretty significant update. My internet speed is 100Mbs, some of these updates basically needed most of the a night to download. The downloads will continue when your console is on stand by, so you pop the game in, get the download going and walk away. You can't just pop a disc in and play. This may not always be the case, it is possible future releases could include the 4k assets on disc, but for now it is the reality.

I am curious to see how this console evolves in the future. During the run up to release a lot was made of its VR capabilities, but MS has gone silent on that. I don't know if there is a pending announcement, but it is something I will like to try out, so hopefully it comes back. There is also an opportunity to push this console beyond what could be supported by the regular XB1 console, so I am not sure how that will play out. Will there be a new XB console released when the Playstation 5 comes out? There are certainly some open questions regarding the future. In the meantime if you want the best experience outside of a custom PC gaming rig, the XB1X is the best available option right now. If you have a great display and sound system, you finally have a simple device that can pump native 4k content through your system.

rating 2.0. Sign-In/Xbox Live Connection Issues and Fix Had so many issues from the start. Would not always sign you in or would not connect to Xbox live. My original Xbox One worked fine. This one was hooked directly to router. Spoke with Microsoft techs and performed all their steps to no avail. Very disappointing. Getting new one sent out. Hopefully next one will be better.
=====================================================

UPDATE.......

Replacement Xbox One X arrived and exact same issues. Finally found a way to fix it "sort of". Turn the auto-sign in option off. Including the controller sign in option. While you have to click the Xbox button and sign in each time it does not give any issues. As stated earlier I had non-stop issues with it not signing in my account or Xbox live even though I had internet connected. Since I disabled that I have had no further issues. If you do another thing to do as well is when you shut it off go to your settings and select the option to completely shut down. I have not had to do that but its an option if you have more issues than me. I hope Microsoft fixes this glitch soon, with the cost of this system it is rather frustrating. One other thing, this may have been a fluke but wanted to mention just in case. During a game I did get disconnect from my account mid-game. Only happened once and signed back in right away but just wanted to put that out there for everyone. Be sure to vote helpful if this helped you. I'll keep updating if anything else comes up.

rating 4.0. Better than a steering wheel stand I upgraded to this from one of the steering wheel table/stands.  After doing a lot of research, I went with this.  It's fairly easy to assemble and is sturdy.  The seat is comfortable, although I regret not getting the adjustable one.  If I did it all over again, I would have spent a little more for the adjustable one.  This one does not recline.  The instructions don't show you how you should fold this up either.  You'll just have to figure it out.  Another thing to consider is the adjustability.  I am 6ft tall and the pedals are adjusted all the way to the front.  If you are taller than that, it may be a little cramped.

rating 4.0. Great for the money I received it today and it was double boxed which is awesome. I opened everything up and found everything well packaged and the instructions are very clear on reeeaaalllyyy thick paper. It only took me about 30 minutes to put together and that's only bc a few annoyances popped up. First, some of the end caps in the tubes were stuck and I had to cut them off. Some of the holes were a bit hard to get aligned and getting the hubs figured out took a bit of time. Most of it was smooth and a lot of the struggle was just random I think and not anything against the manufacturer. Once together with my wheel and pedals attached the first thing I noticed was how comfy the seat is. I sat down and figured out what needed to move and it only took about 5 minutes to get a good seating position. There's obviously gonna be a bit of play given it's foldable but the amount of play is minimal and isn't even noticed when playing. The pedals I hard mounted and had zero play even under hard braking. Overall it's great and worth the asking price. I had some questions about accessories and their customer service got back to me same day so that's something too. I definitely don't regret getting this instead of the play seat

rating 5.0. This thing is the truth Totally worth the price. I needed something with its own seat and WOW what a difference this makes. I can race for hours due to this rig being very comfortable. rating 3.0. Good seat but could be better. So first thing's first, this makes for a good gaming rig and so far I've enjoyed using it with my Logitech G920 and shifter but it's not without issue.

There are a number of qualms I have with this product though ones that I hope others have not encountered regarding poor quality control:

1. The legs on the back of the seat were about 5 mm wider on each side and did not fit properly into the pegs had to use some WD40 and brute force to make them fit.

2. Holes for screws did not line up properly. Had to use a file to widen some of the holes so that screws would go in.

3. The gear shifter platform will not screw in with the shifter attached to it. The part of the body of the shifter abuts the ratcheting mechanism on the seat and doesn't allow for the platform to fully be inserted into the seat. So will have to drill a hole so that the screw can be put in.

4. While the steering wheel swinging out is a cool feature there should be a way to swap which way the wheel swings out. Since the wheel swings out to the left, it becomes an issues when the shifter is mounted on the right as the wire that connects the shifter to the steering wheel blocks exit from the seat. Obviously not an issue when the shifter if mounted on the left hand side.

rating 1.0. Piece of crap! I know other people may get good quality product but the one I got has defect. there is no way to put together the back of the chair. simply a piece of crap. waste me a lot of time to try to assemble it and put it back to the original box.

rating 5.0. great chair for race sim....However Edit: I have been in touch with Next Level Racing's customer service and the we VERY quick to make thing right so I have changed my rating to 5 stars!

rating 3.0. Not easy to build This is not easy to build way to tight with metal bars. More than a one person job. When its together its not as firm as I thought but its comfortable and stable. Its great quality fabric. If you get it make sure to get a mallet or some help or even both.

rating 5.0. Perfect for tall and injured people I'm 6'4, 215ish and have had back surgery. I had a previous Next Level Racing wheel stand but it was extremely heavy and built more for shorter people or kids. This one has the built in chair which is super adjustable. I made the back end as high as possible and then raised the front end up almost to the highest point so that I could lean back but still have a great angle to the monitor. I'm not sitting straight up which greatly helps my back. The wheel is nicely positioned right in front of me and is plenty adjustable to get the right height. My shoulders don't hurt like my last stand because the wheel isn't super high up. The shifter can be mounted on either side and is in just the perfect position, and is also adjustable. The chair is comfortable enough, though I will experiment with cushions and pillows only because of my back injuries but I still love it. The build quality is amazing. I am so impressed with how it all comes together. It is all rock solid too, there is no play with the shifter and the wheel doesn't wobble about. Granted, I have an old G27 which isn't the strongest wheel on the market but I have zero complaints. Even at 6'4 the pedals are at a perfect distance as far back as they can go and if I truly needed to go further, I could tweak things further using less bolts or just place the pedals on the floor directly. I spent a few hours assembling everything and tweaking it all to my exact positions and it all folds up and puts away nicely despite not ruining my adjusted positions. Also, it isn't super heavy like my last stand and doesn't require its own chair since this one is built in. This is the single best purchase I have made for sim racing since the wheel itself.

rating 5.0. Great purchase for the price! I bought this for my brother as he is getting into sim racing. He was able to attach his wheels and pedals with ease. Looks fairly sturdy for foldable cockpit. Happy with my purchase and I would recommend it

rating 1.0. Devestated Literally set it up. Put the wheel on. Sat in it then the whole thing collapsed and ripped my 4000$ pc off my desk. Now my pc wont turn on since my $1200 gaming motherboard is smashed in. Im 180 lbs soaking wet btw so yea...Awesome. Can't get in contact with Amazon. Item definitely was NOT new as advertised.

rating 5.0. Surprisingly solid I'm a pretty big guy. 6' 4" and well over the "recommended" max weight this lists, but i still took a gamble on it because short of spending almost 3 times as much on something else, this was as close to a budget cockpit as i could find.

And it works. I baaarely fit, but I do fit. And i don't feel even the slightest issue with stability. If it can handle me, then you won't have any problem if you are within the recommended weight range.

This seems counter intuitive but I had to add a seat pad, which not only makes it a little more comfortable for me, but it actually somehow gives me more room so the wheel isn't literally in my stomach (mounting plate as far forward as it can go).

the lock system for the swing-out wheel mount does dig into my right thigh a bit, but i'll get some padding for that. It's just too much fun.

rating 5.0. Solid rig with seat Overall it is a great rig. The seat is comfortable and everything (wheel, pedals, seat) is fairly adjustable. The one adjustment that made the most difference for me was the front legs of the seat. At first the wheel base was really close to my knees and I would hit the one adjustment knob sometimes. Moving the front legs up was a big improvement.

Assembly was OK but a little tricky, maybe took 90 minutes. Also the Logitech H pattern shifter (for a G29, etc.) does not quite bolt on fully but can be tightened down enough to work without moving around.

rating 3.0. Very Durable BUT hard to fold. This cockpit is very durable, only a few wobbling but can take a lot of banging and moving when folding with its sturdy metal tubing. I just don't like folding this setup it takes 5 or more minutes if you don't figure out how to fold it the first few times. Its really annoying to fold (plus its really heavy).

rating 5.0. Awesome folding cockpit This is actually pretty awesome. Its strong and holds everything great and once you know how you should fold it up it's super easy to hide away in a closet plus if you have velcro strips the chair itself makes wire management pretty easy

rating 5.0. My husband loves it My husband loves this thing(I think more then me lol) it's pretty sturdy and gets the job done. It's also easy to assemble my husband was pretty tipsy and still manage to get it together but it doesn't fold very compact which isn't an issue for us.
rating 2.0. A $250 folding chair... Way overpriced for a net seat folding chair.

rating 4.0. Probably the best option for a low budget Cockpit Overall i'm pretty happy with this "cockpit"  I Just recently picked up a Logitech G29 to play Gran Turismo and a few other racing games with my kids.  I was trying to stay away from the whole cockpit thing and dumping a ton of money into this but trying to play at my desk just wasnt working.  After a lot of research and reviews I decided to give the GT Lite a shot.  It is way more sturdy than I thought it was going to be.  I am 6'2 275lbs and I fit in it comfortably for the most part.  I ended up adding a headpillow and lumbar pillow that came from a gaming chair I no longer use and it helped a lot.  I really liked the fact that it folded up which was another reason I chose this one.  I have a nice spot in our game room that I can put this in when not in use.  I've only experienced 2 negatives thus far, 1 being a bigger guy my right thigh digs into the bars a bit (which im assuming will be easily fixed if I get some foam or something to pad the bar with) and 2 it can be a little hard to get up out of the chair.  This could be the way I have it set up.  I have it at the default setup so it sits a little low to the ground and I dont want to step on the brackets that hold the pedals so it can be difficult to get up after playing for a while.  Im also worried to put too much pressure on the frame or chair as it is nylon/mesh and im worried it will rip.  BOTH negatives are more than likely going to be individual preference and not a negative in general, just my only issues with it.  Otherwise, VERY happy with it.

rating 4.0. Good but need some adjustment Recently got into sim racing with steering wheel (grew up with gran turismo in late 90s with controller) so decide to buy a simple cockpit, did tons of research and youtube and felt gt lite has what i need.

- I chose Gt lite over other hardcore cockpits for its versatility & easy storage.
- I chose Gt lite over Fgt because i do not need the extra F1 stance with pricier cost.
- i chose Gt lite over Playseat challenge because the foot paddles are bolt-on mounted vs strap mount on playseat, and the ability to raise height if needed for gt lite without diy. gt comes with shifter mount vs buying extra from different country for playseat.

After sitting for 2hrs, it definitely felt way better than using steering wheel mounted on ikea desk, but here are some discomfort i encountered:

- circled in red, that part touches top of my head/scalp which is a strange feeling , i then need to sit lower to prevent that to happen.
- green arrow, because i sat lower , for long period of time my back aches, it isnt designed ergonomic due to mash ventilation, i just purchased a set of neck and back support see if that helps.
- circled in orange, gt version took away the head adjuster from fgt version, its now a bolt-on which is a thumbdown imo.
- circled in blue, needs to use the same easy slider pin mechanism as the front , i just left it at highest setting and would never touch again because its already kind of low anyway.
- circled in yellow, that parts needs more cushion, my bottom thigh can feel the bar thats inside.

Im not sure if the seat i got is a “revision” or not, because the rear bolt-on mount of the seat and shifter mount were very difficult to fit, i had to use rubber mallet and hammer them down couple times (with good amount of strength too) i think only Dwayne johnson & john cena can install it with their bare hands😄. But because of the tightness, it improved rigidity & stability , my shifter plate does not wiggle or rattle like some of the youtube video mentioned, it sits super solid like if its wielded on.
I havnt set my ffb to highest to see how well the cockpit can handle, but so far with my usage its very sturdy , good materials.

So in conclusion, although it needs some adjustments for myself, but i definitely dont want my review to scare away future potential buyers, as everyone’s comfort zone is designed different. Its a good seat and I hope next level racing can improve this product even further.

rating 1.0. VERY DISAPPOINTED Too big and bulky.  Poor design.  Not made for anyone over another 5’-8”.  Hard to fold up and does not fold up into a small enough bundle to conveniently store anywhere.  VERI DISAPPOINTED.  Would gladly return this for a 50% refund.
rating 5.0. Takes seconds off your times Love this chair. I’m sure most people have their setup on a desk like I did, but this chair is a game changer

rating 1.0. Not quite as expected So first of all, this thing isn’t very universal. I have a logic tech g27 with the shifter pedals and wheel, and the wheel and shifter don’t screw onto their spots. rating 4.0. Take time to get setup in comfortable position Good product with one issue I will mention later.  It took about an hour to assemble and if you have a second set of hands around the house get them to help and save some frustration.  It took a little bit to learn how the locking mech works, but once understood it is a genius idea.  I would recommend the F-GT system as it has more flexibility in the setup of the chair over the fixed system the GT Lite has.  The issue I am having is the mounting plate for the steering wheel comes loose in mid game when driving on a road course.  After about 45 minutes the wheel all of a sudden drops into my lap running the run I’m on.  Not sure if this a common issue or I got a faulty knob.

rating 3.0. It’s a little overpriced The good the bad and the ugly

The good: cheap, compact, fairly easy to assemble albeit the instructions are trash, fits a 6 foot 200 lb person easily.

The bad: the seat is stiff as a board, it’s like sitting on a beach chair, adjusting is a pain, instructions are written poorly, it doesn’t fold easy at all with your wheel attached. The ergonomics are awful so you will get worn out after a few races compared to nicer seats.

The ugly: it’s overpriced for what it is. Just spend the extra money. It’s $100+ less at competitors sites occasionally so shop around. The craftsmanship has room for improvement (parts are bent and it doesn’t feel sturdy).

Overall I kept it because it’s too much of a pain to box it up and return it. I’ve raced a few times and get pretty uncomfortable after a few races.

rating 2.0. Fitment is not the greatest I am giving this a two because of quality control.  The steering part of mine came in crooked.  It's enough where i notice but not enough to return it.  I can just make my steering wheel straight if i don't bolt it down.  I don't know how this got past quality control. rating 4.0. A decent cock pit for the price. Pros: wheel is stable enough to not notice it not being stable in VR, if I wasn’t in VR it would be noticeably unstable.
thrust master wheel, shifter, pedals, and handbrake all fit nicely in predrilled holes.
Seat is comfortable enough to be on for a couple hours.
Decently easy assembly
Folds for transportation unlike a stationary cock pit.

Cons:
Pedals aren’t far enough away to be comfortable for me who is 6’ tall
Wheel would feel not very stable not in VR
Seat is only comfortable enough for a couple hours anything past that and my butt is numb
REALLY hard to get in the cock pit if you can’t open the wheel base.

In conclusion: it’s a decent rig for the price, if it were me again in the past, I would have saved for a nicer cock pit.
Good for those who move often or don’t have space for a stationary cock pit.

rating 5.0. Good set up! I've been getting into simracing and was looking for a price-friendly solution. Saw a few other options and based on the reviews I went with the GT Lite. Easy to put together and it's working well with my Thrustmaster gear. I'm happy with my purchase

rating 5.0. Great budget chair with a few flaws... This is a great chair for the price it’s big enough to accommodate my average built frame and then some. First let’s start with the assembly it’s pretty straightforward once you figure it out since the instructions it comes with arent exactly clear on some things the support bar that goes on the back took some extra muscle to get into position as they didn’t line up properly you’ll have to insert each side about a quarter inch at a time to keep it from getting stuck on one side it’s a bit annoying but once you set it to the right hight you won’t have to mess with it ever again. The part that the pedals sit in isnt 100% stable but maybe that’s cause I set it up on carpet but once you start playing you hardly notice it. The bar that holds the steering wheel that swings open you have to hold it in place and push down when clamping in to play or els the wheel will be unleveled. Overall it took about 20-30 minutes to assemble but that’s cause I had to take it apart and fix the order of the cog pieces cause for some reason mine came out of order but it was a fairly easy fix. I do have a few complaints one being the seat is in a fixed position with not much room for adjustment so if your a person who likes to drive leaning back even a bit this will be a bit uncomfortable, the stability strap on the left hand side can only be secured properly when the wheel is in a certain position so if you try to bring the wheel closer you can but the strap won’t hold it in place as the velcro isn’t long enough so you either play with a closer wheel and less stability on the rig or you play with the “recommended” hight and tighten the strap tho I found the recommended hight to be about the right position for an average adult but I can see this maybe being a problem for kids if you plan on getting it for a child. Final thoughts I would definitely recommend this if you want a budget rig or something that can be portable and folded out of the way. This goes without saying but this is just the chair no wheel or pedals included it clearly says that in the description and in the product photos but I still see a few negative reviews complaining it’s only the chair that’s not the fault of the product tho it’s theirs for not reading the description. I bought this chair to fit my thrustmaster tmx pro it was exactly what I needed.
rating 4.0. It is much sturdier than it seems We bought it for Forza we live in a small apartment. It is a great space saver. Easy instructions as well. Plus extra parts if need it

rating 1.0. 3rd party seller- buyer beware First thing I notice were the frame ends poking through the box, then opened instructions (which are extremely detailed) and notice there should have been labeled bags, A,B,C, etc. Nope, just one bag full of loose nuts and bolts of varying sizes so I had to use educated guesses as to what went where. Watched several YouTube videos on setup, should have been easy peasy. Whole mess of things I had to change around, the locking mechanism mainly. 2 hours later and I have things where they are supposed to be, only to realize the wheel mount frame is bent and the latch was broken. This was obviously a return and seller just reboxed and sent out. I’ve heard nothing but good things about this sim chair, the 3rd party seller seems like the POS
*Having replacement sent so I will update if the second one is any better.

rating 5.0. Fantastic, tons of fun! A bit cumbersome to assemble due to the foldable design but not a big deal. Had it assembled in less than 30 minutes with no issues. I am 6'1" and had the pedals pushed all the way forward on the mounts. I would have liked another 2 or so inches to move them forward but not a huge deal.

Overall completely satisfied and well worth the money!

rating 1.0. didn't even a last a month, package came with dents and holes I was SUPER excited to get this cockpit but when I took it out the box, the package was all dented. I dont know if I got a faulty product but it doesn't seem nearly as good the people show I've seen set up and use the product. needless to say I'm very disappointed.

rating 5.0. Solid first cockpit! This is the best folding racing chair, period. Super solid construction, no wobble when you're using it, it's easy to get in and out of, and is fairly customizable to fit players of many different sizes.

If you've got the Logitech shifter, it doesn't REALLY fit that well because the folding hub is in the way of the shifter mounting point. Some other shifters probably fit better, but I was able to mount my logitech with a single bolt and the clamps keep it in place. Not an issue, just seems like an oversight.

The hubs are really easy to use but they do make folding the chair a bit more difficult than I'd like. Next Level Racing offers a video instructional on how to fold the seat, but they don't fold it the same way as it's folded on the marketing, which is strange. It DOES fold up nearly flat with the wheel and pedals mounted, but it is awkward. Again, it's not a major issue because it does work, but its confusing that they would photograph it folded a certain way and then give instructions to fold it in a different way that makes it less compact.

Wire management is lacking, but I'm not sure there's much to do differently. I used small velcro straps.

TL;DR - this is a FOLDING chair so there are going to be compromises made. It will never be as good as a stationary setup, but this is the BEST product for anyone who doesn't have enough space to devote to a permanent solution. Compared to 'other' folding chairs, this is an amazing value.

Some suggestions for the manufacturer:
A lumbar pillow would make a great accessory, I find that I am much more comfortable with a pillow, this could just be a personal preference but the option of adding a matching pillow would've been great.
Would LOVE to have a nylon drawstring bag to slide over the seat when it is folded up. The included strap helps keep it from coming unfolded but a drawstring bag would be a cleaner way to store this when not in use.

current_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. Way nicer than the photos and makes game racing a blast! Given how much price gouging has been going on lately for gaming gear, I was pleasantly surprised the NLR GT Lite was back to a reasonable price. I was also very pleased on how well designed and manufactured this chair is.
PROS:
- Love the swing-out steering wheel mount for easy entry/exit.
- Easy to fold/stow even with the racing gear attached.
- Excellent packaging, double boxed!
- The chair fabric is like faux ultra-suede, super soft yet very strong.
- NLR gets extra points for a very high quality instruction manual with easy links to assembly videos that made the whole experience of setting this up a pleasure.
- Once set up with the G29 steering system, it was solid even during energetic racing!
- We added a little office-chair-padding to the seat bottom for comfort.
- Great, rapid response from the support team.
Cons:
- A few of the screw holes were super tight and took several attempts to get them to go through.
- I have a standard height PC desk and this chair (even with the highest settings) was a bit too low to the ground. I'm exploring some options with the very responsive support staff at Next Level Racing.

rating 3.0. Hit and a Miss! Old tech with poor components and overpriced! Really? Over 7 years since this was introduced with nary an improvement yet it’s being sold for almost $300. Besides a few little flashy metal accents on the pedals and wheel, it’s just cheap squeaky plastic! 3 days of use and both the brake and accl pedals squeak horribly!
Logitech has POOR support for this product with incorrect information about which software to use, old data sheets, and PDFs for the manual that are years out of date and crappy customer service!
Pros: Great wheel for many games as this has been out for years, so many games work with this wheel.
Force Feedback adds a lot to the gaming immersion and shifting/clutch options are nice to have.
Cons: Squeaky pedals on 3rd use - too many unsightly and tangled cables with a loose power connector - awful support - outdated manuals/software/guides - cheap plastic body and frame - gears that clatter and have a huge dead-zone in the mid-wheel section - brake pedal is way too stiff - OVER-PRICED FOR OLD TECH.
Sending this one BACK!

rating 4.0. More useful than I had though initially. Yes the RGB lighting is fun and cool if you already have other iCue products, yet it's more than just an USB port multiplier for charging or wireless headphones.
Pros:
1. heavy duty base that will not easily tip over.
2. the 2@ USB 3.1 ports not only charge, they are full data ports so we were able to plug our new top-end Dell gaming headset into the USB port and activate full control of the settings through Windows 11.
3. the headset jack also allows for 7.1 surround if your equipment allows for it. So we can have both headset types plugged into the same base and just tell the Windows Sound app which type we'd like to use for our applications/games/movies etc.
4. the RGB settings/styles are controlled via the free iCue app and coordinate with the whole iCue ecosystem so we can set the headset stand to reflect system settings status like CPU temp or much more (including just fun rainbow wave colors ;-)
5. Very well packaged/sealed with a heavy-duty USB to PC cord included.
Cons: 1. One Star OFF, because even iCue 4.x is still poorly written software. It's not intuitive, too complex for the simplest tasks, and seems like it was designed by a confused committee instead of seasoned programmers.
2. Not inexpensive, but I'm starting to think there's more value than I had expected initially.

rating 3.0. UPDATE: Did Work great with the Corsair Dark Core RGB Pro SE mouse like Magic, on the 32nd day of ownership this pad stopped responding to lighting commands from the fingerprint control area. Resetting/unplugging and more did NOT fix this. Seems it was just poorly made after all. Yet the rapid and helpful response from the manufacturer helped with the situation. I just wish these were better controlled for quality.
PRIOR:
I was hesitant reading some review that said it did not charge their Corsair mouse. It works fine!
TIPS: 1. This needs more power than a typical laptop USB port to run the lights and charging features. Plug it into a USB power source that has at least a 4.25-5.25v usb outlet like the type you find on a charging brick or the back of a desktop PC (see instructions).
2. Make sure you place the mouse in the correct orientation so that the charging RING illustrated in the mouse manual (online and in the box) lines up with the charging ring on the pad. Try a few positions and then you'll see the charging light on the mouse pulse indicating you're charging. YAY
I really like the colors and the easy to clean surface (soft fabric surfaces just don't stay very clean).

input: rating 4.0. Perfect Mouse So, it seems a lot of these reviews that say this mouse isn't that great are from people who: A.) Didn't research the differences between this and the g502, and B.) review it after 2 hours of use without letting some muscle memory build for using this mouse. First off, I love this mouse. I'm a competitive FPS gaming team captain and this mouse is everything I've wanted out of all the mice I've ever used. People seem to do a lot of complaining about the scroll wheel, saying it's not as nice as the g502's, but that is one of the main reasons i bought it. I do a lot of work with the middle mouse button, using the scroll to switch weapons and pressing it to toggle crouch. I didn't like the idea of a more slidey and weird wheel. This has an incremental wheel (clickitty) and that's exactly what i wanted as well. No accidental weapon switches and no pulling out a nade when i wanted my pistol. I use claw grip and the g402 is great for it. Also, someone in these reviews said the sniper button is out-of-reach for claw grippers but i have not found that to be the case. Every button is extremely easy to reach and click all while keeping them difficult to accidentally click. I also have had absolutely no issues with the supposedly "cheap" cord, as i think its mainly something people pick on when they WANT something to pick on about this mouse. My ONLY issue with this, and the reason I'm not giving 5 stars, is that Logitech Gaming Software REFUSES to install onto my computer (Gigabyte Z97 HD-3, i5-4590, GTX 960, 8GB RAM, 250GB SSD, 500GB HDD, Windows 7 Pro x64). No matter what i try, i can not get the software to install/open. However, I'm perfectly fine with the way the buttons are set up and the only real thing i would change in there is make the LEDs stop "breathing". All in all, aside from software issues, this is THE BEST mouse I have ever owned and serves me extremely well.
rating 5.0. Best mouse I have ever owned Best mouse I have ever owned. I've been playing competitive FPS for 6+ years and this mouse is the best. No question.

rating 5.0. Excellent Mouse! This is, hands-down, the best mouse I've ever used. Very responsive, and even though I still haven't set any custom keybinds or macros to the customizable mouse buttons, I love how well it works combined with the fact that I COULD customize the buttons. The construction is solid, and the materials it's made from are pleasant to the touch and don't feel in the slightest bit cheap.

After using some other gaming mice - in hindsight - the fact that the tops of the left and right mouse buttons are uniformly rounded convex through-and-through with no indentation for your index and middle/ring fingers feels a little odd and less-comfortable than it could be. Another cheapo Hong Kong special gaming mouse sold under the VicTsing name but produced by Easterntimes Tech I recently purchased is actually way more comfortable in the hand, despite it's much lower-quality construction and functionality and the weird hexagonal pattern on the side which isn't very pleasing to the touch. [[ASIN:B01MY8FDMQ VicTsing 2.4G Wireless Gaming Mouse, 5 Adjustable DPI Mice(4800, 2000, 1600, 1200, 800), 7 Button for Laptop Computer Macbook Gamers]]

The thumb wing could also be slightly larger, and the "sniper" button and G7/G8 buttons could be slightly closer to the back of the mouse, as you have to stretch your fingers forward to hit them. But, consequently, I've never accidentally pressed either one of the buttons, so it's a pro/con situation. If you have long fingers and use a claw-style grip, this is the PERFECT mouse for you. I just have short fat stubby fingers and I palm-grip the mouse.

The only other gripe I have about this mouse is that the cable isn't braided, which means it sometimes gets caught on my keyboard, and the USB plug head is absurdly long, which means if I were to accidentally pull on the mouse lead for some reason, the resulting fulcrum would have a higher potential to damage the mobo than if it were to have a shorter plug head.

5/5, would recommend.

There's a lot of room for improvement with this mouse, and I personally feel like it's a downgrade from my g400s. I'd recommend getting your hands on the dated mx518 over this one if you could find one for purchase.
