rating 5.0. Great FPS gaming mouse The G402 is perfect for CS:GO and Overwatch. I went from the MX518 to the G5 and now to the G402. I was trying to find something lightweight and first got the wireless G602, but it was still a little heavy with even 1 Lithium AA battery. So I ended up using the G602 for my work computer and got the G402 for my gaming PC. It was a great decision since I wanted something similar to the MX518 (favorite mouse of all time) and lightweight enough for FPS games. My Logitech G5 (also great) was still a little heavy even without the extra weights, but the G402 has the perfect weight to me. The sensor is very responsive and the shape is comfortable to use in long gaming sessions. I highly recommend the G402 as a good replacement to the MX518 or G5.

Update 10/18: After using the G402 for several weeks; my hand is starting to develop painful cramps. I'm unable rest my ring finger comfortable on the mouse like my old MX518 or G5 because of the small lip and I think is causing the hand cramps (I use palm grip). The body of the mouse is too narrow for my hands; however, if your hand is not that wide then you'll be perfectly fine with the shape of the mouse.

rating 5.0. Blown away by this value I originally bought this to use at work, to replace my cheapo office mouse. I like having the functionality of additional buttons, and comfort played a role as well. However, this arrived on a Friday so I figured I would test it out on some games. I played a game of DOTA 2 and 5-6 games of Overwatch with this mouse and it performed on par with, if not slightly better than my 3 year old G602.

Here is a rundown of every part of the mouse:

Tracking: Flawless. No lag, dropped inputs, or skips. As expected from any wired mouse.
Left/right click: Quick and effortless. Not mushy at all. Actuation point is right at the top. Very little pressure is required to click.
Side buttons: Also great. Mushier feeling, but the actuation point is still at the top. Feel good to use and don't feel cheap.
Size/weight: The mouse is on the larger side, and medium/light weight (108 grams according to Logitech). Feels natural and comfortable in my hand. I have smaller hands and use a palm grip.
Scroll wheel: My only (very) minor complaint. The scroll wheel works, but feels cheap. It has a lot of friction to it, meaning if you flick it it will not carry any momentum and will stop immediately. More of a personal preference thing, but I don't mind it. It may get broken in over time or it may not. Regardless the scroll wheel is inconsequential in the grand scheme of things, and it's not bad, just different from what I'm used to.

Overall this mouse absolutely blew me away and delighted me. It's an incredible all-purpose gaming mouse that I would recommend to anyone looking for a cheap solution that also provides incredible value and functionality (I bought it for $35). It easily stands up to my $60 mouse and is superior in some aspects.

rating 2.0. tl;dr: Unreliable performance and constant disconnects, can't recommend When it works, the G402 is definitely at least a 4-star mouse. As you can imagine, it makes the mouse completely unusable for any competitive or timing based game. I bought the mouse in August of 2016 and I'm replacing it now, in January of 2017. Frankly, since it's a known hardware issue, I don't see myself buying any logitech mice in the near feature, which is a damn shame.

Additionally, though this is a mere quibble after the mouse won't work reliably, the thumb buttons for forward/back in browsers and whatnot are terribly placed. I wound up rebinding both of them so I'd stop accidentally going back in a browser and losing all my work.

-very very comfortable (did I mention very?) very well designed I must say I'm impressed with the ergonomic design haven't felt anything like it before, great matte finish top quality material used
-left and right click buttons are very responsive
-fusion engine built in(software needed to use) will compensate for however insane speed you swipe this mouse and not lose feedback

Cons:
-If your lucky enough to get a working one its worth it but if you have problems with it Logitech support is terrible I see people on the forums waiting 2 months just for a response.
-Dpi maxes at 4000 (not really a con but for some maybe)
-not braided cable (its a rubber cable)
-it is more long, than wide in terms of dimensions, very slim, long fingers would benefit from this(don't really know if this is con for some)

With its cons I still cannot deduct a star for how accurate and well designed this mouse is. I don't feel this is at all a MOBA mouse, and is geared more toward the first person shooter audience. If your into fps and looking for a mouse at a great price this is it.

rating 2.0. not for clawgrip If you are Claw Grip like me Avoid this mouse!

This is not good for Claw Grip, However if you are palmgrip this will be the best mouse for you.

rating 4.0. GREAT Mouse... My only and Only to Play League I talked to Logitech before buying this mouse because I wanted the best possible mouse to play League of Legends.
This baby more than delivers... Simply fantastic, no issues , super fast , easy to customize, and great looking
Love this thing.
Only slight problem is that the Logitech software sometimes bugs a bit , depending on the computer you have. But you can bind the keys in LoL to the mouse buttons, so really the crappy software doesn't end up being a big deal.

rating 3.0. Fixing what wasn't broken Dear Logitech, please make mice with the classic MX500/MX510/MX518/G5/G400 etc. shape. This mouse is supposedly a successor, but in my opinion it's an inferior one. If you need to make the mouse look futuristic or "cool" so it appeals to kids, add some RGB LEDs or whatever--don't change a classic ergonomic design.

This mouse, like the older mice mentioned, is good for people with big hands or long fingers. But this mouse doesn't fill my palm as nicely as those older mice--it's a bit flatter. Also, mouse8/that thumb grip button gets in the way. First, it feels less comfortable than the MX518's smooth thumb grip. Second, if you play FPS games, you shouldn't bind anything to that key because it is too easy to hit on accident. I don't feel like I can confidently grip the mouse without accidentally pushing mouse8.

Also, as others have mentioned, mouse4 and mouse5 are awkwardly implemented--the MX518 did it better.

Yes, this review is concerned solely with the ergonomic design of this mouse. The internals are good enough/on par with other gaming mice.
rating 5.0. Five Stars i ordered for my grandson he liked very much it was what he expected

rating 1.0. double click issue within 3 months I've only had this mouse 3 months and now it is doubke clicking when I single click I DO NOT recommend wasting this much money on a mouse that is going to malfunction within 3 months.

rating 4.0. Awesome mouse for palm-grip users This is without a doubt one of the best gaming mice I've ever used. It is absolutely perfect for palm-grippers, fits my hand like a glove and I can reach any button with relative ease, with perhaps the exception of the 'sniper' button which is located on the left side where your thumb goes. In order to press it I have to awkwardly reach forward with my thumb which forces me to change the way I hold the mouse. If you have a long enough thumb then maybe it won't trouble you as much, then again it's always an option to just ignore it and not use it. Now I should also mention the cord is very stiff and inflexible, as others have pointed out. But otherwise I think the look, feel, durability, and responsiveness of this mouse are all outstanding, and thanks to the accelerometer, you need not hold back when zipping this thing across your desk, it will keep tracking flawlessly every step of the way. I paired it with Logitech's g440 hard mouse pad and am very pleased with this combination. I'd happily purchase this mouse again once the need arises, which hopefully won't be for a very long time.

rating 2.0. Low quality. Love this mouse so far. I've never owned a gaming mouse, and I've been playing for a long time. I admit that means that I have nothing to compare it to; please take note.
I bought [[ASIN:B000UEZ36W SteelSeries QcK Gaming Mouse Pad (Black)]] with it and its a great combo. Smooth movements and it feels great in my hand - finger or palm grip. I like the matte black and the blue logitech logo. I've only had this mouse for a week so I can't attest to the surface materials durability, or the internal hardware.

Until an update later this year, 5 stars!

::UPDATE::
Lesson learned that a review for this type of product should be withheld until at least a year of service. LMB became sticky, fixed itself, and now is unusable. Will not buy again, or recommend.

rating 5.0. just a plain old 5 button one does fine for me I don't need a gaming mouse, just a plain old 5 button one does fine for me, but these days, I can't find a decent mouse outside of the gaming mouse category. So I took a chance, and I'm happy I did.

5 custom DPI settings, and you don't have to use all of them. You can disable up to 4 of them if you want. I only use 2. Sniper button works good, but it's a little too far forward for me. Mouse wheel is superb. Lack of tilt wheel is a huge plus. Every button is programmable, even the DPI selector buttons. Even the "G" light on this thing is programmable, but you can't turn it off completely, which is kind of stupid. The Logitech Gaming Software is also excellent.

I was hesitant to purchase another Logitech product after using the MX400 for a few years, because that mouse had a mind of it's own, but the G402 is nearly flawless for me. I went back to Logitech simply because they have the absolute largest selection of mice and features in production at any given time versus any other company.

My previous experience with Logitech support also left quite a bit to be desired. Their standard respond to any issue on their forums was "install Setpoint" which never solved anything. In fact, Setpoint usually made problems worse. But the G402 doesn't use that horrible software, so that's another big plus.

My only complaint, and it's a minor one, is that Logitech still hasn't provided proper drivers for some products so that Windows shows the proper device in device manager and Devices and Printers. There is a driver for this mouse, but Windows refuses to install it.

rating 3.0. It's OK. 2 month in review.

Mostly playing Battlefield 4 here.

-
Not really an upgrade from the MX 518. I don't really care for the overall shape of the mouse feel on my hand. (the left and right click button placements are too low on this mouse [for me]). I'm one of those gamers that hold the mouse with my hand resembling a "claw shape" when holding the mouse and not my entire hand equally covering the entire surface of the mouse. Tracking and precision of the mouse seems to be a side grade with the MX518 (real world feel - I don't notice any difference).

+
Scrolling wheel is great and so is the small other buttons placement.
Software is solid and lots of solid reliable working features.
It's lightweight.

rating 4.0. The Best gaming mouse I've returned!! This mouse is super high quality, great design...actually top notch design, amazing buttons' placement, everything is great but..... NOT FOR ME

Am a full "Palm grip" gamer and I like some kind of support for my ring and little 'pinky' fingers and this is my problem with the G402 AND G502... I don't mind a flat right side/wall of a mouse like the ones in the Deathadder Chroma, Corsair M65 but the Logitech G402 and G502 has "dip" "groove" on the right side making it really uncomfortable to handle for me PERSONALLY....One game of Dota and my ring, little fingers were in pain! That being the case, I put it back in it's package and decided to return it....

So to wrap this one up, I don't believe the G402 and G502 are made for the full palm grip....the kind of people who like to "rest their hands completely on the mouse".... These mice are "slim" and has no support for your ring and little finger... Other than this, they are the best, top "quality" mice I've ever encountered in every single aspect.

Since I mentioned this is the "best" mouse I've returned, why not mention the other ones I did also return but won't praise them
1) the Deathadder Chroma....CHEAPEST PIECE OF PLASTIC I EVER GOT!! I cannot believe all the hype, 5 star ratings for such a cheap low quality piece of plastic... It is super comfy, works well (when it does) but such a poor quality product it is.... my shortest living mouse I've ever purchased my entire life (including all the $5 -$10 mice i had) ... it died after 4 months of use...
2) Steelseries Sensei..... forced me to use claw/ fingertip grip... wasn't comfortable for me....returned it... ok build/quality but again won't praise it like the G402 and G502...
Hope I helped

rating 1.0. A PC Gamers Review After only two days of use, the left stick started clicking on any rotation. It feels cheap, it looks cheap and clearly, it breaks as if I bought it from the dollar store. With that being said here are the pros and cons.

Pros:
- Better design than xbox360 controller.
- Easier to hit left & right bumpers
- The thumb stick deadzone is much less than that of the 360.
- Bluetooth or wired connection

Con:
- CHEAP in every way. If you don't mind design difference get the 360 controllers or a PS4 controller
- If you are heavy on the left stick it will break quickly.
- Getting this thing to work on Windows 8.1 & Windows 10 was painful to the say the least.

I discovered that the left stick clicking and breaking is common on xbox one remotes. They changed copper springs to plastic springs.

rating 2.0. Great grip horrible quality. The left analog stick started squeaking  after 2 weeks and it is off center, even after all the configuration. While I like the XB360 feel and grip, the cheap feeling is a big let down.

rating 5.0. Very good deal for limited budget gamer I am on a limited budget and have been waiting for a good deal, and this bundle is as good as it can get for me. Even with 6% online sales tax since amazon just opened a warehouse in Baltimore, MD, the $371 final price was still a very good deal.

Even though Xbox One is less powerful than PS4, I am enjoying it very much. Looking forward to new gaming adventure on next gen (MC Collection, Quantum Break, Diablo 3 local co-op with the wife)

==============================================

Updated 11/10/2014.

I have owned and played my console for 1 week and so far have not run into any issue. I like the AC series and am already 20+ hours into Black Flag and still in Sequence 3 because there are so many new things to try.

As for the console itself, it is smaller that I expected which is nice. It does not seem to generate as much heat as my 360 even after playing it for over 5 hours straight.

After installing Black Flag, Unity, HALO Spartan Assault, FIFA 15 demo, Thief Demo and Project Spark, the free space on the HD is down to 200GB. I am getting HALO MC Collection and it will remain installed on the HD (60GB) but the cost of external HD is dropping. So I don't see the internal HD space limitation as an issue. Plus I read somewhere that loading from the external HD USB 3.0 could be faster than loading from the internal HD.

rating 5.0. Just buy One!!! So good. I am a hardcore gamer. Don't listen to the console wars debate. The Xbox One is great for different reasons than other systems. The graphics are fantastic(and I am a PC gamer). The exclusive games are great and the exclusives up and coming look promising. But this shines with multi tasking and media media media. Its a full media machine. Shipping was incredibly fast and accurate.

rating 5.0. More for less What's not to love? For $349 you are getting the Xbox with two free games. The console alone sells for $349, so even if you are not crazy about assasians creed it's still a smart buy.

The bundle comes with digital downloads of the games, wireless controller, headset, and two 14 day live trials (28 days total).

rating 5.0. Great Buy! Definitely Recommend! Awesome bundle. I saw that some people have said it doesn't come with the games, but it gives you the download codes to get the games. Would have thought they would have come installed, but the download codes work. One thing to note- the games are huge files, so they will take time to download. So far, Unity hasn't yet been released (I'm writing this on the 8th of November, 2014) so I only can actually play Black Flag, but Unity is downloaded so I can play it readily at that date. The bundle comes with everything needed, including a chat headset, and good batteries for the controller (Duracell). Overall, this is a great deal. Basically you get two games free with purchase of the console.

rating 2.0. Disappointed with the system... My system has been buggy since receiving. The UI has been glitchy, the controller home button does not always respond when I hit it, and the controller randomly disconnects while playing a game.

The game download times are slow, but not bad since you can kick them off in advance of a release. Speed is obviously much better hard wired than WiFi.

The controller requires double A batteries, no charge kit and no included cable to play wired. The inclusion of an HDMI cable was nice.

The controller feels nice but I am not a fan of the new bumpers. They are designed to be hit with the middle of your fingers for those with larger hands. You can no longer just hit the bumper button wherever you like due to the way it is hinged to be hit on the outside of the button. I do like the triggers better on this controller, they feel very nice.

Overall I'm disappointed in the system. I did not expect to receive a unit with so many bugs this far out of launch. Maybe just bad luck and I got a bad unit, but makes me wonder about quality control on Microsoft's part.

I am debating now to exchange it or just refund it. Feel free to comment if you have any suggested solutions or have experienced anything like I have. I contacted Microsoft support but they were not that helpful; just suggested I return the unit. I may return for a PS4 and get an xbox one further down the line once the kinks have been worked out.

With Xbox's Indie Game - Self Publishing feature they just introduced and some new exclusives, I can definitely say I'm happy with this purchase.

rating 5.0. Great keyboard for the price! Well, to really start this off, I love this combo kit!

I've dealt with an Alpha Gaming bundle for a few months. It was OK to use, but the quality was just terrible. It was a wannabe mechanical-membrane keyboard. The mouse was OK, but the whole set seemed really cheap.

Not with this set though! This keyboard feels so smooth to type on. The clicking from the key switches are VERY responsive and I know what key I'm hitting. The quality of the keyboard really surprised me because I really love the aluminum finish along with the matte texture of the keycaps. The lighting of the keyboard and mouse are very pretty and I love the large variety of colors I can pick from! The mouse is big, but I can drag it around and click all of the buttons just fine! My only "complaint" that I have with this keyboard is that I can't switch to one single color. But that's just a small flaw I have with the set. It's amazing and I definitely recommend it to all gamers and office workers!

rating 4.0. Excellent entry-level mechanical keyboard for the price And that's the emphasis here: "for the price". It is a good keyboard that gives you the satisfaction you get from pressing mechanical switches. Nice tactile sensation and pleasing (or annoying, depending on your taste) clicky keystroke. It's pretty much exactly what I expected and I really have no complaints.

Mine did have a slight issue with the spacebar sticking. I fixed this by pulling the spacebar off and flipping it around. Yes, this does change the angle of the spacebar and it does prevent the LED from shining through on that key as well, but it fixed the issue completely. And for under 40 bucks, I'm not complaining at all.

The mouse doesn't feel like the best gaming mouse (I used to use a sidewinder back in the day) but I don't play games anymore so not an issue for me. It's a perfectly fine upgrade from the basic HP mouse I was using. Overall, I'm satisfied!

rating 4.0. great 4 typing!! No single color mode on this 393L for U.S. I realize this is being sold for gaming, but I bought it as an inexpensive replacement for a keyboard with Cherry MX clicky switches that I loved - but the white letters had faded to the point that when I needed to hit a single key - for example when playing X-plane in dim light - I often hit the wrong key because I couldn't see it. The backlit keys fix that problem completely and they are bright enough - however, rainbow only. Keyboard sections from left to right: red, white, green, blue, purple. No option (that I have been able to find) to have this keyboard, as opposed to some other Havit models, have a single color for back lighting.
The switches are Chinese and are the loud clicky version that I like. Don't know yet how they'll hold up, but at this price point, I'm generally not very worried.
Typing long documents with this keyboard is excellent.

rating 2.0. Scroll wheel never worked I would said if you wanna a good keyboard and mice set, safe money and go for Corsair. The scroll wheel on the mouse never worked. Doesnt have an on/off for the leds. 4.5/10

rating 4.0. Decent Keyboard - Subpar mouse! I used to think that this was the most amazing set until I realized when I got a new mouse, at this mouse is actually subpar.
Don't get me wrong The price point, and the RGB and design are a great aesthetic...
But when I noticed switching between the DPI settings on this mouse, It was incredibly hard to control on my desktop in comparison to my new gaming mouse in which I can control smoothly on my desktop even at 16,000 DPI.

Great build and comfort but definitely not a serious gaming mouse, aside from the RGB
There is no way to control the refresh rate on the sensor as well so , it's no more than a mouse slapped on with a toggle between DPIs and a far from smooth experience for gaming and even casual use it's not that great.
The keyboard however no issues, just a little clicky that's all.
All in all not disappointed at all just so you know the mouse isn't that great!
I myself mostly got it for the keyboard because you can't go wrong for a mechanical keyboard at that price either so, just a heads up if you're looking at something else maybe a little bit more or perhaps on its own , The keyboard on this is decent but the mouse is subpar.
Thanks for reading.

rating 2.0. You get what you paid for. It’s a decent keyboard.  Very clicky. The mouse is way to big. Would be better if the keyboard was white, makes the rgb much better. Lights are barely visible during the day. RGB on the mouse is pretty nice. Overall It is what it is for the price.

rating 2.0. For me, it was super uncomfortable The build Quality is great, the LED's are awesome, and the sound of the keyboard was satisfying. however when playing games the keyboard was very uncomfortable. This is probable due to the height of the keyboard. This keyboard is as low as 1 and 1/8 inches off the desk to as high as 2 inches, and is did not come with a wrist rest. This makes it uncomfortable to use for extended periods of time. This was all that was wrong with the keyboard in my opinion. The mouse on the other hand was just offal. No grip, way to big, not responsive enough and it kept getting caught on my mouse pad, "it had sharp plastic on the bottom".

rating 4.0. Love it with a few quirks. Overall I love this keyboard and mouse combo. I love the mechanical keys and are very responsive. I love the color options for both. I would still recommend this even with my two dislikes:

1. At times if my hands get sweaty I find the grip on the sides of the mouse isn't "grippy" enough and when I lift the mouse up it starts to slip.
2. When using it for teleworking, I have had coworkers mention it's a loud keyboard if I don't mute my mic. At first I was gonna ding my rating one star for this but then it started frustrating coworkers that annoy me so I'm gonna let it keep the star on this issue.

So overall, minus one star for dislike #1, totalling four stars. Would recommend especially if it's on sale.

rating 2.0. Takes more effort to type. Liquid destroys it. I liked this keyboard, but I spilled a cup of coffee on it and this ruined it. It is not liquid-resistant.

The keys are higher, have more travel than what I am used to, and have a louder click. It took more effort and if I did a lot of typing then my hands would get tired.

My previous review is here:

The keyboard seems to be of pretty good quality. The key presses have a good feel to them. The switches are a bit loud, but I assume that I'll get used to that. Gamers seem to like that sort of thing. My mother thought that it sounded like a typewriter, although it is not as loud.

I kind of wish that the keyboard had a lower profile, like the cheap $12 keyboard that I was using. I have to raise my fingers slightly higher on the keyboard to type. (I am not sure if I am going to like this or not.)

My problem with the mouse is that it is not comfortable in my hand. Whereas some laptop mice are too small, this mouse is slightly too big. I have about average size hands for a man, and I found the mouse too bulky. I have an inexpensive Logitech mouse that fits perfectly.

rating 5.0. Great quality I have been using the keyboard/mouse for work, and they are great. I like that the keys do not have the letters just painted/stamped on, but they are actually inlaid into the key using clear plastic. This means the letters will not fade and the backlighting shines through making them easier to see/read. I also like that the keys are raised up above the keyboard, and make an "old school" typing sound.

rating 4.0. Very pleased! [[VIDEOID:a8c2e3e326ebb84d777765d9dc83a854]] I had bought this because of some issues I had with a logi wireless set.

I love the light patterns on it, and the ability to lock the WINDOWS button is a sanity saver. I'm not so sure I like the darker yellow colored portion but it is still very visible. It seems a bit loud to type on, but it doesn't miss a beat at all.

It comes with a small plastic tool that you use to remove the keys, which just 1 day before this review came as another sanity-saving feature after a small mishap.

I'd say the only complaint I have is the media key sets are opposite from each other (media control F1-F4, versus system volume/mute F9-F12)... that and the lower keys close to you are a little too easy to bump if you have something sitting between you and the keyboard, but don't let that sway you from purchasing this nugget!

OH, I forgot to mention the Mouse that comes with it is also pretty awesome. A few light patterns to that as well, with the DPI switch that has multiple levels of sensitivity.

I recommend this to any gamer!
rating 5.0. Great!!! I love this keyboard and mouse! It looks great and feels perfect. The mechanical feel really separates this product from competitors. Highly recommended!

rating 1.0. No user manual in box or available for download Watched the first customer video for this product on the Amazon site.  He showed the user manual for a KB 393L keyboard.  I had no manual in the box I received.  Worse yet there was NO information for the KB 393L keyboard available on the user site.  NONE.  No downloads for a user manual or drivers, if needed.  Since the keyboard is configurable and has keys not found on a standard keyboard, the user manual is a must.  There was no user information on the mouse either.  Since the product reviews shown for this keyboard were so high, I believe Amazon is lumping reviews for early keyboards from Havit together.  That is a poor practice.  You will notice that nowhere on the Amazon product description is a model number shown.  It shakes my confidence in the Amazon rating system.  Regarding the keyboard itself, the key stampings are a bit unusual and several keys have what looks to be commands stamped on them that can't be understood without a manual.  I do like the way they implemented the windows key deactivation/reactivation. The lighted keyboard is cool.  The mouse seems too big.  I guess I will be returning the product.

rating 5.0. Unbelievable Deal! [[VIDEOID:4e734e40beca0b6ed8862fd48df45e2e]] I am ALWAYS skeptical when i find things like this online, and this time was no different. However, i am blown away at the quality of this combo set for the price. The mouse is everything i hoped for and the Keyboard is a well-built Aluminum casing with multiple built in color patterns. Each color function key has atleast 3 settings applied to them(press FN+the key up to 3 times) except for the on/off function. If i could add just one thing, it would be to put 2 of these patterns together with their built in custom key mapping, however this only allows you to set up custom key BACKLIGHTING. Overall, i am thrilled with this, i will update if it dies on me quickly or if any issues arise.

rating 2.0. Worst mouse (I'm so mad about this mouse) I missed the return window for this... keyboard and mouse work, and look pretty good, but oh my god the mouse is so uncomfortable.

Don't cheap out by getting discount low end versions of a premium product (gaming peripherals)

rating 2.0. Cheaply made & lower quality, but cool looking and functional, with short use life Mouse died after a few weeks. It is very light and cheaply made, but looks good. Keyboard is more solidly constructed, but the responsiveness is lacking and doesn't feel like a true mechanical keyboard.  Great pair for first build gamers, but not a long term investment.

rating 2.0. Meh Keyboard is not rgb each section is its own color  it has a bunch of different effects but the colors are always the same. The feel of the keyboard for typing is ok but mouse didn't work well for me.

rating 3.0. Rainbow yes RGB NO! First keyboard was missing several key caps and my computer would NOT recognize the mouse at all. Returned and the replacement was much better. The keys have a nice feel and there are FIVE different flash modes but each key is NOT RGB. From left to right of the keys are red, yellow, green, blue and violet. You can change the way they flash but you can not change the colors. The mouse has a nice feel and nice colors.
So overall I have happy but my expectations were not met.
Bill

rating 5.0. Awesome! I don't actually get this for gaming. I wanted a nice, sturdy, and functional keyboard with a positive response, for working full days with lots of Microsoft Office, copy and paste, and sooner socialized software.
The switches are very good. Very every response, with a nice tactile keyboard. Easy to find everything on this fill sized 104 key layout.
The LED lighting is nice, as I can keep the lights down, but still so the keys when I need to orient myself. The most is great, too. Fills my beefy mitt, nice and solid.
The price was very good. A bargain compared to similar speced keyboards.

rating 5.0. Very sturdy for the price. Not great for pudding caps. Great keyboard, upon receiving it I was immediately in love with the quality of the material, truly feels great on your fingers as the caps are soft. The click is absolutely fantastic and the cable is sturdy instead of a cheap plastic.
Main note before purchasing: the lights are "static", every section of the keyboard is locked into its own light color and will never change, effects work only around fading in and out the colors, it looks amazing on its original black keycaps, and the color effects look fantastic on them, but if you plan to switch to pudding caps, this keyboard might not be what you need.
The mouse feels a bit big on my hand (but I have small hands) so might just be a preference, the lighting is gorgeous.

rating 1.0. Not a very good keyboard and mouse! The spacebar is "corner cut" and constantly gets stuck and becomes a sticky key. Also there is only one color preset on the keyboard which is rainbow and the mouse cannot stay a solid color.
rating 1.0. AVOID lasted 5 months then the A key for whatever reason just stops working. no spills, no damages. just doesn't work and when it does, it double taps. it has spread to the W key too
rating 1.0. Ok if it works Keyboard is ok. Mouse did not work. Get what you pay for.

rating 2.0. Was immpressed but already hace LED going out I was initially impressed, however I have own this for less then 2 months and already have dead LEDs. The overall construction is pretty solid but this has me worried about the rest of the components. If you don't care about the lighting then sure, it works good, however I will be returning this and purchasing a different item, preferably one with a warranty.

rating 4.0. So far so good... I've owned it a few months and I read all the negative reviews before buying it. Works perfectly so far though.

Things to know: this is not a gaming keyboard, despite the marketing. It's a blue mechanical, which is better for typing than gaming. However, it IS better than a standard keyboard, so if youre using one of those, even this is a step up. If however you have the money and youre looking for a gaming kb, get a cherry/red.

This kb also has fewer light settings than I thought it did, and the owners manual is one page. It does not explain how to add your own light pre-sets to the kb, you have to look that up on the internet. Otherwise, its a solid product that feels great with a mouse that seems even better.

rating 2.0. Dont waste your money The keys are clicking cheap. The box came in wet on one side so much it tore like a paper sack. 2 keys were off right out of box and one retainer lock under enter key was broke. I'm able to repair everything  but cheap item. Wasn't even that impressed with lights . Mouse is only good thing so far.
rating 1.0. Worst mechanical lasted 4 months Worked for 4 months then just doesnt work, complete trash would not recommend. Spend more on a known name, dont get ripped off like me

rating 5.0. 4.9/5 Stars!!! This keyboard and mouse combo is great!! This is a perfect solution for me, in my home office PC. The only reason I didn't give 5 out of 5 stars, was because my old keyboard had an individual key for the calculator, this keyboard you have to press the Function key then F8 to get to the calculator. Besides that, this is an excellent replacement for my 6 year old Microsoft keyboard that died on me. All keys are working flawlessly! I love the RGB colors and other colors for both the keyboard and mouse. If I was an avid gamer, I would consider this combo as well. Some may disagree with me on that statement, but I don't play many games myself. I just wanted a replacement keyboard and mouse which is wired, and this does the trick, and at a cheaper price than combos that aren't RGB.
Also if you're worried about the brightness of the RGB being emitted, no worries there! I turned off my lights and tried to see if those lights would bother me in another room, and they're not bright enough to bother you for sleeping. Also you can turn both of the RGB lights off if they're bothersome for you. The keys have a great feel for typing and the keyboard has to tabs on the bottom to elevate it if you were curious. If this is something for home office or gaming that you need, then get it, you won't go wrong here!

rating 5.0. Excellent starter gaming keyboard and mouse This is my second purchase of a Havit mechanical KB and mouse combo. The first one was with a palm rest, it was a PC setup for a family, and it was excellent, they loved it. I have another build and my nephew doesn’t like using a palm rest, so I decided to get him this combo instead. It is a good discount without the palm rest.

I wish the packaging opens from the top and not from the sides. Keyboards + mouse are easier to unbox that way. But other than that, this combo is excellent. Once you take these out of the box, the first thing you’ll notice is how premium quality this product is. The aluminum top frame feels very rigid and won’t bend under pressure. The keycaps are made of plastic with a matte finish. Comparatively, my primary KB has smooth keycaps and they retain skin oils. So, this matte finish is easier to maintain and clean. The blue switches are really clicky, majority of gamers love clicky keys. But these keys are a little softer to press compared to mainstream mechanical KB blue switches. But tactile feedback is still excellent. Testing this KB + mouse combo on some FPS game gave surprising results. Feedback is good and no key delays or lag. I’m not a big fan of RGB lights, I just set them to “breathing” mode and forget about it. This KB+mouse combo has customizable RGB lights. You can customize them using software, but you can also change them using the FN keys, no need to install additional software. Same thing with the mouse that has a button to change RGB style. You can change the DPI settings on the fly on the mouse to 6 different settings, but there is no default for 1200 DPI which most gamers use, the next best thing is 1600 DPI which is only a very small difference in my opinion. I was able to game with this keyboard for 2 hours straight without any problem. Performance wise, it only took a few minutes until I got used to the feel of the keyboard and mouse and back to my gaming style.

Overall, this combo is an excellent option for budget gaming builds. They perform on par with those $100+ branded ones. If you prefer a keyboard with palm rest, then look for their other variant for an additional cost, but if you’re ok without a palm rest, this combo is for you. Will I buy this again? Well, this is my second set for another build, so yes. Highly recommended.

rating 5.0. Best Ultra Bugget True MechBoatd Far exceeds expectations!
Hard to believe the quality is THIS good for such a low price!
If I were gaming with it I would go with the full RGB model but for my desk at the office this more than meets what I thought I would get for the price.

I have more expensive mechanicals that cost 5 times as much and I genuinely like this board just as much.

Feels great!
Sounds great! (Blues)
IS Great Build Quality!

And...

The mouse is genuinely of decent quality as well!

For the price if you want a budget true mechanical keyboard with a decent mouse that has adjustable DPI tracking...

I don't believe you will do better than this kit.

I will be buying much more Havit now that I have experienced their price to quality ration.

Elated!

rating 4.0. Decent ... for the price I paid At the current price (even though it has both a mouse and keyboard), I wouldn't consider it a great deal.  I paid $20 less than the current price and I was ok with that.  The keyboard was built well, but it was way too wide for me.  I ended up switching to Kemove 60% keyboard and am very happy with my choice.  Also, you can change color schemes on this keyboard, but you can't change the color of individual keys.  I knew that before purchasing, but I still thought it was worth mentioning.  I have also since replaced the mouse with an ambidextrous mouse.  The mouse wasn't bad, but being a lefty I needed something a little different.

rating 5.0. Very nice. Responsive. Zero Ghosting. Full rollover. On September 17, 2020 my razor black widow keyboard 2016 ver. Desired to forever sleep. Mechanical keyboards of that nature cost quite a bit. So in-order to satisfy the immediate need for a keyboard I came to Amazon, after looking at the selection available I was able to settle on this due to its price point and included mouse (mine was an old stock dell mouse).

To start this is a very clicky keyboard, and the rainbow color pattern and modes are very nice. Compared to razor greens they are less heavy of a press. For me this is a big plus. The build is solid nice look and feel to it. That piece of aluminium is solid. Plug n' Play.

Mouse is fantastic, The RGB patterns are nice as well. Feels a lot more solid than my stock dell mouse. DPI selection is nice. Plug n' Play as well. If you so choose to download the software it ads a selection of settings such as DPI setting colors. You can choose the speed of the colors and reprogram what the buttons do. You can also set up a macro. Mouse move speed, enhance pointer precision, Scrolling speed ect.

This is a solid pick 5/5
rating 1.0. Key stopped working in less than 6 months Have had it less than 6 months and my D key is shorting put and only works some of the time. Would not recommend

rating 5.0. Great value for the price This is the first keyboard I have bought. At 34 dollars for a mechanical keyboard and a mouse its an amazing deal. The keys are light and easy to press. The Leds options are really cool.

The only downsides I have with this keyboard is that its loud, for me it works fine I don't have to care about noise but if sound matters to you then I wouldn't recommend. The other thing is how loose the keycaps are, I have only used hand me down keyboards (non of which mechanical) and by far this keyboard has the loosest keycaps.

Other than those two things for the price i got this at its an amazing keyboard and i would highly recomend.

rating 5.0. Best budget keyboard and mouse combo This combo is out of question THE best budget keyboard and mouse combo out there, if you like to hear the ''clicki'' sound when your playing or typing this keyboard is for you, the keys are a little squishy tho but if you can look past it itll be great, the RGB is bright enough to look cool but not too bright to disturb, the keyboard itself is very very tough its a very hard plastic on the bottom and a metal finish on top, and its very heavy and wont slide around. The mouse, in my opinion is perfect, i wouldt want to change it, it simplistic and minimal which is a big big plus for me, the RGB is amazing, its pretty heavy and pretty big, so if you like small light mouse your not gonna like this one, over all the for the price its a steal i highly recommend if you are just getting into pc gaming and dont want to spend a lot of money.

rating 4.0. Excellent bundle Keyboard 5/5 but Mouse 4/5 While looking for a mechanical keyboard seems to be all the hype today, this one came in at a little bit of a surprise the fact that it comes included with a mouse and actually has good reviews for the price surprised me. I'm sure there a bit better boards out there but for the price the metal plate used in the keyboard is very very sturdy and deck flex is minimal.

The lighting is very uniform and I have no complaints about it, it doesn't come with a manual but you can get the manual from Havit's website as well as the program to control the dpi settings on your mouse.

Speaking of the mouse, it's good it ain't great sometimes the scroll wheel feels really tight and sometimes it feels great, maybe mine has some dust inside contributing to that. Lightning is good and the dpi settings are definitely a plus for me.

rating 3.0. Not the best :/ It's REALLY clickly, if that's not for you, consider getting key dampeners like o-rings. It's not as bright as other keyboard but the RGB patterns are pretty good. You can even record some combinations for custom colors. HOWEVER, it does not have a full range of color. Each key can only display one color, meaning your keyboard is stuck in a constant gradient (they don't change colors). The RGB patterns I mentioned are only for brightness and different patterns. The colors of the keys do NOT change.
rating 1.0. I can't click Ctrl button I'm so disappointed because one of most important key is damaged. The packiging was very well and no scratches.

rating 2.0. The letters are all messed up Lots of the letters are not fully done they  all have lines through them its like they machine that etched the letters out in the keys didnt go deep enough in some spots

input:
rating 5.0. SO much fun!! My 6 year old daughter LOVES iSpy. We had the computer version of Spooky Mansion, and it was her favorite game. She played it all the way through twice.

Anyway, when I saw this title, I wanted to give it a shot, but I was a bit concerned that it would be much like the computer game. I needn't have worried. The little skeleton host was the same, and the general goal of the game is the same, but the puzzles are all different, and while some of the rooms are similar, they are different enough from the computer game to still be challenging. We opened the game for Christmas, and played through the first "level" (11 puzzles) all in one day--not because they are too easy, but because the game is that addictive. The only downside is that you cannot zoom the items more. I would like a little better zoom at times, but, then, that might make the game less challenging. If you like iSpy, I definitely think you'll love this title.

rating 5.0. I Spy Spooky Mansion is GREAT! I purchased the I Spy Spooky Mansion for Wii for my 9 year old grandson for Christmas.  Needless to say, he was ecstatic!  He has the older Spooky Mansion for PC, but this one is more suited for his age group.  He is quite savvy with Wii games and is sometimes challenged to find items on the Wii version.  It's a great game & he is having a good time playing it!

rating 4.0. Another Fun I Spy Game I bought this for my four-year-old's birthday after he had mastered Ultimate I Spy, and he loved this game. He's beaten it a few times, but keeps going back to run through it. The game is not as scary as I originally feared, which was a relief. He couldn't put this game down, and likes it as much as the original I Spy Wii game.

The only downside is that it's a bit short, but it's still longer than we initially thought. There are enough minigames to make it a fun experience, and each individual room has some tricky items to find. There are a couple of minigames that are still a bit tricky for him, but as he plays them over and over, he's starting to get better at them.

This would have been a five star review had it been a little bit longer, but it's still a great game for younger kids.

rating 4.0. I Spy Spooky Mansion I was looking for a present for my nephews 7th birthday. He loves his WII games. So I looked for a game. When i saw "I Spy Spooky Mansion" by Scholastic Games I thought it would be fun. I was plesently surprised when my nephew played the game the first time. Not only was there no violence he had to read and he really had to think to figure out the clues for the hidden objects. He was so used to just shaking the controller and pushing buttons to play a game. Nice change to have to actually use his brain. I would definately recomend this to anyone looking for a game that is fun and sneaks in some learning along the way.
rating 4.0. LOVE I Spy games Sincerely enjoyed the game. My boyfriend sits and plays them with me and we have a lot of fun. Wish they made more of these for WII.

rating 5.0. Fun Game Great family fun, however the entire game can be completed in one sitting.  Since the game was bought used, there was one technical difficulty with it....at certain points the game "froze" and had to be restarted. Fortunately, it restarted from the level we had progressed to, and didnt have to replay the entire game.

rating 5.0. Great game for the kids (and adults) The I Spy series of games is great for kids and adults.  I grew up with the brand and it crossing generations now on the Wii.  We hope that some new themes come out as we enjoy finding the clues and searching for items as a family.  Good for working together which is sometimes difficult when many games are for 2 players only nowadays.  Definitely recommended.

rating 4.0. Nostalgia This game was just as I remembered it and the 3-D type graphics that the Wii added to it made it even better than the pc version I played as a kid. Not only did I enjoy replaying it again as a college student but it was still a bit challenging at points with things being hidden in the background instead of just in the scene you're engaged in. That said I also like that in this version a lot of the search scenes you have 360 degree control so you can pan around it to look. There are some short load times from place to place because it has auto save feature, a plus in my book but not for everyone.
After I had beat it I passed it along to my 6 year old nephew who also really loves playing it. His brother who is 5 also likes to play but lacks the coordination to click on items with the wiimote and has to be helped through pretty much every step, just a warning for anyone wanting to purchase for that young. But the older one really does like how interactive it is asks to play it constantly. Well worth the buy with the whole family getting in the finding stuff.

rating 4.0. there's more to do than you expect This is a really fun game. I gave it 4 stars instead of 5 because as you solve puzzles and collect clues, the game keeps saying, "you're almost done" , "this is the final clue" etc. only to find out you have to go through all the rooms and solve new puzzles many, many times.

rating 4.0. Fun Fun game with the kids. They and I were entertained throughout. Some of the items were a little small and a touch more zoom would have been nice but did not slow gameplay. I do recommend it if you like the books.

rating 5.0. Great game! My 40+ year old daughter and I (70 yr. old) played this WII game and couldn't go to bed because we got so involved in it trying to find the objects! It keeps you on your toes going from room to room in the Spooky Mansion trying to find every thing so that you can escape the mansion.  Great game!

rating 5.0. We love this game We love this game. Even though it is an older game we still play it. Its easy enough for a younger child to play it, but interesting enough for parents to play along as well. We really enjoy the mystery to it as we play along. The children like being on the lookout for each hidden object. Sometimes there are more difficult areas for the kids to do on their own. Parents must play along and help with this or it could get very frustrating for young kids. Overall, I really like this game!

rating 3.0. Okay if you like repeating the same thing over and over again. I play this with my 10 y/o we love it, but too have to keep going back over and over through the same house for the next challenges gets old fast.  would be nice to have additional rooms or building to explore.
rating 5.0. Five Stars We all enjoyed this game. Wish they had others

rating 4.0. Definitely Fun Love this game! It really gets you thinking and is challenging at times.  Once you’ve completed it, it becomes a nice distraction to go through it again. Definitely addictive in a good way. Wish you could unlock new puzzles though. By the third completion you know it’s time to set it aside for a while to forget where the objects are.

rating 5.0. I Spy Spooky Mansion I purchased I Spy Spooky Mansion for my nieces and we all played together.  There are several rooms with clues where you need to find the listed items.  You need the Wii remote connected to a nunchuck to navigate the rooms.  Some clues require a bit of thinking (“bat” might mean the animal or a baseball bat).  There are also little mini games built in.  You can save your progress.  I think it’s a great Wii game for anyone that enjoys I Spy type games.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. A Great Family Halloween Game!!! Just in time for Halloween! This game arrived quickly! This game was not an expensive price and very affordable price. I stumbled upon this game when I was searching for Wii games. My daughter and I love this game. We love I Spy games!!! rating 5.0. DOOM for Switch is an excellent port! Fantastic all around. Seriously.

Gameplay feels solid and smooth. Takes some minor tweaking on the sensitivity and button layout - once you find your preference, though, it just feels right. Personally prefer playing split joy-cons or with the joy-con grip over handheld due to the Switch's placement of the sticks - feels slightly uncomfortable to play handheld for very long, but it works if that's your preferred mode of play. Also, the two modes of Arcade style and Campaign offer a generous amount of replay value. I haven't played the multiplayer, but I'm sure it will only further add to the long-lasting appeal of this games life on the system.

Graphics have been slightly downgraded - really only noticeable when you slow down and examine the environments, weapons, and models closely. Let's be honest, though - you won't be slowing down very often in this game. So it works and, ultimately, the downgrade in visuals helps keep the frame rate smooth. I have noticed no stutters, slow down, etc.

Overall this game fits perfectly on the Switch's system; it's quick and easy pick-up-and-go gameplay works ridiculously well with the consoles portability factor. Not to mention, Doom fills a much needed FPS role for the system. It will surely satisfy the long gap in the genre at least until Wolfenstein II: The New Colossus and Metroid Prime 4 release for the system.

Whether you've played DOOM before or are a newcomer (like me), I believe that this specific port will be more than worth your time visiting (or revisiting). It looks good, feels great, and is a technical wonder that can only further bolster future AAA 3rd party support for the system for years to come.

Thanks Bethesda. And well done.

rating 5.0. Yes - with forgivable flaws Overall - this game is awesome, especially for the Switch.

Great single player campaign with lots of secrets to uncover and items/upgrades to unlock.

Multiplayer is really fun, competitive and it's super fast to load into a game and get playing.

The graphics are pretty awesome for the Switch - it's not a cartoon game or simple sprite side-scroller.. it's a full-on 3-d environment, scaled down from it's predecessors. I have not played Doom with higher resolution, so it really does not bother me knowing the graphics are "muted". Once you get into the game, it becomes really fun and engaging and you don't even pay attention to the graphics. The bad part about the graphics is that there is no field of view (FOV) adjustment which makes things look like you are playing on a 1:1 aspect ration instead of 3:4, or that things look a tad bit narrow. I hope that Bethesda Studios integrates more options to adjust the graphics beyond motion blur and chromatic aberation.

This game is great - I would recommend it.

rating 5.0. Doom - Switch Edition; or, how Bethesda put other 3rd party developers to shame Full disclosure: I have a PS4 and i’ve never played the new Doom before. I like the series but I just wasn’t interested in trying it. However, once they announced a Switch version I instantly had flashbacks to when I rented the SNES Doom port as a kid. And I had the same exact thought I had back then: “that cover art looks so cool”. I couldn’t resist picking this up, knowing well that it was a full priced port that lacks the “good graphics” of the other consoles.

And I regret nothing. This game is awesome. I think no matter what system you get this for, you’re gonna have a great time. That said, I do recommend:
- don’t adjust the brightness when the game first prompts you to, it’s a trap. I set it so low based on the instructions that it was impossible to read the text. You can adjust the brightness later under the video option in the settings menu (it’s referred to as ‘gamma’).
- I prefer the motion blur on high (which is the default). I think it compensates for the degraded visuals. You’re mileage may vary, but I think it looks pretty darn good that way.
- I turned the horizontal and vertical sensitivity down a bit (settings > controller). Doomguy runs around like he’s on roller skates and I felt like this helped me play better.

rating 5.0. Great port This review needs to be done in two parts. First the port to the Nintendo Switch. It has been done very well. The game looks great (not as good as PS4 or Xbox), but still great. The game runs well docked or in handheld mode. The only complaint I have is the sound will occasionally get really quiet and won’t go back up until you reload the game to a checkpoint. Kind of annoying but should be able to be fixed in an update.

As for the game it is good. The game play is challenging. The story or lack of the story is fine. We all just want to be killing demons and that you will. It really reminds me of a Metroid Prime game which is good. If you are wanting a mature FPS buy this game.

rating 4.0. The first great FPS on Switch Doom on the Nintendo Switch is an interesting experiment. You are taking a graphically intensive shooter from 2016 and porting it to a console that really shouldn't be able to run it, but it does! Don't go in expecting a pretty game, however, because many technical compromises had to be made to get it on the Switch. The resolution more often than not hovers well below high definition(usually around 600p). When blown up on a tv above 27-30 inches, the game looks downright blurry and a bit muddy. The real achievement here is being able to play Doom on the go. In handheld mode(and especially tabletop mode with a Pro Controller) the game takes on a completely different light. The resolution(and even frame rate) becomes less of a problem because of the smaller screen and further viewing distance.

Doom on Switch is the full game that we got last year, except now it's portable and can be played in many different ways thanks to the versatility of the Switch. Even if you already have a copy of Doom on another platform(even PC), I would still recommend picking up the Switch copy purely because it is something we haven't yet seen on a 6 inch handheld, and it's pretty damn cool to take a game like Doom on the go.

rating 5.0. It's pretty sweet. Ignore the haters. I only play Switch in portable mode, so I couldn't rate this on a TV. Also, I've never played this game before. This is the best, most adrenaline portable fps I've ever seen. I like it and don't know what all of the fuss is about.

rating 4.0. Doom on the go plays great It's a game that's entirely about slaughtering demons, and everything in the game is built around slaughtering demons, while progressively optimizing the flow with which you slaughter demons.

The story is really stupid. Some researchers on Mars use hell to power their research station and end up getting themselves slaughtered after accidentally opening a portal to hell. But the protagonist very clearly doesn't care about the story, and takes no interest in anything happening, he's just there to slaughter demons on Mars and then go to hell to slaughter more demons when he's done slaughtering demons on Mars.

Never played any other version of Doom, only played it on the Switch. There were a few occasions where the frame rate noticeably dipped, and it wasn't even for a prolonged period of time, just several seconds at a time before everything got back up to normal. Otherwise, I don't know how the experience compares to other console versions, but I had a great time, and I loved playing it on the go in handheld mode.

rating 5.0. Compromises had to be made, but this is still a very impressive port Incredible that id and Panic Button were able to get this running as well as it does on the Switch. It's the same great game that came out last year on other systems, but it excludes Snapmap mode, but also includes all the DLC for free. Of course, there had to be some compromises to get this game running on inferior hardware, with the game running at 720p 30fps with resolution scaling where needed, but it still manages to look, run, and play just as great as other versions. Even the online multiplayer works surprisingly well here too. If you own a Switch and are even remotely interested in first-person shooters, this is a must-buy.

Just keep in mind however that even if you buy the physical cartridge of this game, the game does require a nearly 9 GB update download for the multiplayer, so either free up some space or get a good MicroSD card and you should be good to go.

rating 4.0. Great game if you limit expectations This is a great game to have for the mobile platform. They have done a good job with the port given hardware limitations of the switch. No, it won't be comparable to the computer version, but as someone who just wants to play a fun first person shooter game on a portable console, this is great. The last Doom I really played was Doom II, and this is a huge improvement vs that game yet keeps some of the character. It is a very fast paced game and standing still will generally result in getting torn up by the nearest demon.

I didn't notice any slow down issues where it became problematic for game play, but if you are one of those people that notice a frame rate drop from 60 to 30 fps, you probably shouldn't be looking at game reviews for confirmation bias on the switch platform. This is a fun game that should bring at minimum 10 or 15 hours of entertainment value, maybe more if you like getting good at it. Controls are always a bit of an issue on the consoles, as they are a bit slower to react and less precise than a mouse, but you get used to them after awhile, but will probably never reach the level of control as you could on the computer. The other issue is fonts are a bit hard to read on the mobile, being very small.

I find the game a bit stressful to play and my oldest son makes fun of me since I jump a lot or start stressing out under the onslaught of demons, but I really enjoy this game on the switch.

rating 5.0. I love this game I love this game. I've played it on a high end PC at my brother's house, and while the graphic degradation is obvious, the gameplay is barely affected by it. rating 4.0. Fun, addictive and essential FPS experience on Nintendo Switch I consider this game a must-have if, like me, you don't have a PS4/Xbox/PC that you've already played it on. I'm a few hours into the single-player campaign and I'm really enjoying the intense bursts of action and the apocalyptic atmosphere of Mars. It's incredibly satisfying to tear through hordes of demons and feel your power and inventory grow. It's a real pleasure to have a AAA first-person shooter title on a Nintendo console, and to be able to take it anywhere.

I'm having more fun with the online multiplayer than I ever imagined. You'll always want to play one more round, it is addictive. I love the variety of game modes beyond simple deathmatch and the many different weapons and playstyles that you will employ. Playing as a demon is a blast. I think multiplayer has the potential to be popular and long-lasting on the Switch because it will no doubt be the best FPS experience on this console for some time to come.

There had to be some performance cutbacks in order to make this game run on a portable console, which was an impressive accomplishment in itself. The game runs at 30 frame-per-second instead of 60 and the resolution caps out at 720p and will sometime drop to handle more stressing moments of gameplay. Those who place a big emphasis on graphics or are used to playing it on a more powerful system may be turned off by the downgrade. Personally it hasn't affected my enjoyment at all. I've been playing it mostly on my TV and it still looks and plays beautifully. In handheld mode, the main problem is that in-game text might be too tiny to read - hopefully they correct this in a patch.

I wish they included gyro motion aiming like in Splatoon - maybe they could still add it later. It has taken a little bit of adjusting to get used to the two-stick controls again. You may want to change the sensitivity and motion blur settings until you are comfortable. I've mostly played on the Pro Controller and I think that's the best way to play.

Multiplayer requires a 9 GB download. If you buy the game digitally then that is another 13 GB download. It can really eat into the Switch's 32 GB of storage so I thought this was a good time to buy a micro SD card to add more space on my console.

Despite the graphical downgrade, Doom is a massively fun and singular FPS on the Switch. Multiplayer is a blast and I expect to play countless rounds in the weeks and months to come. This game is an excellent and essential addition to the Nintendo Switch library.

rating 3.0. Great port but lacking in optimization. I've played this title on all available platforms, and , for obvious reasons, this is the lowest quality port of Doom I've seen. However, the sound in this game is very lack luster on default, especially when playing multiplayer. I will be playing this game again on the PlayStation 4 and Xbox one s to compare sound dynamics to be sure that it's not just this port of Doom that is lacking. Over all, its not worth the full AAA price since this is a port of an old game, but if you've never bought the game before and you only own a Nintendo Switch, wait for the price to drop before getting this game. rating 5.0. Don’t listen to the anti-Nintendo idiots who have Doom 1 Star. I completed Doom on the Switch today.
I also own the PS4 version.

It’s shocking that this game is running at all.
The effort made by Bethesda should be applauded and supported.

The 30 FPS frame rate after the update is very smooth and locked aside from a two intense moments. I look for this stuff.
The PS4 frame rate is an inconstant 60.

It’s a non-issue. Everyone has a locked 30 online and it’s fair. It’s reletive. It’s not like PC where some people have an advantage over others with varied rates.

The resolution is the one caveat. The switch upscales the 720p signal to 1080p.
It’s dynamically scaling, so sometimes is appears to dip below 720 while docked when there are large outdoor environments. That can flyover the game a soft look.
This is still an incredible looking game.

And this dynamic resolution doesn’t matter when playing portably.
As a portable system, it’s shocking to see how good Doom looks. It’s worth it for that.

55” 4k tv? Expect a soft image.
But again, the scale, polygon count and visual effects are shocking to come from a system threat is 1/10 the size of a PS4 and only used an 1/8 of electricity.

Doom will be a huge lan and online hit for the switch, especially in handheld mode.

rating 5.0. A must own for any Switch owner who's never played DOOM 2016 before or anybody who wants a near perfect portable port of it* Not having an XBO, PS4, or a powerful enough PC to handle DOOM, this is the first time I've been able to fully enjoy the game and was glad to pay $60. It's a fantastic port and i didn't notice much of a graphical difference between Switch and it's counterparts until I watched side by side videos on YouTube. There's a noticeable difference when you aren't moving, but in Doom if you're not moving you're probably gonna die which obviously works in this game's favor. It's now one of my all time favorite FPS's and one I definitely see myself revisiting in the future. It's a technical achievement for the Switch that I really hope gets more serious 3rd party publishers interested in releasing more games on the console.

If you've never played Doom 2016 before this is a must buy. If you have played Doom and are willing to pay the extra money to have a slightly if practically unnoticeable graphic decrease just to be able to play it portable then it's also worth a purchase

*If you don't have a Micro SD card yet, now's the time to get one because there's a 9 GB download before you can play

rating 2.0. What's the point? Minimal grip, trigger hinders gameplay. It doesn't add much to grip onto because the "grip" part is short. It doesn't fill the palm of your hand and gives you nothing to grasp when carrying it. The CTA Digital PS Vita Grip is much better in this regard.

Also, the trigger doesn't have the same movement/action like the PS4 triggers. Instead they are just extensions of the PS Vita triggers, which are essentially on or off. There's no gradient, no curve, just a straight push down. It makes it much harder to play games.

I'm not giving it one star, but the two biggest selling points don't really work for me. What's the point of a trigger grip if the trigger hinders gameplay and the product doesn't really provide a grip?

rating 1.0. Does not enhance the Vita AND makes the triggers worse. This item is a waste. A good handheld grip accessory needs to serve 2 functions in order to properly replicate the comfort of a console style controller: 1) It needs to flare out at the bottom on each side in order to bring your wrists into a more natural position (giving the "wing" shape that we associate with most modern controllers), and 2) the accessory needs to add more thickness to the back of the device in order to provide a better "grip" - giving your fingers something to curve around, thus removing the burden of holding and positioning the device from your fingers, and thereby alleviating fatigue. The PDP PSVita Trigger Grips address function 1, but fail to address function 2 in any meaningful way. The accessory lends no additional thickness to the Vita itself, leaving the system still rather difficult to hold over extended periods of time. Furthermore the trigger function, which is the accessory's primary selling point detracts more from gameplay than it adds. The shape of the new extended triggers is comfortable, but they add an unpleasant mashy feeling that really detracts from the player's response time. I would not recommend this accessory, inexpensive or not.
rating 2.0. slippery This grip was made for children or people with small hands, also is not easy to hold with both hands, you have to apply a lot of force

rating 4.0. Gorgeously Great Gaming Grip! This is a great grip for gaming, and though it may not work well for certain types of games, I haven't had any trouble playing any of my games with this grip. It feels a lot like an Xbox controller, giving the hard-to-hold vita some much needed bulk. Though I would like it to be a bit longer on the bottom, it still works just fine as it is.

It snaps on and fits well, as tight as you need it without damaging your system, and the grip is a special rubbery plastic that minimizes your chances of dropping it, which I especially liked with my history of dropping expensive devices.

The only real problems I had were the fact that my vita can't fit in my pocket anymore, and the fact that it takes a bit to get used to, about a day for me. Another thing that bothered me was, like I said before, I wish the bottom of the grip was a bit longer, along with the fact that the triggers, though upgraded to a more console-like type, felt odd when pressing, and were lowered below the original vita triggers, which was most of what needed getting used to.

Other than those small problems, most of which I got over after the first day, this is a very good grip for its price, especially when looked at next to its other, oddly-shaped, uncomfortable competition. This grip is going to last you a while, along with possibly extending the life of your system by lowering the chance of dropping, and, if you do drop it anyway, giving it a bit of extra padding for protection.

rating 1.0. Grips are too small for large hands and triggers feel cheap and nasty. Sadly, this device has failed at both of its functions for me. For one, as many other reviewers have complained about, the grips on this are too small to be of use to anyone with big hands. This really baffles me, as the entire point of a grip product like this is to make these handheld game systems with barely any room built in for holding it easier to hold for those with large hands. Such a thing is almost essential for the Vita as unlike other devices, the majority of the back of the device is a "do not touch" zone for any game that uses rear touch pad inputs. This grip is so small that playing any such game renders the grip completely unusable: you still have to hold the Vita in the same way you'd hold it without the grip to avoid having your fingers touching the rear touch pad. That is, if you don't want to inflict pain upon yourself. You can curl your fingers behind the grip which makes my fingers feel uncomfortable and even worse, causes the ends of the grip to dig into my palms which quickly becomes painful. Basically, unless you have small hands, this thing is pretty much useless as a grip.

The other feature are the triggers. Unlike the grip feature which might as well be non-existent to my large hands, the triggers actually hamper gameplay by being awful and really cheap feeling. The biggest problem I have with them is that the amount you have to press them to register an input feels vastly greater to me than what the bare Vita shoulder buttons require. This is insanely noticeable to me while playing Uncharted on Crushing difficulty. Previously I could easily hold L to peek out of cover to aim, quickly line up a shot, quickly press R to fire and then let go of L to pop back down. This is no longer possible with the trigger grips on my Vita and the game is much harder now. I'm either out of cover for a fraction of a second longer than I normally would be to take a shot and get killed (anyone who has played Crushing themselves knows how even an extra fraction of a second can be the difference between life and death), or I don't get a shot off at all due to not jamming the trigger down far enough for the game to register a button press, which generally leads to a greater number of live enemies who are now able to advance on my position and kill me that way. They should have made the entire trigger piece less tall so you don't have to pull as hard to get an input, or used more than a tiny little nub on the inside to press the Vita's button. Inside of the grip is a tiny little 5/32" by 3/8" nub that presses the Vita's shoulder button. Something with more surface area may have had an easier time of pushing the button (the Vita's shoulder buttons are designed so that you can push it anywhere and it should work, but they work better if you can press the entire thing down rather than just pushing on one end of them).

The other problem is just how cheap the triggers are. They're so stiff that every time I pull them, they feel like they're going to break or worse, damage my Vita. They're also really stiff, it takes a lot of force to push them down far enough to register anything and again, it also takes a bigger, or perhaps deeper, push to register. All of these problems with the trigger just combine to make using the shoulder buttons a much harder, much less accurate affair. It's really quite horrible, and are what earn this grip their 1 star rating, rather than the 2 or 3 stars they could have gotten as just a grip that ended up being too small for my big hands but could have had use for those with small hands. The triggers completely ruin this thing, I would not recommend this device to anyone because of them. Take a look at other Vita grips, every other grip I've seen does not cover up the Vita's shoulder buttons. Buy one of those grips and forget about this awful trigger grip.

rating 5.0. Bigger Hands? PS3 Clone Controller? Wish Granted! (Mr. Master Reviewer ^.^)

As far as the actual packaging to the product itself, this is a perfect solution for those who want to play their PSVita without being strangled down by their own hands. After hearing the good news I decided to bite after some thought. It has its ups and downs. Generally I use it for Remote Play with my PS4 when I'm home and rarely when I have my Vita on the go. rating 5.0. A Good Find I constantly saw trigger grips for the Vita in stores and kept thinking they were completely unnecessary. What was the point? Why would someone spend $20 on this? As the months passed I read so many positive reviews about the PDP Vita Grips that I decided to give them a try. I can honestly say that I was wrong, these things are fantastic!

The vita slides in very easily and locks into place. I never feel like the Vita is going to accidentally come out, although I'm sure with enough force it would. The grips feel great. They're not plastic or sleek and have a matted-like texture that definitely improves grip even for long hours of playing. The larger L and R buttons work very well, but they do need a few hours of use to work smoothly.

Games like Uncharted, Resistance, Madden, Metal Gear Solid and Assassin's Creed have become a lot easier to play since I've put this on my Vita. I highly recommend this product.

NOTE: Obviously your Vita will not fit into most cases with these on. As I often travel with my Vita I've never had a problem constantly putting the Trigger Grips on and off.

rating 4.0. Pretty Good Overall This grip fits the Vita snugly and is easy to hold. With this grip, the cramping has all but gone away, and I'm able to game on the Vita for a much longer period of time, so in that sense, the grip does exactly what it's supposed to do.  My issue with this grip is the build quality.  I've now had it for less than 30 days, and the triggers have begun to stick and make a lot of creaking noises.  This does not bode well for the future.  I'm contemplating whether to return this grip and get another one, or just hope that the triggers will never become more than a minor annoyance.  Other than the sticky triggers and questionable build quality, I am satisfied with this grip and would recommend it to anyone looking for something to help alleviate the cramps that comes from using the Vita.
