rating 5.0. A lot more than a good grip Looking for a grip for your PS Vita? Yeah, you should probably get the Dreamgear grip, which is more comfortable than this one (a friend has it). On the other hand, is this grip comfortable at all? Yes it is. I can play for a full battery charge without feeling any significant pain on my hand, thumbs; my hands has never gone numb either since I started using this product. So why buy this one when I knew that the Dreamgear grip was a little bit more comfortable (I tried the Dreamgear grip about a month before buying this)? I was intrigued by the triggers themselves and since it was cheap and on sale at the time I decided to give it a go.

So, as I opened the kinda simple packaging I started to regret getting this grip but once I held the piece of equipment in my hands that feeling went away and never came back. The apparatus is well build, it won't break easily and it fits perfectly on the PS Vita, leaving space for all ports and connections including an opened yet protected rear touch pad.

Probably the material used for the grip is a bit slippery and the controller-like ends are a bit pointy. It will take time to get used to those, but where this grip shines its at what it offers to compete with other similar products, that would be the actual triggers. If you have ever held a Dualshock, you will feel at home. The trigger are awesome, I've been playing Killzone Mercenary with the trigger grip non-stop and it feels a lot better than playing it without the grip. It is more responsive and more comfortable as well. Not only Killzone, I play now Dragon's Crown, Persona 4 Golden and mostly every other game with the trigger grip. The trigger grip has not come out off my Vita since I bought it.

So, yeah, you could find better grips for comfort, this one is not the best at that but it works efficiently in that aspect. Add that to something extra, it's a grip that gives you something more than a better gripping: Playing games on a Dualshock controller with a screen. Because this is what this trigger grip offers: A full fledged console controller experience.

rating 3.0. Has its pros and cons I've been using this Vita grip for several months, and at the time, it was the best (or only?) thing on the market. It works as advertised.

Pros:
The charging port and card slot aren't blocked with this grip, so you don't have much need to remove it.
It snaps on easy, fits snug, and stays on (mostly - see Cons).
The "grip" portion is a little better than having no grip at all.

Cons:
Every once in a while, one side or the other will snap "off" if you put pressure on it while playing. Rarely though.
The grips could be longer. I don't have "big" hands but I'm an adult, and they don't quite fit into my palms right.
The "triggers" work, but they always feel a little stiff. And over time, they leave little marks/dents on Vita's L1/R1.

After several months with this grip, I've decided to do some more searching. I don't think the "perfect" grip exists. One with longer handles and no "trigger" attachment (or maybe a better one), but one that doesn't block the charging port and card slot.

rating 2.0. Mediocre product I bought this based on the hope that it would make the Vita more comfortable to hold for longer gaming sessions, plus provide some additional protection for the device itself. While it makes a minor improvement for the former, it's rather poor build quality provides little, if any, protection whatsoever.

During game play the bottom corners of the Vita would pop out of the case, which can be mildly disruptive to controlling your game. The case itself is an empty plastic shell that's held together by a few screws, screws that tend to work themselves loose over time.

In the end, my unit lasted several months, and now is in the trash. I'd give it 1/5 stars but it did feel better to hold, if only it was more durable and the bottom corners clasped the Vita more securely.

rating 3.0. Not for me. I only used this item for about 2minutes before immediately taking it off when I had noticed that this product was peeling into my Vita's face protector. I'm not talking about screen protectors that cover just the screen, but ones that cover the entire front face of the Vita.

It "digs" into the protector from the top near the triggers, and caused it to bubble up. One side had to be removed because it was no longer secured to the Vita.

The finish is a matte black/grey on both the front and rear, but what concerns me the most is the underside of the triggers. It's just plastic there, so if you use this, you might want to put a piece of rubber our some tape, so it doesn't scuff your triggers.

If you have a full face cover, then stay away from this product. If you use just a screen protector, then you can use this with no issues.

rating 2.0. Not the worse thing out there, but the worse where it counts I wasn't all that upset when I first got this PS Vita addition. Trying to play a game that utilizes the R2, R3, L2, and L3 buttons on the back touch pad is difficult, so I was searching for a grip that let me hold my hands where it would feel less cramped.

These setbacks were not make or break for me, and it would've received 4 stars probably. However the real problem has reared it ugly face a few days later. The triggers are starting to stick and this is a bad thing when you are playing shooting games... This is why it's been given 2 stars. To me, that's a big problem.

Overall, it fits "well" in my hands and leaves all the ports open for charging, power, volumes, memory card, vita games, etc. so it's not a POS, but the sticking triggers is a real setback. Maybe I'll oil up the actual trigger grips and this will solve my problem, but be aware this is a possibility for you to.

rating 2.0. Numbness and Tingling The grip has a pleasant "rubberized" feel. That's really the nicest thing I have to say about it.

Your Vita slides in shoulder buttons first, then you snap the bottom into place like the way a cellphone case clips onto the face of a phone. Once installed, the grip feels like a natural part of your Vita. While the Vita feels secure in the grips, a couple of times I must have grabbed it at that magic spot where the leverage was just right to snap the Vita back over one of the clips that hold it in place. It only came out half way...but it was enough to make me scramble to keep from dropping my Vita.

The grips have trigger shaped buttons that are situated on top of your L and R shoulder buttons. These triggers work just fine, but I don't like the feel of them. They feel spongy (for lack of a better way to describe it). Still, I never had issues with missing a shoulder button press regardless of how I feel about the grip's shoulder buttons.

I bought this hoping that the grips would make it easier to endure long play sessions on my PS Vita. I wanted to like it...I really, really did, but using this grip puts my ring and pinky fingers to "sleep" (they get numb and tingly). My Playstation 3 controller also does this to me...so, if you have issues holding your PS3 controller for more than an hour or so, you may have problems with these grips like I do.

I have no problems with numbness or tingling when using a XBOX 360 or Wii U Pro controller, so I know it's just the design of the grips. I'm just saying, you may have issues if PS3 controllers give you problems like they do for me.

It's not a terrible product, but it's a bad product for me. I wouldn't buy this grip again...but your mileage may vary.

The Cyborg STRIKE 7 keyboard is almost perfect. I am replacing a Razer Deathstalker keyboard with their new Blade screen. Though the Blade screen is prettier, and more customizable the absence of a number pad and its shoddy construction left me wanting. I have owned every high end keyboard save from the company that makes DAS keyboards and by far the Strike 7 is my favorite. Below is a list of the pros and cons I have found:
Pro's
1-Completely Modular design. Modular seems to be a buzz work nowadays but to most companies it seems to mean t can just be taken apart and put back together. And while that might be the technical description, I do not feel that it fits. The Strike 7 not only comes apart but you can put it back together is several different configurations. The screen section can go in 2 spots or somewhere else on your desk, the keypad can go on the left or the right or somewhere else on your desk. It is actually pretty amazing AND unlike the Razer Deathstalker it has a metal frame which is anything but flexible.
2-Key feel, this is a non mechanical keyboard which is one of my only complaints. At $300.00 why not go a little further and make it mechanical. Anyway with that said it is the BEST membrane keyboard I have ever with NEAR mechanical feel without the click.
3-Lighting- I broke my Razer Black Widow Ultimate and though it had good lighting, the Strike 7 not only illuminates the keys but the entire membrane layer. SUCH A BIG DIFFERENCE.
4-Size, if you don't want or need all the add ons connected or are in a space limited area the primary keyboard is a wonderfully small form factor, and unlike many other premium keyboards which are justifiably huge, this one CAN be huge or can be small, nice to have the choice.
5-Website-Unlike Razers site the cyborg website is easy to use , navigate,and is good looking. I LOVE razer stuff but have never really liked the website beyond the color scheme.
6-The screen- I LOVE the launcher screen , you can fit 3 rows of 4 icons? And they are actually the icons on the program. The screen is MUCH more responsive and stable than the deathstalker screen. It is simpler granted, but ill take simpler and always works vs. powerful and unreliable.

Ok the fanboy section is over, here are some dislikes and criticism's

CONS(not actually cons just things I would have liked or am nitpicking)
1-Out of the box the caps/numlock/scroll lock lights did not work, a quick call to cyborg and they told me to install the drivers ( I did not in my excitement) and problem fixed.
2-The macro creation on the screen is IMO unnecessarily complicated, im not new to this feature and found it a bit of a pain. However I was doing it at 11pm so maybe im just a moron.
3-Color changing based on section would have been awesome, but seems not possible.
4-It is not a mechanical keyboard. Seriously who do I have to bribe to get a keyboard as awesome as this or the deathstalker in a mechanical version... WTF guys at 300.00 whats another 100.00?
5-Out of the box the directional keys to the left of the box control volume, and so do the controls on the screen section. I kept messing with my volume accidentally.
6-The 3 large buttons on the right hand side of the screen had their use squandered by using them to change colors.... Profile selector would have been a better use for buttons so big.
7-The large glowing circle....MAKE IT DO SOMETHING...I don't know a volume knob, or brightness level..something!

60 DAY FOLLOWUP REVIEW
After about 30 days with the keyboard here is what i have to add to my previous review
PRO'S
-THe key feel is still better than any membrane i have ever uses, NOT as good as mechanical, but far better than any membrane.
- the lighting is fantastic far better than anything else out there as both the keys and the surround glow
-the screen is good as a clock, or a stopwatch..but thats it as of right now it is a reallllly expensive clock.
-the construction is first rate except for the things noted below.
CONS
-my keyboard was missing a wrist rest and cyborg has beeen suppppppper slow to fess up
-the wrist rest with the button and wheel on it simply does not work, ive tried everything DOA
-THE SPACE BAR IS PATHETIC!!!! seriously it squeeks and clacks like a 5.00 walmart keyboard.. for 300.00 thats absolutely pathetic!!!!!!!!! it feels flimsy as hell
-the left shift key is loose and loud... seriously for 300.00 this is the construction i get?
-the wires to allow the AWESOME modular feature are NOT sold in longer lengths for those of us who would like to move stuff around.

Overall i actually like the keyboard IF IT COST 150.00 or less for $300.00 IT SUCKS!! too many damn issues and not enough functionality in the screen. It will get replaced the milisecond razer pulls its head out of it behind and makes a machanical version of its deathstalker.

rating 4.0. Nice keyboard but.... I received this product in the mail yesterday, so I haven't had time to adjust to the keyboard and its nuances. I did want to say the following:

Pro's:
Nice packaging
Love the customization options of not only the LCD launcher, but the position of the LCD and the ability to separate the number pad from the main keyboard. I also like how they included different keys for the WASD and arrow keys which allows you to personalize it more.
I like the feel of the keys over the mechanical keyboard that I've been using

Con's:
Have only encountered one (and it's really the reason I'm writing this). Driver installation and support is just absolutely horrid. The drivers did not install well on my PC (Win 8 64bit) and I just got the keyboard functioning. Attempting to submit a ticket on the website just returned me to the "submit" screen and didn't appear to send it and the support hours are 9 to 5 PST M-F (which was bad for me since that is my working day and I didn't want to take time off to install a keyboard. In addition to this, they provided a word doc on their site to 'fix' this kind of issue, but it in no way shape or form worked.

What I did to fix it:
Yesterday, I read an article regarding uninstalling the Razer software. This did not work for me.
Today (the Razer software was still uninstalled),I uninstalled the Razer Keyboard from the device manager. Then installed the STRIKE7 software. Rebooted (keyboard still did not work). I noticed that the LCD was functioning (volume buttons changed the volume on the PC), so I started unplugging all the modular pieces until it was the keyboard and LCD. I then unplugged the keyboard and plugged it back it and BAM! It started working. I then started plugging and unplugging other pieces and I noticed the LCD rebooted and the keyboard still worked. I put it all together and turned off the PC, turned it on, and still functioning.

I don't know why it didn't work until I unplugged and plugged everything in again. I'll be using this keyboard now so I'll update this post with a more relevant rating once I get used to the board.

rating 4.0. Alot of buck for a lot of bang TL;DNR : A heck of a lot you can do, pretty pricey for most keyboards, touch screen module is awesome IMO, be warned: some minor (or major) issues.
For full review, keep reading
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
This is my first gaming keyboard that I have ever gotten for myself and looking back it is quite a price. I will say that I am a fanboy of Saitech/Cyborg (now under Madcats), so my review could be bias in favor of them. Like all Saitech / cyborg gear, it feels quite modular, and allows the user to customize the layout and keys quite easily. Such as, I personally have the num-pad section on the left side of the keyboard, with the fuction strip and the screen module attached to it. I use this setup because it allows me to have all of the customized keys (labeled C1-C5 and M1-M4) along with the Control Module and the active palm rest (which has a wheel and a thumb button that can all have something assigned to it.

Now, I think the two main reasons why this keyboard can sell any quantities is this:
1) its control module is capable of darn near anything you'd want. It has embedded team speak functionality, such as names will light up when speaking, show icons for mute/disabled/ and the like; heck, it can even let you change rooms in TeamSpeak3 if you click on the room name. It allows you to have almost perfect control of your audio mixer, like the embedded windows sound from mute to 1% accuracy in the leveling. You can scroll left and right if you have multiple sources of sound, such as iTunes, steam, system, a game, and your internet explorer. There are also timer functions that you can customize to anywhere from 1 second to 59 minutes 59 seconds. There is even a stopwatch with a lap setting, which is useful when timing your commands, which is my next topic.

2)The customization of the key commands. Numbers shall we? On the function strip: 4 buttons; on the num-pad module, 5 buttons; on the active palm rest: 3 buttons; on the LCD screen: 12 buttons.
Each one of these 24 buttons (two being sneakily designed as a scroll wheel), can have any macro you can think of attached to it. Example: want to go back and do a World of Warcraft:Burning Crusade raid solo, and only have to hit one button to do the whole thing? Well, if you have the patience to do make it, there can be a macro for that. Playing Mortal Kombat and want to have a button for every finisher? You could make a macro for that. Forget a complicated Photoshop shortcut? Make a macro / key press for all of the shortcuts and you'd be set (Officially, I don't know how many Photoshop shortcuts there are, but I'd wager there are less than 72). Oh, that's right, each button can have three separate macros/commands, based off of which mode that is active. Another thought, I truly don't know what the maximum is for the macros, if there even is a max. One more thing, the software allows you to time the response of each key press / key release, so if timing (like that WoW raid) is an issue, you can still do it.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
But, with all awesome things, there is probably a drawback. I can think of four right now.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1) This keyboard is expensive. If you look at other keyboards that have programmable keys (but don't include an LCD touchscreen) I'd again wager that you'll save a good 30-60%. Case in point, the first four razor keyboards searching amazon with the text "Razer programmable keyboard" resulted in a minimum of 50% off. As for what those keyboards offer, I don't know.
2) This keyboard is massive. my Gateway - 23" HD LCD-LED Widescreen Monitor (which is probably discontinued now I can't seem to find anywhere to buy it) is smaller than the total width of the keyboard. Barely smaller, but still. With the wrist rests and the touch screen module, you're going to have to make sure you even have the room to fit this.
3) the power supply is finicky. Every time I've done a major move of this keyboard, it takes about 20 minutes of playing to get the powersupply to keep the backlight on the keyboard and the touch screen working long enough for the computer to read its inputs.
4) Some software that comes with this keyboard (specifically for the touch screen to have its functionality with system controls) has now caused problems for the fourth time, while I was writing this very review. What happens is the process runs out of control and goes from averaging about 8-12 MB of ram, it ramps up to 13GB (which is usually the amount of free ram I have when idling). I have yet to find a fix. If I find a fix in the future (like a new update or something) I'll update this review about it.

rating 1.0. Support is about as good as the item Purchased this item in May 2013.  Immediately had an issue where my system would not boot with the keyboard attached.  Contacted support on phone and there was mention of an issue where the Strike7 was not recognized as a keyboard, but once I started a support ticket it was never mentioned again.  Support closed the ticket after 7 days with no results.  I boxed the item up and put it in a closet waiting for software/firmware update.  In November 2013 posted a second ticket with similar results, although they did offer to replace the unit.  I sent it back on my dime, and within a few days had a replacement.  Plugged it in...............Same exact thing. rating 5.0. 5 Stars This is my first review, just as a precursor. I have owned this keyboard for about 1 year and I originally bought this keyboard for gaming as it is supposed to be used for and I used it on gaming probably twice. After learning how to quickly program the macro buttons and keys, this keyboard is now used for work. As in it never leaves my office unless I am working from home. The programmable keys and macro buttons sped up my productivity ten-fold. I constantly switch between the macro screen and the shortcuts menu on the touch screen. Here is a list of my pros and cons:

Pros

*Sturdy Build
*Great feel (even though it is not mechanical)
*Easy Programming
*Flexible Use (Gaming or Work)
*Plenty of Options
*Lock Windows Key option (I can't tell you how many times I have accidentally hit that button)
*Interchangeable WASD/Arrow keys

Cons

*The program used for activating the keyboard profiles has an icon in the tray for quick activation, this icon/program does not always start up. It is a mild nuisance and an easy fix; unplug the keyboard from the computer then plug it back up. Admittably, this could be a problem with the computer but I figured I should list it.
*No headphone jack (could use the USB slots for one though)

I hope this helps any potential buyers.

-Joe

rating 3.0. Uncomfortable and no microphone audio in headset. I ordered these because of the reviews, however, after receiving them, two things made these headphones impractical for me:

1. The ear pieces not only cover your ears but also muffle out room ambiance including other peoples voices, and there's no audio of my voice coming through the ear pieces so my voice sounds very muffled and unnatural when I speak. Some may prefer sealed ear pieces when trying to monitor audio in noisy environments, so if this is what you prefer, then maybe this is a 'pro' to you.

2. After wearing them for about 10 minutes, I noticed they were way too tight and became uncomfortable.

My kids have a Plantronics Rig 500 headset that is the most comfortable headset I've ever used and has vented ear pieces that allow you to naturally hear your own voice and the ambiance of the room including peoples voices, so you don't have to pull the ear piece off your ear to hear other things. And when talking, it feels natural because you can hear your own voice.

rating 1.0. Good sound, Bad microphone I figured I'd give these headphones a chance since they were only 25$.  Now, if youre looking to just listen to music, these headphones are good.  However the microphone in this headset is so bad, i cannot recommend anyone purchase these at this price.

rating 1.0. Stopped working after a few months It stopped working after a few months and I have been completely unable to find out how to contact the company for the warranty (or even what the warranty is).

rating 5.0. Great value for the money First thing that surprised me was the packaging. Nice matt black box with UV coating. It does make it feel like a higher end product. English on the box was good too, both things making it stand out compared to most asian products.
Design is sharp and clean, and construction was solid. I features a cloth covered wire, which is a nice touch. These do not feel cheap at all.
I tried the earphones with different sources.
First I tested them with my Bluray + Onkyo amp. Used Mad Max Fury Road. Quality was very crisp, better on the higher range than on the bass. But definitely full. When used for what they were designed, they were great. VERY comfortable, couldn't feel them even after many hours of gaming. I tested them with GTA V and COD:MW3. Quality was very good. I feel like noise canceling could be improved slightly... but over all these work great. I even tried them with my phone (Samsung Note8), since I use it heavily, including gaming. They worked perfectly, the only issue being the cable wouldn't fit thru the phone cover's small opening. No big deal at all.
Overall, really impressed. Do recommend them!

rating 4.0. Great Quality - But Check Your Settings! I received this product for free in exchange for my honest review

So this is a somewhat unique review since I can't use these headphones for much myself. They're actually, legitimately good headphones (well, kind of, gimme a sec), but I do some audio production as a freelancer and I have several pairs of pretty expensive headphones and monitors that are just better. Even for gaming, I have my condenser mic set up at my computer, so... Yeah.

Anyway, I want to be super clear - if you're in the market for new headphones and these are in your budget, get 'em. If your head isn't huge and unsightly, these will be the most comfortable headphones you've ever worn, and they'll sound better than anything else in your price range with a little tweaking. Now that right there is the tricky part: the tweaking.

So, again, I do some audio production. So on all my computers I have an equalizer set up in my audio drivers, and by tweaking that equalizer I've achieved a glorious sound from these headphones. WITHOUT those settings, though, these headphones sound slightly muffled and mid-heavy. So, if you're looking to use these casually as plug-and-play headphones on a system with no equalization settings (most systems DO have these settings, they're on your phone, your iPod, many computers and/or most music programs, etc), maybe give these a miss. You need to be able to adjust your EQ to get the best sound out of them.

Next up, the microphone. This is what I figured would be predictably the weakest spot, since most built-in microphones are total garbage. Shockingly, this one is really not bad, it's not tinny or muffled, it's a little quiet but not unreasonably so, and it's got great compression/dynamics. My only complaint is that the mic arm/stem/thing is super stiff, it took some doing to get it into a good position.

These headphones also have a function where they light up. I'd be way into that if it weren't super duper secondary - the audio is all transferred through the 3.5mm jack, not at all through the USB. But, the LEDs are powered exclusively by USB. So, in order to have the sound come through AND have the LEDs function, you have to plug both cables in. Which is annoying. Especially when, like me, you have a computer whereupon the 3.5 audio output you use is not directly next to a USB port. On my laptop, these are on opposite sides. On my primary desktop, they're on the front and back. On my secondary, they're next to each other - but the USB ports are dead on the front, only the ones up the side work. So I can't have my sick awesome LEDs flashing, which makes me sad. If these transmitted audio via USB and could do that AND power the unit at the same time, that'd be cool. Or, if they just had a battery option, which you could charge them up. Either way I'd be into it.

The cable on these is freakin rugged. The whole thing is built pretty solid, but I'm extra impressed with the cable, it's awesome.

The earpads are the biggest, softest, most luxurious pads I've ever felt on headphones under $100. Pillowy marshmallowy squishy delicious wonderfully soft earpads. It's kind of a shame the headband is such a tight curve, and/or that my head is so big. If the whole assembly didn't squeeze my noggin just a little too tightly, I'd recommend them for anyone with a less massive dome because I bet that to anyone else they're insanely comfortable overall. Also, as for external noise, they certainly don't let much through. They're not perfect, but they're quite effective considering the price. Much better than most regular headphones at this price point.

Like I said at the start, I'm not planning to use them all that much, but they're great backups for listening/gaming and I do recommend them to anyone looking around this price point - as long as you can tweak that EQ, you're golden.

rating 5.0. Great gaming headphones at a GREAT price! Bought these for my girlfriends' son. He's been using them for a good two weeks now so I finally asked him how he liked them and here's what he said: "I think they are an overall good headset. When I am playing games, there is the directional sound so I know where people are and know where the sounds are coming from. I also like that you can mute the mic and turn up and down the volume. The headset makes my head feel a little uncomfortable but after wearing them for a LONG time. (He's a kid that can easily play 8+ hours in a single session) Overall for the price and quality of the headset, I would recommend these to anyone that games and I would rate these overall a 9/10"

Hope this helps someone...

rating 5.0. Very Affordable Great Quality Headset My family is filled with gamers to include my husband and 4 out of our 5 kids. They always play online and interact with online gaming friends. They appreciate a good quality headset. We are Prime members and the shipping was pretty quick. We ordered the headset on January 24th and received them January 26th.

This headset has good sound quality and is noise cancelling. My 9 year old use the headset the most and I have to call him multiple times for him to hear me or sometimes I have to remove them from his ears. The inside headrest and earpads are extremely soft and durable. The earpads are labeled left and right. It also has an adjustable mic. The headset can be use with multiple devices to include iPad, cell phones, computers, mp3's, laptops, iPods and any other listening device. You can listen to music, watch movies, play games or listen to any audio. My son really likes this headset and it doesn't break the bank.

The headset not only receives great sound quality, it projects it through the mic as well. There is also a volume control feature located on the wire. Another really cool feature is the LED lights on the side of the earpads. The headset lights up when in use. This is a really great buy if you need a great sound quality headset especially for gaming.

FTC Disclaimer: I received this product/service complimentary, at a discounted price, or reimbursed for the cost in hopes that I would review it, all opinions are genuinely my own based on my experience. I only review products/service/venues (good or bad) that I think would be beneficial or of interest to my readers/ viewers.

rating 5.0. An Impressive Underdog When I received these headphones I didn’t expect them to be anything special being only $25. They came inside a mesh bag that was inside the box. I compared them to my main headphones (Steelseries Siberia) and they ended up exceeding every expectation I put up even though they weren’t much. The headphones are lighter than my main headphones and they’re quite comfortable too. Be wary that if you have large ears they might not fit these headphones. The headphones can be surprisingly loud and therefore block most outside noise, the only takeaway would be the pretty low bass that it gives off. The most impressive part is the microphone. Microphones are never the main focus for gaming headphones but this mic competed almost on par with my mains. The only problem would be that the mic is a bit too sensitive for me and picked up even the lightest of breathing with the sensitivity put at 40% on discord. The RGB is a nice effect but I never payed too much attention to it. If you need a pair of budget gaming headphones then these compete and could be better than the flagship headphones at this price.

rating 4.0. Good quality for the price Overall a good comfortable headset. The sound quality comes through nice and clear. The Mic is quieter than I'd like it to be but was able to adjust mic volume and boost in the pc settings to get it to where I wanted it. The braided cable is over 6 foot long and nice and sturdy. The PC adapter is braided as well and great quality. the inline controller makes it easy to find the mute switch and volume control. the padding is super comfortable and thick enough so you shouldn't have any discomfort from the headset it self. LED on the side is nice but none adjustable. over all build and quality are solid and will work for most peoples needs with out any issues.

Pros:
voice chat and game audio come in crystal clear
Long braided cable
Led illumination on side logos
soft ear and head pieces
adjustable
comes with adapter for PC mic in and audio out
Mic folds out of the way when not using it
volume control and mic mute on inline controller

Cons:
Mic is a little quieter than I would like

rating 5.0. Great budget headset I purchased this gaming headset as a birthday present for an XBox gamer, and I seriously considered keeping it for myself.

The construction is solid, with a decent weight and good materials. The boom mic is rigid, and folds up and down smoothly, with a touch of resistance. The padding for the ears and head are thick, but very comfortable. It's hard to say how durable the headset will be over time, but based on my initial impression, I think they will hold up quite well to daily use.

I tested the microphone on VoIP and cell phone calls, and the person on the other end said I sounded great. The audio quality is surprising good, the highs and mids are clear, the bass is present, but not overwhelming. I tried the headset with several genres of music, and they performed well with each.

The recipient liked the headset just as much as I did, and it's getting constant use. At $19.99, this is a solid buy.

rating 5.0. Great comfortable quality at a great price I am very surprised at how nice this headset is at this price point. It's very comfortable even when i wear my glasses. It weighs just under 12 ounces (not including the cable) The box included a tyvek type bag to protect it if you want to take it with you to a gaming session. The top of the headset is padded and very comfortable. I love that the earphone part is around my ears and not on them. This makes wearing them for an extended time possible. They are not noise cancelling but because they fit so well, they do block a lot of extraneous noise. I use mine predominantly for music and they are great for that. I also was please with the microphone quality with Dragon Naturally Speaking. It's now my preferred headset for that. Note - when using the microphone you do not hear the mic through the headset on a PC. I don't know how it works on a gaming system. I only have an old Nintendo Wii and don't use it there.

The USB plug on a PC seems to only be for the lights on the sides of the earphones but they look cool so i keep it plugged in.

rating 5.0. Unexpectedly good for the price, love the lights, and connectors I bought this device for my daughter, but I start testing it for me just for comparing this one versus my another 160 USD headset... OMG... I mean for the price, this one is more than awesome, let me tell you why.

# The noise cancelling headphones works amazing, note that I say headphones, not microphone :) the Microphone has the normal 3.5 mm jack microphone noise, that normal Noise is very easy to cancel using your favorite software for video production like XSplit or Camtasia
# The Headphone includes a very beautiful light set, very impressive for your streaming, the lights look cool and shiny. Like all other lights in the world they require electricity, that energy comes from the USB cable, and that is important. This Headsets aren't USB headsets, the USB cable are just for power supply, for the lights. If you don't wan't the lights just unplug the USB and everything will work fine, just no lights.
# The cable length is forme a key feature. I work from my workstation and I have other devices connected to it. So I'm in my desktop and for me is very important to have a long cable to help me connecting one thing on one port and the other thing to another device etc. Also I need to wake up from the desktop to grab coffee, going to the printer etc. The long cable give me extra flexibility.
# The Headset has a 3.5 mm single jack for audio and mic which is the typical single cable supported by Consoles, but in PC very often you need separate audio and mic jacks, the Box includes the jack splitter and I need to say that this is very importan for my streaming configuration because I do use the PC as Mic input but the Audio output comes from a separate device independent to the PC, So I can split the headphone and the Mic to different devices,
# The devices includes Microphone Mute button... seems like Ok normal thing but I need to say that I had another 160 USD HeadSet with same button but the Mic isn't 100% muted, this Jeecoo device is 100% Muted if you press the button.
# The volume control is another very interesting feature, specially because the other headset (160 USD) that I have doesn't have this and ,as I have say previously , I use another device for audio output (PC screen Audio output) that does't allow me to change volume, this devices has that included and my other 160 USD device doesn't.

rating 5.0. WOW - Best Headphones I've Ever Had Congratulations! This review should save you a lot of time and effort in looking for your next headset.

I'm a casual gamer, but I game a lot. I was looking for an affordable long-term headset. I've owned big name headsets from Razer and HyperX, but I can't believe how awesome the Jeecoo Stereo Gaming Headset feels. For the price, it doesn't get better than this.

rating 5.0. Great Headset, Great Gaming Experience and a Great Price. I don’t consider myself a hardcore gamer now, but in the years past I did a lot of FPS games starting with DOOM, Quake, Half-life, and Duke Nukem.

My first foray into multiple player games was Americans Army, the free MP game put out by the US Army that is still available today.

When playing AA with my “clan” I purchased a Triton AXPC 7.1 Surround Sound Headset for I think $130. It worked well for the game and did give a kind of a surround sound experience.

Fast forward 13 years way to much corporate work and real estate and no time to play games.
Well, that was until getting a new laptop with COD Black Ops installed for free. While my old Triton headset still worked, I was looking for a combo headset that I could use for both gaming and making calls for real estate.

Since the Jeecoo Stereo Headset had both a USB and a 1/8 mini-jack, I took a chance on it.

I have to say first off from a gaming standpoint, it’s a world of difference between laptop or even desktop 5.1 surround sound speakers.

While going through the “training” for COD Black Ops as an FPS, the headset was remarkable in its ability to immerse me into the game. I can say that after an hour of learning the game, my heart rate was about 165-175 on my heart rate monitor and that was just because of the “game” the headset added in realism with the sound.

Now I have not played in a multiplay game yet so I cant not attest to the quality of the “voice out” with it other than phone calls I talk about below.

For less than 1/4 of what I paid for the Triton, this Jeecoo Stereo Gaming Headset was worth the money.
Now for my real estate business, I have to make phone calls every day. So having a headset that can work with my Android Phone was another decision in picking this headset.

I made 20 calls with it and after each call, I asked my clients their impression of the sound of my voice and each one said clear and crisp with great tone.

So on quality of the sound out, ability to connect to my phone, laptop and desktop at this prices point, you can't go wrong with the Jeecoo Stereo Gaming Headset. 5 Starts from me at this point.

rating 5.0. High quality at an affordable price. When opening the package, these headphones speak of quality. Nifty little packing bag inside the box, user manual, and splitter came along with the headphones. The sizing clicks a little snugly, which I love because I tend to use the smallest setting and don't like it when headsets constantly open up on me, especially when in use. These will not do that at all, a huge plus. The cushioning is just right for long term wear. One of my favorite aspects was the braided cords. My last set only died because of the cord, this set will be far more durable.

Next, testing the sound:
I started with my phone. Sound quality was good, although quiet. I was worried at first, but then realized because of the noise canceling qualities, the sound didn't need to be as high. This will save my ears in the long run.
On my computer: Sound was louder and has fantastic range. I am not a sound expert, but when I play certain games, I have to discern between noises "behind" me or near or far, and this headset did so beautifully. They have fantastic balance with highs/lows and solid mid-range sound. Additionally, I can distinctly hear the different instruments in an orchestral piece, without any one section dominating or drowning out another.
Next test, microphone:
For my next test, I video called a family member to test out the quality. I was told crystal clear, and the sounds my children made from father away (and the TV in the other room) were very faint or non-existent. Loved that as they can be loud, but I still want to be heard. I didn't yell, just talked conversationally, and didn't sound tinny or digitalized. As a lady, this can be harder to accomplish it seems with a microphone.
Next test: long term comfort.
I talked for a solid 2 hours with these on. No discomfort over my ears, or on my head. It feels as if these were made for me. I will have no problems playing any games for a while, or extended phone calls.

Pros:
-Noise canceling feature is amazing at this price range.
- Sound quality is everything I ask for in headset.
-Microphone quality is fantastic.
-Quality touches throughout (braided cords, splitter included, nice quality padding).

Cons:
- Would have liked an option without the LED/usb part. I have limited usb slots, so this won't ever be used. Not a deal breaker though, and some individuals may really love this feature.
- Microphone/volume control on cord seems a little less in quality or design. The mic button "slips" easily, so I am a little concerned it might get switched to off when I don't want it to. The volume wheel seems a more flimsy part, so I worry about durability/longevity. Again, not a deal breaker.

Overall:
I love these, and would recommend them for anyone who wants good sound quality at a great price. The noise cancelling quality is also well above what one would expect at this price point. Very satisfied with my purchase, and would buy again.

rating 5.0. Excellent, affordable headset Really excellent product. The cushioning is soft and is very comfortable against the skin. The length can be adjusted easily and the headset looks very nice, particularly with the lights on. The sound insulation is pretty good, living in a city I could only just barely hear sirens and car horns honking from just outside my window. Certainly enough to cover up most ambient noise or people talking / playing music in another room. Sound quality is crisp and localized, and the volume can be adjusted easily by the scroll wheel on the cord. I've been told that microphone is very crisp and clear. It does a good job of picking up my voice consistently without having to speak up, doesn't peak badly when I do, and I haven't had any issues with it picking up ambient noise or anything. It also stays where it is set to which is very nice, it doesn't droop down over time due to head movement or anything. The cord is fairly long as well, so if you need to get something nearby in the room you don't necessarily have to take the headset off. Overall I'm very happy with it, and would recommend it to anyone who is looking for a wired headset.

current_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. Extremely comfortable for smaller people These are my new favorite gaming headphones!

Style - 5/5 : I personally LOVE the all black color with the small trim of rainbow lights. They look so slick! These headphones are large for my head but less bulky compared to most gaming headsets out there. The microphone swivels up and down easily and doesn't feel too loose or too tight. The wire connecting the headset to the computer is corded and has a nice weight to it. The headset itself is pretty light weight but doesn't feel cheap at all (in my opinion).

Comfort - 5/5 : I can wear this headset for hours without getting a headache. The padding is thick and extremely comfy. I can wear these over my glasses without them smashing my glasses into the sides of my face. My head is also on the smaller side and these are neither too tight nor too loose for my head. This is the most comfortable gaming headset that I own

Sound - 4/5 : After reading other reviews I tried playing around with the settings of my volume in game and then the sound was great. The mic is really responsive and people online can hear me very clearly. The only reason why I'd knock it down a point is because it's advertised to be noise cancelling. These aren't completely noise cancelling, but they do muffle out a lot of outside noise.

Quality for price - 5/5 : Overall I would say this headset is 100% worth it for the price. I wish I had found these a long time ago, would have saved me a chunk of cash that was spent on crappier headsets. These are definitely the best gaming headphones that I own that are under $30!

rating 5.0. Top quality VR at a great price! Lots of fun, family loves it! This VR headset is amazing, it is by far the best VR you can buy for the money! The build quality is very nice and solid, and the unit feels like it's high quality materials.

Screen: The screen on the Oculus quest 2 is very nice and clear, it has a resolution of 1,832 by 1,900 per eye. It also has a 90Hz refresh rate.

Quality: The unit itself is very well constructed and doesn't feel cheap at all. It has a nice weight to it, but is not so heavy as to feel clunky on the head. The controllers are built well and feel nice in the hands, they don't feel cheap and plastic like.

Operating system: The Oculus quest 2 uses a Qualcomm 865 processor XR2 variant and it runs very smooth. I have not had any performance issues so far and the unit has not shown any signs of slow down or throttling.

Apps and games: The Oculus quest 2 has a decent amount of content, they have quite a few games that are a lot of fun and they also have streaming apps so you can watch your favorite shows and movies on the headset in VR. I Love the rollercoaster game and the Star wars: Darth Vader game is worth the price alone.

Overall, this is a worthy VR headset. It is at a great price point, it is of great build quality, it has fun games for the whole family and it is portable. I can't say enough about this VR headset, if you are on the fence about this headset, just get it already. You will love this VR headset and your family and happiness will thank you!

rating 5.0. Even Old Dogs Can Learn New Tricks I'm a computer-literate senior. I find this fascinating: it literally opens new vistas. rating 3.0. I want to love it!! I am an FPV Drone Racing Pilot, not many know what that is, but some will, I got into it and got out of gaming, just didnt see anything in games that compare to flying until one day when a friend of mine got one of these and brought it over to my house.. WOW, When I put it on I knew I had to buy one..... and here is my review one month later...

Pro's - This has so much potential, it is a lot of fun and very immersive, many things to do with youtube and other programs that make the free part of it great..
The games are not bad priced and seem to be pretty good for the most part, plenty of titles and more coming out is great.
The daily deals and things like that make the games even more affordable..

Con's - OK, so lets start with the size, it is a bit large and a little heavy, I do understand that most of the weight is the lenses, they are pretty heavy due to how they are made, but no OLED screens for a box like this was I think a mistake, with that type of resolution even the older games would look better and a lot more clear for what their lens does.

It's glitchy, and I mean glitchy, the first one I got had a sound issue and I could no longer communicate with others on the oculus, so I turned it in and they sent another one.. But this one has many glitches as it just will go out without warning and not come back on, then I wait for a moment and it will start up again and I can play.. but many games crash and the system crashes on a pretty regular basis, now I do play a lot so? but shouldnt matter, that should be the goal..

Battery life, now this is for not only gaming but for watching movies, hanging out with friends, and trolling in a social environment, which means that you will most likely wear this thing for hours.. battery lasts about 2 and half hours of constant use.. I bought a 10 thousand MaH battery to strap to the back and it works really well, but ads quite a bit of weight to the headset. With the lipo tech these days, they should have a battery that lasts at least 8 hours.. The battery I bought lasts about 8 to 10 hours of use.

They need to come out with the super deluxe bundle package.. Many things you will want to get pretty quick... The battery, extra headset, the one they offer is not bad, but was really spendy and I got an aftermarket one for quarter the cost. carry case that will accommodate the extra large heads trap.

FINAL THOUGHT - Buy or no?

Well I do have fun with it, I find myself in it quite a bit, but I see the games getting bigger and bigger, the size of the memory is not even close to what it should be, so I got the 256, which I will be out of room soon.. I would also suggest getting the extra strap and battery as well right off the bat.. you will run out of power getting it set up then you will want to explore but wont have the power.
I would say its a good buy at 300 bucks and not bad at 400.. but much more improvements will hopefully be coming ..
I will recommend this product as it is fun, immersive and will eventually be what everyone is doing.

rating 2.0. Average toy I was very excited to get and try out the VR set. It is okay. I can think of better ways to spend 400 dollars and get more enjoyment out of it. Sound was pretty good as far as loudness, quality as average as you can get for those miniature speakers so no surprise there. I was a little disappointed with the video quality display. For the tutorials the quality of the display is pretty crisp. But if I watched a YouTube video or movie quality was definitely degraded, Pixelated at times. Not sure if it's a limitation of the headset resolution or quality of video at time of recording.

As an adult, would not buy again.
If I was a kid I can see how all the above mentioned wouldn't matter and would certainly keep me entertained.

rating 2.0. Updated review: User Experience Testing? Don't think so. VR has finally started coming of age. I am very much enjoying this device but I'm also very annoyed at having to have a Facebook account. I'm also not very fond of the way the device daisy-chains to connect to Steam, and I've had problems across the two VR systems. There is a learning curve here for sure. That said, this is a whole lot of fun.

UPDATE: lots of usage later I have discovered that this device is perfect for running a real-life emulation of what gaming was like in the early 90s.

Conflicts and drivers and incompatibilities and half-ready features and buggy, homebuilt games. Oh my. I am not sure what the timeplayed to timefixing ratio might be with this thing, but I have to admit that my 'fun' threshold has been crossed.

Here are some of the things you will learn about when you get one of these, to save you some time on Google: Oculus native vr, Oculus Link VR, Steam VR (always crashes eventually), Sidequest, Oculus developer account, VorpX, Virtual Desktop, games that support 90hz and games that do not, trying to reclaim your Steam titles from the Oculus, accidentally buying the native, link, AND steam versions of an app. The list goes on and on. Also, to me, anything that makes me take the headset off and go back to my pc is a strike against. Oculus link will fail at least 30% of the time.

Also, if you want to attempt a wireless connection to the device you have to have a very specific network setup for it to even almost work. You must have your PC connected to the router by CABLE so that the only wireless connection is from the mask to the router.
*** I accomplished this by hanging an extra access point from my Ethernet connection, bridged to my WiFi. Connecting directly to the private AP worked better in Link, almost perfectly in Virtual Desktop, but, Sidequest stopped working because you can't change the IP in there-- anyway, nothing is easy on this thing.

I'm going to file this under "really cool and almost works novelty" and go back to gaming on my 3080/240hz modern setup. I'll strap the two ancient low-resolution, low-performance monitors to my face with the snorkling mask as an experiment but I've given up on any notion of actually playing any of these titles. Most of the time you end up taking your mask on and off to troubleshoot things and never progress in the games, unless you like jaggies and blurriness, freezes and skipped frames.

Also, if you plan on getting the most out of this VR thing you need to have a VERY large, empty space to play in- you can choose to play seated and stationary in some games, but you still may end up knocking stuff off of your desk. Be careful.

Which brings me to the METAVERSE. OK, I understand what Zuck is going for. Here are some problems that will put that idea in the ground: * Headaches and nausea for everyone thanks to the low quality graphics, if you wear glasses you will have problems. *ALL of the "meta" apps are creepy and featureless works in progress. I've never had any desire to either float around as a noseless cartoon head with three fingered hands or play a hyper-realistic sex robot. *The user community is, by definition, very very young and very very spoiled. This is a rich kid's toy (or a toy for a retiree with too much time on his hands.) It takes a beefy computer to make this work. Not cheap. *Mandatory Facebook integration: I don't expect this to last. (BTW, anyone who thinks that Zuck didn't know that "Meta" means "Death" really needs some critical-thinking skill training.) There is something creepy going on here.

So the "metaverse" will be populated solely by people with money and time and huge empty play spaces who enjoy playing ping-pong with strangers on the death network. This is not an idea destined for popular acceptance. Don't invest in it. This is not ready for prime time yet, and it appears that Facebook is intent on developing the technology solely to enslave people with it. This thing COULD be fun, but it ain't there yet.

rating 3.0. Not great, not terrible Oculus Quest 2 is really great if your intention is just to play Beat Saber all day. But the reality is that practically speaking, it's a lower resolution and lower tier VR experience. Facebook's own Horizon Workspaces doesn't work very well on the Quest 2, because it has low resolution and trying to focus your eyes on your computer screen in VR Is not very fun. Immersed (another VR workspace app) is okay, and low enough latency for decent work in VR but the resolution of the Quest 2 will still bite you.

Apps like YouTube VR are okay, because the screen is practically at the optimized FOV convergence point. But the reality is that the display is so low resolution that 360 videos kinda don't look great on the display anyway. It's really a shame.

A different head strap is mandatory. This hurts a TON after wearing it for any extended period of time. Impressively bad head strap. Will leave red marks on your face.

Finally, the biggest thing I haven't mentioned is that the device itself runs Android, and it's a pretty low powered android device. Unfortunately, this means that apps like YouTube VR can lag and stutter, which really sucks more in VR than any other device. In VR, lag and stuttering means that your view suddenly freezes, which immediately creates motion sickness. Not great.

But it's an okay first experience for VR. It's a great Beat Saber machine. For anything other than Beat Saber, you're probably better off waiting for the Quest 3 or getting a different VR headset with higher resolution displays and better FOV lenses.

Basically said, too bad, your fault, here is a link to our great products that are at a great price". Imagine that, "hey too bad you aren't getting your $50, here is a link to bunch of stuff that you dont want and didn't ask for, spend more money on our website while we laugh in your face".

rating 4.0. Excellent with caveats This is better than my PSVR by heads and shoulders.

The wireless freedom, the games, the experiences all feel top notch. I am quite pleased. I am in love with Real VR Fishing and Eleven Table Tennis.

I removed a star due to a few things. These aren’t big things, but definitely things you need to consider before buying. Most of them mean hidden costs.

1. Hidden cost of having to buy a better head strap. Seriously, the one included is very uncomfortable.

2. The case this goes in is extra and doesn’t fit my new 3rd party head strap.

3. Battery life isn’t great and I will probably need to get a back up battery to make the experiences last longer.

4. My wife gets very sick using it, even in stationary games. Glad I tested this out before I got a pair of them.

5. The package is very barebones. No controller guards or no rechargeable batteries for the controllers. Crappy head strap. No case or even lease wipe.

6. Facebook account. Ughhh. Soon this will be removed, but ughhhh. Facebook is the worst thing ever. -11 stars for this alone.

Bottom line—If you want a top notch VR experience without wires and a fancy computer or PlayStation. This is the thing to get. Just be ready to spend a little more to make it a far better experience.

current_product_reviews:
rating 3.0. The best wireless option today VR is great, i've been doing it since 2016. I got the first Quest when it came out then the second when it came out. VR is much more than gaming. It's just as much educational and social as it is gaming. I'm old, so to watch video games go from Pong to the VR of today was an incredible journey.

Pros,
1) It's wireless VR, amazing, great fun and exercise.

Cons,
1) A Facebook account is required/isn't required, eventually it will be. The problem isn't so much that an account is required, it what Facebook/Meta does with the information they thoroughly collect about you, me, our friends and relatives, so everybody and how we all relate.

2) Facebook/Meta was never a Operating System company before they bought Oculus. So you end up with updates that screw things up or take features away. For example, when the Quest first came out, you had parties and other communication options in VR, but with an update, Facebook took those things away and required a data vacuum account (Facebook). Another example, the IPD setting got messed up with an update. I then had an IPD pop up in middle of the gaming view regularly. There was an adjustment you could make to disable it but required a sidequest hack every time you used the headset. There is a long thread in a Facebook forum of all the users affected by this but i think Facebook/Meta simply ignored us.

3) They stop providing controllers for your headset. For example, if you own a Quest 1, they stopped selling controllers for it a while back. If it breaks or wears out, you'r SOL. Pretty sad they don't support their customers. They should have a warning to let us know that after you buy the Quest, spare parts will only be available for a limited time. You currently can't replace a Quest 2 controller. Controllers break, things happen. If Facebook/Meta isn't going to allow third parties to develop controllers then Facebook/Meta needs to keep offering them for people who invest into Facebook's products. We all don't have Zuckerberg money, some of us scrape to get our hands on a headset. Mark might want everybody buying a headset every two years, but many people can't afford that.

I've come to the conclusion that Facebook is all about collecting our data and the Quest is a tool to accomplish that goal. I don't believe that they are serious about VR. They bought VR, they didn't develop it. The day another company comes out with a decent wireless VR headset, i can't buy it fast enough.

I rate VR 5 stars, Oculus developed something amazing.
I rate Facebook's/Meta's implementation/purchase of it, 2 stars

rating 5.0. Works Great as a Replacement PCB for Older Fightsticks on PC & Some PS4 Games This was used as a PCB replacement for a Mad Catz TE Fightstick that was not working on my PC. The quick connects that this encoder came with were a bit wide for the Sanwa buttons previously installed, but they still work. The quick connect buttons are not insulated on the tips, but if you're working a plastic or wood case this shouldn't matter much. One weird thing is that my joystick movements were detected in reverse - Up was down, left was right, etc. I had to remount my joystick 180 degrees and then it worked perfectly.

After installing the new PCB in my fightstick, I tested it on my PS4 with SF IV and it worked great, I'll test it with SF V later today and see if it has legacy support. I had to move some of the connections around and use the "gamma" Fightstick setting so that R1 acted as a heavy punch and everything mapped correctly. I did not test the turbo buttons and only wired the main 8 face buttons, as well as "start" and "select". I imagine that this works with the PS3 as well since it was working on PS4.

I am unsure if the PCB for the Zero Delay has any markings on the rear that tell you what button should be plugged in, but after looking online I found the following button mappings: I'll cross reference this with my own wiring once I open up my fightstick again.
Starting on the left of the bottom row of the PCB moving right:
1. Triangle (Medium Punch)
2. Circle (Medium Kick)
3. X (Light Kick)
4. Square (Light Punch)
5. R1 (Heavy Punch)
6. L1 (L+M+H Punch)
7. R2 (Heavy Kick)
8. L2 (L+M+H Kick)
9. Select
10. Start

My PC had no problem registering the fightstick and it worked well on MvC3 after using a custom button mapping.

If I come across another cheap TE fightstick, I'll likely replace the PCB with another of these Zero Delay encoders instead of using a much more expensive Brook PCB.

Was looking for a cheap option to replace my failing pcb in my 2-3 years old madcatz fightstick pro, very easy to install this thing all included cables are plenty lenghty. As others have stated I had to turn around my sanwa jlf pcb because I was getting backwards inputs trying to hook it up the default way, no big deal though just remove the gate n turn the pcb over to the other side and problem solved, another thing this device should default to digital input because every time it loses power it returns to analog and unless you got extra buttons its kind of a pain in the ass (i made lots of modifications to my case to where I can open the old wire compartment and I taped the ground and wire close by with some slack in one to quickly switch over to digital inputs when I need too) really hoping this thing lasts the test of time as I am rough with my sticks.

rating 5.0. Had an old SF PS2 fight stick that I couldn't ... Had an old SF PS2 fight stick that I couldn't use anymore. Ordered this PCB to see if I could breath new life into the stick.

Works perfectly on the PC. I had to do some soldering since the metal receptors on the stick's buttons did not fit on the stock brackets that came with the PCB, but it works perfectly.

10/10

rating 1.0. This is an incomplete kit that is not out-of-the-box ready ... This is an incomplete kit that is not out-of-the-box ready for use as a MAME controller. It DOES NOT come with a 5PIN wiring harness for SANWA JLF5H controllers. The SANWA-JLF5H-HARNESS that comes with most SANWA controllers is not ready for this kit, unless you want to solder the wires directly to this board. You will need to order this separately!

rating 5.0. Works great in my diy desktop arcade machine Works great in my diy desktop arcade machine.  If you run hyperspin you will need to get their add on script so the joystick will work with the menu wheel.  Go into your emulator and set the controls to your key bindings.  Joy to key app might be useful too.  I didnt need it however.

rating 5.0. Works just fine for my button box I made a small button box for use with Sim Racing. I used the included wires but cut the connectors off the ends and soldered them to my buttons. Plugged it into my PC running Windows 10 and it recognized it as a gaming USB device/joystick and worked perfect from the get go.

If I ever need to make simple USB button or switches again I will buy another one of these.

rating 2.0. Over 100ms of input lag I finally got around to using this. I bought it because it was advertised as “zero delay” but when I use it with my ddr pad it has over 100ms of delay, making it unusable for rhythm games.

If you just want this as a controller board and don’t care about speed, it will work fine. But it’s too laggy for dance games and probably other fast games as well (fighter games, platformers etc)

rating 5.0. Perfect! Purchased this to convert an older iCade arcade controller to a generic USB arcade controller...  For those that are unfamiliar with the iCade controllers, they were proprietary iPad controllers and wirelessly sent 'keystrokes' to iPads instead of controller commands rendering them useless on anything but iPads...  I replaced the wireless keystroke control board inside the iCade with this USB controller, installed the USB cord and presto, I now have a wired USB arcade controller that works on any USB device...  I'll be buying two more when I finish up my full sized arcade emulator cabinet to use for the controls...
rating 1.0. Got new version instead of old and it didn't work as old version Somehow the new version is so terrible to use for button box.

rating 5.0. Works Well for Android NVIDIA Shield TV I took a gamble to see if these would work better than the Xinomotek dual controller usb encoder, as that one was giving me issues on my NVIDIA shield emulation arcade with hyperspin (wouldnt show up as two controllers, just one and the button mapping was all over the place). I purchased two of these instead and used a USB splitter to hook them to one USB port and it worked great. Wires were a little short, barely reaching some buttons but not worth docking points since the price was awesome. I have two independent controllers in the system with all 12 buttons and sticks. For those interested in the NVIDIA Shield mapping for these devices:
K1 = X
K2 = Y
K3 = A
K4 = B
K5 = Z
K6 = C
K7 = L1
K8 = R1
K9 = L2
K10 = R2
K11= LeftThumbButton
K12= RightThumbButton

rating 5.0. Best console ever made to date! There is no learning curve exspecially it you've used an Xbox One. Setup will take a few mainly due to an system update. After that if you have 4K TV you'll want to check your settings to make sure 4K output is selected. Also, if upgrading from Xbox One / Xbox One S, using an exturnal hard drive will speed up and reduced bandwidth usage of having to download games and apps again. You can also use Network Transfer and copy games from your other Xbox.
Performance is unmatched by any other console available today and for the next few years. You will notice reduced load times for all your games you own currently. (I can't say this will always be the case as game devs truly take full advantage to this power house).
Games look amazing and yes even games that haven't been patched yet for the X. It is a nice and clean looking console. If you have a game that supports HDR and 4K you will not be sorry. Absolutely amazing well worth the money!

rating 4.0. Great console! A very impressive piece of hardware! The power in the box is apparent as soon as you boot it up. The several games I have played so far (AC: Origins, Middle-Earth: Shadow of War, Battlefield 1, and Bioshock: Infinite) look absolutely stunning! Seeing pictures and watching videos on the internet of what this console can do serve it no justice.. you seriously need to see it with your own eyes! There is a lot of untapped potential in this console for gaming, but that will fall squarely on the developers shoulders, and since Microsoft's first-party AAA titles have been lacking this year there are only a handful of titles that truly take advantage of the hardware. But, that will change and continue to grow into 2018 and beyond.

rating 5.0. Great upgrade if you have an older XBOX ONE. The XBOX ONE X is a great product, and I am happy that I upgraded from an older XBOX ONE. It runs smoothly, quietly, plays 4K Blu-Ray, etc., supports HDR, but what I have found so unexpectedly great about it is that it plays Amazon Videos flawlessly and much better than even an Amazon Firestick. When developers can take full advantage of its capabilities it will be the console to have.

rating 5.0. Better than expected The Xbox One X is amazing. It is so much better than I expected. 4k with HDR gaming is the future even old games like Gears of War 4 and Halo 5 feel like new experiences due to how powerful the X is.

All games run better, all games look better and games even have faster loading times. The X is so impressive of a console and worth every dollar. Would highly recommend.

What is probably most impressive is that you don't need a 4k TV to get the benefits. It even looks better on the 1080p TV in the living room! However to get the most out of the console, I do recommend having a 4k TV which you can pick up for a very reasonable price right now.

rating 5.0. Controller issues. Having to troubleshoot with Microsoft. So far, I am not impressed by the system. I was nervous upon seeing the issues people were having when they bought their system.

Cons so far.

1. The controller will not function properly. It keeps moving to the far right of the menu and sometimes it is non-responsive. I tried to sit infront of it, so the sensor would properly see the controller. No go. The batteries said they were full charge, and less than one hour, were dead. I replaced with new batteries, and I am still having controller issues. Maybe it is the fact it is Wireless controller, thus they do not work properly. I tried to hook up a corded X-box 360 controller, hoping it was backwards compatible. It was not.

The controller is a big issue for me as it effects everything you do on the system. I am thinking about returning the system.

2. I updated the system. Bad idea. Everything messed up with the controller when I updated.

== UPDATED == 11-30-2017

I spoke with Microsoft Xbox Support about my controller issue. They suggested to use a USB cable from your cell-phone to plug into the controller and the Xbox. Apparently, when the system downloads new patches, the controller also needs to be updated. The only way to update the controller is from a USB cable. As odd as that sounds, everything is working.

rating 5.0. Amazing As an owner of a PC with SLI GTX 1080 Ti, I have no difficulty in saying this Xbox hands down provides the best living room experience. Nothing comes close. 4K streaming, 4K Blu Ray, 4K high end PC quality gaming in the living room.

The only complaint I see people have is with the price tag. Honestly, I find that ridiculous as $500 is a steal for how powerful this box is. You'd need a PC where the graphics card alone is $400-$500 to run games like the Xbox One X does. You absolutely get more than you pay for out of this console if you care at all about the visual fidelity of your living room games.

To summarize, I have nothing but positive things to say about the new Xbox. The build quality is superb, the box is whisper silent, and the box is rock solid stable. Xbox 360 and Xbox OG backwards compatibility. All Xbox One games(including non enhanced ones) run noticeably better on this- they don't drop frames where the old Xbox would and games with a variable resolution stay locked at the highest resolution. 4 free games per month that you get to keep forever simply by having Xbox Live. Get yourself a huge external hard drive and you're good to go.

rating 5.0. Exceptional product for the price I bought this primarily with the intention of using it to play video games, but I've found myself using it quite a bit to stream 4k content from Netflix and watch 4k blu-ray discs.

As a game console and an entertainment hub, it does phenomenal. I had an Xbox One prior to this and I'm really glad I upgraded, it was well worth the money. New games look absolutely stunning in 4k (Wolfenstein The New Collosus is a personal favorite) and older games play in a much higher framerate, at a better resolution, with drastically reduced loading times. Load times for games like ReCore took almost 3 minutes on my old Xbox One; now on my Xbox One X it is about 20 seconds or less.

Beautiful, compact system with an excess of power and a 4k Blu-ray player built in. I couldn't be happier. 10/10 recommend this system. (Goes without saying, to get the most out of this system you should have a quality 4k TV connected to it).

rating 4.0. 40% More powerful than any other console, Full 4K... whats not to like? I was an early adopter of the Xbox One original. I liked all the functionalities that it offered, but honestly i had a lot of problems with it. It was slow, loading my tv guide took forever... to the point i thought it froze. it would lose connection to the internet randomly. And admittedly, maybe it was just mine. So the Xbox one S came out and i passed it up, as i figured eh, still not really the console i wanted. In comes the One X.

40% more power, full 4K. Its fast, games look amazing, channel guide actually loads fast, this is the Xbox One that should have come from the start. I like this console so much it convinced me to purchase a 4K TV to go along with it. I watched some Amazon 4K streaming, played Forza, and Assassins Creed. Not a single hiccup during these play times, this machine handled it like a champ. It also now has the power supply built into it, so no more power brick. Just a simple removable cable running from the Xbox to your wall.

I am taking 1 star off because of two small problems i have had with it so far. I was having trouble getting my controller(s) to start and connect to the console randomly one morning. Held them right in front of it, walked around the room... nothing. Simply did a hard reset, and everything was back to normal. Similarly, Wifi constantly kept dropping one time... i'd connect and it would drop within 5 minutes. Only thing that solved that was a hard reset again. I've only had the system for about 5 days, so i was a little surprised already to be having issues.

Besides those easy fixes, the console is a beast, and i love it. If you have any questions please feel free to drop a comment and i will get back to you !

rating 5.0. Just as good and in some ways better than the first party controller! Great price on this and the quality is on par with the NES Classic first party controller that comes with the official unit. The buttons and directional pad are super responsive and have a nice satisfying feel to them when pressed. The plastic of the body and buttons feels perfect and I love the design and texture on the face artwork. The start and select buttons are rubber like the original NES controller as well. Overall excellent quality. It's nice that the cable is 6' as well, compared to the way-too-short NES Classic first party controllers short 2' cable.

My only minor issue would be that the A and B buttons are convex instead of concave, but this doesn't affect anything aside from my personal preference for an NES controller.

rating 5.0. Portable and Perfect Size When I first tried using the original Switch I felt that it was too large in the hands and had a considerable weight to it which took away a lot from the handheld experience. I’m happy to say that the Switch Lite fixes all of these issues thanks to its lighter and smaller overall footprint. It’s a delight to play with and the battery life seems to be about as good as advertised. I’m getting an estimated 3.5-4 hours with Link’s Awakening which is a fairly demanding game. The screen is also extremely bright and looks absolutely beautiful. The colors are also a lot more muted than I had previously thought they would be. The Gray and the Turquoise both look very nice, though I like my gray one the most because of the nice contrast with the buttons. Speaking of the buttons, they feel extremely good. The buttons are more “mushy” than the regular switch but feel very satisfying. The inclusion of the d-pad is also a welcome addition and it feels just amazing. It’s definitely the best d-pad I’ve ever used. The d-pad has the just the right amount of tactile click and mushiness making it a dream to use. If you can forgive the lack of TV output, this is the best handheld gaming console there is and in my opinion the best Switch there is currently.
