rating 2.0. Very Limited This is not the next step forward in The Sims - It is a sideways step for the series. It doesn't overcome the technical limitations of the previous games and in many ways it regresses.

I would suggest you watch the video from LGR - The Sims 4 Review if you want to see what it's like.

The Sims is supposed to be about telling our stories. I have found it very difficult to tell stories in The Sims 4 due to the limitations of the game and the creative expression they have removed from the game. I'm just too limited now.

CAS:
Creating Sims is fun but the lack of a Color Wheel is killing me. You are limited to and stuck with the predefined colors EA decided to give us. And not all clothing has the same color options. I looked at a blouse that only had 3 color options but a pair of pants had 10 color options.

Build/Buy Mode:
There are lots of things for you to spend your money on and building houses is easier than ever.
That said you are unable to be creative and make the house you just spend an hour building the color you want. No Color Wheel! You cannot change the color of anything. This leaves us with rooms that are nice but creatively not what we want. If I want to tell my Sims story in a room with exclusively Pink furniture it's not possible.

There are no dishwashers anymore or sprinklers for gardening which makes a large garden very difficult.

Oh and you better spend your money on things that are unbreakable because they got rid of the repairman!
That's right - no repairman and with just two Sims my toilet is breaking every two days. I bought the unbreakable toilet, it's expensive but a must have.

Terrain Tool:
Gone. You can paint the ground but that is it.

Pools:
Gone. You can create purely decorative fountains that take up space and have no Sim related interactions but no pools.

Basements:
Gone.

Travel:
You must now travel to leave your home lot. There is a boring white screen with a green Plumbob you are forced to endure anytime you want to go anywhere in the game. And the time of the loading screens is random. I have had it take anywhere from 10 seconds up to a whole minute to "travel". The world is very limited and closed in.
You can see your neighbor across the street but you cannot walk across the street to their house for a visit.
After a hard workout at the gym if you want to go next-door to the Club and have a drink you cannot walk the 10 steps it would take your Sim to get there. This is ridiculous!

I have found that The Sims 4 is not balanced gender wise at all. My single female Sim when to the Club to meet cute men. They game generated 4 women. I took her to the park only to have the game generate 10 women. This would have been fabulous if I was telling that story but I wasn't. The game needs more balance so you don't end up with more of one kind of Sim then others.

Community Lots:
You can Travel to the Park, Museum, Club, Gym and Library. Yes, that it is. There are 4 community lots and 1 park in each world.
The following Community Lots no longer exist: Supermarket, Book Store, Pool, Spa, Graveyard, Stadium, Military Base, Stadium, Restaurant, Theater, Hospital, School and Science Lab.

Cars / Taxis:
There are none. That's right, no cars. Any vehicles of any kind that you see are just decoration.
Cars don't even pick you up for work anymore!

Babies:
You no longer hear that special music when your Sims try for a baby. Now the female Sim must go into the bathroom and take a pregnancy test at the toilet.
(Personally I liked the baby music. You knew if you needed to try for a baby again without having to leave the bed)

There is also no longer a way to influence the gender of babies anymore. They took away the ability to eat Apples for a Boy or eat Watermelon for a Girl. It's all up to chance now which is very true to real life but limits stores you can tell. I can no longer have a woman that tries and tries for that baby girl only to end up with nothing but boys - hehe

Babies are now stuck in a bassinet until they turn into 10-year old children. You cannot walk around your house with the baby. All interactions must be done through the bassinet. And in a very creepy display of growth hormones gone out of control one day the baby jumps out of the crib and turns itself into a 10-year old Sim.

Toddlers:
There are no toddlers. That age group is no longer a part of The Sims

Teenagers:
Teenagers look exactly like Young Adult and Adult Sims. You cannot tell them apart. Apparently they just couldn't be bothered to make Teenagers shorter. This is makes for very awkward situations when you are looking for a Sim to hook up with. I was completely creeped out when the Sim I was looking to hook up with turned out to be a teenager.

Control Panel:
We now have 5 different control panels we must work with to get things done. (we used to have one that did everything)
1. The control panel in the bottom left corner shows pictures of your household, what they are feeling, any actions you are preforming and the cell phone. (I did notice we can have less actions queued. We have gone from being able to have the Sim performing one action and having 7 more queued to being able to have the Sim performing one action and having 5 more queued.

2. The control panel in the bottom middle is where you will find the time controls of Pause, Play, Fast Speed 2 and Ultra Speed 3.
Missing and no longer available is Ultra Speed through Current Action. (this option was my favorite and I used it exclusively so this hurts my gameplay a lot)

3. The control panel in the bottom right corner is where you will find Aspiration, Career, Skills, Relationships, Inventory, Simology and Needs.
And in a very odd extreme move of micromanaging in Simology you can see how many times your Sim has used the toiled, showered, etc. Is that something people actually want to track???

4. The control panel in the top right corner is where you will find Build Mode, Gallery, Up/Down Floor, Walls, Camera Controls, Notifications Wall and the Options Menu.
I will tell you that for Camera Controls are only options are now Zoom In/Out and Pan Left/Right.
They have removed the Tit Camera Up/Down options. (this is killing me, I like to view my Sims at a certain angle and I just can't get to it with out being able to Tilt the Camera!

5. The control panel in the top middle is only shown when you enter Build/Buy mode. This is where you will find the Hand Tool, Eyedropper, Sledgehammer, Design Tool (don't get excited it only gives you option to change something to one of the limited predefined colors), Undo, Redo, Lot Info, Save to My Library, and Move house.

Wow that is a lot of Control Panels!!!
They take up a lot of space on the screen and as a player your hand is moving that mouse all over trying to get done what you used to get done with 1 Control Panel.

Controlling your Sims:
This you will be very familiar with. This is much the same as before except that every time you select an object or Sim with your cursor it will have a White Chalk Outline on it.
If you Travel with one Sim and leave another at home to work on a skill you will no longer be able to Control the Sim at home. In fact the Sim you left at home will stop performing all tasks. I took a Sim to the Park to fish and I left anther at home playing the Piano to work on the music skill. After fishing for a bit at the Park I switched control over to the Sim at home and after a Loading Screen the Sim walked onto the Lot from out of the void and was standing on the sidewalk.
You cannot control two different Sims in two different locations at once!!!

Careers:
The Careers available to Sims are Astronaut, Criminal, Culinary, Entertainer, Painter, Secret Agent and Writer.
That is it. If you had a favorite Career you liked to play previously like Business, Medical, Science or Sports you are out of luck.

Oh, because a car never arrives to pick up my Sim with a little honk I never know when it's time to go to work. My Sim has been late 4 days in a row and the boss called to say she will be fired if she is late again.

Emotions:
This game is all about emotions. In fact I would say this game is Emotion Overload
Everything your Sim does makes them feel something now. Sometimes you just want your Sim to read a book to level a skill and you don't want to have to deal with them being BORED over it.

Ok, I'm going to stop now.
Is the game fun? Yes? It is worth the money? NO!!!

rating 5.0. GREAT game! Lots of entertainment! I love this new version of the Sims. Yes, there could be more added but those will come in future expansions but I absolutely love the multi tasking, which they can do up to three things at once so long as they don't clash with each other. I'm able to skill or write books and have them listen to the stereo at the same time to get their fun up AND work. It's so much easier to manage your sims now. The loading screens are super fast and because they make the game run smoother and faster, I'm all for it The babies are tied to the cribs which makes them super easy (I always thought they were annoying) and I was disappointed there is no toddlers but all in all this is a really great game. Sims 3 couldn't hold my attention and lagged like crazy but the Sims 4 runs smoothly on Ultra high settings AND I've already logged 50+ hours and still haven't gotten bored with it.

rating 1.0. Origin... enough said First piece of software that I refused to install. Origin is DRM and it is intrusive. I read though a good portion of the Origin End User License Agreement and it wants to collect data beyond the license key authentication. I don't do online gaming or download games from digital content stores so I have no use for Origin other than for authentication. If it was used just for authentication then I would accept the terms and install the game.

I tried the create a sim demo on a friends PC and I really was not all that impressed.

Guess I'll stick with The Sims 3. At least installing Origin is an option there if you buy the physical disc. EA will get no more money from me again for PC games.

rating 3.0. I spent my first few times of game play going from disappointed to down right irritated by things it lacked For anyone who has played previous sims games, Sims4 is disappointing in many aspects. I spent my first few times of game play going from disappointed to down right irritated by things it lacked, having said that, the new tools for avatar creation are good and many will find the new tools for building a plus as well.  If you are expecting everything to be an improvement over Sims 3, think again. Lots of missing content such as food processors ( in Sims 4 they are forever cutting themselves ), dishwashers, and swimming pools to name some.  Career changes may be disappointing for some ( I hated that the business career was gone as well as singing ) but now your sim can be a stand up comic or an astronaut.  One thing I very much disliked was that although I gave my sim the "neat" trait, my sim cleans the wax out of their ears while making salad than proceeds to toss salad with their hands, just gross.  Don't expect the game to be bug free, having said that, EA is pretty quick to fix bugs as they are discovered.  To me the game seemed over priced, in my opinion, about twice what the game play is worth.

rating 5.0. The Sims 4-The Positive Review... I have been playing the Sims games since the fall of 2000. To date, I have every base game and nearly every expansion. Negative reviews are very common when a new base game is released. Unfortunately, avid Sims players are hoping that the new basegame will be an extention of the last release with more "wow" factor. This has never been the case and this release is no different. Its clear to me from the barrage of bad reviews that this is what players were thinking they were getting. For the record, this is NOT an extension of the Sims 3. Its a whole new game.

My daughter and I have been having a blast with it. Sure, there are no pools and no toddlers and no cars, not yet anyway. But those missing elements where completely forgotten the first time I had my sim go visit a neighbor and they stood on the porch and drank a cup of coffee and talked. The animations were incredible. From that moment on I was in love with this game. This base puts a focus on the interactions between Sims to OTHER SIMS not just a sim and his or her environment. Although, the interactions with their environment are much improved too (does anyone remember when Sims didn't have fingers?!?!?) Not only do they have fingers but they work realistically which is really cool. My daughter and I find it fun that our families can visit each other (I moved a criminal in next to her snooty sims Muhahahaha) You can go from map to map too which is nice. Although I miss cars, the traveling feature is ok. I don't necessarily like the reloading in between but its a small inconvenience. I do like the fact that sims who live together can go to different places and you can easily put one on autonomy while you play with the other. I see mischief brewing with those cheating sims!

From a playing standpoint, I really love the way the skill building works. It doesn't seem to take as long for one thing. This means more time "hacking" or "trolling the foums" or having coffee with the neighbor. Their needs seem to be treated a little differently too. It doesn't seem to be such a crisis when they have to pee but the "I have to pee" walk is hilarious. The facial expressions are great too and the movement with hands while they talk. The way they retooled their movements all around is facinating to me and its great fun watching them.

I was a bit clumsy with Create a Sim and continue to be a little but I'm getting the hang of it. This is partially me as I've never been a huge fan of Sim Creation as it is but that is because I favor the building element. I think it takes less time now to design a nice looking (or not so nice looking LOL) Sim. I also like the fact that you can change the walk. This is one more thing that makes the sim even more individualized. There a so many little and big tweeks you can make that I think even if you wanted to make two sims look identical, it would be difficult.

Building is taking a little bit of training on my part. This has always been my favorite part of the game and every new base game brings some interesting changes. I miss the pitch forward and back with the camera but I like the snap to location feature and the ease of turning the camera by clicking and twisting the mouse. You have the option of switching to the Sims 3 camera operation if you wish. Its a handy feature. I chose not to use it since I still have the Sims 3 available if I want to play that. I noticed immediately that since this is a base game only, building a nice environment is easy and quick. This is due to the limited amount of items you can lavish upon your spoiled sims. However, I strongly encourage everyone to play around with it. The different wall heights and the split grid are a couple of things that give builders an opportunity to build unique homes for their sims. I was able to build high walls in my last house which was a single level. I added overhead cabiinets and put pictures and lights above them. This gave a depth the room I never noticed in previous games. with the grid split into smaller sections now, you can put things like windows at different heights. If you love to build like I do, then you will find many uses for this little feature. I love the menu devoted to "Clutter." This was a genius addition. Thank you for that!

In conclusion, I believe that once people actually get into PLAYING the Sims 4 rather than complaining about it, you will be pleasantly surprised by its uniqueness from its former counterparts. Its a lot of fun and it has given my daughter and myself a lot laughs. Experiment with it! You will find a lot to love.

rating 1.0. This game plays like a kick in the gut. I'm going to say this: the Create-a-Sim is the best feature. THAT'S worthy of being called Sims 4. Sims 4 CAS lives up to what a sequel should be: Improvement. The rest of the game is a Facebook app. Yes, EA is charging people $50-80 dollars for a Facebook app.

I've played this series since the first. I thought Sims 3 was the worst out of the 3, due to the bugs, glitches, and patches that did MORE harm than good. I thought Sims 4 was supposed to make up for all the fail Sims 3 gave us and -eventually- fixed.

My God. I was so wrong. This game is such a disappointment... it hurts. I don't know which is more painful-- the fact that EA didn't even TRY to make a sequel, or the the that EA is still greed-hounding plans to charge fan-sheep for the things they removed from Sims 1, 2 & 3.

Between EA's gimmick to delay the press versions of the game so the reviews could be late and mostly bribed, this company has proven to be d***ish on a comedic level.

The only good reviews (besides EA plants) for this game consist of people new to the series, or have crap PCs that couldn't handle Sims 3--- So yeah, Sims 4 would be a great game to them. That's what Sims 4 is created for. That's fine for them.

I'm not either of them. More than 50% of my gaming library is from EA, and I would buy their games without hesitation, but after Sims 4, Dragon Age 2, Mass Effect 3, Battlefield 3... I can honestly say: "I am officially done with EA."

If this game came out after Sims 2-- I'd love it. If this game came out as some sort of Sims 2 HD Remix, I would've fallen in love with it.

No, this game is Sims 4-- a sequel worst then the WORST Sims game in the entire series (aka Sims 3). They removed the pools, they removed the toddlers, they removed the color wheels to make things easier for Sims 4 programming-- and yet the sleeping babies, the grim reaper, building mode, and the rest of this "improved & more intelligent" sequel is even wonkier than Sims 3 on it's launch. The controls are horrific. The Facebook UI is just the worst. You can't even leave the house without a loading screen. Of course the Sims are smarter. The game got dumber! The graphics at Ultra High look like something from the Nintendo 64. You no longer have a world, you get Pangea, a bunch of plates where your Sims live. There is no Story Progression. No choices of patterns or customization.

So, I'm guessing Sims 5 will no longer have sleep, The Grim Reaper, Build mode, and 8-bit graphics?

If you HAVE to get this game, then wait until drama ends, and EA goes cold turkey on whatever gave them the "idea" to create & release Sims 4. Better yet, just download the Demo, the Create-a-Sim feature is the best part of the entire game anyway.

I returned the game, just looking at that thing made me regret ever buying the series.

rating 5.0. I think this game is a lot of fun. I loved The Sims 2 I think this game is a lot of fun. I loved The Sims 2, and disliked the The Sims 3 intensely, because of the way the Sims themselves acted. It was like pushing robots around the screen. So, The Sims 4 really seems to pick up where The Sims 2 (T2)  left off, and I'm having a lot of fun. Granted there are a lot of things cut in this game from T2 & T3, like toddlers and pools. But you know what? The Sims are so fun and interesting, it makes up for the lack of the kids. I don't really care about pools, they look nice in your Sim's yards, but I'm sure we will get them in a patch or expansion. Anyway, if you liked The Sims 2, I'm pretty sure you'll like The Sims 4.

rating 5.0. Addictive I know a ton of people have complained about the Sims 4 because of the lack of toddlers and pools. rating 2.0. Dumbed-down disappointment destined for the bargain bin I have been an avid simmer since Sims 1, and even dabbled in creating custom content. Sims 3 was such an improvement over Sims 2, which itself was a huge leap forward from Sims 1. So of course I had to get Sims 4, with great anticipation of the improvements. HAH! I'm now furious that I spent eighty bucks to preorder the Premium Edition Sims 4 and ended up with something akin to Sims 2 for console. It is literally more like an app game.

Everything we loved about Sims 3 is gone! I remember in Sims 1 and 2, the hours wasted on custom content sites, downloading yet another recolor of a dress just so my new sim could have something different to wear. Sims 3 made any recolor possible, for clothing, furnishings, etc. A truly customizable world that sim fans wanted. Well forget that in Sims 4! Nope you will have a handful of color options to choose from for each clothing or furniture style, no color wheel or patterns to customize, and remember the lazy color-blind creators that worked at Sims 1 & 2, who gave us the mint green fridge that didn't quite match the dishwasher? Yep, those design nightmares have returned, so good luck finding things that actually go together! At least we had a dishwasher in Sims2 even if it didn't exactly match the other appliances. No dishwasher in S4, no cars, no laptop... What is the point in giving our sims outfit options (5 possible outfits can be created for each type, ie everyday, formal, etc.) that our sim will randomly choose to wear, when the choices are so limited? Even the hair and makeup that we used to be able to customize to any color, with highlights etc, nope you will have a handful of fake flat hair colors and preset makeups to use. I'm sorry but the removal of the colorwheel and pattern choices, absolutely no valid excuse is acceptable EA! OK so you can pull on the sim's body and shape it, that is cool, but you can't do it to the hair and those choices are ridiculously limited. Frankly I could use the Sims 3 create-a-sim sliders to create as unique of a look that I needed for my game.

Then there is the personality dumbdown in Sims 4. No more favorite colors or foods, and the choices of traits are very lacking. Remember the fun of having an insane, neurotic sim who was afraid of the dark? All you get now is insane. Remember having the ability to make a custom life wish? Pick from a handful now. Ok, maybe I can forgive all that and just enjoy the game play. What the...? Where is the world? It is smaller than the original Sims 1 neighborhood! And you can't even go outside of your sims little area of about 6 lots without TRAVELLING -- remember that dreaded load screen wait in Sims 2 whenever your sim wanted to go visit a friend or the library? It's BAAAAAACK! Seriously? On my system it isn't the 'go make a sandwich and take out the trash' wait of Sims 2 load screens, but why remove the other best thing about Sims 3, the seamless open world?? And forget customizing the world, no world edit is available at all. There are so few empty lots, the new building options are really pointless because there is virtually no place to build anything. If this wasn't enough, I was shocked when my sim went to work, and I couldn't even click on her to view her simology or to see what was in her personal inventory.

In Sims 4, 99% of the game has just been dumbed down to nothing. In game play, the few positives are that the sims can multitask, and you have more family relationship options. There are more social interactions, they take selfies (a lot!), they feel happy or sad, they walk funny if they have to go to the bathroom, in fact you can give your sim a swagger walk or whatever as part of create-a-sim. Little things here and there that are cool, but to me that isn't enough. These should have been in addition to, not instead of, the advancements made in Sims 3. So, like many no doubt, I'm going back to rich and open world of Sims 3, an infinitely better use of my free time than this pathetic excuse for a 2014 game. Too bad I can't get my money back though. I'm sure this title will be in the bargain bin by the holidays.

rating 2.0. Not Worth $60, Definitely Not Worth $80 I just uninstalled Sims 4 and packed it up to return. I'm giving it two stars instead of one because it's playable, and there are some fun features.

I'm mainly returning it because it's not worth the price. I'd have been annoyed at paying $60, but this was absolutely not worth the $80 I paid for the Premium Edition. Some funny hats and whatever all else there was in DLC, plus the "hardcover book," wasn't worth the extra twenty in any way. I think EA has the idea that Sims fans will be happy to pay a premium over their already high prices to get anything *special*. Well, maybe some of us will :/ but the excitement wears off quickly.

My main complaint about this game is that there are no paper playing instructions. At all. The single-fold pamphlet that comes with the game tells you how to install, warns about epilepsy, that sort of thing, but has no play instructions, not even a quick one-page guide to controls. There's no manual on either of the disks. No manual at all. The very thin hardcover book is basically a puff piece, a hardcover commercial full of colorful artwork that basically says "Look at all the pretty, cool things we did! Check out this concept art!" and that's it. It was interesting to look through once, but in no way replaces an actual manual. I prefer printed manuals, but even a PDF on one of the disks would've been better than nothing, and functionally much better than this pretty piece of uselessness.

There's an in-game hint system that pops up little boxes explaining things as you start to play. Which is nice, but expecting us to remember every detail from all the hint boxes is ridiculous. And if you're about to click on something when a hint box pops up, the click dismisses the box, so you can't read what it said. Oops! Gee, wish I could've looked up whatever those hints had to say in a manual somewhere.

While playing the game, my Sim wanted things I hadn't been told how to give her yet. Again, we need a manual where we can look these things up. Or at the very least, if the development team thinks this hint system is the be-all and end-all of learning the game, there should be a tutorial household where you start (remember the one from the original Sims? Which came with a manual?) where everything is controlled, and the player is never asked to do things they haven't been told how to do yet. As it is, playing was a long exercise in frustration and anger, punctuated with occasional short periods of "Hey, cool."

Add in the fact that the first two times I quit the game it was because it crashed to desktop, despite the fact that my PC is significantly better than the game's minimum requirements. I've seen other reviews discussing how much more stable this game is than 3, but I'm not seeing it. Sims 3 behaves just fine for me, but 4 is the fragile one. How much worse will it be once there are a few expansion packs?

After spending a dozen or so hours playing a few different Sims, I went back and played Sims 3 for a while, and remembered how much fun it was. A lot more fun than 4 -- and I only have a couple of the expansion packs for three, so yes, I think it's a fair comparison. So I uninstalled 4 and it's going back to Amazon tomorrow. They're happy to refund me, and shipping is less than $5 if we drop the package off at the UPS pick-up across the street. Good deal.

In a few years, when I have a more powerful computer and Sims 4 is more reasonably priced and it has a few add-ons to add to the gameplay, I might try it again. For now, I'm going back to Sims 3.

rating 4.0. Come On Guys, Give it a Chance I have played it for a couple of weeks now and I like it. I was feeling stupid for pre-ordering after all the bad reviews but I'm not sorry at all that I bought this. The graphics look way better to me. I like the personalization that comes in Create-A-Sim with the ability to make different shapes and sizes. I do miss the open world so much but the neighborhood is open at least and that's good. There are a couple of things that I thought were really frustrating in the previous sims that the sims 4 did away with. In the past, pregnant sims couldn't work while pregnant and they were off work at least a couple of days after their kids were born but in sims 4 they can still pursue their career goals while starting or expanding the family. There's also the ability for mother sims to breast feed which is really realistic. Also, they brought back career rewards. In sims 2, you would qualify for certain rewards if you reached a certain career level and I really loved that and now they are back. The exchange in sims 3 was frustrating too, because you'd have to close the game and install something from the sims 3 exchange and then go back into the game. In sims 4 you can access the "Community" while you're actually playing your game and it is so fast. There's only four lots to visit right now so that's kind of sucky but I'm sure there will be opportunities to get more community lots and things when they put out the expansions. I don't like building in the game but the people who do will probably really like the ease of building in this game. If I like my house but need to add or change a room, it is really easy in sims 4. Like other reviewers, I find the controls more difficult in some ways but I like that you can left click and swipe to toggle the camera. That's easier than the circle that you had to use on the bottom left hand side of the screen in sims 3. I'm looking forward to what comes, but right now I am enjoying learning about the new sims 4!

rating 4.0. Decent cheap pair of controllers for Couch Co-Op So, most of the reviewers that are giving this negative feedback on the basis that the sticks share inputs with the d-pad and the buttons, seem to have not grown up with the original Dualshock style controller that these are a copycat of.

In the middle of the controller, between the sticks, is a small button titled "Analog". Pressing it will light up a red light, at which point the analog sticks are analog sticks and no longer share inputs with the d-pad and the buttons. This is a throwback from the days of the original Playstation, where early games could not function properly if the analog function was enabled.

That said, my first order of these came with one badly assembled - the vibration motor assembly had broken inside and was rattling around, and only had two of the four discs it was supposed to, so I returned those two and ordered another pair. They arrived and work great.

My experience with these controllers comes from previously ordering other "generic" controllers of the same design with different brands on them. Overall the entire sequence of these controllers runs about the same.

The buttons are a bit stiff to start, usually, and wear in after a little while. The triggers are not analog, and generally they have a single press-point near the middle of the trigger, and pressing lower on the "2" trigger can cause the trigger to turn inwards a little without pressing the button - something to note. The sticks are fairly responsive though they have a pretty wide dead-zone and their active analog area is actually really short - it's very easy to overshoot a particular area and go for either 0 or 100% in any direction. The soft buttons "Select" and "Start" are clean and responsive. The d-pad is not the best but it functions, even if it isn't 4 distinct buttons but instead a full plate with the four buttons sticking out around the plastic shell (as compared to D-pads on an xbox controller which are a full plate that's fully out there, no shell covering). Some of the better non-knockoff gamepads use separate discrete buttons for the D-Pad but I haven't seen that very often.

Overall, this gamepad set is decent quality, will probably wear out after a while but I can play some couch co-op games without too much difficulty.

Bonus: In Windows 10, when it was able to "set up" the controller all the way to the internet, it properly mapped the buttons to the same mappings the Xbox controller would use - previous iterations of these did not, and the lower button on the 4 buttons didn't map to the same xbox button as it should have (so number "3" would map to "X" instead of "A").

rating 1.0. Wrong item sent 4 times! I first ordered these despite the bad reviews because I needed a controller that resembled a PlayStation controller to go with my Raspberry Pi setup. First order I received was perfect. Two independent USB controllers, worked fine on the Pi, similar weight and heft of a real PlayStation controller and for less than $10 a piece, it was a bargain. Order a couple of times more for friends and they all came the exact same way and worked fine. All tested, no problems. Was ready to give this a 5 star review. Then I decided to make some Raspberry Pi setups with these controllers to give as a present to my kids, nephews, etc. Ordered two more (meaning 4 controllers altogether) and to my surprise they arrived quickly but the controllers were not the same. They share the same USB connection this time. So it's two dualshovk-like controllers with one usb cable connecting both at the same time to the computer or Raspberry Pi. This is not the same thing that was sent before, so I though that might have been a mix up at Amazon's warehouse, even though the bar code on the first ones were the same as these wrong ones. I needed separated controllers, so I requested a replacement from Amazon, and they sent me the exact same ones again. The barcodes all match so it seems is that this SQDeal seller sent the wrong item with the same barcode to Amazon's fullfilment center so Amazon is shipping the wrong thing. I am returning these also, and if it I had not ordered similar controllers from another seller, just in case these came wrong again, Christmas would be ruined. Again, the first time I ordered these, the controllers were exactly what I wanted. Now having received 4 wrong items afterwards, I would not trust this seller to send the correct item.

The rumble motor on it is a bit different too, it almost sounds like it was placed too close to the case housing as it buzzes quite loudly when triggered by a console shutdown, though that may be individual to this unit. We'll see how well it takes to a life in a hardwood floored house with a large cat and two small kids that snag the cord and cause it to fall off the table and TV stand regularly.

OF NOTE: Had a bit of weirdness when I ordered it. It showed delivered to my location by USPS 4 or 5 days before it actually showed up. I had actually gone through the missing package process and bought a slightly up model Power A replacement at the local Best Buy when it showed up. I kept it and had Amazon charge me again for it and kept it as a spare. So.....if it says it was delivered and it's not there yet, it might just show up, just late.

rating 4.0. Good for the price if you're looking for an extra no frills controller. Not as sturdy feeling as the OEM controller because it doesn't need batteries and doesn't have the components needed to house them and it was obviously manufactured more cheaply to keep the price down.

I'm surprised something so cheap had the independent feedback motors for the triggers. The 10' cord is an excellent length for gaming at a comfortable range.

It has the 3.5mm audio jack but not the input for a headset, which is fine for me, since I don't game online. If you have a mic'd set of earbuds or headphones, the mic *will* work.

This controller is LOUD and does make make a lot of noise when the rumble motors are active; my best guess is because it's not as well insulated with thick plastic like the OEM controller. That being said, I wouldn't use this as my primary controller based on that alone.

The "A, X, B, Y" buttons are actually more clearly marked than the OEM controller. Well done.

Minus one star because of the noise, but because it was exactly what I expected and wanted: an inexpensive guest/family controller, I think it is deserving of four stars.

rating 4.0. Good for the price. Excellent primary or secondary controller. I was looking for a wired controller since my wireless controllers would constantly disconnect for no reason and connecting a usb cord wouldn't stay firmly in place and would disconnect any time I moved or put the controller down for whatever reason.  I read a lot of reviews on different controllers and decided to give this one a try. To me, it doesn't really feel cheap. It is a tad bit lighter than the original but still feels comfortable in the hands. It has textured back grips which are nice. The analog sticks are textured and almost identical to the original. They seem a tad loose but I haven't experienced any functionality issues because of this. The triggers and other buttons are very similar to the original. The middle "home" button isn't quite as smooth as the original, but that button is rarely used in-game anyway. The main thing that I have read negatively about and will agree with is about the bumper buttons on the top. They feel rather cheap and pushing them down hard makes you feel as if they may break. They are very "clicky" and I wont be surprised if/when they break (the only reason I didn't give a 5 star). Overall I will be using this controller as my main because of the wireless connectivity issues I am having with the originals. For the price, I think this controller is well worth it. As long as it doesn't break within the first few months I will definitely buy again if needed, because the price vs value is perfect.

I would highly recommend this controller to anyone who is looking for a good quality, inexpensive controller for their Xbox One!

input:
rating 5.0. A Seisure in Game Form If you're looking for a game for the Wii that REALLY shows what the Wii controller can do, look no further - This is it and then some. It's fun, addicting, fast, simple, and is as difficult as you want to take it. You can easily beat the game in an hour or two, but it doesn't end there, that simply unlocks multi-player mode, which is the highlight of the game anyway.

There's a few different multi-player modes, which will have everyone in the room waving their arms around like idiots (but in a good way of course) and each one is unique and exciting.

Another highlight of this game, which I didn't hear anything about prior to playing it myself, is the way each "form" is taught. A "form" is the way of holding the controller for a particular micro game. A very serious-toned voice explains how to hold the controller, while the screen turns gray and soft dramatic music plays. Of course what the voice is actually saying is ridiculous, which goes perfectly with the theme of the game.

One more thing that adds an edge to this game is the use of the Mii feature, which is very well integrated and gives you a chance to see your Miis in something besides Wii Sports.

If you want to have fun with your Wii, or you're looking for a great multi-player game, here you go. Shopping doesn't get any easier than that.

rating 2.0. This game was not really my thing -- too frenetic! I read the reviews here and decided that I would give the game a try. I really felt that this was for a much younger audience. I played it for a couple of hours and worked through the mini-games but found myself getting bored. I could DEFINITELY see how this would be a good party game or an ice breaker. I decided to resell my copy and buy something else like Elebits (which I really enjoy).

I cannot pinpoint what bugged me most about this game except that I think I was expecting a lot more. No love lost, I know that a lot of people really enjoy this game. I personally am having much more fun with Sports, Elebits, and the Trauma game.

rating 2.0. Overpriced and Repetitive WarioWare: Smooth Moves is entertaining, once you get past the HORRIBLE story-mode. They should have ditched it altogether - really shoddy Flash(R) cartoon graphics mixed with a very childish and overly colorful world leads to you being more embarassed when your friends are around than anything else.

The gameplay is pretty good though - you have about 2 seconds to realize what you have to do, and another 3 or 4 to get it done. Very fast paced. Boss Stages are probably what is the best part about the game. Those are a lot of fun.

If you have a kid, I'd reccomend sending them outside rather than buying this game - it may mess with their head. If you're an adult, try to swallow the story-mode and just get it done.

I bought. I'm still not positive if I should have.

rating 4.0. Warioware: Fun Moves I've played it a few times and it was fun, but the kids love it.  It's hilarious to watch them play.  The games are challenging enough for my 10 year old to enjoy but most are easy enough for my 5 year old to pass the levels.

rating 4.0. Best In Its Series, Yet Best Of Its Console! This has got to be the best Wii game released yet! Over 200 crazy microgames (205 to be exact) to play! Probably the best Warioware additon so far!

There are only a few...disadvantages that this game has:

1) Lack of Instruction: For every microgame, there are instructions that consist of one or two words and are only shown for a split second before the microgame starts. Most of the microgames have self-explanatory instructions, but a select few are much too vague, and will have you going to the Temple of Form and doing them over and over again until you get them.

2) Lack of "Juiciness": When I say juiciness, I mean how fun a game is to play after doing all of the things the game has to offer. After you unlock all of the microgames, minigames, Elephant buildings, etc., the game is pretty much useless, with the only reason to play it being to see how a certain microgame went.

... Other than that, this game is great! It has me thinking about getting Warioware: Twisted! already!

P.S. If you haven't already noticed, the title screen is like a mini game in itself. Play around with the Wiimote on this screen and see what I mean!

rating 3.0. Good game, but not great Some of the minigames are a lot of fun, some are really difficult. The controls can be really sensitive sometimes and sluggish others.
My biggest complaint is you have to complete the single player game to unlock multiplayer. Was a bit of a bummer when we had a few people over and couldn't do multiplayer yet. And once you do unlock it, it's not head to head multiplayer.
I enjoyed Cooking Mama Cook-off a lot more.

rating 5.0. Great game! Very fun, entertaining, interactive, definitively needs space for jumping, troting, moving, and having lots of fun!!! We all like having a try because it attracts like a magnet all people around.

rating 4.0. Helps with fine motor skills I am a mom who just got the Wii (mainly because I wanted it). I also have a 5 year old son who hates writing in school because he is not very good with his hand fine motor skills. This game builds his confidence and his motor control. It's very silly and fun and he loves sautéing and shaving. Great family game. I rented it to try it out and HAVE to buy this one.

rating 5.0. Truly Smooth Though WarioWare: Smooth Moves may not be the deepest of games, it is still by far one of the most entertaining. The game makes very deep and incredible use of the Wiimote's motion sensing capabilities, moreso than even games that are not peppered with motions like WarioWare is.
The game is amazing and provides hours upon hours of entertainment for one to twelve players, a very large range, especially for a video game. Multiplayer is turn-based, and only one Wiimote is required to play the game.
So, if you want to use your Wii to its full potential (and maybe get a workout in the process) don't hesitate to add WarioWare: Smooth Moves to your gaming arsenal.

rating 5.0. Fun and funny This is our family's favorite wii game.  It is even fun to watch others play it.  But then we all end up jumping up and asking for a turn.  THe turns are not so long that the watchers get bored.  I'm not sure why it is E10+ and not just E.  There isn't anything in the game that is any worse than Bugs Bunny silliness.
rating 3.0. Good for kids not so much for adults This game is a bunch of short races.  Now too much other then that.  Kids love it.  As an adult I don't get why.  They play for hours.

rating 4.0. Simple review! What you NEED to know This review will be very simple, I will just list a few pros and cons that I feel are essential to know, so I won't bore you with a huge long review and get right to it!

PROS:
Casual Gaming
One of the best casual games I have ever played. Very easy to pick up and play for a minute of 20 if you wanna.

Fun:
Extremely fun! i have played almost all games (203 games total) and they are outrageous and hilarious

Replay Value:
I consider it high!....200+ games, and you can play them over and over again, they will get harder!

CONS:

Multiplayer:
They really dropped the ball here, it honestly sucks! You are forced to pass the controller between players, and this becomes annoying because the game has about 18 different motions or poses you have to do for each game (for example, where to hold wiimote) This is annoying because the transitions between minigames are only about 3 seconds long

Story:
It is nonexistent, although this game consists of 200+ minigames, none of the levels are connected. At least the GameBoy version connected the characters together. This just feels like a bunch of games thrown in to one place

BOTTOM LINE:
This game is fun and funny. It is a very good casual experience and I recommend you at least rent it. I would not pay more than 20 bucks for it

rating 5.0. My kids still love this game....after 4 years of playing it! Got this Wario Smooth Moves game for my kiddos 4 years ago.  My kids are now 20, 17 and 13...and they still play this game from time to time!  It offers quirky little mini games and random puzzles to solve.  The graphics are amusing and the music is non-annoying.  Overall the game has withstood the test of time very well and my kids have fun playing it, even now.

rating 3.0. Fun and random One of my favorite games, due to its randomness.  A bit disappointing that it is only one player, but still fun.  The great thing is it jumps from one thing to the next, different activities, different games, great for those moments when you have 5 minutes before you need to run out the door, and just want a quick-fix of wii!

rating 2.0. Disappointed. I expected it to be better. I had the WarioWare for the Gamecube and I absolutely love that game. I still play it to this day. It is interesting, strange, and an absolute blast to play with other people. Other than the fact that they made the layout to playing the game more... "attractive," for lack of a better word, I was disappointed in the actual games and the storyline (they repeated Jimmy's story with another guy that looks exactly like him, his story to begin with was boring, now I have to watch it all over again??). Some of the instructions for holding the remote, although amusing, was a bit annoying when other positions would have been better for certain minigames. And the actual minigames didn't feel fresh, or new, or even as funny or exciting as the the Gamecube version. One last thing, it was frustrating to note that you couldn't even play the multiplayer version until beating the single player one. That's ridiculous! The best part is to play with others. In the Gamecube version you could unlock the next stages as multiplayer.

Overall I expected WarioWare: Smooth Moves to be a better, funnier, and more enthusiastic version than the Gamecube one but it ended up feeling like a huge cop out.

rating 5.0. Jimmy P.'s intro is well worth the price of admission I bought this game for my nieces and nephews; they loved it. I knew they would - i had the original WarioWare on the GameBoy Advance. But I had no idea I would have so much fun watching them have so much fun. Some mini-games are easy, some are more confusing/difficult, but they're all fun and a lot of them are hilarious. You will laugh. Now I'm playing it by myself and I LOVE it. I have to beat their high scores to impress them!

rating 5.0. this is probably one of the most fun games you can play with a group of friends As a gamer with +1,000 games in my collection, this is probably one of the most fun games you can play with a group of friends. rating 5.0. Smooth Moves is great fun. If you've played other Wario Ware titles Smooth Moves is great fun. If you've played other Wario Ware titles, you know what to expect - several characters, each with a simple story, where you play frantic micro-games. Since this is on the Wii, the games focus on the Wii-mote. There are various forms where you hold the wii-mote in certain ways and you quickly switch between games. It keeps track of which games you've played and beating different character's chapters unlock new chapters or other game modes. There's also a wii-mote + nunchuck mode. It has several multi-player options as well in which you basically take turns playing the micro-games.

The presentation on this game is absolutely wonderful. A soothing voice provides a somewhat sarcastic introduction to each form. You can play through each micro-game on it's own (each has three levels) or try to beat your scores for the different characters. The story is just enough to provide some color to why you are doing all this. The characters are all great as always; who doesn't love Mona, Ashley, and 9-volt? Rather than cramming motion controls into what should be a standard-controller game, Smooth Moves is possibly the best demonstration of what the Wii-mote was intended for.

rating 5.0. Impressive keycaps - sounds silly, but they really let the RGB through! Overall, I have high praise for these keycaps. They do not change much to the feel of the keys, as hey, they're keycaps. They do sound a little bit sharper than the stock double shot ABS that I'm used to hearing when I'm typing, but that's not bad thing either. This is likely due to the crystal section being a slightly harder plastic, causing the resonance when the key fully depresses. Personally, I like it as I'm using a Cherry Black key switch which is linear without any click - they key bottoming out on the plate now has an almost clicky sound, which I do much like!

The good - RGB for days! All of your glorious RGB lighting will come through on this keyboard. I happen to use a keyboard w/ a white base plate, so the RGB is incredible! My Cherry Black's also have a clear switch housing so the light really comes through.

The bad - well, I have very little to complain about - except the fact that for some reason my kit had 2 Windows keys and not a FN (seems strange, but whatever). Being on a TKL keyboard I also have a FN key which the kit does NOT include. The set is a full 104 key kit, but a typical 104 layout does not have a FN key. This would be greatly appreciated if it could be added in a future kit. This can be seen by the spacebar of the picture attached here.

Irrelevant gripe - many TKL keyboards use the FN + another key to have functions like volume up/down/mute, adjust RGB, etc which will be screened on the factory keycaps. While no one can really know what you will need on what keys, maybe a few stickers or something else to add to the keycap to help you remember what those functions are would be nice. Its a little nitpicky, but now I actually have to remember those key combos. Meh.

Otherwise, this is a great keycap set and I would highly recommend it for anyone who is getting into keyboard keycap sets, keyboards as a whole, or wants bonkers levels of RGB coming through on their keyboard (assuming it can be well lit).

TL;DR: Clear key bottoms = lots of RGB lighting. Missing FN key a bit sad, barely enough to distract but not enough to stop me from suggesting you buy it!

rating 4.0. Great for RGB, but no Fn button Used these to replace the keycaps on my Razer Turret keyboard. There were two issues I had. 1) There is no Fn replacement cap, but there were two Windows keys. Given that many keyboards today have a Fn button and not two Windows keys, that's a real miss. Also, the spacebar in the box doesn't line up with the Cherry stems on the Turret. I get that there are non-standard sizes for spacebar, but it was a bit disappointing nonetheless. Still, the look is nice with all the caps other than those two replaced.

Side note: I had to Dremel down the side of the Menu button to get it to properly seat with the original Fn button from my Turret, as they were too close together and rubbing against each other, preventing using one button. It was a simple mod and works fine now, and you can't really tell since the plastic I removed was clear anyway.

rating 4.0. Interesting I put these on my new Mountain Everest Max, after having HyperX pudding keycaps on there for a week or two. I normally use pudding keycaps, but I think these are interesting and look pretty awesome... for me I think they complement my desk PC with tempered glass lid, and the Cyberpunk color scheme I have going right now.

Some caveats:
- I wish the black keycaps didn't have the white border around the outside.
- I wish the keycap tops were made of PBT, not ABS or whatever they are.
- The top edges of the keycaps are, let's say, "well-defined" - not quite "sharp", but not rounded. They're not a problem for me, but some people might want a more rounded profile.
- The font goes well with my sci-fi desktop and sensor panel font.
- I wish the keycaps were thicker. They are ~1 mm thick, and I wish they were 1.5-2.0 mm thick.
- There is no Fn keycap. Instead there are two Windows keycaps.

I haven't had these on long enough to evaluate how oily and shiny they get, but I expect they will get shiny rather quickly. The tops are rather smooth and they are not PBT.

Overall I'm glad I bought these. They look good, show off the RGB, are not too expensive, and are available (mech fans will feel the pain on this one). I expect I'll be switching out within 6 months, but so far I like these.

rating 5.0. Great Deal! Solid quality! Easy Assembly This product is very well constructed. The key caps are solid and well fitted for a standard size keyboard. If you have a Razor Blackwidow Elite keyboard that is the one issue you will run into. Personally I love the flashy look this inexpensive product has given my keyboard. The lights are much more visible but the actual switches are still somewhat hidden which a great plus. Installing these caps were quite simple. The only slight difficulty you might run into is when dealing with larger keys. You will need to remove the clips from the old caps and place them on the new ones. There's glue on the old ones so you'll need to carefully take them out with a flathead screw driver, but it is easy to do and they old clips fit right into this product. Overall a great way to give your lighted keyboard some extra flare.

rating 5.0. Great! But a couple of things to be aware of... I received these today, and I have to say that for the most part I like them. The different colors on my mechanical keyboard really shine through, making for a truly unique experience. However, there are still a couple of things to note before purchasing.

First, depending on the type of switch you’re using there will be a noticeable difference in the sound quality. In my case, I am using a mechanical keyboard with blue switches (see the photo done in daylight for clarification). Usually, there is a loud ‘THOCK’ sound- though some prefer the term ‘CLACK’- when pressing the keys. The thock sound is actually what one hears on the release of the key, but I digress (LOL). Due to the fact that I can’t use all 104 keys because of the way the keyboard itself is configured, the difference in sound quality is more pronounced between the original keys and those with the new keycaps on them. For some, it may not register and that would be great. However, I am both a writer and musician, so I can tell that the sound is a bit more muted when pressing on the clear caps. Again, not a noticeable thing to some, and might even be pleasant to their ear, but to mine not so much.

The second thing to note is that these caps will not fit on a mechanical-FEELING keyboard. Yes, I have one of those as well, and there is definitely a difference. In fact, I originally bought these to jazz up that keyboard, which also has the RGB colors I love. Although mildly saddened that they wouldn’t fit, I still counted it as a win because they were a perfect fit for the mechanical.

All in all, I’m still going to give this product a five star rating because the quality of the caps are quite good, and definitely sturdy. I didn’t have to worry about breakage when handling or pressing down on them to place. So no, I’m not returning them. In fact, I already have another keyboard in mind to use them on, which has red switches. Still a clack, but more in line with how these keycaps actually sound so any slight difference shouldn’t be as noticeable as it is on this keyboard. However, I felt I should leave this review for a fellow ‘blue switch’ user, who perhaps craves a stronger sound when typing for long periods of time. For me, it’s one of the most motivating things in the world when crafting novels.

Happy Typing/ Gaming!

rating 5.0. Looks great - Check your Keyboard Layout I love these key caps and they look amazing on my LogitechG Pro TKL Keyboard. Of course a few things are a miss but it's nothing against the product its a result of my keyboard layout.

Logitech uses a 5.75U space bar, 1.5U CTRL keycaps and an off-center caps key, so while you can replace the caps key and the CTRL keycaps the space bar doesn't work at all.

The color shines through beautifully! I've been happy with the look of these and were extremely easy to replace. If you have a standard layout keyboard give these a shot if you want the light to shine through.

Delivery was quick (same day) and the packing was great as they had split the keys in 3 vacuum packed bags laid out IN ORDER which made it really easy to find the keys needed in order to replace.

rating 4.0. Great looking keycaps with small personal issues. I purchased these keycaps mainly because they are almost identical to another keycap set that I have for my Corsair K100.

This set also includes the outline "ring" around the keys that go well with the K100's iCue wheel. This set also fits the bottom row of the K100 perfectly, which is a common issue with some other keysets I've tried out.

The keys feel and sound nice to my preference, however, they are much more slick than the standard keyset. So, they have a very smooth feel to them that might wear and cause issues, but I've not encountered that yet, so time will tell.

RGB light through them is fantastic! They look beautiful and aside from the space bar on the K100 only having the center light for RGB, the other keys all glow wonderfully.

I do wish there were some G key options for this set, but it's good overall in my opinion.

rating 1.0. Left-click issues seem like more of a "feature" than a bug The mouse feels great in the hand, and great to handle. Coming from smaller wireless mouse, it was an adjustment, but I came to enjoy it a lot. I'm also a Corsair loyalist. I have their RGB mousepad ffs. I love Corsair. I hate that I had to ditch this mouse for an MX Anywhere 3, but I swear, if you want a decent mouse experience, just get that, it's work its price. Onto my issues:

My first mouse started double-clicking after about 3 months. I made sure the firmware was updated, tweaked the click response settings, and did that "mouse reset" thing before RMA'ing it, as it became impossible to work with. 2nd mouse pretty much came with the issue but I straight up ignored it for the first week. I swear when I researched this mouse I didn't know of the left click issue, but now they're the top reviews. So either the entire product, not the particular units I got, but the entire product design is defective, or this "double-clicking" thing is actually an intended feature. In either case, do not buy this mouse.

input: rating 5.0. No fail guide is a winner! Love, love love this product. Comes with two protectors and a no fail guide for placement. Highly recommended.

rating 5.0. Easy to apply tempered glass for the Nintendo Switch. Recently purchased the Nintendo Switch screen protector. The box/design of the package looks great. Comes with detailed instructions on how to clean the Nintendo Switch screen with (included) cleaning wipes, dust remover, and microfiber cloth. The Easy applicator allows the tempered glass to align perfectly with the Switch screen.

Only problem I encountered was there were 2-3 flat air bubbles that appeared under the glass after it was applied and applicator stickers removed. This could've been my fault, but the included instructions said in the event this happens to wrap the microfiber cloth around a credit card and basically roll the bubbles out. This seems to have worked. Other than that, no other problems. The tempered glass looks great on the screen. Highly recommend.

rating 5.0. Easy Installation Screen Protector Screen protector was flawless loved the guiding tray to install as I hate installing it by my own placement as I usually mess it up somehow. I was able to install this on mine and my brothers switch being there’s 2 inside. Just watched the YouTube vid that they had online and was able to load in the 2nd screen protector just fine.

rating 5.0. Perfecto Muy fácil de usar. Incluye video en QR para seguir los pazos. Incluye todo lo necesario para poder hacer una limpia instalación del protector.

rating 5.0. Bubbles were tricky but overall satisfied When I got my Switch my partner recommended getting a screen protector that came with a tray for easier installation. This was the only one I could find and it looked promising so I went with it. I don't have the steadiest hand so lining screen protectors up just right has always stressed me out a bit. The tray this comes with made that a piece of cake; the protector is right where it should be.

Once I pulled all the stickers and everything off however, there were two very big air bubbles in the middle of the screen despite my best efforts to avoid it. Following their instructions I used a credit card with the included cloth to smooth the bubbles to the edge, and even after getting them smaller and close to the top edge I was really struggling to make them disappear entirely. They would go away for a few seconds and then slowly reappear. After a lot more time, effort, and frustration, I got the bubbles to go and it's been absolutely fine since, so overall I am happy.

rating 5.0. Just had to leave a review! 5 glowing stars! I did not get this product for free or anything like that--I was looking for an easy way to add a protector as every time I try to manually it's either crooked or bubbles won't come out etc.

This is the one! They even have a scan code to a video on how to install which I followed and had NO issues! There was the tiniest bit of bubbling but it immediately went away by the barest touch with the cloth. Thank goodness!

Instructions are very easy to follow, just be sure your installer is pushed all the way around the switch device so it can vacuum seal the arrows to the top. Looks like nothing is on there (even turned on and played a game to see) and it's staying on perfectly.

I play this Switch by myself with no kids, so I can't comment on how it handles with potential rough usage, but for being either in the charging dock or holding to play it's absolutely perfect! :)
rating 1.0. Garbage Worthless, instalation is a joke nothing but air bubbles

rating 5.0. Easiest Glass Screen Protector I've ever used on ANY device. The applicator for this screen protector is the best I've ever used. It was by far the easiest placement, fitment, and application I've ever had after countless phones and tablets. The glass went on absolutely perfect, the lines are so clean and even you wouldn't have been able to convince me this wasnt factory.
The cleaning kit is pretty standard, but the standard absolutely does the job, so no complaints there. The applicator doesn't just align it perfectly, but it allows you to keep even pressure across the whole glass while you rub it to adhere it to the screen, so once you remove the applicator, there are already virtually no bubbles, and the ones that are there take one or two swipes of your finger to remove.

I would buy this screen protector for everything, and probably will going forward if it's the same method for all of their supported devices.

If you're like me, who stresses out about screen protector applications because tiny faults drive you mad, this was an unexpected surprise and I cant suggest it enough.

When I emoted the frame, there was NO bubbles at all!  I am so proud of the installation I did.  And the next time will be just as easy.
