input: rating 4.0. Review from first time Madden buyer... As the title says, this is the first year I buy Madden for my PS3.  I've purchase Madden games before so I kind of new what to expect but this my first on a Next-Gen console.  To me, the graphics and gameplay are great.  Play-calling system seems fluid and easy to navigate.  The commentating sounds really good as well and keeps the game entertaining.  I can't compare it to the previous Madden's so I can't say if its an improvement or not but for me its a solid Football game.  I recommend it if you've never played Madden and are on the fence on purchasing this game.

rating 5.0. Best football game since NFL 2K5! I just got this game yesterday, and so far it is awesome.  The graphics are great, the sound is stellar, and the presentation is perfect.  I really enjoy the new Game Flow option.  Key word- option.  You do not have to choose to use game flow.  Game flow picks the plays for you, like a coach would.  This makes sense, because you play as the team, not the coach.  It's fun and it gives you a chance to try new plays that you may not think of using.  You have the option to not use this feature if you choose.  The controls are easy to pick up and every play feels smooth.  I also got a $20 game credit with this purchase :)  I really enjoy this game and I'm excited to keep playing.

rating 2.0. Inferior to NCAA I used to be a HUGE Madden fan, but after a couple so-so years and finally Madden 11. I'm done! It looks great, it's shiny and new but underneath it all, it's nothing more then last years version with new problems.

Whats good about it?
The NFL license and game flow (sort of).

What's wrong?
Game flow, why must I press X all the time to have a play called, just give me the play and let's get to it. The Players, they fell like their running in sludge, unlike NCAA where the players feel like they react to the lightest touch of the stick. The Commentary, I'm already bored of it. The Coordinator, no #$@% I want to make the sake on 3rd and 5. No I think I'll stand and watch the qb throw for a first down. What kind of insight is that? The Load Times, for the amount of time it takes to load the game should look more realistic. The Animations, I hate to break it to everyone who doesn't own NCAA but Madden 11 uses the same crowd animations right down to the gear the fans wear. They just changed the logos and colors. Also on that topic, the pre-game players off the bus animation, their the same with just a different head dropped on the body. I understand that they wouldn't want to do a animation for every single player in the game but have the bodies look like they belong. Payton Manning looked like a refrigerator with arms and guess what so does Jay Cutler. I didn't know Manning and Cutler where that big. Great realism there EA.

I'm sure some of you will love this game, and that's cool, everyone's different, but for me it was a waste of money and I will no longer be buying Madden in the future. NCAA is by far superior.

rating 3.0. What BS!! okay so this may just be me..but i have never played a video game where the game is so heavily directed to one team..

havent bought madden for a couple years so had fun playing for the most part..graphics are good etc..however after just being beat 73-37 by the saints..i have to say wtf..the ai is crooked and stupid unbelievable things go the saints way..

for example brees threw for over 700 yards and 5 tds..now im not saying the saints couldnt beat my bears but when you have jeremy shockey in the middle of the field on about the 40 and urlacher running full speed ahead of him..then hit sticks him..only to have shockey run him over????? are you kidding me?

just thought it was odd the saints are pretty much unbeatable..

a side note..this game is basically a pick it up and air it out..most of the teams just throw the ball without developing a run game..and it turns out to just be a shootout..not really my idea of authentic football or fun..

rating 1.0. This Game is Not for Experienced Madden Gamers Sole Strengths:

Auto-Turbo, Quick Online Play (even though it isn't fun losing online for once), Engaging Coaching Staff

Growth Areas (MANY):

The controls are different and much more difficult. For example, WE CANNOT USE THE DIRECTIONAL BUTTONS TO CONTROL OUR PLAYERS ANYMORE. I don't know about other people, but I always used the directional buttons to play Madden with great success. Now we are forced to use the left analog stick to control our player, and the right analog stick to break tackles, lean forward, lean away from contact, juke, and high step. We cannot change this to the directional buttons, because, GUESS WHAT, there is a new feature to make offensive/defensive adjustments called the Strategy Pad using the directional buttons. Just to move your defensive linemen, it takes a series of pressing several buttons compared to before. It is also difficult to perform the "Hit Stick." I remember back when the Hit Stick was introduced on Madden 2005 and improved defense/Truck Stick on Madden 2006 (as well as QB Vision, but I was never a big fan of that to begin with). Due to the complex controls on Madden 2011, there is no time to perform defensive assignments (press coverage, align defensive linemen/linebackers) before the play clock runs out. On top of that, there is no defense whatsoever, EVEN IF YOU PICK THE BEST PLAY POSSIBLE. It is very difficult to get a pass rush and defend passing plays, no matter what play you pick. It seems like your opponent always has a receiver open. As for running plays, running backs burn you, as well as break tackles easily. If you are defending Adrian Peterson, Chris Johnson, or Ray Rice, you will get burned on the outside, NO MATTER WHAT PLAY YOU PICK. On top of this, the soundtrack is abysmal-pathetic.

Personal Impression:

I rarely buy products that I am not happy with because I do intense research before purchasing anything with my hard-earned money. Since I have been a Madden gamer for over 15 years, I did not think twice about purchasing Madden NFL 11. For the past 15 years, I have made it a tradition to buy Madden every year. Unfortunately, due to personal reasons, I have not played a Madden game since Madden 2008. Do not let that bias your perception of my review because game developers should be trying to attract new gamers, while preserving the current Madden fans who are buying their games.

I was so keen on receiving this product in the mail. Unfortunately, I already sold it because it seems impossible to even win a game on here. I used to DEMOLISH Madden Gamers on PS2 online. I had a 100-0 record at one point on the PS2. This Madden game should not be called Madden. It is on the brink of becoming known as a more polished NFL Street.

Feedback:

We have to learn much more complicated controls for no reason. Madden was great before with just pressing "triangle, left, right, up, or down" in order to adjust the defensive backs, as well as pressing "L1/R1, left, right, up, or down" to align the defensive linemen and linebackers. Now we have to press one of the directional buttons to bring up the strategy pad, another directional button to choose which defensive assignment to perform, and a final button to perform the assignment. Hello, I know this is a video game, but we only have 5-6 seconds to align before the offense snaps the ball. EA, we are your customers, and you are not letting the gamers have any control over the games we buy. Let us use the directional buttons to control our players, ease the controls, improve the defense, improve the offensive line, and discard the freezing/glitches/lame soundtrack. C'mon now.

rating 1.0. worst madden game ever If you like the idea of the computer throwing for over 300 yards and rushing for over 100 yards against you every game regardless of difficulty settings buy this game! Every time you think you have a sack against the computer, the QB gets rid of it a split second before your defender gets to him every time. It doesn't work that way for you however, because on offense you only have about 2 seconds before you get sacked. When you try to run the ball, you get tackled after 2 yards almost every time. In 2 games against the Lions and Jets offenses (hardly top scoring) the computer scored 98 points on me? If I had it on the hardest difficulty setting I could understand that. When everything you do on offense gets stuffed and the computer has a field day against you in every freaking game IT IS NOT FUN, madden has been going downhill for a few years and this game is the worst. I would rather have taken the 60 dollars I spent on this game and set it on fire.

rating 4.0. Best Madden yet Better locomotion moves, better receiver moves. More realistic. Best Madden yet. If you already played Madden 10 don't expect major improvements. Some people complain about that, but how much can you improve on a football game after 20 years ? I would not complain if Madden 11 had a lower suggested price but if you sold your old Madden 10 and got the $20 credit to buy another video game then the upgrade would seem more reasonable.

rating 1.0. Worst Madden I've Ever Played in 18 Years! Where to begin...

-You have to enter a code from the manual to access online features. If you sell the game, the buyer would have to pay $10 for online access since you've already used the code.
-There are annoying ads throughout the game (more than I've ever seen in a game). The news ticker is just an endless ad for other EA products. There is a stupid pop-up ad on screen before EVERY play.
-The pre-play shortcuts have been removed now that you have to hit the cross button prior to choosing your pre-play changes.
-There is no longer a "turbo" button, making plays very slow and not exciting.
-The menus look unrefined.
-The rosters don't automatically sync with real life (i.e., why is T.O. still a free agent in the game?).

All-in-all, I'm going to sell this on Craigslist for a loss...Disappointing.
rating 5.0. Excellent Game is fun and is a sure upgrade from madden 11. Game play is much more realistic.

rating 2.0. Huge step BACKWARDS I almost never write reviews, but this game is so disappointing I need to vent. I have been playing Madden since the super Nintendo days. The 1st Madden for the PS2 was amazing. Madden 2011 feels like I'm back to playing on the psone. "Fewer Buttons, More Moves." That's what is written on the back as one of the new features. What it really means is, "we were too lazy to assign a control to each button, oh and you can't change the controls to your liking." There are 4 buttons on the top of the controller that do absolutely nothing. You can't even reprogram the controller. The way to do hot routes has completely changed. Now I have to hit a 4-5 button combo to shift the d line to the right. Why can't defensive linemen do swim moves anymore? The kicking meter is back to the super easy way of kicking.

I don't get why a PS3 game would have fewer button options than the PS2. It makes no sense. You should be able to play with the control sticks or the buttons. You should be able to completely customize the control layout as well. The playbooks also seem to be lacking. These are all the same plays I've been seeing for the past 5 years. Not a single new play, aside from the wildcat. Madden 2010 for PS2 is far superior to this game.

Basically EA wanted to make the game more accessible to the novice player, which is fine. I would have had no problems going into the settings, and changing everything from simple play to experienced, but instead of adding easy modes to an already great game, they just neutered the game. You go to the button layout menu, and you can just look at what the buttons do. That's it. Look, circle makes a ball carrier do a spin move. Guess what, there's like 5 buttons that have no function. Want to move Stiff arm from "x" to L1, too bad. You can't change anything.

Allegedly there is going to be a patch in September to update the controls. That's just fantastic. EA shipped out half a game, yet are still charging full price. I think I'm done with Madden.

rating 4.0. Censored Madden Music I like this Madden about as much as the rest of them. Maybe I'll get annoyed with it after a while but for now I'm enjoying destroying everyone with my Saints. What I really want to say something about is the censored music. What a joke. In "Welcome to the Jungle" they censor "watch you bleed" among other things. Isn't this the NFL? The most ridiculous one though is "Let it Rock", you can't say "And you do your crime" or "stuck his middle finger to the world" and I understand that at least but they also censor "So you pray to God"? What a joke. Are they afraid of offending all the atheists who play Madden? I think they have lost their minds.

rating 3.0. Another year of motion shifting, morphing, suction, or tumbleweeds Gameplay Positives

Gameplay is a lot better than Madden 10', especially running the ball. Running the ball never felt so good. The running animations are new. In addition, the new auto-sprint makes the player feel as if he is really running the ball. This feature can be turned off. While running, the weight and acceleration of the player can be felt, while running. The best part about the auto-sprint is the player can focus on just hitting holes, without having to press other buttons. Players can now let the sticks do the talking. The left stick moves the player. Some have not gotten used to that. However, the player can move a lot more freely than using the directional buttons on the previous Madden games. The left stick does the jukes, spins, trucking, studded steps, and even high-stepping. Various catching animations have been added, as well.

Gameplay Negatives

Many of the tackle animations or lack of, are the same. In fact, the game does not have much physics or double hit tackles, at all. There are too many one-on-one tackles, and other would-be tacklers just bouncing off. This would be known as the tumbleweed. Then there is STILL the morphing, suction, and motion shifting animations. Of course, this is something that continuously hurts the game, yet it has not been fixed. I have seen some new tackling animations, but there is just not enough. It would be nice to hear pads cracking loudly on each snap, including violent hits ala [[ASIN:B000NIJ366 All Pro Football 2K8]].

Injuries are way too prevalent in this game. On every other series, a player on the user's team or the computer's team goes down. Injuries do occur in football, but the Madden producers went overboard. Passing is not so easy. Depending on the mode, players can look forward to a defenders getting in their QB's face, so get rid of the football!

Some crazy things go on, while playing. I noticed when the 2 minute warning comes, it tends to happen when the play is barely over. Sometimes, the commentator mispronounces player's names. The end of quarter highlights are terrible. It is almost like they pick one random play and show it. The same can be said for the halftime and post game shows. There is no commentary. Plus, the highlights are not in sequential order. When they show the play of the game, most of the time, it really is not what I consider to be the play of the game. This really irritates me. If the Madden team wants to learn how to do highlights on halftime and post game show correctly, look no further than All-Pro Football.

Game-Flow

The system automatically gives the player the best play, according to the situation. This feature is like an automatic version of the "Ask Madden" option, while choosing plays. Of course, if players want to choose plays manually, they can turn this option off.

Strategy Pad

This option gives players the various ways to make many adjustments, depending on the situation. Now, the player can see what buttons to push, so the adjustments can be made. In the past, the player had to remember which buttons to push.

Graphics/Visuals

Just when I thought the graphics in Madden could not look any better, the Madden team shocked me, again. This starts with the realism of the player's faces. The players look VERY real, especially with and HDTV. The faces are increasingly vivid, which enhances the realism. For example, I was playing against the Rams. I could not believe how real Sam Bradford looked. Sam Bradford has a unique face. However, the Madden team got his face 100% accurate to how he looks in real life. The uniforms are more vivid. The helmets are very shiny. The field, stadiums, shadows, and lighting have more detail than ever. Also, the player models are a little bigger than the other versions of Madden. I guess it is the way the camera zooms in on the players, once they get the ball. Many uniform accessories have been included for players to choose from, such as face paint, nose strips, new helmets, socks, and Reebok and new Nike cleats, such as the popular [[ASIN:B003VKZROA Nike ZM Vapor Carbon Fly TD-396256-110-Wht/Blk]].

Commentary

The commentary has improved. Gus Johnson's commentary brings lots of excitement and enthusiasm to the gameplay experience. He has exciting reactions to just about every action players make, especially on a deep pass. I even heard a surprising, yet funny commentary from him, when a player was returning an INT for a TD. When the player was at the 30 yard line, Gus Johnson started singing the Batman song (da-da-da-da-da-da-da-da-Touchdown!!!). That was funny. On the contrary, Chris Collinsworth's commentary is still very boring and scripted.

The Verdict

Overall, the game is OK. This game has many new features to keep the average Madden fan excited to play. However, the Madden producers must at least revamp the tackling animations. I believe this is the last year, which the Madden producers will be able to get by, without focusing on this issue. Until they change this, I will NOT purchase another Madden title. The lack of effort to change these issues is why I give the game a rating of three.

rating 2.0. Game Breaking Issues Most Madden hardcore fans have already purchased this and thus to them any review except thier own is likely unessicary. However for a casual fan, or someone new to the series there are several issues with the game that would lead me to strongly recomend buying Madden 10 over this.

There are plenty of reviews on gameplay issues both strengths and weaknesses so I will ignore those and focus on the overall cause of people quitting.

First and most obvious in both on line and offline mode (though more commonly in off line Franchise mode) the game will Freeze at a loading screen. Saving often will help prevent loss of hours BUT at the end of a game (again specifically in franchise mode)you are unable to save until it loads from the game screen to the menu screen. You will find yourself playing the same game against the same team over and over with NOTHING counting or just simming through it. If you are buying it for Franchise mode you will be sorely dissapointed.

The only other glaring problem is on line. Is MUT (Madden Ultimate Team) you play on line. Your opponent can quit at ANY time and neither of you will recieve ANY reward for the game, not coins, not win losses nothing. In ranked regularly ranked matches your oppent has until Half-Time to decide to quit with (again) NO PENALTY and no reward for you. And finally there is NO fairplay on line like in Maddens past. Nothing to prevent your opponent from going for it on 4th and 40 or on-side kicking every kick off

So again forgetting all gameplay issues good and bad, new features and old brought back if you are thinking about buying this game for either Franchise mode or On-Line mode I highly recomend buying a previous version or at the very least looking up other reviews on this issues. EA has issued statements saying they are going to be "patching" but what or when is up in the air. Their first patch is scheduled for the start of the football season (though this could be just a updated roster or the patch to thier strategy pad).

If you have waited this long I can only say try to wait another year so you won't suffer the frusteration with this game and still can be hopefull for the next.

rating 4.0. Solid Improvements, nothing revolutionary The graphics/ realism and the life like movements are good.

The new quick play calling is decent; I could take it or leave it.

I think team to team the game seems to be a little more competitive. Over the past few years I have found it more and more difficult to run, 2011 it seems to have fixed this. Running is easier so far.

This madden still has those crazy momentum shifts of old, were you are just dominating on team and then they all the sudden they break wide open and score multiple touchdowns. I will say this year it is a little easier to swing the momentum back in you favor.

From someone who has purchase Madden most years since it started, this gets a solid B+. Worth buying, just as additive as ever.

rating 3.0. Franchise Mode is Virtually Unchanged I know no one needs this review, you are probably like me and every year you complain about the game and then go right out and buy it again the next year. I mean seriously, if you want NFL football what other choice do you have? None.

However, I feel like I have to vent a bit on the fact that yet again, Franchise Mode goes without any attention. I mean it is virtually untouched from the year prior, so if you had complaints about last years version, be prepared to relive them.

Rumors are Franchise will be completely rewritten next year, I hope that's the case.

rating 3.0. Change the Buttons Back, STAT!!! I appreciate that EA tries to tweek the game to make it better each year.  This time, they took a step back.  They changed some of the controlls to require more button presses than they did last year.  I see another reviewer said a patch is coming.  I think the game will be good after that.

rating 2.0. Downgrade from 2010 Very disappointing. EA Sports--shame on you. The online game play, which to me has been the cornerstone of success for the Madden franchise, has lost nearly all potency and credibility when compared to Madden 10. In addition, the new gameplay and in-game audio happen to also jump out to me as major deficiencies with the 2011 game.

rating 2.0. Watered Down I haven't owned a Madden game in probably five years and apparently I haven't missed much. For some reason I got the urge to buy this one and have been largely unimpressed.

While I find the gameplay to be about on par with what I expected (Gameflow is completely optional for all those that have complained), the franchise mode seems to have become a complete after thought. I honestly think it it had more depth and presentation when I last owned Madden on my X-Box. I'm not really getting much out of franchise play and I don't have 31 friends to make an online franchise worth my time. This used to be the most fun aspect of Madden for me and now it's mind-numbingly boring and lifeless...

If you haven't purchased Madden '11, skip it and wait to see if EA follows through on its promise of overhauling the game for the 2012 edition.

rating 1.0. Awful Watered down, terrible game. Worth $20 at most. The same Madden, only with less features. I miss the icons for speed players, great hands, etc. Please don't make the mistake (like I did) to support this declining franchise.

rating 2.0. Why Did They Mess It UP!!!! Disappointed and not worth a penny over $25.  I should have stayed with Madden 10.  Madden 10 by far one of the best Maddens to date.  This one almost went back to the stick figures, shadow players, Commentary  terrible.

rating 5.0. Great Game! There are several people who are veterans of Madden that would argue that this is just a another roster update (although some players like TO are not on their current team) and does not really change from Madden 10. For the more inexperienced Madden crowd this is a great stepping stone. The gameflow setup is really great for those of us that aren't play savy and just want to sling it or go...all...the...way!!!!! The graphics are insane on my ps3 and older plasma, and I could only imagine the clarity if I were to upgrade my tv. I don't use the online feature much but for those online gamers it seems pretty robust and brings global gaming right into your home.

The gameplay itself is pretty neat. I am not sure what was in Madden 10 or pervious versions but the use of both analog sticks makes the game that much better. Juking with the ball carrier looks pretty realistic to the real thing and the hits have gotten much bigger, but not to the point of Blitz or anything too over the top. The detail in the fans and stadiums is similar to the Fifa line EA has put out and several other EA sports games. The commentators are pretty good and dont use the same line that often, this will probably change after a few seasons of playing though. Practice/Virtual gameplay are great for the newbie looking to learn the skills necessary to dominate/enjoy the game, although I remember these features being in the older versions via my BestBuy test drives.

If you are a die hard Madden fan than this is the game for you as well as if you are still a newbie. However, if you are a purist or are very particular of your video games this may indeed just be another roster update.

The only flaw I would like to workout or figure out (could just be my ignorance to this game) is how to update the roster using an official NFL roster or official Madden roster. Similar to the dynamic rosters on NBA Live 10 or MLB the Show 10 where you can just log in via the online feature and have the rosters/players updated for the season you are about to start. Granted the dynamic DNA season in NBA Live is a different setup since you can only play the one season with the trends a players is experiencing. A simple roster update feature in Madden would be great.

rating 2.0. Its ok, if you like buying the same game again This year's madden boasts the best animations yet. On top of that it looks very pretty, and the game day presentation reminds me of espn 2k5.

Franchise mode hasn't changed at all. No improvements have been made which is a shame since its my favorite mode.

Now onto the bad:
Rosters are severely flawed (missing players, horrible rating choices). They should really just hire someone to make sure they get the rosters right, i mean there are only 32 teams and 17 weeks(incl bye week) of football. Would it be that hard to release a weekly update for any roster changes or player injuries.

NCAA has an online team create website that you can download from, and madden is still living in the past with an in-game team create.

I don't know if its just me but since i got the game i have not won one single button-mash scrub event (where the players pile up and fight for a fumble). This pisses me off so much since i like playing on a my own custom hard difficulty (somewhere between all-madden and all-pro).

Overall the new features kinda suck. I have never touched the auto play-calling feature (calling the plays is half the fun for me), and i still do not like the play-calling screen (wish they would just go back to the old ps2 screen).

Also the online code that comes with the game makes me want to buy the game used just to spite the the jerks at EA.
rating 5.0. Madden 11 Game was definitely improved from the Madden 10 version. Physics were better and provided a better experience as a gamer and a football player/fan.

rating 2.0. Disappointing Effort I've played every version of Madden (except 10) since the early '90s. I was hoping Madden '11 would return the series to its roots and bring the fun back to video game football. Unfortunately, my hopes were dashed. The franchise mode (my preferred way to play) has been streamlined and lacks the depth of past iterations. On the field, the game just isn't much fun. There are serious balance issues when playing against the CPU. The game is very biased to the passing game. There are sliders to adjust the balance in the run game and pass game but I was never able to a satisfying combination that felt right. I'm very disappointed and don't have time to waste on mediocre games so I'm moving on. Here's hoping Madden '12 is better!

rating 4.0. DON'T BE FOOLED ONLINE! I've heard this Madden get mixed reviews. I personally think it is a great improvement in overall gameplay as well as visually. As I'm used to playing old school madden, I'm not a big fan of the GameFlow system but I could see it being helpful to the younger players. Overall the game is a lot of fun, but I feel like the All-Madden skill gets harder and harder every year, or I'm just getting worse at it. There is definitely a significant difference in the skill levels. It takes a little getting used to the new stick controls and the fact there is auto-sprint now but I think it was an improvement that needed to be done if Madden wanted to get more realistic.

I guess it's time to get more critical. My only concerns were the online play. I don't like that you have to play at an all-pro level. If you're QB can scramble long enough, there is always a player open, and most of the time he's deep. That would not happen in All-Madden. Also, there is a glitch you can use in your benefit online. If you are losing you could just pause the game and it usually only gives you 30 seconds, but if you intermittently "challenge" the play and cancel, it restarts the 30 second count, frustrating the other user. Very cheap in my opinion and I and other have lost matches due to the sore losers.

Also, I personally think EA was very smart in a devious way when they ask you to pay $10 to have online access. What they don't tell you is if you bought the original, there is a promotion code on the back of the instruction booklet that allows you to play online for FREE. I believe the $10 charge is if you sell your copy of madden but have already used your code, they must pay the money, making EA another quick $10 every time a used copy gets sold.

Overall, good job EA.

rating 1.0. Epic Fail Madden 2011 is AWFUL! It is a game for the novice gamer. I am a big Steelers fan who never had a problem playing D, until now! Do not waste your money on this piece of crap. This is coming from someone who has bought every edition of this game for the past 15 years! I am simply continuing my franchise with the 2010 version, I advise everyone to do the same.

rating 1.0. Addicted to Madden no more Cory R really had the best review on here, and summed it up well when he said he would rather mow his lawn instead. This is a truly a slap in the face to anyone who has been playing Madden for at least the past 5 years. EA has finally exposed their exclusive rights greed by putting out a product that insults the players intelligence. Gameflow is the worst thing ever. Not only is it annoying to hear the guys voice talking over everything about running the ball on 3rd on 12 in the fourth quarter down by 10, but it's redundant because there is still an ask madden button. If you didn't play this game before, you might enjoy it for its simplicity and great graphics. But I don't get that feeling I used to when playing online or with friends where it was generally competitive and a decent amount of skill was required to play it. I mean this franchise has been out for 20 plus years. Either way I will NOT be buying this game next year. Or the next year for that matter because I'm that irritated I spent 60 bucks on something so shoddily put together at what seems like last minute by the developers. rating 4.0. rating 2.0. Disappointing Gameplay The major draw of all the Mario Party games are the mini games. Overall I am not impressed. The games are not creative and do not foster a competitive atmosphere. rating 4.0. Great Edition to a Family Classic! Growing up, I played every Mario Party game and always looked forward to the next one coming out. The latest edition for the Nintendo Switch is a refreshing update to the classic Mario Party Franchise. This game is one of the best games for a family or friend group! Until now, the Nintendo Switch has really lacked a family game like this. I’ll touch on a few more details:

Controller
A lot of people are complaining about only being able to use the Joy Con. I don’t mind much at all. If you’re playing a party game, aren’t you playing with a group anyway? I will rarely play this game off the dock. If I do, it’ll be with somebody else, and the Joy Cons will do. If you don’t like the feel of the tiny controller, consider buying “AmazonBasics Grip Kit for Nintendo Switch Joy-Con Controllers”. These help make the Joy Cons feel more natural and easier to play with.

Board Game Mode
The standard playing mode in the game is a ton of fun. Players roll dice to move around a board to earn coins and buy stars to help win the game. Minigames are played every turn to earn more coins. This mode is great, just like the classic games! One downside is the size of the boards. They’re smaller than they have been in other games.

Minigames
Super Mario Party features dozens of creative and fun games for the whole family to enjoy. The minigames are simple to play and difficult at times to master. You can play this minigames in the board game mode, as well as in separate minigame-only modes.

Overall, this game is the BEST FAMILY GAME ON NINTENDO SWITCH. If you’re looking have some fun times as a family or a group of friends, this is the game for you.

rating 1.0. Would not recommend Good:

Bad:

COMs get multiple Allies (aka multi-dice turns)

No handheld mode
Not really a navigation menu; have to run around different zones a la Splatoon
10-round plays (~1 hr) only allow for getting 1 or 2 stars

Not a fan, would not recommend

rating 1.0. Super Disappointing As others have already mentioned, the pro controllers aren't supported. You can only use a single joycon. I could forgive that, but there are only 4 maps to play on, and one of them has to be unlocked. They're all small, repetitive, simple maps. There's almost nothing new or innovative in this game. Except maybe for the special die each character has, which basically makes some characters unplayable because their bonus die is awful (including Yoshi, my personal favorite).
All in all, it's a huge letdown, and certainly not worth $60.

rating 5.0. Another fun party game It's a great addition to the Mario Party series. I'd say it's on par with the other Mario Party games, which isn't a bad thing. They're all very similar, and all very fun to play with others. Almost all of the negative reviews are complaining that you can't play it in handheld mode, but it's a party game! It's literally in the title! It's meant to be played locally with friends, so why would there be a handheld mode? If you are looking for a solo game, then this (and every other Mario Party) simply isn't for you. With that being said, my rating is really 4.5 stars because I see no reason why they don't allow you to use a pro controller. Most adults don't have small enough hands to comfortably grip a single Joy-con for extended periods of gameplay. Also, others complain that there should be more online gameplay. I'm not an online player so it doesn't apply to me, but it's something to keep in mind for those that are.

rating 5.0. Can I get a high five!!! Really enjoying this version of the Mario Party series. After several plays, the wife and I are really enjoying the game. I decided to update my rating to five stars

My first experience with the Mario Party series was #8 on the original Wii. The wife and I loved that version and it is still our go to one for a great Mario Party experience. We did not like how the players moved as a group in #9 and #10 so we rarely play those anymore. We also did not like that when you came to the end of the board, the game was over. We liked being able to play a number of turns and going around the board a number of times in #8.

This Switch version has gone back to the independent movement of each character so that was a huge plus for us. Kudos for going back to that format. They also removed and end space to each board so you can go around as many times you like depending on the number of turns you play. Number of turns is either 10, 15 or 20 turns... at least on the one board I played (Whomp board).

The Whomp board that I played was pretty small compared to what I have been used to in the previous versions. I hope the others are not like that. If they are... a bit of a bummer but we are okay with it.

The graphics and colors look beautiful to us. Really rich and vivid. We like the high five feature... even with the rival characters like Wario, Waluigi, Bowser, etc.

The mini games are a blast to play too. I could never stand the ones that were based solely on luck. I liked the ones that required skill, quickness and timing, etc. and so far all of the mini games are not based solely on luck... at least the ones we have played.

The Sound Stage feature of the game is fun to play as well. It is a timing thing done to music. You have to do various motions with the joy con controller to the beat of the music.

In terms of being able to use only the joy con controllers, I have no problem with that and am used to using the small controllers with most the games that I have. Should the option be available to use alternate controllers? It would be nice. Does not affect my enjoyment (so far) of the game.

In the meantime... have a great time with this new version. The wife and I will!!! Now go out and party!

rating 5.0. GREAT GAME! Don’t listen to the sad haters! They’re just upset they still live in their parent’s basement.

rating 4.0. Great Party Game I have been a fan of Mario party since the very first one on N64 but have not been buying the games as they seemed to have strayed from the original more than I would have liked. However, this one seemed to go back to its roots--so I knew I had to get it!

What I like:
-you can edit the length of the game (and it give you the estimated amount of time to complete it. i.e. 10 turns is approx. 60 min).
-you can practice the mini-games before hand (and EVERYONE has to be "ready" before the game starts--otherwise you stay in the practice mode).
-you can play the mini-games or other fun short games on your own if you do not want to play for an hour or more.
-it is VERY friendly to those who are new/unfamiliar to gaming--so it is an essential game to have!
-you can change modes at any time (just fyi "normal" level is actually rather easy if you are an experienced gamer).
-the game boards are different and have some fun elements to them!
-the switch remove vibrates and pings when it is your turn or somehow you are involved in what is occurring in the gameplay.
-lots of super funny and clever minigames (some are definitely throw backs to the original game)

What could have been improved:
-I wish you could skip or fast forward through computer players turns if you wanted. Playing by myself was like 2-3 minutes watching them, and only 1 minute really playing (including my turn and the mini-game).
-as is expected, some of the mini-games are not as exciting as others. Many of the team/cooperative ones I found to be rather boring (such as sorting the sports balls into the different basket). However, that is just my opinion.
-there are only 4 party boards that are fairly small.
-you need to use joy-con controllers to play (which is a minor thing to me but important to note).

Overall:
-In summary, this is a fun casual-playing game, and I am glad I got it. Some of the dialogue is very cheesy but that’s fine. I feel like this game (and Smash Bros) are two party games that are essential to have. This one is definitely more friendly to those who are inexperienced gamers--but can still be fun if you have a group of experienced gamers as well!
rating 5.0. Really captures the feeling of the first one, definitely worth picking up. I haven't enjoyed a Mario Party game this much in a VERY long time. I am very pleased with this purchase.

rating 2.0. Meh. Once again Nintendo gets it wrong Boards are way too small. And the ease of getting coins is ridiculous, especially when stars are only 10 coins. Mini games are ok, but I expected more. Why would you make the boards so tiny?  Makes rolling the dice and the actually board game close to pointless. Theres also only 4 boards. For a $60 game, there should be a lot more content.

rating 1.0. I was so hyped - the minigames looked amazing... I've played all the Mario Partys and it seems every generation, it gets worse. I watched all the trailers and some of the short gameplays for this game and it looked really good. Needless to say, I was disappointed, and bored after a few rounds.

Pros:
-Partner System is an interesting edition, especially when they can participate in minigames.
-Some neat random interactions
-Good character selection.

Cons:
-Boards are too small this caused an average a star gained by someone every other round, and it wasn't rare that with your partners, you could traverse nearly the entire board in one round.
-Only 4 small boards to choose from.
-Boards are very bland, undecorated compared to previous Mario party boards, and mostly uneventful.
-Some of the voices of the characters are butchered, what the heck did they do to boo and lakitu?!
-Stars are only 10 coins each.
-No competition. With stars given out like candy, there is little strategy involved.
-Not many diverse minigames, 95% of them are 4 player, there is less than a handful of the others (1v3 and 2v2).
-Most minigames seemed winnable with your eyes closed and a simple shake of the controller.
-Items in the game are fairly generic. Also, "Item shops" if you want to even call it that, only have three items.
-Points awarded from board and minigame play can be spent on...unlocking tutorials. Previous Mario Partys had unique items you could buy and put in a room to display.
-Limited to using one joycon - I'm a big guy, my hands are huge, I need to be able to use other controllers as well. I found myself hitting multiple buttons at once on accident frequently.
-Online is extremely limited and simply not even worth trying at this point.
-Can't play in handheld mode

The game feels incomplete and at best, a decent entry into a handheld Mario Party game, however, the Switch is also a console hybrid, so this feels out of place due to its lack of features. Even with future updates, this game needs some heavy revamping. This feels like the release of Bomberman R, which was a game released for the Switch that lacked a lot of basic features previous Bomberman games had--they're still trying to patch things up to bring it up to speed a year later!

These lazy releases cannot continue to go unnoticed. Hold your coins for Mario Party 12, or at least, a couple months when they (hopefully) release more features and the price goes down.

rating 3.0. Fun minigames for the whole family The game has a ton of minigames; and there is plenty of variety for kids and adults alike.

Although the problem is that most of the game can be cleared by just shaking the controller. Does not matter if you move it up and down, left and right or forward and backward, as the instructions tell you: you shake it and the game works just fine. Disappointing to be honest.

The other issue is that if you have a full size controller like the pro controller or compatible controller, it won't work. The game accept only the small controllers (the ones that slide on the switch, to be clear); so if you want to play with 4 controllers, you need to buy 2 extra small controllers.

Beside all, the game is decently long; each session last between an hour and 2 hours; depending on how many turns you want to play for each player; and there are 3 different boards to play with.

Beside the main mode, there is a simple single player mode that you unlock once you unlock most of the mini games; and last about an hour. The other modes are a multiplayer with multiple Switch console (4 different games support this mode); a mode where you row on a raft with other 3 players, and every time you pop a baloon on the way, you play a minigame.
The last mode is a sort of rhythm games collection; which last like 20 minutes if you play all the modes (standard, hard and a variant).

Is it worth 59 dollars? Absolutely not; unless you play party games all the time. After a bit all the games get old quickly, and unless you have friends at home to show the game, you won't play it much probably.

rating 4.0. Almost the perfect party game Almost perfect.
I got this right before a group of friends came to my place for a seasonal party. So perfect timing. However first flaw showed up instantly - limited to 4 players. The switch can handle 8 controllers however this game has a hard limit at 4. It feels more lack of wanting to rebalance and build the game than a hardware limitation.
Second gripe is the reliance on luck. The bonus stars are meant to offset this a bit, however winning minigames does not really help you win the game, good dice rolls do. And you have limited control there. For a game with only 10 turns, for example, you shouldn't be able to roll a 0 two times in a row. Yes your standard dice can't, and maybe that's enough balance to have options, but a reroll ability or some type of throw me a bone mechanic would be nice.

That's all I got negative. Otherwise this game is excellent.
-The coop river rapid mode is an oddly amazing addition after one of your friends feels like the game totally jipped them in party mode. My friends wanted to replay this one a few times.
-The coop party (vs) mode has really cool mechanics and options that I sort of wish the main party mode could have.
-The dance games are actually pretty fun, as long as the controller is reliable.
- the number of characters is great and everyone at my party could find someone they liked
- the load times are great and the instant sample of every game before you play is very well done

I'll give this a 5 if they update and add any of these
-handicap mode, especially if you want a casual game, to add mechanics for a reroll or something to balance the randomness of dice
- A fast mode, where the minor actions, happen at a 2 or 3x rate. This would be especially nice for AI players.
-Extra player support, even if it's just a stylized way to have 4 teams of 2

rating 5.0. Classic formula polished to perfection Super Mario Party feels at old and new at the same time, and this is a great thing. The "who asked for this" mechanics of a shared car, dead-end board designs, and kitchen sink game modifications (like the rocket-car level from MP9) give way to a core "Mario Party Mode" that feels like the earliest (and best) entries in the series. But layered over that is a level of polish I didn't expect - a ridiculous "story" to tie everything together, cinematics to introduce and explain every board, a vibrant overworld like Splatoon or Nintendoland which feels so much better than a simple menu (but there's still a menu if you prefer!), and tons of extra modes that add variety.

The game's new modes are cordoned off into discrete activities, so there's no confusion what you're getting into. The core Mario Party is front and center, but flanked by two new modes - River Survival and Sound Stage - with even more modes going outwards from there. The overall organization of the game is very good at not overwhelming you, despite having around 10 distinct experiences to play. Sound Stage was a pleasant surprise, and River Survival does the unexpected and makes everything cooperative.

While these additions are nice, the core Mario Party mode is probably what buyers are getting this for. I think there are probably two main camps here. There are those who want to play a LOT of this game, master every minigame, and have tense competitive battles with friends or the AI - this is how I approached the first half of MP titles. Now, I'm firmly in a second camp, using Mario Party as a way to pass the time with friends of widely varying skill levels and investment. I'd say the game is for the latter crowd, but I haven't played enough to master it, so maybe I'll have to revise this statement.

The RPG-like mechanic of building a team of "Allies" throughout a match gives you increases to every dice roll, and a larger pool of dice selections. Some dice have sides which move you 0 but give/take coins, others have different twists on the 1-6 dice - which is always available in addition to your player-specific die. The overall effect of allies is, matches which start slow quickly accelerate to genuine high-stakes strategy in the final few turns.

The minigames are all well-made, but simplistic. Allowing the tutorial screen to function as an infinitely-looping playable version of the minigame is fantastic, so no one feels like they didn't understand the game before it started. Again, owing to differently skilled players, it seems Nintendo wants to make these games both easy to play and easy to master. Despite being over 5 hours into the game, I've rarely seen a repeated minigame, so I can't make any sweeping generalizations here, except that I've had a blast playing most of them. A covered-up game mode in the main plaza tells me it will open after I unlock all the minigames, so I'm excited to see what else is in store.

The 4 boards I've seen so far (and from what I'm reading this may be the whole set) are all fun, and cater to different styles of play. Some require you to depend more on luck, others on long-term planning, and others careful dice management. However much I wish for more boards, I do realize there are 4 very different stages in Partner Party, one giant level with many branches in River Survival, an entire set of rhythm minigames for Sound Stage, and frankly they gave me more than my money's worth in content.

So in summary, this is a Mario Party game that tries to return to its 20-year-old roots, but refreshed for the new generation. The multiple new game modes will keep things fresh and exciting for many sessions with your friends, but if those friends require a deeper sense of competition it may not fully satisfy, with easy to play minigames and a high luck factor. For Switch owners who liked the idea of sharing their console with someone anywhere, this is the best delivery of that promise since Mario Kart.

rating 3.0. Great Game Poor Service Let me just star out by saying this game deserves a 4.5 at least. The game itself is very fun to play with friends. Thanks for reading!

rating 2.0. Unreliable motion controls and practically no online play I love Mario Party, both the boards and the mini-games, and I really like that there's a lot to do and collect in this particular game. Of course, there has to be, because online play only supports 10 mini-games and 0 boards, so it's pretty much useless. Realistically, you're either going to be playing with people in-person or by yourself.

The boards are fine, and the mini-games are really fun... when you can get the Joycons to actually work.

This is the only game that I have that forces me to use only one Joycon (with cap - not optional), so I'm constantly taking my Joycon out of and putting it into my controller grip, and getting the cap off of it is so difficult that I'm worried that I'm going to break my Joycon every time that I try to play this game. :(

When the joycon is set up and you're trying to play the game, it will sometimes (depending on what mode you're in) tell you how to play and which motions you're supposed to use to play the mini-game at hand, but the actual gameplay rarely functions with the motions that you're told to use.

Depending on which Joycon you're using, the visual that they give you is actually pointing opposite of the direction that you need to use, and whatever movement the game is looking for usually isn't smoothly reflected in whatever motion they're telling you to do. You can, however, do completely unrelated movements and often get the game to work, but by the time that you figure it out, the game's over.

Most of the games are also set up so that if you falter in the beginning, even a little, it will cost you the game. Between that and the point above, people who know how to play the mini-games and have figured out the workarounds will have a huge, unfair advantage over those who are just starting out.

I love Mario Party games, and I love mini-games, and I really REALLY want to like this game, but due to the lack of online play options, and the number of mini-games that rely solely on unreliable motion controls, I just can't justify recommending this one. :(

Also, the fact that I was supposed to receive my item on release day but instead got it 3 days later is partial to why this has a 2 star review.

current_product_reviews:
rating 4.0. Good but not great I haven't played a Mario Party game since the Nintendo 64. I never bought the Wii and never wanted too. When I saw this was coming out, I pre-ordered it because I loved the Nintendo 64 ones. I did read up before that you CANNOT use this in handheld mode OR with a Pro Controller. That is disappointing because if you want to play with 4 people, you better have 4 joy-cons. I just purchased a $70 set along with the plastic controller housings so its a bit more comfortable to play. This should be stated on the packaging.

The only other complaint I have so far is only 4 boards. How is it that my N64 games had more boards? And 80 minigames is fine, but why not 100+? This is 2018 and I feel that the games and their content should be vastly improved over games from 15 years ago. I am sure there will be DLC down the road but that's not right to make people fork over money for additional boards, characters, minigames, etc etc. The board you unlock is terrible and the island map is probably the 3rd best. I think the soundtrack could be improved and the maps themselves more lively and interactive.

That being said, this is the formula I remember. 10 coins, buy a star. I'd like to see the cost be 20 coins if you play longer than 10 turns, but with 10 turns you probably only have 4 or 5 stars being bought during the game. I gave this 4 out of 5 despite the previous issues because the game is fun and that's what it's about. Sure more boards would be nice or playing it in handheld mode, but to be honest I only travel with my switch when I am on an airplane so I already set it up on a stand when I'm in the air. The minigames are fun, the graphics are nice, and it's just a fun game to play. I buy games to have fun and as long as it's fun, I'm cool with that.
other_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. A Must Have! I purchased the original Diablo 3 for the X360 last year and loved it. Playing with 3 other people online is the best way to play or with someone on your couch. What keeps the game addictive is the loot. Fighting elites and bosses in hopes of finding legendary gear is always fun. When I heard it was coming out on the X1 I wasn't going to buy it. But I am glad I did as I am hooked on it once again. The expansion Reaper of Souls is tremendous. A lot of new additions, but my favorite is the Adventure mode. You do bounties instead of following a story and eventually can unlock special "Rift" guardians which drop tons of great gear.

The upgraded graphics to 1080p and 60fps def make the game look better than the X360 version. My characters and progress carried over also which was a big plus. All in all Diablo ranks as one of the best games I've played in a long time. The game has some strategy and RPG elements, but at it's core its really a button masher. That doesn't take away from the fun of the game though. All 6 characters play differently and theirs a lot to keep you busy with.

I highly recommend to anyone looking for a new game to get into. I never played Diablo before it came to consoles but I'm glad I did.

rating 5.0. FPS shooter for the hardcore and casual player I'll be the first to admit, I am extremely competitive. So when playing any game, I play fairly serious. For the hardcore gamer, you can definitely have a challenge on your hands battling other players. But if your more of a casual gamer or not very good, the game has AI bots walking around for easy kills, so at least you can still feel like your helping your team out. So often games like COD, BF, and Halo, inexperienced or casual gamers cannot enjoy playing online. Constantly getting killed by those better than you or having unlocked everything, it's not fun or fair. At least with the AI bots, casual gamers can enjoy playing online and getting kills. Yet hardcore players can fight other players or find a game type that more suits them.

As for the gameplay, it's fantastic. It's frantic, fast paced, and action packed. A lot of items to unlock and different abilities keep the fun coming. 15 maps is also welcome as it never feels like you've played the same map 50 times in a half hour. Being able to free-run on walls and go wherever you want is really nice. Being a pilot up against a titan, you never feel like you have no chance. You can jump on the titan, rip their access panel off, and shoot their core. Anti-titan weapons can do some damage as well. Everyone gets a titan which is nice. The more kills you get the faster you can deploy one. Titans are so much fun to play as since they offer different play styles and layouts.

All in all, I've been waiting for a game like this for a while. If you like FPS, multiplayer, and the ability to free-run and jump into a giant robot and wreck havoc, do not hesitate and buy this. The only mistake you've made is not buying it sooner.

rating 4.0. Good but frustrating I have only played the multi-player and I have to admit it is very frustrating. It is almost like Halo where you have to unload a whole clip to kill someone. Not to mention there are numerous "NO WAY!" moments like your shooting someone and they turn around and kill u with one shot. Bullet detection is terrible. Watching the kill camera is comical. You wont even be in the picture and somehow the bullet went through 10 walls to hit you in the ankle and kill you. There are only a handful of guns and most cannot be unlocked until very very late. So everyone just runs around with the SCAR with a red dot. Grenade launcher doesnt even detonate if fired at someone. The game is good, just not fun in my opinion. I will stick to Killzone 3 which has a slightly more realistic approach. Couple shots your dead.

rating 5.0. One word: AWESOME! Best Review, right here This game is simply awesome. The two people above me must have been playing Viva Pinata too long. This game isn't based off of old school Transformers or the new movies, it's completely seperate so of course the voices and look will be different. Graphic wise, they are terrific. Lot going on so you don't have much time to sit and look at everything. Let me break the actual gameplay and not complain about stupid things such as the voice over or how they look (they all look awesome BTW)

GAME MODES:
-Escalation
Waves of enemies come at you (4 player online) and after each kill you get credits to be used to health, ammo, guns, or unlocking doors that lead to bigger areas. You get to play as real Transformers in this game mode. It's a lot of fun helping your teammates, reviving each other, pooling credits together to unlock a room. Def a lot of fun and pretty difficult.

-Multiplayer Death Match, etc.
You get 4 classes (Scout, Scientist, Leader, Soldier) and get to customize each class (Autobot and Decepticon form). You get to choose their colors, so for Autobots you get red, blue, green, etc. while the Decepticons get purple, cyan, pink, grey, etc. You pick from 1-3 different looking chasses which is cool. Get to customize both Autobot and Decepticon form. Each type is VASTLY different from the other. Anyone who says they can't tell the difference between a solider (tank) and a scout (car) must not be very good at games. The tank is slow with a lot of armor while the scout is quick with low armor. It's so much fun to fly around the map as the scientist then drop it right on people. You get points for getting kills, assists, doing specials, etc. Also there are kill streaks (3, 5, 7) which are different for each class and can change the score really quick. As you level up you unlock more abilities and specials. 6 game modes so there is a lot to play.

-Single Player
Haven't even touched it. I am excited for the online 3 player co-op. Beating the single player campaign unlocks items for online multiplayer so it gives some purpose to play.

GRAPHICS:
Anyone who says the graphics are not polished or good, please get your eyes checked. The game looks beautiful and I've yet to see any lag or frame rate issues with 5 on 5 online. The levels look terrific and the detail on each Transformer is amazing. You can see the pistons moving, wheels moving, and the transformations from car to vehicle are awesome.

SOUND:
I think the sound is awesome. Peter Cullen is back as Optimus Prime. The noises when transforming are different from each class. Explosions, gun fire, its all very well done.

GAMEPLAY:
Transforming is easy and looks like it should. Whoever complained about the weapons being useless clearly is terrible at this game. Rocket launchers that lock on to cars, guns that heal team mates, grenade launcher that you can detonate grenades, mines that seek out the opponent, grenades that heal team mates, etc. Tons of guns, tons of abilities, and lots of fun. Driving the tank is much more difficult to manuever compared to the scount which zips around the map.

All in all, this is a fantastic game. Read the real reviews and you'll see why this game is getting awesome ratings. Been a while since I had a game that I care about online and offline. If your one of these hardcore Transformer fans who know everything there is to know, maybe you won't like this game. I like game cause its a blast, not cause Optimus looks different.

rating 5.0. really enjoying this rpg with fast arcade combat Hi,

I am only about 5hrs into the game but I am really enjoying everything Curt Shilling and his team put into this game.

The graphics are colorfull and up there with the best I have seen.

The quest and map system is really layed out well so you dont have to wander around you know where to go.

The combat is fast and fluid and the weapons are great. You can use a sword, a bow, daggers, a staff, magic, and use can use like 4 different types of weapons at once. You can block with a shield, roll, go in sword swinging, use stealth and do a silent kill with daggers, lots of options for varied combat.

The character models look good. I play as a female and the armor makes her look like a female even her helmet. And some of the swords are as big as the character.

Tons of quests and enemies and big bosses.

There are combos you can use and allot of character customization from the look of your character to increasing different attributes and learning combos when you level up.

The tutorial is integrated good into the game so it does not seem like a tutorial.

Just about everything that they could have done right is in this game.

If you like rpg's but need some fast fluid arcade like combat this is the game for you.

The game seems huge. Been playing for 5hrs and finished 4 or 5 quests. Only 1 mission based. A lot of the quests have more than one part and this makes them seem better than just killing this guy or getting this item. They seem more like an adventure. I would say so far good story.

Loading times are quick.

Also something rare for a 3rd person game. So far no bugs.

Usually when I get a game I am thinking about the next big thing that is coming out. But I think I will be content with Reckonong for a long time.

Kevin

rating 5.0. Great game! Bottom line this is an absolutely great game for those who like RPG games, especially RPG games that are similar to skyrim and the Elder Scrolls series. I bought this game not expecting it to be nearly as good as skyrim, however in all honesty there are things that I would argue are even better than skyrim. Overall this game is great, the world itself is made up of many smaller areas that you can run to and from through narrow passages. I found this made the world feel slightly constricted though the world as a whole was incredibly big. The fighting style is also very good in this game, it kept you engaged by requiring a certain amount of skill while not being too overly challenging. The quests and missions were great, however I found the side quests slightly overwhelming. You would walk into a town and immediately the map would be flooded with little exclamation marks that signified a new quest, the side quests themselves were nice and quick though. It was not the kind of thing where you would spend a couple hours on one side quest, usually you would have to go kill a certain creature or steal something and return and that was it, which made for quick side quests. The main quest line was very engaging as well, It took me around 20 or so hours in order to complete the entire main quest, unfortunately I felt the plot to be slightly boring, I wish there would have been a little bit more to it, but alas it is certainly not a huge issue. the last thing I wanted to talk about was the ability to completely change your skill sets at any time, I loved this feature because it meant that I could spend skill points without being worried I would regret it later. I could go from being a mage to being completely melee very easily. Overall this game kept me very interested, I really hope that they come out with another one. Put simply, this is the only game on the market right now that could possibly give skyrim and the elder scrolls series a run for its money. If you liked skyrim, I would highly recommend this game.

rating 5.0. Love it It feels almost like a mix of the brightly colored, animated characters and brilliant landscapes of World of Warcraft combining with the intense action and fights of God of War. Yes, you'll be getting a lot of side quests. You'll be spending a lot of time going around the Faelands gathering items, killing so many enemies, and returning to unhappy citizens to turn in quests for your rewards. Is it a bad thing? Absolutely not. It helps you see everything the game was meant to be as well as adds depth to certain side characters. There are different factions you'll be fighting for and watching as they progress down the chain as they start trusting you more, you'll explore the mysteries of a land where the immortal Fae rule, and just how big of an effect one person can have in a world where fate is supposed to be the one thing that can't be changed. This game will keep you going for hours upon hours. One note of warning though. I played on Normal difficulty for about 9 levels with little to no issues in terms of how "Difficult" Amalur's enemies would be. Decided to restart on Hard mode and am greeted with longer lasting, harder hitting enemies, but still not super challenging. As long as you remember to block, dodge and parry, combined with your ever increasing abilities, you're not going to have many troubles. At least for the first 10 levels that I am knowledgeable to. Not very far into the story as of yet, too many unsettled citizens in need of my heroic efforts ;)

rating 3.0. Average I don't know what makes a good game for some people, but this game was average for me. And you don't have to spend hours learning a combat system or spending forever figuring out how to customize your weapons and armor because it's not heavily stat-based. Why it didn't get anymore stars from me though, is because the story line sucked. I mean, I need an engrossing story-line usually that keeps me wanting to play, and this one is completely lacking in that department. rating 4.0. A simple but fun game I'm not a heavy gamer! Therefore, I will write a different kind of review: I'm looking for a light/mild game to waste a couple of hours. Kingdoms of Amalur: Reckoning fits the bill.

Many things in the game reminds me of World of Warcarft. In my opinion, the game is more about setting up a future MMO.

Who am I: (I liked Dragon Age, ok Dragon Age II was dumb. I liked Oblivian and Skyrim was fun but just ok. I did not like Star Wars and I tossed out Assassin's Creed within the first 30 minutes due to the harsh language.)

- It's a fantasy role playing game. I like playing a game where my toon is extremely good, he won't steal etc. Maybe next time he is going to be an evil little guy... I wish I could drop quests that offend my toon's core beliefs. I decided I'm not going to steal from these people or kill their father. However, every time I come to their small village there is a hunter's mark over dad's head. So, thumbs down.

+ The graphics are pleasing to look at. I don't want to spend hours staring at atomic aftermath, and/or dark and depressing impoverished neighborhoods. They are not ultra high end but pleasing. The graphics are bright enough (on full bright settings) that I can see them well on a projector in a home theater. So, thumbs up for the graphics.

+ Killing mythical beasts and monsters is good. Killing people is bad. So, thumbs up there.

+ Background music should be just that. It should enhance the game play. If I come to an open forest it should be light and airy. If I enter the evil castle it should be dark and heavy. Good selection. I hate games that make you listen to the same score over and over again. Thumbs up on the music truly being background music.

-/+ Sound effects are OK but not great.

- Story line, don't know. I (skip) 80% of what the NPC say. I have given up on trying to follow it. Therefore, I am in the mode, just give me the quest, head for the circle, and press (X) to kill what's under the hunter's mark.

+ I have the game set on the easiest settings. So, you can kill just about anything if you have enough healing potions.

- I should never have to go to the web and google to figure out a way to complete a task. Ok, had to do that 3 times so far.

+ It has fast travel once you found a city.

- Not a house to buy in every city. Therefore, limited to only 155 things to save. Only found one house so far and had to google that one.

- Only found two back packs so far (7500 gold OUCH). Therefore, you can only carry 90 items.

? Gold is hard to come buy:-? If you are buying stuff from the vendor there isn't enough. Armor is about $33,000 at level 14 I only have $200,000 Therefore, you can't outfit your toon from the store.

? Gear mostly warrior stuff drops. I'm play'n a mage. I wonder if I played a warrior if mostly mage stuff would drop.

bonus code for the Ultimate Treasure Hunter Pack:
bonus code for the Fate Touched Weapons Pack:

- The Bonus items took almost 48 hours to arrive. I had to download it twice.

+ The treasure hunter pack is cool. Now herbs show up in the mini map.

- The fate touched weapons are useless. They are all in the foot locker in my house.

+ After you download your bonus stuff you don't need to be on-line. If I wanted an on-line game I'd go back to WoW. I keep my PS3 off the web. So, I like the fact you don't need a web connection. It infuriates me when I purchase a game and I have to sign up with Sony and also with the game company to get "more" content. This game does start off (each time) where you can spend REAL LIFE MONEY to buy more stuff. I will never do that. So fare you don't have to. Spending real money to purchase your virtual toon a virtual couch for your virtual apartment just doesn't make since to me...

- Why is a magical minion always a skeleton? Why can't I pick my minion's toon like a hunter can (ok, pet peeve left over from my WoW days!)? I wish you could aim you minion (choose his target). I'm fighting a group of monsters for my life while he's off kill'n a chicken. I'm a mage, I wish I could use the minion as a mini tank. Oh no, my WoW is showing...

If you looking for an enjoyable, time wasting, that doesn't offend your visual senses game this is the one for you. If you want to spend your time in an apocalyptic aftermath killing zombies that use to be your neighbor while listening to NPCs using the Lord's name in vane and acid rock you won't like this game.

Why 4 stars, It's fun!

Why not 5 stars, I gave up on reading/listening to the NPCs chat. Therefore, I gave up on the story line. Then why not fewer stars, It's still fun.

OK, after a long ramble of words: Would I buy the game now that I know what I know: Yes!

rating 5.0. An acquired taste... Well, let me first start by saying that when I began this game, I really, really, really didn't like it; I thought it too much a Dark Souls-Skyrim-Dragon's Dogma rip-off.

However, I was bored enough to continue playing. And, at the fifteen hour mark, it started to catch and it all made sense; sure this game takes many of its components from other highly successful RPGs, but it does it in a style completely all its own.

It can get extremely overwhelming at time, especially when you've got 3 main objectives, 25 side-quests and 5 to 10 tasks, but I've simply controlled my rate at which I take on quests so I don't get overwhelmed and confused.

Of course, this game is far from perfect; the bow and arrow system (auto-targeting) is weak (in my opinion), and the map is a pain in the buttocks -- not to mention how difficult and confusing it is to switch between quests and the map. I also hate how you have to destroy items (or sell them) if you fill up your inventory; although this is nice later in the game if you invest in the "Mercantile" skill, then, you get paid for every item you destroy (and anyone who's played the game knows you do A LOT of item destroying in the beginning).

Graphically the game is beautiful, although a little "cartooney." Personally, I prefer more realistic looking RPGs, e.g. Dark Souls. Regardless, this hasn't affected the fun or playability of the game.

So, in a nutshell, this game's a slow starter but really grabs hold when it counts. It's fun, entertaining and worth the money. And, most importantly, it'll keep me occupied until the Dawnguard DLC drops in September.

rating 5.0. Crisp clean graphics, Amazing amount of content I did research this game a bunch before it came out. I read all the reviews I could find, and it looked like a lot of the reviewers had some problems iwth the characters being flat or boring in this game. I will say this is not so much the case for me.

This game has a few things that make it stand out for me. The skills system is varied and fun to play around with. The play styles available; warrior, mage; rogue; and the various combinations of the three, create a highly customizable character with completely varied play options for how you will be killing your targets. The skills system allows you to decide what your character can do non-combat wise without being restricted. You could be a trap dispelling warrior for instance or a peculiar lock picking mage type.

The combat is probably the best part of this game, since you spend much time (at least I did) perfecting your ability and skill setup you probably want to use this stuff. The combat is very flowing and the control scheme is intuitive. I don't know how else to describe it.

The story is interesting enough, and the world is interesting. Without giving spoilers, the world you are in is allegedly fate-bound where everything has been decided by fate up until recently, when an immortal marched his armies and enslaved much of the world under his foot. You had died during this war, but a crazy gnome used a magical invention of his to revive you.

Being alive was not of fate's design, so you are an instant enigma, unbound by fate. This gives you the ability to do a few things. Immortals in the world are bound to repeat their stories over and over and over, reviving if they die in the stories. With you around, things that would normally just repeat over and over again suddenly become very permanent. Heroes that die do not come back. Villains that are supposed to be in one immortal's story start to expand beyond that story, affecting other heroes and their stories. Basically the world starts getting more chaotic, and it's all because of your revival by this machine.

There's more to it of course, other facets of the story, and if you like story book stuff this game has many little mini-stories that are interesting. There's also these stones throughout the world that cite you poetry in a rather creepy way. It's all very immerse. And it goes on for hours and hours, zone after zone, for probably a good 40 + hours of gameplay in a full play through.

The only real gripe I had is that I could wipe everything from my skills/abilities. This basically meant there was no reason to start the game over ever as I could just refresh everything. This basically discouraged me from every re-playing anything I had done in the game. oh well..
