rating 2.0. The game that was so close to being amazing. This game is truly creative and wonderful to walk around in, but they really should have hired better GAME designers. The worlds, stories, and art are top-notch, but the gameplay is stale, easy, incredibly repetitive, easy, boring, and did I mention easy? This game is so easy that you can cakewalk the entire thing on the hardest difficulty. The talent system is almost impossible not to break after using only a few points, and the rare/epic gear that drops at low levels will overpower the gear you find for a long time. I tried playing this game on what I found to be the worst class, using the worst talents, on the hardest difficulty, and was still able to breeze right through the opposition. The gear you find will be plentiful as well, and you will be selling a lot of it. This leads to an ungodly amount of gold in your pocket right away, so you'll never be short on any item you should need to keep yourself alive (not that you need many of those anyway). The combat feels like an adventure game with RPG elements at first, but quickly turns into more of a hack 'n slash style once the only combo you will ever need is discovered (then that combo just becomes more powerful over time). One strategy can be used for every foe save for one or two, and the fights become dull very fast. The main "super ability" is your Reckoning Bar, which fills up very very quickly and turns you into an even more powerful death machine upon activation.

Aside from the complete lack of challenge and repetitive nature, I love everything about this game. The creatures are familiar to fantasy lore but with new twists, the environments are bright and fun to explore, the story feels new, and the characters you meet keep you caring about what is going on around you. If this game was simply re-mastered with nothing but a balancing patch, I could probably give it 5 Stars. But as it stands it plays more like a beautiful but easy hack 'n slash than an adventure/RPG. If you don't kind easy games, then I'm sure you will love Kindoms of Amalur.

rating 2.0. Beware: No in-case pamphlet, No saves to Media..rant in progress! I am sure others have already ranted about the fact that you are only purchasing access to 97% of the disc content unless you happen to have high speed internet access. So I'll leave that gripe alone.

I would give this game concept 10 stars out of 5, except there are some execution problems (largely due to politics, why does that have to infringe on my gaming pleasure???)

The game:
I love the environments! The game play is very similar to the latest elder scrolls, but greatly improved IMHO. I like the rendering engine they used better than the one in Elder scrolls. I like the way that they implemented "mapping" as a "skill" so you can enhance your mapping abilities if
that is something that is important to you. Things like that are really thoughtful. I love the brighter than usual dungeon environments and the stylized graphics, especially how some plants glow and grow as you approach akin the Avatar movie. So, I wanted to support the fantastic work done on this game, I even bought a new copy for the PS3 (on Amazon) instead of used.

The execution: (the new politics of gaming)
With the lack of in-case pamphlet and the save game copy lockout and the online activation codes, I can't recommend buying this new. I don't have high speed internet, so I am locked out of part of the game until I can find some way to get online, update to the latest firmware (losing the "other OS" option that I currently have) and get the game registered online...that's a pain. For me, I would get all the same stuff with a used copy. The online codes are useless to me.
So unfortunately, I have to give it only 2 stars because of the glitches and politics of EA and Sony.

Problems:
I got about 46 hrs of playtime into it (13% trophies). I have experienced some oddities with the game. Some of the quests are kind of glitchy. On one, I was supposed to kill 5 of something. I killed 2 of them and happened to kill the main one (I suppose) and so it marked the quest as completed, weird. Also, I got to level 12 and could no longer purchase skills from the basic trainers, even though I only had a 1 in Mercantile, for instance. I am not certain if there are secret qualifications you have to meet to be trained or if it was glitching.

EA seems to have decided for us, that an in-case pamphlet is no longer viable, to tell you things like, what the screen icons mean and how to attack and special attack, etc. They un-thoughtfully included a "manual" on the in-game menu, if you can find it. The in-game manual, besides seeming grossly generic and incomplete (it refers to the "attack" button instead telling you its the "square", etc), is a pain to go back and forth between the game screen and then back to the soft copy of the manual to glean the information you need. When you could have just consulted a small booklet in your hand. 10 hrs into the game you really don't use it much. But at first, you might use it a lot and I think there is no excuse for it. They have no problem including plenty of other papers and codes in the case, they really aren't saving any paper. Plus, that was one of the reasons I always preferred to buy new games when possible instead of used games...I hope this is not a new trend. You need to let them know if you are dissatisfied with these new trends and commit to boycotting EA games if this is important to you.

As I mentioned, I am one of the tiny minority of 75% that DOESN'T have high speed internet. Another BIG problem I found is that EA has LOCKED OUT backing up your games onto media like memory sticks, etc!!!!!
They now try to FORCE you into buying a PS Plus membership to back up your games. This is getting ridiculous! Would like to know how this is implemented on Xbox, maybe I should have gone that route instead. I discovered this great injustice, because my install got corrupted somehow while I was sleeping and the machine was off...Hmmm.
I lost 46 hours of in-game time (about 60 hrs real-world time). I tried reloading the game and it wouldn't even run after that. I finally had to add a USER2 login to my PS3 and install Amalur under the new user before it would even install properly. Have never had this type of problem with any other PS3 game.
Also, this is the ONLY game I have seen so far that requires PS+ for backups. Once again, if you don't let EA know about your feelings regarding these changes, they will become permanent. I had heard of people boycotting EA starting last year. It didn't really mean anything to me until I became a victim of their new paradigm first-hand. It's obvious to me they have no desire to take care of their customers.

I can't say whether the game was to blame for the PS3 corruption or not.
Bottom line - Sony and EA have gotten WAY too greedy for me.

rating 3.0. If you don't mind to play a really easy game... go for it. Background:
- Play it around 90 hours
- Completed around 200+ quests

Evaluation
The most serious issue, is how easy the game is, game fighting mechanics are great, juggle combos, evasion, magic and special abilities, blocking, parry attacks and everything... so is quite easy to kill the mobs

You can craft your gear, and if you master crafting and sagecrafting, that is quite easy early in the game, the resulting gear will be better than any gear found in the game, so... then you stop looking at what you pickup or what the shops have to offer.

You can reset your character N times, just have to pay, early in the game it would be hard, but later on money isn't a problem, the game get so easy that you don't even need to use potions....

Good graphics, decent storyline, but it's killed by how easy the game becomes as you progress specially if you switch to a Mage... that's like a walking God, let's say I killed groups of 10 enemies in less than 10 seconds in the last part of the game.
rating 3.0. Not so good Meh. Not very exciting. The leveling system is crap. Stuck in a terribly low resolution, so you can't see the beautiful world around you at all.

rating 5.0. I absolutely love this game I absolutely love this game. By far my most favorite RPG ever. You have the freedom to mold your character into whatever you want him/her to be, without having to specify at the beginning whether he/she should be a warrior or mage or rogue. You get to start however you want, and then go whatever direction you want with him/her, depending on what kind of abilities you want, or you can do a combination of all. This also lets you use all kinds of great weapons, without them being limited to a particular kind (warrior, mage, rogue etc.). It's a huge game too, it took me a white to beat it. There is a lot to do, lots of side quests. I wish they made another one. Loved it.

rating 5.0. Awesome Great battle system. In my opinión the best there is so far. This game is really fun to play although it is true that the side characters are somewhat forgettable. Anyway, It is still a great game.

rating 3.0. Gorgeous, but too scattered to hold my attention For starters, this game is gorgeous.  I was swept away by the atmosphere and stunning environment.  The game is big, smooth, fun to run around in, and generally amazing...at first.  I look to games like this to unveil a good story line over time.  This never happened in this game.  The central conceit of the main character being 'fateless' is interesting, but not wel developed.  Quests are myriad but essentially meaningless.  Each individual quest is relatively short lived, and even the quests that try to build into the story don't feel really interdepende t.  For me, the quests devolved into a reason to run around and pick up loot while looking at the incredible graphics.  This heldme for about 20 hours of gameplay...not bad.  But it never got more interesting, and there was a ton of content left to play but i ran out of patience.  My wife liked it, and kept playing, but soon I turned away and went back to Mass Effect.  Combat is fun, music is good, graphics are peerless, and story is nonexistent in anything more than a rudimentary fashion.  I expected more from this game...it was so close to a home run.  I'll keep my eye open for improvements if there is a sequel.

rating 5.0. Surprise pick of the litter Like to spend more time playing and less time thinking about consequences of choices and sorting loot ~ this game has absolutely beautiful graphics ~ choice quality storyline ~ I could not ask for more in a might/magic game.

rating 5.0. Best Game of 2012....so far This game has it all...action, adventure and just enough rpg aspects to keep it fresh over and over. Love the combat mechanics and how easy it is to pick up and just play. The more technical aspects of blacksmithing, alchemy, etc. seem complex at first but are explained well and the game really gives you the freedom to experiment. One of the best games I've ever played on the PS3 or any playstation system for that matter. A must have. The only game I am/was looking forward to with more anticipation is/was Mass Effect 3. So that's saying quite a bit.

rating 2.0. Warning: can't backup save games (PS3 version) 2.5 stars. The game itself is fine - it's colorful, easy, and the combat is great. The story and quests are bland.

WARNING - the game takes many hours to play (50-100 hours), but the save game files are Copy Protected. That means no backups, unless you pay a Playstation Plus subscription fee. I've knocked off one star rating due to the developer's decision to disable save game backups.

rating 5.0. I'm sold! In a world where we have Elder Scrolls or the equally fabulous Dragon Age from Bioware, is there room for another high fantasy RPG?

The answer is a resounding YES. With a game studio founded by former major league baseball pitcher Curt Schilling, Kindoms of Amalur: Reckoning (KAR) is an immensely detailed world. The game guide is about as thick as Skyrim's and the wide variety of class choices and skills are there as well.

Your character awakens in a pile of corpses only to realize that his/her existence is owed to the Gnomes' creation of the Well of Souls...and you are their first success. Although everyone else in this world has Fate interweaved with their being in an almost Calvinsitic pre-determined sense, your character's first Fate-reading reveals that your Destiny is open. With your origin and blank slate of Fate a mystery, you journey forth and examine your own mystery as well as go out and find your own purpose in this world. You can cast spells, become a rogue, a fighter, etc., or become a hybrid of all these. Personally, I'm following a fighter/mage path and selecting my "Destiny" according to my mood (since you are "fateless.").

How does the gameplay fare? Well, conversations are similar to Bioware's conversation wheel scheme. Skills and perks follow Elder Scroll's scheme, albeit a somewhat watered down version of it. Where I find a siginifcant difference, however, is combat. Imagine a combat scheme that is a hybrid of Elder Scrolls and God of War. Yes, I said God of War. You start out with a basic move of swinging your sword about with a variant of holding down the attack button after your first attack to perform a vicious upper-handed blade strike. As you develop your character, you'll be able to unlock additional combat moves for devastating effect. Other weapons, like daggers, move a lot faster and the combat style requires you to rethink your attack strategies. You also can wield shields, bow and arrow, etc. as well as staves and other magical items to wield fire, ice, lightning, etc. It's one thing about this game that I like above Elder Scrolls; combat FEELS like I'm delivering blows rather than the "floaty sword swing" that I find in Elder Scrolls. I'm not knocking Elder Scrolls, mind you; I love Elder Scrolls immensely, but I find the combat scheme in KAR a great deal more satisfying, eliciting an "ooooo" when I manage to great a devastating blow in.

Artistically, KAR is stunning. Todd McFarlane's name is shown on the label and his artistic influence shows quite strongly. It's extremely colorful and creatures are vastly different from one another. Lighting and particle effects are beautiful and the framerate doesn't appear to be impacted by the visual feast on the PS3. Entering an inn, I'm greeted with warm colors and a fireplace. I can only imagine that the PC version is even more impressive. However, for a PS3 title, kudos to the developers for creating a smooth-running gorgeous game. This is in stark contrast to Skyrim which seems washed out, cold, and barren. That said, Skyrim fits its setting; the Nords are SUPPOSED to be living in such a place. However, KAR has me running back to it sooner simply because this is a world teeming with life...life that I would love to preserve through adventuring.

This is a pretty watered down review, but I welcome any questions via comments. I'm having a great time with this game and I think that you would be proud to have this beside Dragon Age and Elder Scrolls on your shelf.

-- Edit 4/23/2012 --

Twenty hours into the game and I've barely scratched the surface. I find myself enjoying the side quests and faction quests immensely and I'm taking full advantage of both to build my character up. It's amazingly fun and addictive; I just completed the House of Ballads faction quest and the Fae race is truly created around a concept that we find alien. They are not simply a race that looks different; they embody a culture and history that is based on repetitive pre-destined stories...but something has gone awry. I won't spoil it; however, you can bet that your character impacts a lot of what happens in this world...you're more special than you know. Great high fantasy!!

rating 3.0. Just Didn't Make It This game was highly rated which led to me buying it. Honestly, it was quite boring. You are sent to do quests (Main, Faction, Side, and Tasks) and will at any given point have over 20 to do at the same time. It doesn't sound bad, but that just means you are constantly running from place to place and never really getting a chance to work on the story. Running around for hours just gets boring and all of the side quests seem like more of a chore than an enjoyable game. Another awful thing about this game is the combat. It is extremely repetitive with little variation which is sad in a game as long and thought out as this one is. It froze on me twice and has a couple of problems that could mess you up (not letting you drop quest items even after the quest is over so you can't carry as much for the rest of the game). The game isn't all bad, the graphics are nice and it does have a lot to do in the game (meaning it is long). If you did every quest in the game (without DLC) you would be looking to be around 70 hours of gameplay. Overall, I would say it is a 5 out of 10 easliy one of the 5 worst games I own. If you are looking for a good RPG of this style (swords, archers, wizards) I would recommend Dragon's Dogma. Much better combat and story with more hours of gameplay.

rating 4.0. This game is worth your time First off the many graphical glitches the demo had aren't present in the game. Kingdoms of Amalur is a good game. Its art style is similar to Fable with more of a deeper RPG feel. The game has everything needed to keep players busy for many hours. There are multiple side quests, several ways to build your character from mages to warriors and rogues. The real fun of this game is the battle system and many weapon types. The combat isn't boring like some games get. The games crafting and smithing system work well once you level them up. You only get one point at a time so you have to decide what area you are going to focus on. This means you won't be both a master smith and alchemist at least not early in the game. Your level in each area determines what items you find to use so it's a bit restrictive. The dialog system is odd as sometimes you get a dialog wheel and other times you get one word to click on. It works but doesn't feel quite like you're having a conversation. The characters I've encountered have more to say so you get some background on why you are doing the quest un like Skyrim where people just said hey go get this for me. I recommend the game because its battles are fun the story has some detail and you can play how you want. The developer claims there's over 200 hours of quests and I believe them since there are quests everywhere.

Update: You can redistribute your skills and change your character build for a reasonable fee. If you no longer want to be say a mage you can switch your skill set to something else. I am finding the merchants prices on items and to be a bit high on items though. Gold isn't made so quickly like some games so you need to spend carefully. The game has many spells you can learn but you can only map 4 at a time. It would have been nice if they made a way to map more spells for use.

Update: There's a lot of side-quests lore and collectables in the game. The main story is not its strongest point as most of what's going in the world is learned by exploring new areas and doing quests. You can't really power level to max all skills nor do you need to. The weapon smithing and gem crafting are fun when fully leveled up. My only 2 gripes with the game are its inventory system is severely limited for space and the menus are clunky to scroll through. The 4 spell limit is a bummer too because it forces you to really choose what spells you like. (The way both Dragon Age games used the R2 button to double scroll the buttons would have have really helped this game out.) I do really enjoy playing this game and recommend it to anyone who enjoys Rpgs.

Pros
Many ways to play the game mages warriors or stealth rogues
Play how you want...steel, sneak and many quests allow a peaceful or combative solution.
Detailed alchemy crafting and smithing systems
Fun battle system
Many side quests
Beautiful environments with a huge world to explore
Good story and voice acting.
100+ hours of play time
Doesn't lock up my PS3

Cons
Restrictive leveling only 1 point per level to spend on skills like smithing alchemy sneaking persuasion etc. It takes several points to max an area so you'll want to focus on being either a mage rogue or warrior. This can be changed for money but you still wont be able to max all skills until at very high levels
Some of the graphics on the characters look like they needed more polish
Sometimes the camera goes off into la la land in conversations.
Load times are around 20-30 seconds when entering buildings (it varies some go fast some are slow)
Some slowdowns with graphics engine when a lot is going on.
Camera can blind you at times in combat
I encountered a nasty glitch that made the whole TV screen twitch every 10 seconds and it would not go away until a previous save was loaded. My character got stuck in a spell cast mode during a battle so I lost a half hour of play time.
Only able to use 4 spells at a time
Some minor graphical hiccups occur in some spots

If you have two kids and they share a switch, either only one gets a Mew or you have to pay another $50 for a controller you already have that isnt worth it in the first place.

Also pretty ridiculous considering the save file situation on the Switch (no backups even with Nintendo Online with this game.) If your system dies or is lost, the file goes with it and along with that heartbreak it adds insult to injury that you'd have to buy a brand new Pokeball Plus just to get a Mew back. Bad move Nintendo.

rating 4.0. Awesome, but needs updating! This is a fun controller for the new Pokémon Let's Go games, but certain aspects of this controller make it seem like Nintendo REALLY needs to update the firmware.

The bad:
First, there is no way to easily get to the home menu and end your play session when you are using this as a controller. You need to use a Joycon to get yourself to the home menu, or press the power button on your Switch. This gets annoying, because when you're using the PBP as your primary controller, the Switch doesn't want to let you connect a pro controller even if you just want to use it to go home.

In the game, you can shake the PBP to use it as the X/Y button...sometimes. This doesn't work when you're in battle. If you shake the PBP to see details about attacks (Y) it will not work. I've also noticed this happen in other menus like the clothing trunk. Seems like a small oversight, but gets annoying.

The battery life seems lacking. To my knowledge there is no way to see a warning about low battery life! The Switch fully prevents you from using the PBP to go to the home or quick settings menu, so you can't see the battery level icon. I found that this will last 5-6 hours, then suddenly die. Luckily it seems to charge in about a 2-3 hours (but it doesn't alert you when it's full, so I wouldn't know).

The good:
Other than the software/firmware issues, this is so fun to use! I love that I can feel like I'm actually throwing a Poké Ball at the Pokémon on my TV! The wrist strap and ring makes sure the PBP is secure in your hands. The controller is also good for moving in the game, but sometimes it's hard to orient your character because you're using a circular joystick on a sphere, so you often lose your orientation if you move it in your hands slightly.

Mew:
Quite simply, you get one Mew per Poké Ball Plus. This is tied to the serial number so once you claim Mew that's it. You also can not soft reset for nature, Pokémon Let's Go saves as soon as you claim your Mew. You can't take your own Pokémon on strolls until you claim Mew, but otherwise you can claim Mew at any point in the game from the Communication menu.

The extra:
Taking your Pokémon for strolls is cute, and you can choose to play with your Pokémon my holding down the joystick. I also love that you can connect this to Pokémon Go and use it as an enhanced Pokémon Go Plus. Rumor has it, that if you connect it to Pokémon Go while you have a Pokémon on a stroll from Pokémon Let's Go, that Pokémon will automatically spin PokéStops for you!

Conclusion:
I think the Poké Ball Plus is a wonderful and fun idea, but some updating is definitely needed to smooth out the hiccups.

I really love it!

rating 1.0. Power Armor arrived with defects, horrible smell, terrible product First of all, I'm one of the few girls who is crazy into Fallout. I'm married and a mom. I was super excited to play this. It's an OK game, a little bit boring with some good bits. I'm not loving it as much as FO3, NV or even 4. The main reason I got this was for the helmet. Mine arrived perfectly packaged, no damage to the outside.
Upon unpacking my helmet, I found a huge gash that was painted over on the front. Obvious manufacturers defect. I guess it adds character. I was hoping to be able to wear it for cosplay and you probably could, except for the horrible chemical smells (basically like huffing spray paint.) It's not easy to see out of and has very poor ventilation. It's life-size so not really easy to display. I'm trying to figure out what to do with it, and at the moment very much regretting the purchase.

Not a super huge issue for me but I am annoyed it'll take up valuable real estate (dwindling hard drive space.) rating 4.0. 4 stars, when it could be 5 As an avid Call of Duty player, I consider myself to be decently good. Not the best, but certainly I'm in the top three of the leaderboard on almost every multiplayer match. My ability to win matches and produce high numbers has NOTHING to do with my skill in the accuracy department. I suffer in this area. Most of my average accuracies for AR's is around 18.00 to 20.00%. The main reason I fair well in most matches is due to map knowledge and paying attention to where the enemy is spawning. I track where my teammates are and can usually locate enemies before they locate me. It's all about tactics.

So, when I saw this little device in an advertisement online, I thought I would give it a try. I figured at least it would help my accuracy. After using it for about one month now, I would have to say that the only real benefit is when you spray without looking down your sights, or when you use shotguns. A quickscoping sniper might actually like this little tool too, because it does help you find the center of your TV screen so that you know your shot is lined up quickly. All in all, I would say it doesn't necessarily improve your gameplay. Either you're good at these games or you're not. There's just so many other skills involved besides having really good accuracy. Quick hand/eye coordination is also essential, besides a good internet connection and no LAG.

I give it 4 stars because it is a little pricey for what it does. I could see it being maybe worth the $15 to $20 range, but not more.

The main reason I give it 4 stars instead of 5 is simply because there is not an ON/OFF switch. You plug it in to the USB port and it immediately lights up. When you turn off your Xbox, it continues to stay on and glow unless you unplug it. I would rather have an ON/OFF switch than have to continuosly plug it in or unplug it. If they added the switch, I would rate it a 5 star item.

rating 5.0. It Will Make You a Better FPS Gamer This is my second purchase of the HipShotDot. This time for my son's video game systems.

I picked one up at CES last January and have been in love with it ever since.

Generally speaking, I suck at first-person shooter games. This actually makes me a decent player. With my 36-yo eyes, those faint reticles are getting harder and harder to see, even on a big screen TV.

The HipShot puts a bright red LED dot on the reticle so it is always there. It is great for those games where you are using a sniper rifle and have to make no-scope shots at close range.

My friends know that I'm using it too and will often call out over XBox Live if a particular kill was a HipShot kill. It happens nearly every match that I get a kill or two that I would have to chance at if it weren't for the HipShot. Some of them have gone on to buy their own HipShotDot and feel the same about it.

I just wish no one else knew about this and I could keep it all to myself...

Highly recommended.

input: rating 4.0. Classic Nintendo platforming fun for the family Another fun Nintendo platformer that truly exceeded my expectations.  I enjoy Yoshi games but didn't have huge expectations for this game as I expected it to be simple and maybe a bit of fun.  I actually have had some fun replaying levels to try to 100 percent them and much like Mario I enjoyed some of the unique ideas/takes on levels.  This game is also even more approachable for kids much like Kirby star allies was.  The game as you expect from a Nintendo first party title is incredibly bright, beautiful and runs incredibly smooth.  The choice to make worlds that look like a paper mache construction paper class projects / dioramas ends up being brilliant and fun to look at.  My only real complaints are that the game is far too short and I wish there were more levels. There also could have been more depth added and while you don't play Yoshi games for any level of real complexity he often seems shortchanged from having a longer deeper playing experience.  I do think this a great family game and a really good one for younger/newer gamers.

rating 3.0. Pretty much what you would expect Yoshis Crafted World is pretty much what you would expect from a modern Yoshi game. It's traditional 2D platforming that doesn't offer much in the way of challenge as it does with it cute visuals and appealing art style. Speaking of graphics, the game definitely looks quite blurry, not including the depth of field effects and pixel crawl you'll notice while playing. Woolie World on Wii U actually looks slightly cleaner by comparison( I checked) so prepare to be underwhelmed with the resolution. I believe the game runs at a dynamic 900p, and it occasionally dips below that. Handheld mode looks even worse, with sub-720p resolution(I think it's around 540p most of the time).Performance wise, the game feels solid, running at an almost consistent 60 frames per second the majority of the time. This is one of those games where I would recommend trying the demo out before purchasing. If you enjoyed the Crafted World demo and even Yoshis Woolie World on Wii U, there isn't much reason why this game would turn you off. The world map is set up slightly different than Woolie World in that, you collect flowers as your main ticket to entering new levels. Upon completion of each stage, you unlock the 'flip' side, which gives you the reverse perspective on the level while asking you to find 3 hidden poochies. The main draw of this game is found in its cardboard aesthetic, along with viewing the levels from different angles. The problem is that Crafted World doesn't take this mechanic far enough, and they really should have given the player manual control to manipulate these stages(Think...Super Paper Mario). Basically, the only time you will ever see multiple angles of a stage is when the developers specifically wanted you to, and that is quite a shame. Like with previous Yoshi games, this is downright easy to the point of being fairly bland, but from a pure collectathon standpoint, this one is still worth a look since it's enjoyable to search everywhere and find everything. Overall, this game definitely feels like one of those filler Nintendo games that act as as tide-me-over until something better comes around. If you like spending time collecting everything and you don't mind having to complete the game in order to get to the more difficult challenges, then this game is definitely worth picking up. If you weren't a fan of Woolie World(or the Crafted World demo), then I would say you probably should pass on this one since it doesn't really 'do' anything unique with its systems to make it feel like something revolutionary, or even different.

rating 5.0. Great game for all ages! I got into the Yoshi games this year.  They are a great mellow game that is just pure fun.  They are a platforming game focused on exploration.  You can easily run through the game and beat it quickly, but the point of the game is to explore and get all the collectibles. These games are very well made.  Nintendo really puts time and creativity into these games.  This new Yoshi adventure is cool.  The level designs are unique from other Yoshi games.  The music is great and the costumes are a lot of fun.  I like Yoshi Wooly World on Wii U the best.  They game has more challenge than Yoshi Craft World and is great for multiplayer.  Craft world is also multiplayer, but it’s a little more frustrating because it feels crowded. The characters get in each other’s way, rather than work together like in Wooly World.  I also enjoy new Yoshi Island on 3ds.  You can’t go wrong with any of them.  I would recommend picking this up for Switch owners.  However, if you have the Wii U then grab a copy of Wioly World.  It’s a lot more fun and challenging.

rating 4.0. FUN GAME EVEN FOR AN "OLDER" CROWD BUT NOT REALLY CHALLENGING I had never been drawn to Yoshi games to be honest, not like Mario games have. This game appealed to me mainly due to the "crafty" design and the fact that I wanted something new to play! I watched several game play videos on YouTube and decided to give this one a shot. I am 31 and obsessed with anything Mario. Mario Odyssey proved to be very challenging even on some of the beginning levels. This so far....has not. I can power through levels in Yoshi's Craft World without much challenge. I think the most challenging part is simply the "scavenger hunt" aspect of finding the hidden flowers and such. I have not finished the game yet and I hope as I continue to play...the more challenging it gets. BUT with that said....sometimes I do not want to throw my controller at the TV screen in a rage while screaming profanities :) . A nice casual game like this is good stress free fun! The visuals are STUNNING. I absolutely love all the details, textures, and fuzzy yoshi affect. It is FUN to look at at and FUN to play! I am really enjoying it so far and I highly recommend it for those who want a relaxing game and do not necessarily need or are in search of a huge challenge.

rating 5.0. A "kid" game for all ages! So, let's just get this out of the way. This is very much a "kids" game. The gameplay, music, graphics, etc.. all seem to be geared towards kids. However, my wife and I are currently a couple of hours in and enjoying it! (We're both 30+, no kids). It's fun, it doesn't take itself too serious, it CAN be challenging, and there is a surprising amount of depth to the gameplay and scenery.

I would certainly recommend this game to anyone, any age. It's not terribly difficult, but it's nice to not stress out when playing a game.

rating 5.0. This has become my favorite game for the Nintendo Switch. The time the developers took to create this game was well worth it. I highly recommend this game!  They took all that was good about Yoshi’s Island and bumped it to a whole new level. The graphics are so adorable, and the differences in the separate worlds were well thought out. The addition of 2 modes of gameplay (mellow for beginners and classic for more experienced players) is great! You can actually switch mid-level is you wish. There’s enough challenge to appease any level of game player. I’m a seasoned Nintendo player and my favorite games are the Super Mario and Yoshi games. This combines enough of the originals to give nostalgia while transporting the player to a new Yoshi world. You can choose your color of Yoshi while you are on the world map before going into the levels, and after a certain amount of completed levels, can purchase costumes from gumball machines. Costumes give you anywhere from 3-5 extra hits. You can wear them or not it’s up to you. Buy this game. You won’t regret it.

current_product_reviews:
rating 4.0. Enjoyable in Single Player Yoshi's Crafted World is a platformer that follows the Yoshi formula that we all love- eat enemies, turn them into eggs, and throw them at things! The game can be played solo or two player, but I do not suggest two player as you will frequently run into each other, latch onto the others back, and eat each other. This is goofy fun for a minute but gets annoying after a while and makes completing levels extremely difficult. Solo player works just fine and is fun.

The game opens up with a over world which you choose your destination from. Once you complete the level, the next one will open up, unless you do not have enough flowers (collectible). These flowers and other collectibles are hidden through out each level and are enjoyable to find. Overall platforming is fun as there are plenty of areas to explore and enemies to battle. The game looks great for the switch and runs smooth, I would recommend this game.
other_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. Improved Version of an Amazing RPG The Legend of Heroes: Trails of Cold Steel - Decisive Edition is an excellent RPG that fans old and new will love. For starters, this game is packaged with a beautifully designed steel case, a CD with music on it, a 50 mira coin, and an instruction manual with little comics in the back! Talk about getting your money's worth! Whats new in this version?- added lines of dialogue, updated graphics, turbo mode, and all DLC is unlocked!

In the game you play as a teen boy named Rean, who joins the newly developed Class VII at Thors Military Academy. This class consists of some of the best characters I've seen in RPG's, with each of them having unique personalities that keeps you interested through the whole game. Through out the game you attend school, bond with friends, and go on field trips that generally involve you fighting evil forces and investigating drama, which is excellently written. While at school, you can bond with your classmates. Through these bonding events, you will witness heartfelt moments and even some romance that will keep you wanting more. Not once was I ever bored playing the game. Story 10/10

The combat system is superb! the game is turned based and each fighter is unique. For example, one staff user is primarily a healer, while the other is an attacker. Knowing this, you can change up your teams to fight how ever you wish. Skills are determined by the orbaments you have equipped, and crafts are unique to each character. Enemies have weaknesses that you can reveal via attacking them, and squad members can be linked to attack together. Overall combat is a lot of fun. Combat 10/10

You have to understand that this is an updated version of a Vita/PS3 game, so it is not the most beautiful thing ever, but it still manages to look great. The colors are spot on, the characters look clean, the art is beautiful, battle effects are cool, and the environments are simply OK. Also, the game runs at 60 fps. Voice acting is really good and the additional lines added to this game are appreciated. Music fits perfectly and is actually enjoyable, but the battle music does get a little stale while grinding out levels, but that is expected. Graphics & Sound- 9/10

Overall I highly recommend this game!

rating 3.0. Needs Improvement But Fun The Division 2 is a good game, but makes various mistakes that are not fixed with a day one patch.

This setting is similar to the first game. You are a division agent activated in Washington DC and you travel to various locations to protect the people and prevent further havoc. The story is not very interesting and the characters are pretty stereo typical and boring. The gunplay is pretty solid and the guns feel good. I had a good time using standard assault rifles, SMG's, LMG's, and shotguns. I am not impressed with the marksmen and sniper rifles and feel they need some improving. Enemy AI is more intelligent than ever before as they constantly flank and push you from your position. The enemy also has the best aim know to mankind, as they never miss, even while walking side ways holding a machine pistol in one hand while taking hits. Also, all of the big players on twitter said their are no bullet sponge enemies- that was a lie, so expect it. One plus though is the shear amount of skills to choose from, giving you an arsenal that is highly customizable. Overall combat is great.

Now for my complaints- the graphics are not that good, and the people saying they are must be playing a different game. The rendering is horrendous and every thing looks the same. The skills are cool but do not work 75% of the time due to a bug. for example I will throw out a turret, and it will instantly disappear and need a cool down. Same goes for literally all of the other skills (this started when I hit level 10). My character constantly got stuck on nothing and I would glitch into the ground. Playing solo is not fun, and in fact can be difficult because the skills do not work most of the time. Lastly, the character apparel section is really bad and they give you limited clothing so they can entice you to purchase some with real cash.

So far I would recommend this game because it is fun, but be aware of the issues that others will not mention. I'm sure this will be fixed in a patch, but this review is for the game as is.

*During the period Amazon reviewed my review, the developers fixed the skills bug*

rating 5.0. Great Sound, Great Feel These have been my favorite headphones thus far for gaming. This fit great and the cushions do not hut my big ears like most others. This product plugs into your controller, or into the console via USB. While I hear no difference when turning on the 7.1 surround sound button on the USB hookup, it really doesn't matter.

Also these are durable! I have gotten angry, regrettably, and threw my head set down multiple times, and still they stand! Lastly, the packaging was superb- I would highly recommend this product.

rating 3.0. Trapped In A Mediocre World The Caligula Effect: Overdose is an upgraded version of the original Caligula Effect for PS Vita. Not much has changed except for updated visuals, cleaner battle interface, and new perspectives on the enemies. Even with these additions, the game is simply boring.

Trapped in the world of Mobius (you are also trapped in "Mobius" in The Evil Within Series.....real creative). You fill the role of the silent protagonist who joins the Go-Home club- a group of teens in high school who are trying to escape Mobius. These characters are semi interesting, but I ended up not caring much about them at all, especially your character. The enemies are musicians who use music to keep teens in Mobius, and they are also semi interesting, but nothing special.

Traversing dungeons is tedious, boring, and confusing. The only saving grace to dungeon crawling is the combat, which is pretty interesting. The combat system is turn based with an interesting time and combo element. Every time you select an action, which you get 3 a turn, you get to see how it would play out, which is actually pretty cool. This allows you to set up combos by popping up enemies and stringing hits with you and your team. The battles are pretty easy and I was never in any danger of losing.

The character design and art are good for the main characters and they all have a uniqueness to them. The voice acting is good for Japanese, but I much prefer English in my games- to each his own. The music is Japanese pop inspired, and is pretty good.

The social system is the worst. You can text your friends ridiculous questions over and over again, and their is a ridiculously big social tree. Befriending other students unlocks them on the tree and gives you some kind of boost, but this tree is gigantic and the characters are so boring you will not want to do it.

Overall I would recommend this game to those who are fans of JRPG's and are looking to get their fix in, but I would not suggest this to someone looking to venture into the JRPG world for the first time.

rating 5.0. Dark Delightful Fun Understand- This game is a Visual Novel/JRPG Hybrid. Now that you know, this game is way better than I expected! Lets break it down!

The character models and art look outstanding and are some of the best I've seen for a Compile Hearts game. Each character is unique as they are "bugged" and have some cool alterations. Each seem to have an alteration on their body revolving around bugs, such as spiders, hornets, centipedes, butterflies, etc., making for some unique looks, which is appreciated. The environment is lacking graphically, but it is better than the standard Compile Hearts game. I also do not know the frame rate, but the game runs smooth and I have never experienced any crashes or lagging- Visuals- 8/10

Combat is enjoyable, but can be on the easy side, so I suggest turning up the difficulty. It is turn based and you are free to move wherever you'd like in the combat zone. The only obstacles you encounter are "bugged zones", which can cause corruption, and ultimately kill you, but can also give you state boosts and SP (magic energy pretty much). If you hit 80% corruption, you go into beast mode and pretty much go nude (privates covered, barely), and do tons of damage to the enemies, but remember, you do not want to hit 100%! Their is a good variety of enemies and you can knock back enemies to cause massive amounts of damage, pretty much volley ball with enemies. Characters are mixed with long and short range classes. My favorite thing about combat is the chance to unlock new skills by mixing and matching other skills. This is greatly appreciated, and gives you a reason to try new things-Combat- 9/10

Jiggly boobs- that's right, more Compile Hearts boobs. This is a dark game, but it is not lacking in the cute girl, jiggly boob department. Depending on the character you choose to walk around with, every step causes their boobs to bounce, and I can appreciate the physics and time that went in to make that happen. During combat, their boobs also move with their actions- The Jiggles- 10/10 lol

The story is actually really dark, and death can be gruesome. I do not want to spoil the game but their are conspiracy theories, cults, murders, torture, and much more in this game. While the details are primarily in the text, some times images accompany the text, and look pretty cool, as nothing really ever "crosses the line". There are a lot of light hearted moments which fit in really well but the twist and and complexity of the story are just right! The story jumps between the real world and digital world, so you have to remember to jump back and forth if you want to continue the game, which actually is an interesting mechanic. Lastly, you reach points where you have to make a decision, which could lead to death and reloading your game, this is fine as you can 90% of the time save right before making a choice, which i noticed some reviewers missed. You just press the touch pad and the option to save comes up during text- Story- 10/10

The voice acting is really good and the characters are all unique, making for some delightful cut scenes and conversations. You really start to care about these characters and want to see how their stories play out, which is needed during visual novel sequences, which I used to hate. The music is good and the only sound effect I dislike is the heavy breathing of the character while running, but no biggy- Sound and Dialogue- 10/10

Overall this games story has been incredibly enjoyable and the combat is just right. I would highly suggest!

rating 1.0. Left Alive But Better Off Dead This game is not good, by any standard of measure. The graphics are dated, controls are terrible, story is laughable, and the characters are weak. The WORST part is the little computer saying "The enemy is approaching" over and over again. The shooting mechanic is crappy and the enemies are bullet sponges. Please save your money and pass on this game.

rating 3.0. Context Is Key Kingdom Hearts 3 is an alright game with semi fun combat, good visuals, silly Disney characters, and good music. The issue with this game is it's context, which is important in these types of games. You could literally play all of the Kingdom Hearts games, and still have no clue what they are talking about. Characters will often repeat things over and over until you simply do not care anymore. Later in the game I found myself skipping cut scenes as I could not take it any more. Besides all that, it is a fun game!

rating 4.0. Good Entry In The Series Darksiders 3 is a fun game that runs good for what it’s worth on my Xbox One X. My biggest complaint is that the controls feel clunky while platforming and the game needs to pause to load when traveling throughout the area. The Voice acting is good, story is good, combat is fun, and level design is great. It’s nice to get away from an open world game every once in a while and play a game like this.

rating 5.0. Wonderful Remaster The Spyro Reignited Trilogy is a well done remaster of the first three Spyro games on PS1. The graphics are superb with a cartoon style of design that fits the series perfectly. The music has also been remastered and sounds so good. For us adults who played these games back in the day, it is extremely easy now and requires little effort to clear most stages.  So far, I have had no bugs or crashes in my game play and would fully recommend.
rating 5.0. Standard Standard Sony quality controller.

rating 4.0. Amazing RPG Radiant Historia is one of the best games I've played on the DS and Radiant Historia Perfect Chronology adds to an already great game. The story is top tier as you can travel along multiple time lines, which will effect other time lines. It's not as complex as it sounds but know if you do something in one time line, it can effect the other so you can move forward. The battle systems is fun as it is grid/turn based that allows you to set up combos and deal massive damage, or get punished by your enemies. One thing I did notice about this game is that it is substantially more difficult than the original. It feels like it takes a lot longer to level up compared to the old one, but its still fun. You will just have to grind a little. The game art is good, but not as good as the original. Of course the music is good as well! overall, I would recommend this game.

rating 5.0. Amazing Sci Fi Prey is one of the greatest games of this generation and I absolutely love it! The graphics are the same as dishonored, which is perfect, and the environment is amazing. The Talos Space station is a blast to explore and their is much to be seen. The skill tree and abilities are perfect for the game and include human and alien trees to choose from. Outcomes are effected by your choices, which I always enjoy in games. The story is outlandish but still fun and has you guessing the whole time. The gun play is ok and honestly does not need to be any more advanced then what it is for this game.  Overall I would recommend this game!!!

rating 5.0. Great Strategy RPG God Wars is a fun SRPG with enjoyable characters and a decent story line. God Wars is set up like any other SRPG with a grid map or board with different types of terrains and features that affect your movement and abilities. You also have a class system for characters, allowing you some freedom on how you want your characters to grow as they level up. I do suggest that you align them properly though, or you will be punished at higher levels. The visuals are good and the anime scenes are beautifully done.  The music is good, but does seem repetitive. Overall, I would recommend this game for the Nintendo Switch.

rating 2.0. Ridiculous First of all, why can I not play a standard party match with my friends online via their new online set up? All you can do is play 5 mini games with your friends, opposed to the whole game.  That is a huge negative. As for the standard game for single player or multiplayer (in person), the game is fun and enjoyable most of the time. Unfortunately the computer will have the upper hand in matches as the star will constantly move to them, it's ridiculous. This does not mean you cannot win, because you can, but the computer gets an unfair advantage big time. All in all, the board maps and mini games are simplistic and fun for any age, so I would recommend, but if you are going to attempt to play online with friends, I would not recommend as that would be a waste of time and money.

rating 1.0. Unhappy Few I literally cannot get past the first part due to the game crashing. I have gave it 6 attempts and it crashes every time after an extremely long loading screen. Looks like it could be a cool game, but it is not even playable.

rating 4.0. Spartans Creed This game is essentially a mix of Black Flag and Origins, but with out the legit assassins. This game focuses heavily on ship battle and constant gear grinding, both entertaining for a while, but become stale after a few hours. The combat is fun and spartan kicking people is bad ass for sure.  The story is OK and what I really like is the "Witcher" type dialog choices that have real consequences, unlike previous entries. Overall this is a beautiful game with some fun mechanics, but the loot grind can be a pain.

rating 3.0. Not Bad, Not Good Metal Max Xeno is an RPG that was originally created for the PS Vita but has received an upgrade for the PS4. Unfortunately, the upgraded graphics are still low quality, but that was to be expected. The story is basic and you play the role of a character who has lost everything and is now a "hunter". Using tanks and fighting skills, you fight monsters and robot frequently in a barren desert. The desert came about from the destruction of the world essentially, so the environment is pretty bland. The music is unexciting and at times annoying. The only thing really going for this game is the interesting characters, besides the main character of course, and the combat. The combat is your classic turn based style with different skill and attack options commonly seen in other RPG's.  Also there are a lot of references to sex in this game, but that is fine, as it makes you laugh a little at how outlandish it can be. Overall this is a good game for those who don't expect much from an RPG and like PS Vita style games.

rating 3.0. Fun but too blurry This is a fun game with a great cast, good music, and gameplay, but the motion blur is completely unbearable. I'm told it is only the PS4 pro that is blurry but it is the worst I've seen in years on both my 4k and 1080p tv.

rating 5.0. The Past Perfected For The Future I have been playing Octopath Traveler non-stop since it released and I have not even scratched the surface of this game! Let me say this- this game does not have an overall ultimate plot like most JRPG's, instead it has 8 different plots for 8 different characters, allowing you to play out the stories in which ever order you desire. Thus far, each characters story has been enjoyable and are not heavily cliche'd like other games. The voice acting is good for the most part and there are a lot of side stories to interact with. To complete these side stories you utilize class action skills such as- Thief= Steal, Merchant= Purchase, Dancer=Allure, Scholar=Interrogate/Advise, and Hunter=Provoke. Plus these skills are good to have overall as you can gain new items or information.

The visuals in this game are amazing as they bring back so many memories of JRPG classics but now in HD-2D. The water, snow, sun rays, and desert sand effects are beautiful and deserve praise. The character sprites and cities look good and are well designed. The music is amazing and different regions have different battle music, making battles so much more bearable. The combat is great too as you get to set up a 4 man team and battle how you like, but remember whomever you start with will always be in your team. The combat takes notes from bravely default and you can stack up extra actions, which can be used to deal tons of damage, or prevent an enemies from using their ultimate attacks on you. Enemies have weaknesses which can be used against them to break their guard and leave them open for critical attacks. My only complaint is that enemies do not have health bars, making certain battles that much harder. Lastly- ensure you are properly leveled for each region or you will get obliterated, no joke.

Overall- OPT is a beautiful game with great stories and an amazing battle system that old and new players will appreciate.

rating 4.0. An Excellent RPG Vampyr is a narrative driven RPG that puts you in the shoes of a doctor who happens to be a vampire. This truly is the perfect set up as you are torn between saving lives or taking them. Like the Withcer 3, in terms of narrative, your dialogue selections dictate the out come of conversations, and influences future endevors. The world consists of about 60 NPC's who all have their own stories and dialogue, giving you a robust amount of conversations to be had. Every character is voiced, which is a huge plus and the voice actors did a good job. The combat is ok and could use some imporvement, but you can use special vampire skills, melee, and firearms. Enemies consist of lesser vampires, vampire hunters, and regular vampires. Visually, the game looks great, but the NPC's got little love and are slightly less than desired. The music is perfect for this game and the sounds effects are spot on. Also, the game runs good and does have some frame rate stuttuer occasionally, but not often (be sure to download patch).

I also did not get my dlc but that is an Amazon issue, not the developers, so life goes on.

The folks at Dont Nod did an outsatnding job for what they had in terms of budget and I would suggest you buy this game day one to support this small developer and to help them grow as they have a lot of potential. Besides that this is an excellent game and I'd give it an 8/10.

rating 3.0. Alls Fair In Love and War In attempt to reinvent itself, God of War returns as a much more story driven cinematic game. Leaving behind the fast and crazy fighting of the past, God of War flops on levels that most reviewers won't acknowledge.

First- the game looks great! It is a graphical achievement for the PS4 but unfortunately still sees some frame rate issues on the PS4 Pro. The level of detail on characters and in the environment is amazing, so hats off for that.

The story is ok as it is just another journey that seems small but has a lot of struggles along the way. During the story you do see your two main characters grow and bond, which is nice.

Now for combat- This is probably the worst part for me. The over the shoulder camera is not meant for combat at all, and it's ridiculous. Your actions feel slow and weak, and some monsters do substantial amounts of damage in little cheap shots. Out of my 12 hours of play time I've maybe died 5 times, which isn't bad, but the fighting is just not fun at all, or more like a love hate, as in it is fun sometimes, but most times not.

Character cuatomization is simple and easy to understand, just be sure to loot as much as possible as upgrades and gear can get pricey at first.

I would recommend this game to some who values graphics and adventure over combat and fluidity.

rating 3.0. Good- But no Far Cry 3 or 4 Far Cry 5 is an OK entry in the series that often leaves me more frustrated than satisfied. Visually the environment looks great on my PS4 Pro but the enemies and water don't look all that good for this generation. There are very few character designs so almost all of the enemies look identical (beards, long hair, and nasty white shirts). But for what the game is, that stuff isn't too important.

The combat is fun but often frustrating. The enemies have super human sight and they appear by the dozens-Seriously. Stand in the road for 30 seconds and the game turns into essentially a hoard mode shooter. Wave after wave after wave....and you cannot take very many hits at all. Heaven forbid you have partner, as they constantly go down or get in your way. The selection of weapons is small and it takes sometime to unlock them, unless you spend real money on silver bars of course- bad move Ubisoft.

The quests are the same as always, the main villains are interesting, and the skill tree is leveled up by completing challenges and missions, which I actually like. Overall this is an OK game but it is not nearly as good as Far Cry 3 or 4.

rating 5.0. New Player Friendly As a Monster Hunter noobie I can say with confidence that this is one of the best games I've ever played this generation. Monster Hunter World looks beautiful, runs great, and the enemy designs are fascinating.

The combat can be difficult to get used to, but after playing the first couple monsters solo, the combat grows on you. This isn't some God of War or Dynasty Warriors mindless hack and slash games. You actually have to time your strikes and position yourself correctly or you will be punished.

Crafting is awesome in this game as well. Each monster has their own armor set, which is great! giving you the opportunity to design so cool looking gear with the stats you desire. Weapons are the same way, but unfortunately not every monster has a rarity 8 weapon, which would have been nice.

Playing with friends is fun! as you can coordinate attacks and help each other grow as hunters. Playing with strangers is another thing.....I have tried multiple online sessions but we often fail hunts as other players die too frequently.

Other than that, this is an amazing game and I highly recommend it to those who have never picked up a monster hunter game before.

rating 1.0. Would Not Recommend This is by no means a good game. Kingdom Come is a first person rpg that fails on almost every front. The combat is wonky, the menu is slow and jumbled, the dialogue is ok at best, the missions are simple. The game does look good graphically, a little buggy, but good. Also the worst thing about this game is the saving....I mean come on! you can't save the game unless you buy some over priced booze in the game? what a joke. If you are a working adult with little free time, then this game is not for you.

rating 5.0. Ys at its best Ys VIII: Lacrimosa of Dana is the best Ys game I have played yet, and I own all of the games on PC and Vita.

In Ys VIII you play as Adol Christin, an adventurer with red hair and a knack for getting into sticky situations. This time, Adol is stranded on a deserted island inhabited with dangerous beasts. Your objective on this island? to save stranded citizens, build an operating base, and fill in your map (just like Ys VII). I will not spoil the story but know its not all lollipops and sunshine. People will die, and there is plenty of drama- as you can expect from being stranded on an island with strangers and monsters. Overall Story- 10/10

The combat is very fun but can take some getting used to. There are three attack types- Slash, Strike, and Shoot (pierce). During combat you will have to switch in-between the three to topple your foes and earn bonuses. Adol is a slash character and wields and sword, Laxia is a pierce character who wields a rapier, and Sahad is a strike type who uses and anchor. The combat is fast paced and incredibly enjoyable as you can assign attack skills and do EXTRA attacks to deal massive damage. Overall Combat- 10/10

The game is beautiful and of course has that typical Falcom design. Character portraits are designed like anime characters and are beautifully drawn. Environments are pretty basic but still stand out and are enjoyable to explore. Of course this is the PS4 version so naturally it looks better than the Vita, but the Vita version looks good too from what I've seen. I have experienced zero frame rate issues in my 30 hours of gameplay. Overall visuals- 8/10

This game really did exceed my expectations and is one of the best games I've played all year. My amazon score is a 5 out of 5 because I absolutely love it and know you will too!! Also, the game comes with an art book, sound track, and reversible cover art. Buy it!!

rating 5.0. A Wonderful SRPG God Wars: Future Past is a fun SRPG that brings back memories of when games were simple and fun. Similar to Final Fantasy Tactics, God Wars consist of board game type landscapes where the player must tactically decide where to place their characters and battle. Your characters can take up various classes such as warrior, priest, mage, and much more. Unlike most SRPG's that have been coming out lately, God Wars is straight to the point and does not involve any pointless dating sims and other bs.

The game looks great on PS4 but it was obviously designed with Vita in mind first as it can look dated to some. You can basically look at this as an enhanced Vita game, which is perfectly fine. The character designs are great and the art work is beautiful as the designers used traditional Japanese culture and water paintings as inspiration. The cut scenes are very well done and deserve praise for their beauty as well.

My overall ratings:
Story- 7/10
Game play- 9/10
Graphics & Design- 8/10
Music- 8/10
Localization- 9/10

My amazon score is a 5/5 (I Love It) as I have had zero issues with this game and am very satisfied.

rating 3.0. An ok game, with charming characters. This is your standard visual novel game with SRPG elements that give you a break from the tons of lines you must read. I liked the anime so I gave this a try and have actually enjoyed it, that said- I cannot speak Japanese, so I had to read a ton of script to know what's going on. If this was in English, I could hit auto play and watch it like a tv show.

I really enjoyed the cast of characters, and the goofy parts of the story put a smile on my face. The art is amazing and so are the character designs. The combat is fun and very simple, just wished there was some more of it.

Overall- this is a fun game for those who like visual novels and reading allot.

rating 4.0. A Good Jrpg! Dark Rose Valkyrie is an enjoyable JRPG with typical elements such as character affinity, turn based combat, stat boosting, side missions, and boob jiggling- yes I said it.

To start, the graphics are ok and the frame rate is a little crappy at times, but it's understandable due to the studios low budget. The frame rate issue is primarily in the over world, which is larger than most compile hearts games from what I know. During combat- the frame rate is smooth and so are your character actions, so have no fear. The character design and art look amazing as they brought on some artists from the "Tales of" series. Overall graphics- 6/10- which is standard for JRPG's like this.

The combat is smooth and is allot of fun, even when grinding. You can boost various stats such as strength, firearms, skill, agility, and etc. that in turn boost certain characters attack, defense, and speed. Upgrading stats also unlocks skills, which there are a ton of. It should be noted that these weapons you wield are huge, similar to god eater weapons. These weapons are cool and can double as guns to hit the enemies long range. Setting up battle formations is important too as you can set the support characters in the back and the attackers in the front. Overall combat- 8/10

NOW- for the Jiggling boobs....after when the battle, depending on who got in the last blow, they will show an animation of them twirling around or what not. During this, the girls boobs jiggle perfectly. SO if you like that, there ya go. It should also be mentioned that once your uniform takes so much damage, it will be blown off, thus making you fight in your undies until you get it repaired. This is rare and has not happened to me as I repair my stuff frequently. Overall fan service- 7/10- LOL

As for the story, I will not spoil it but you are apart of a military force that fights monsters that have spawn and are destroying the world. As you go along with your team, you will discover a traitor and you will have to discover who it is. This is interesting because most compile hearts games do not have this kind of stuff. The mystery element really makes this games story allot better, and makes you wonder who you should get close too. There is also an affinity scale in which you can tighten your bond with the ladies through interaction. Do not compare this to Persona 5 or you will be let down. But remember this is a much different game with a much lower budget. Overall story- 7/10

The translation is very good and Idea Factory deserves praise for a job well done. The voice acting fits the characters very well and did not annoy me at all. Overall localization- 10/10

Overall this is a good game that has been getting bashed on due to its low budget and the high standards set by Persona 5. If you can look past that and truly enjoy JRPG's, then pick this game up and give it a chance. Also, there is a ton of free dlc that can help you out allot if you like playing harder modes. Id give this game a 7/10 and a 4 on Amazon (I like it).

rating 5.0. An Amazing Game Persona 5 is an excellent game that proves that turn based/narrative driven games are truly the best way to go when it comes to JRPG's.

Persona 5 features a confidant system where you can spend time with friends to increase your bond and unlock skills. It features a romance system and multiple choice responses which range from silly to serious. The persona system consist of capturing persona's through ridiculous dialogue and using them in battle. Persona's have unique abilities and can be fused with one another to create even stronger personas.

Also, Persona 5's story is very serious and uses real world events, such as abuse, rape, drug use, and much more. Making the story meaningful and dramatic. The main character is mostly mute the entire story, but that's no problem as the supporting cast has plenty of lines and they are voiced perfectly. Character design is perfect and the visuals are great as well. One of the best things in this game is it's music. I highly suggest you listen to the sound track.

The only con is the dead lines. You live in the real world and each activity you do passes time, even when it really shouldn't. With that free time you can level up confidants, attributes, or explore mementos (dungeons).

Overall- Persona 5 is a master piece and I highly suggest lovers of RPG's pick it up.

rating 3.0. Cat & Mouse Long story short, Little Nightmares is a creepy game of cat and mouse with bad controls. For me, I did not find the game to be very scary and most of my deaths were due to not having a clue where to go and the controls, which I just couldn't get down. Visually, the game looks great and the ambiance is perfect. The game is also filled with puzzles and tasks, which can sometimes be difficulty to solve, but not too bad.

Overall, this is a fun little game and the developers did a good job, but I'd wait for a sale.

rating 5.0. Recycling has never been so fun! Prey is a single player first person psychological horror game that is very light on the horror, similar to Bioshock. I thoroughly enjoyed every second of this game...except for the ending, which was not very satisfying. The shooting mechanic felt right for this game, and the enemies were smart enough to always give me that constant challenge. Being made by Arkane studios, this game feels and plays allot like Dishonored, which is not a bad thing at all. You upgrade your characters skill tree, which is abundant with options, you can go in guns blazing or stealthy, and your decisions have some impact on the story, but they are so minimal that you do not have to worry about reloading saves.

Graphically on the PS4 this game looks great and I never experienced any lag or game breaking glitches. The load times are longer than I'd like but it makes sense. The story is good and keeps you guessing the entire time, and human interaction is slim, but made no difference. The ship you are on is huge and contains allot of materials to read, and audio logs to listen to. That said, I generally hate that stuff, but this game made it feel meaningful and I began to enjoy all of the readable content.

This game is also full of side quests to compliment the story and often lead to upgrading material and new weapons. These side quests are fun and really let you explore sections of the ship that have never crossed your mind. You can also access terminals to read emails, download schematics, maps, and safe codes giving the world depth and purpose. The crafting system is excellent as almost everything on the ship can be turned into materials. Giving you allot of options- ammo, guns, med kits, skill points, armor repair kits, and etc.

Prey is an excellent game that earns it right to be called one of the best games made this year. I would give it a 9.5/10 due to the unsatisfying ending.

rating 4.0. An excellent Sequel Note: I have completed the Game and have begun a New Game +
This is an excellent sequel with a lot more choice and outcome sequences than it predecessor. The graphics are excellent and the gameplay is awesome, unfortunately the music is too loud during conversation sequences, making it hard to understand what they are saying with sub titles. There are times when the frame rate stutters, but it was a rare occurrence. There is a 4GB update right off the bat, so I'm sure they are aware of the minor performance issues. Technical stuff behind, the character customization of augs is abundant and carter to the experience that you wish to have. I personally was all about stealth and hacking, so my combat skills were minimal but sufficient enough to handle a tick when necessary. Character interaction and dialogue options are abundant as well. You can chose to align with augs, humans, or just be neutral (you know the right thing to do). This game has a replay value of 8/10 in my opinion. The biggest gripe I have is the obvious loose ends at the end of the game, but its no biggy.

rating 2.0. A game without purpose This is a really cool concept, but simply does not work for those who enjoy narrative and purpose. Honestly, there is no purpose. It's a game of discovery, so if you like scanning trees and rocks (which all look alike) then you will love this game. Multiplayer elements and purpose would have made this an outstanding game. Bugs aside this is currently a 2 star game.

rating 3.0. Fun combat makes up for dull story Not as good as I thought it would be, but its not too bad. This game lacks content and the story is kind of bland, half of the time I was just going along on very vague dialog telling me what's up. Graphically, the game runs a good 60fps until you get into a big battle and the character models look good as well. You can tell the game was made on a low budget which is very disappointing because this game has so much potential with it's great combat system and visuals. This game does remind me of Infinite Undiscovery though, which was a hidden gem on the Xbox 360. I hope Square Enix learns from this and supply more funds for the next instalment in the future.

rating 4.0. Great RPG with alot to offer This game literally feels like you are in an 80's mars movie, and I can dig that. The combat is challenging but fun, and the amount of character customization is outstanding. The story and in game missions are fun but some can be tedious due to frequent back tracking through underground passages. The companion system is great too and I have never had an issue with companions not cooperating. This game does start slow, and I know that is a turn off to most, but I am 20 hours in and I am loving it! Be sure to give it a chance because the Technomancer has a lot to offer to those who love new RPG's.

Pros:
Graphics
Level Design
Character Customization
Combat Systems
Companions
Story
Affinity System
Decision Making
Crafting

Cons:
Music
Facial Expressions
Slow Intro

rating 5.0. Unexpectedly Good Censorship is not an issue, to be honest, they still show plenty for you people who like that kind of stuff. Besides that, the gameplay is great and the visuals are good as well. I am very happy that the turn based battle system works so well. The only issue could be the jpop stuff but it's easy to look past.

rating 5.0. Great for people who prefer digital downloads. Great card with a lot of space! I have filled up 40GB so far with lots of PS1, PSP, and PS Vita games. Good product overall, no issues at all.

input:
rating 3.0. Another year of the same (unfortunately) I wanna give this game the benefit of the doubt. But it’s tough and I hope they update some of the franchise mode/exhibition modes in the next few patches, but this one is hard to like.

I’ve always been a Madden fan since as early as 2001 and 2005. I played 20 for a bit and remember playing 2005 for hours and hours. Unfortunately, with the release of MUT in 2010 EA has decided to focus on micro transaction game modes-and to beat them to a bloody pulp while monopolizing the football simulation game market. More microtrasactions, more fake game modes to distract you from a lack of franchise mode, and more lies and fan neglect.

Im not kidding...
If you have a moment look up the news story related to #fixfranchisemode. There’s a whole social movement media against it. As for frachise mode they literally haven’t done anything with it since 17 and who knows if a game like 2005 will ever exist again. As consumers we support this jerky company by buying their game and microtransactions.

Otherwise, it’s okay for the updated roster and graphics, but I don’t intend to spend anymore of my hard earned money and time and effort on this hot pile of garbage like I’ve done in the past. It gets old after a while and this company has no intention to want to try and improve creatively.

6/10- don’t recommend at all

For example, the coin toss is gone, you pick whether to kick, receive, or which end to defend in the menu before the game loads. Is this to save time? Can't be, because they still make the team lineup when it's clearly going to be 2:00 warning or end of quarter. rating 5.0. Lots of fun for this relative newbie gamer/life-long football lover! I've played other versions of Madden NFL before and have even played the 2020 version.  Generally speaking, if you are not a more advanced gamer you probably will only notice the more superficial cosmetic changes from the most recent versions.  If you are a more experienced gamer you will notice slight (and some not so slight) changes in the way players move, their unique abilities,  and interesting coaching options that really give you control of the flow of the game.  Again, this is a lot of fun for a newbie (especially with the difficulty level turned to rookie) as well as long-time gamers.  Unlike other games that require a certain level of skill to have any fun, this game rewards experienced and unexperienced gamers alike.

rating 4.0. Updated: They made it right Updated: They reached out to me and made it right. They have great customer service to fix this. I would recommend working with them again and trying their products.
