rating 5.0. If you’re sceptical don’t be, I’m a certified tech and a happy customer From what I can tell the company that reproduces these are using pretty much the same exact model that Nintendo used from what I can sell in the reason this is because the patent on that has been up for a long time and also up for grabs for manufacturers to be able to utilize and if they use them properly you end up with a quality product which this is. Hopefully this will boost some feedback and help this company out so we can get a price cut wink wink lol but please I encourage anyone else to try these out and be just as impressed as I am.

rating 3.0. Works, ok construction, not zero force connector. This pin replacement works but there are a few issues I have with it.

1) The bottom pins (can't really see the top pins) are all slanted to the left. This appears to be just poor construction. This does not seem to affect the pins making a connection but I can see them getting worn down fast.
2) This is not a zero force connector like the original is. You have to apply a good amount of force to insert the card (like on the top loader nes) and you don't even need to press down on the cart because the top pins have already made contact.

I will probably look at another version in the future but I can at least say this will work for repairing your front loading NES.

rating 4.0. Seems to have helped a bit Got a few cartridges to work on the first try, a few others did not.  That could be the cartridges themselves.  If you're having power flash issues, might as well give this a shot.  I discovered my old pins were just dirty, no bending.  Still I put in the new part.  Overall pretty satisfied with the results.  Shows noticeable improvement.

rating 5.0. Worked right the first time Love it! I bought an NES that wasn't quite working well. After cleaning the contacts with 91% IPA and using canned air to try and fix the problem, I figured the pin must be bad. Plugged the new pin in and lo and behold my games now work. Tetris and I can continue our relationship.
rating 5.0. Great replacement part Perfect fit for replacement. However it didn't fix the common problem for the original NES. But it's not the fault of this part. Great product for replacement!

rating 5.0. Easy install, most games work right away! I was a bit scared cracking open my nintendo, but I decided to go for it.  I didn't even bother to take any pictures as I was taking it apart, and still managed to get it put back together no problem.  If you are still firing up the old NES, this is the way to go for a tune up.

rating 5.0. Great product! This product is working well for me and has not failed me whenever I start up an NES game. I would highly recommend it to anyone who's having issues with their NES detecting games.

rating 1.0. Not worth it. There are better solutions on the market now. This didn't work for me and it's fairly obvious it wasn't user error. If I wanted to straighten pins, I would have just doctored the OEM connector. I installed it anyway, thinking maybe there was something I didn't know about pin placement. Yikes, it was graphic corruption apocalypse on my screen. I put the OEM back and it went back to just having one or two corruptions on the screen, so my intuition was right. I should have sent this right back immediately. There are new products on the market now that don't use these ridiculous pins for the cartridge connection and I think I'll give that a try instead.

rating 5.0. Abundant Entertainment and Gaming I am a CV1 owner (Original Oculus Rift), I love my Rift...and will not part with it. I now have the best of both Oculus worlds. That said, the Quest 2 is a stellar piece of engineering with unbelievable software support from Facebook. I was amazed with all the entertainment available to the Quest 2 that is not available to the PC based Rift. Now I am going to list the accessories I bought that will bring this miraculous piece of technology to a stellar level. You can thank me later. Everything can be bought right here on Amazon.
1) The Bobo MR2 pro Head strap with battery (Purchase an extra battery for unlimited playtime $29) $59.99
2)The Odio one headphones (same specs as the $99 Logitech headphones endorsed by Oculus) I purchased for $19 used (new $34) incredible sound.
3) The Aukeer 3.5mm Audio Cable $6.99 (Short audio cable for Headphones),
4) A MOBOREST 1/4'' (6.35mm) Audio cable adapter $6.99
5) If you intend to use for PCVR, the Syntech Link Cable 16 FT ($21.89)

These accessories will turn your Quest 2 into a VR Beast. Purchase the above for less than the pro elite strap with battery from Oculus (not including the link cable). There is no comparison with the quality and value of the above items as opposed to the Oculus product.

The Oculus Quest 2 as a modern VR competitor and console has no peer.

rating 4.0. Perfect items but not perfect for people wear glass Update:
For people who wear glasses. Please get a pair of insert prescription lens, it gives you a new VR world.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Things I like:
All in one VR, you don’t need to do any cable connection, everything could be connected by WiFi. Oculus provide air link to your PC(still beta version), so IT WORKS on YOUR STEAM ! But it may disconnect occasionally.

Things I DON’T LIKE :
(1), it takes time to wear because I have a glasses. So I need to put VR on, adjust my glass to a comfortable position until I can see clearly. My current glasses has large frame, which doesnt fit into the VR, so I have to use my old glass that has smaller frame to fit, which still make me feel super tight when I put VR on.
(2), All in one device means the processor will generate heat. I don't think this device has cooling system. So if you play the game for a while, your head will feel hot and sweat.

Overall, I still recommend it.

rating 5.0. So amazing As a middle aged female, I was skeptical about ordering this, my son is a big gamer and I never understood the attraction until the oculus 2. I am hooked. Setup was easy. rating 5.0. I am absolutely blown away! I have always loved the idea of VR and I have tried it a handful of times through store kiosks and local arcades that have them. But I was never able to actually own one myself because I never had a PC powerful enough nor the room space to be able to use it effectively. But I recently got a new apartment that is huge and I finally scrapped the extra money together to buy the quest 2 and I have nothing but good things to say about it. I love that it does not require any cables to be able to use it when it is charged so the freedom inside the games feels even more immersive. I also love the picture quality, I was nervous at first seeing as how it is not using a PC to be it's graphical power source but I was wrong, it is gorgeous and I literally feel like I am in another world. I will also say for anyone concerned (I didn't give a crap at all btw), it was just announced yesterday that a Facebook account is no longer required to use this device, so if that was a deal breaker for you then you're in luck! So far I have played Superhot, VR-Chat, Arizona Sunshine, and Vader Immortal and they were all breathtaking experiences and I was in my glory the entire time. The controllers are super accurate and feel great to hold, every button is perfectly positioned on the controllers. I also found that the 128gb model is plenty of storage for how small most of the games are install size wise so I feel there is no need to spend an extra $100 on unneeded storage. One thing that may be an issue for some is the head-strap built onto the headset itself. I had heard from a few people that the (sold separately) strap extension is a MUST for people with even remotely bigger heads, I find as someone with a big head that the default head-strap is completely fine for me. Luckily I opted to take a risk and not buy the separate strap extension because it would have been a waste of money for me. You may want it if you wear glasses though which I do not. But, obviously no device is perfect so I will say what I do not like is how short the battery life is but I suppose it is a good thing because it forces you to give your eyes a break so it is what it is. All and all, I would highly recommend this device to everyone. It is amazing.

rating 5.0. The most enjoyable purchase that I’ve made in quite some time! I’m new to VR and had no idea what I’ve been missing! This is unbelievable! The Oculus 2 provides enjoyment for so many of my different interest, I honestly had no idea how useful it would be. If you’re looking to purchase a piece of exercise equipment for daily cardio look no further, the Oculus makes your workout so much fun and time flies by so quickly. It’s not only amazing for physical fitness, the brain gets its share of exercise as well. After a fun filled workout, I typically enjoy some virtual travel and often go chill at some of the most peaceful destinations on earth at the click of a button. On lazy days you can kick back in your recliner and have just as much fun doing a number of different things available on the Oculus as well. The content available is growing at a very rapid rate, I think that no matter what your interests are you’ll find so much to enjoy and will not regret this purchase. I can’t describe how life changing this has been and I honestly feel it has improved my health and quality of life. I could never really get interested in cardio or any type of exercise and stick with it until the Oculus came into my life, my health paid the price and statistics say the same for so many others in this country. Take action and encourage others to do so as well!

rating 5.0. I am loving it... After reading so many FB comments *I barely go on FB* I convinced myself I would never be able to figure out how to get it to work when it arrives. Doom, Gloom and a lot of Zoom - if I couldn't figure this out by myself I was out a lot of money! By the way, has anyone else notice the huge price comarison of the Quest? Anywhere from $299 - $699 for the same Oculus Quest 2? Anyway, jus sayin...

Today - It finally arrived and boy was I terrified opening the box. I stared, should I send it back - can I do this... well, a long time later I did it, I tore the plastic and out came my Oculus Quest 2 with two cool controllers.

Within 15 minutes I was hooked up to WiFi, FB and playing tennis. I got tired and did meditation for 10 minutes just floating in space. The realty is - its plain awesome! It looked so real that I found myself suddenly afraid of heights! Then it was time to charge the quest - which took around 2 hours.

Yes, it was that easy!!! I love it! So, lesson to everyone - if I can do it all by MYSELF you can too - because you are awesome! Especially if you reading my review. Thanks!

A quick note: You have the option to move the eye piece to make you vision clearer. The eye piece does not rotate. You must take your thumbs and slide right or left.

rating 2.0. Incredibly Frustrating Setup. Sketchy, unnecessary prerequisites To get right to the point, I cannot recommend this device. While it did eventually get set up, there are unneeded hoops to jump through that cause problems both on the technical side and on the trust side.

To set up, you are required to have or open a Facebook account. I get that Facebook bought Oculus, so that's where it stems from, but there is no valid technical reason for this requirement. Why is this needed? Simply to boost FB registration numbers? To make a grab for personal info? It does not inspire trust.

The second irritating, unnecessary hoop is requiring you to install a mobile device app and to pair the headset. I ran into problems with this which took using a second phone to get it accomplished. Thankfully the household had a second device available to use. Then, once that was finally done it seems unneeded any more since the headset works without doing anything on that paired phone. Tech support told me that my mesh wifi could be the problem. Really? All a mesh wifi does is present a single SSID to devices connecting to it, how can that interfere?

Ultimately it did get working, and tech support was responsive, so it gets a second star for that.

rating 4.0. Significantly better than the Oculus Go that preceded it More immersive sound and reactive responsiveness. Picture quality also much closer to high end.
One star off for not making it abundantly clear that games from prior unit could not be retrieved directly from my already used by me Oculus account. It appears that you must upload to a PC first, then download to the Oculus 2. A direct from account download would have made so much more sense but likely would have raised the price point of $299 definitely the sweet spot for this unit.

I truly could not decide if I should buy this (They double storage and kept it same price as it was during first release of the Quest-2.). For those who have asked if we're aware that the price for these units runs from $299 on PRIME to $699 (from some individual sellers), here's the deal. Originally released at $299 with 64GB storage. Then units were discontinued due to issue with head straps. Oculus team reworked the head straps but the units weren't available for awhile. So those who purchased them decided they would sell their remaining units for more than twice the Prime price w/o the extra storage. A lot of disgruntled users who apparently didn't stay current on the Oculus news of this item paid the $699 because they didn't realize same unit with more storage was being released by PRIME for the original $299. Oh well.

rating 5.0. I'm Going To Go Ahead And Say 'Amazing'! I'm very impressed with this headset! I wrestled with whether to pay $100 more for double the storage capacity, but after perusing apps in the Oculus store and averaging app sizes, I determined there was too little value in that. Down the road, if I get REALLY into VR in general, I might wind up regretting that decision, but I honestly think that would be the only thing I'd regret about this device.

Oculus was experiencing some kind of server issue that prevented me from completing registration and setup right away, so it was about 4 hours after I unboxed it that I was able to use it. And boy, was I blown away! I still have a VR headset that requires a phone be stuck in it, but I haven't used it in a while for just that reason. This one, of course, doesn't need that, and the resolution is really, really good. Your IPD (Inter-pupillary distance), or the distance between your eyes, can be accommodated by selecting from 3 different widths. I read one review that said the maximum is 68mm, but their IPD was 72. In that case, the image will appear blurry. For that reason, I suggest checking your IPD before you purchase.

What's in the box is simple, the headset, two controllers (with batteries), a spacer for those who wear glasses and a silicone piece that makes it comfortable to wear against your face. It's very well balanced and doesn't feel heavy to me, but there are optional head straps you can get if you find it too front-heavy. I also like the passthrough feature that lets you see your real-world environment if/when you need to.

I was pleasantly surprised at the quality and immersiveness of the sound, but I will probably get some ear buds soon so I can get a fuller volume level (I usually have the volume on about 6-7). But it sounds great. The 'Home' environment, which is set up like a house or apartment, is really cool, and there are several different designs available.

I found the battery life, stated as about two hours, to be pretty accurate, but I bought a 10-foot USB cable so that I could have it plugged in and playing while it's charging (yes, you can use it fully while it's charging). There is a head strap available with a place for an optional battery to extend use time without worrying about a cable, but that doesn't seem necessary to me, at least not at this point. I just got done using it, and I was in VR for about 3 1/2 hours, and battery life was still 82%. So far, I have used it for probably 20 hours total since I've had it, and the controller batteries are still at 100%. I'm sure that depends on what you're doing, though.

I have watched a couple of movies, and with the ability to have a curved screen (or flat if you prefer) that views even larger than my 60" TV, it's just amazing. I've also played a few games, watched some videos of dancers, concerts, and awesome, immersive tours of places like Switzerland, Italy, the Matterhorn, Great Lakes, under-sea stuff and so many more. All of it has been awesome. And so far, I have found enough good, free content that I haven't gotten around to purchasing anything yet!

Another effective feature is the voice control. You can do almost anything using voice commands, including powering the device off. I haven't explored that feature too deeply yet, but I have started and stopped apps and games, checked local time and weather and performed several other functions just by telling it what I want. You just double-press the 'Oculus' button to get it to listen for voice commands

One complaint I know many will have, is that setting up the device REQUIRES a Facebook account. BUT, you can set it so that your FB profile info, including your profile name, will not be revealed in VR if you happen to do some multi-person thing, and neither is your Oculus username revealed to your FB contacts, so that doesn't bother me at all. The only thing I wish, and this is so minor it's really almost petty, but I do wish it came in black. That isn't even worth deducting stars over. Overall, I just love it, and I highly recommend it!

rating 3.0. Heavy on your head! This a fun toy to view videos attend concerts, Billie Eilish was very cool and I’m sure play games. The problem is it’s not well balanced, it becomes very uncomfortable after a short period of time. And, it gets hot! You will constantly be adjusting the headset. Now they supply a silicone covering for the foam around the facile part that goes against your face. It makes your face sweat and slides down even when you have adjusted the Quest to your head and face. There isn’t any circulation and it also sometimes fogs up the lenses. So, I purchased the Elite strap which adds more weight, but is supposed to stabilize the headset. Meh!
Really what they need to do is reduce the weight of the product. So much weight up front makes the headset uncomfortable after a short period of time. Someone suggested to purchase the Elite Strap with extra battery pack on the back. This is good if you want to extend gaming time, but adds more weight to the headset. Do I really want to add more weight to an already heavy headset? I don’t think so. Again it’s an ingenious wireless gaming product/toy I hope in the future they figure out how to reduce the weight by miniaturizing the contents of the headset. I gave 3 stars because it’s amazing technology, however it’s not perfect by an stretch. Game on!

rating 3.0. What I don't like about the cable is that although the volume and ... Update 2020: My wire finally died although I've not used it much the past couple of years. I found another one on eBay for under $9.00. The only real problem I had with this cable when using with an Xbox Elite controller was that you could never tell whether or not your mic was muted and the jack that went into the controller always made a scratchy, annoying sound... which is inexcusable for Turtle Beach.

I bought this cable to use with my Turtle Beach X12 Headset and Xbox One Elite controller (I was told this also works with the regular Xbox One controller), and it does. If you have an older XB1 controller without a headset jack, you will also need to buy the adapter (separate item, can be found on the Turtle Beach website) that connects directly to the controller. This cable would then plug into that adapter and also into the inline volume/chat/mic mute controls on the Turtle Beach headset (IF your TB headset has the inline controls).

For Turtle Beach headsets similar to the X12, which has the inline volume, chat volume and mic mute function on the wire, this cable replaces the shorter Talkback (removable) cable that normally goes in the jack between the inline volume/chat/mic mute controls and the Xbox controller. You plug one end into the jack where that Talkback cable normally goes and the other into your controller (or adapter, if needed).

What I don't like about the cable is that although the volume and mute button on this cable are both inline, there's no light to let you know when you are muted. Also, my X12 headset has Mic Monitor (this would let you know that your mic was on by allowing you to hear yourself in the headset when you talked), but this cable cancels that out. I plan on buying another headset in the future so I'll have one for my 360 and another for my One, so hopefully it won't bother me too much for now. Also, I don't care much for having to add another wire to my headset, but I guess this is the way it has to be done since a regular 2.5 mm to 3.5 mm adapter won't work.

current_product_reviews: rating 5.0. Let's Older Turtle Beach X42 Headset Work, With No Issues --  With the NEW Xbox One Controller (3.55mm input jack on it). Purchased this item to get my old Turtle Beach, X42 headset to work with the new Xbox One controller, with the 3.5mm input.  I tried several other 2.5mm to 3.5mm adapters and NONE of them worked.  This item has a 2.5mm input, for the older 2.5mm, male plug on the X42.  The end that plugs into the controller has the 3.5mm plug, which plugs into the new Xbox One controller, with 3.5mm input hole.  Both chat and game sounds work normally!!  It works straight out of the box, no fuss, no issues.  If you have the older Xbox controller, you will need to get the stereo adapter for the controller, and test that set-up out.
other_product_reviews:
rating 5.0. Don't use Turtle Beach Controller Adapter, If You Have A Newer Model Xbox One Controller I got an Xbox One and had to buy a new headset--figures. I read the glowing, universal reviews for these and bought it. Right off the bat, there was an issue, I could barely hear people talking. When I attempted to turn chat volume up, game volume had to go down. To hear people chatting, I had to turn the game sounds down to almost nothing, and the chat volume was then slightly lower than medium volume. This was fine, as long as you did not want to hear the game sounds--NOT!! I was using the headset with the supplied Turtle Beach controller adapter. When I bought the Xbox One, I was told that I had one of the newer controllers, which could handle a headset. I had presumed that they meant that this required the usage of a controller adapter, such as the one Turtle Beach supplied. I removed the adapter and just plugged the headset into the controller--EUREKA!!!!! I was able to have game volume and hear chat o.k. I found that when you use the controller to access the 'settings' on the Xbox, that there was a section to the left, which had recognized the headset, and gave you control settings for the headset, through the Xbox console. I adjusted those settings and now am happy. I have put the supplied Turtle Beach controller adapter in a drawer, where it will remain!

UPDATE:

I recently found the Turtle Beach, X50 headset. This headset is advertised as being specifically for the Xbox One controller. This is the controller with the 3.5mm plug hole, on the left side of controller, which was introduced in June, of 2015. This headset does not come with the controller stereo adapter, as it is not needed for the new controller. To control the volumes/mic on the X50 headset, you use the 'settings' menu, from the Xbox One. It gives you control for game/chat volume, or turning the mic on/off. This is not an amplified headset, like the XO One. The cost of this headset is $34, currently, as the normal price.
